## [v20.0.68]  -  2026-07-17  -  G3 correction: repo-wide roll re-targeted one patch forward after an exact-value collision

v20.0.68 — corrects a CI regression introduced by the v20.0.67 repo-wide roll (previous entry, below). That roll brought all 11 canonical version markers into agreement at v20.0.67, and did fix the two checks it targeted ("QC static invariants" and "Verify all version markers agree"), but the SAME commit turned a third, previously-green check red: "G3 — skill content change requires skill-version.txt bump."

- **root cause:** G3 fails a push whenever a file changes inside a skill directory (`XX-<name>/`) without that skill's own `skill-version.txt` ALSO appearing in the same diff. `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt` and `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` had each independently been pre-bumped, by Unit 118's own G3-compliance commits, to the exact value (`20.0.67`) the repo-wide roll's target also landed on — a same-value collision. So when the roll touched those skills' OTHER tracked marker files (`_index.json`, `_qc-summary.md`, `browser_manager.sh`/`.py`) to bring them to v20.0.67, `skill-version.txt` itself produced no diff (already correct), and G3's diff-presence heuristic read that as "content changed, version not bumped."
- **why the standing fix doesn't directly apply:** this repo has hit an adjacent case before (`d00c4b5c`, "re-bump skill-version.txt... G3 race with concurrent merge-train") and resolved it by bumping the affected skill's marker one patch AHEAD of the colliding value. That works for `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt`, which is not one of the 11 drift-checked canonical markers — but it does NOT work for `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt`, which IS one of the 11 (`scripts/version-markers.json`); bumping it ahead alone would have reopened "Verify all version markers agree" instead of closing G3.
- **fix:** re-targeted the whole repo-wide roll one more patch forward, v20.0.67 -> v20.0.68, via `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.68`. Every marker file touched by the roll (both `skill-version.txt` files included) now carries a genuine value delta in this commit, so G3's diff-presence check passes honestly — no cosmetic/no-op edits, no CI-logic changes.
- **v20.0.67 is not reverted or rewritten:** it stayed a real, correctly-agreeing 11-marker state for one commit and keeps its own valid annotated tag and CHANGELOG entry (this repo's standing doctrine never rewrites released entries); it is simply superseded one commit later, in the same spirit as this repo's existing "renumber on collision" convention (see v20.0.66/v20.0.67's own notes) — applied here post-tag instead of pre-tag, for the first time, because the collision was only discoverable by CI after the v20.0.67 push.
- No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic model added/removed/substituted; client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers. Annotated tag `v20.0.68` (`git tag -a`; `git cat-file -t` == `tag`).

## [v20.0.67]  -  2026-07-16  -  U87 (GK-25) fix leg — golden-quest is now a REAL driver run, not a hand-assembled fixture

v20.0.67 — `skill6-v2/fix/gk25-u87-golden-quest-real-run`: GK-25's own judge (score 6.4, SEND BACK — see `ledgers/evidence/U87-GK-25/README.md`) traced all five of U87's blockers to one root cause: `51-signature-presentation/examples/golden-quest` was hand-assembled (`run_method: provers-direct`) and never actually driven through `deck-intake-driver.py`. This closes 5 of the 5 blockers honestly and reports the 6th as NOT closeable in this pass (no fabricated receipt) — the fixture repair only, no engine/prover code changed.

- **fix (fixture, `51-signature-presentation/examples/golden-quest/`):** replaced the hand-assembled `sp_intake.json` with the real output of walking `deck-intake-driver.py --signature --next`/`--answer` one question at a time (choice-first, q1..q8, frame selection), reusing the fixture's own existing answers verbatim (GK-D3's replay path — no client answer discarded). The result carries a genuine, HMAC-signed, strictly-ascending `turn_ledger_provenance` block that `prove_sp_intake.py`'s `AF-SP-INTAKE-UNPACED` gate verifies PASS as of `--as-of 2026-09-01` — three weeks past the `GRACE_WINDOW_UNTIL = 2026-08-15` cutoff the old record was silently riding on borrowed time from (that cutoff was the most serious of the five blockers and the prior run agent did not report it). The SAME driver's standard `--next`/`--answer` flow (`presentation_type=signature`, `signature_source=from_scratch`) produced `working/copy/intake.json`, which golden-quest had never carried before — `prove_sp_routing.py --run-dir` now exits 0 instead of `AF-SP-TYPE-UNDECLARED` (exit 2). Added `working/copy/arc_allocation.json`, a genuine per-slide converting-arc allocation deterministically derived from the fixture's own `sp_structure.json` phase/tag data (not a stub — `_chk_arc` only tests presence/non-emptiness, so this could have been faked in five minutes and deliberately was not). Added `broken-variants/sp_structure_D_phase_reordered.json` (slide 40 `teaching`→`avatar` on the real deck) + its `REJECTION-RESULTS.json` entry — GK-25 spec:2085 clause 3 names the phase-order check specifically, and no prior archived variant tripped it (the prior run agent's fail-first citations were for the no-pitch and process-integrity checks, not phase-order). `README.md` Reproduce recipe gained the missing `prove_sp_routing.py` line (its absence meant the documented recipe could not surface the routing failure) and a `prove-deck.py`-writes warning.
- **NOT closed, reported honestly:** the prompt-floor receipt. golden-quest's README already documents that this fixture never runs `P4-RENDER` (paid kie.ai image generation) in this environment; `working/prompts/` genuinely does not exist, so `prove_pres_prompt_floor.py --dir` correctly fails closed (exit 3, "not a directory") rather than passing. Closing it honestly means either authoring real 9,000-18,000-char image prompts (a large, separate body of creative work, out of scope for a process-fidelity-of-intake fix) or an owner ruling descoping prompt-floor for a fixture that intentionally never renders. Filed, not faked. See `ledgers/evidence/U87-GK-25/README.md` for the full per-blocker accounting and every receipt's raw captured output.
- **evidence:** `ledgers/evidence/U87-GK-25/` — 6 named receipts in phase order (routing → intake → structure → arc → no-pitch → prompt-floor) with monotonic microsecond timestamps, all freshly captured against this repo, none inherited or hand-written.
- **Ripple:** this is a fixture-content-only change scoped to `51-signature-presentation/`; no code in any prover or `build_deck.py` changed. `51-signature-presentation/skill-version.txt` + `SKILL.md` frontmatter bumped `1.0.10` -> `1.0.11` (G3). The repo-wide 11-marker lockstep roll + annotated tag is the merge-writer's step at actual merge time, not this branch's — this PR is a draft and is not merged. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic model added/removed/substituted. **Note (conflict-resolution rebase):** this unit originally targeted v20.0.65 before this branch had seen U78 or U110 land on `main`; renumbered to v20.0.67 while rebasing onto current `main` (which had independently landed v20.0.65 = U78 and v20.0.66 = U110 in the meantime) to avoid a version-number collision — same convention U110 itself used against U78. No repo-wide version-marker files touched by this renumbering (heading text only); the atomic 11-marker lockstep roll + annotated tag remains the merge-writer's step at actual merge time.
- **Ripple (repo-wide roll executed here):** the deferred step named above — "the repo-wide 11-marker lockstep roll + annotated tag is the merge-writer's step at actual merge time" — is executed by this commit: `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.67` rolled all 11 tracked repo version markers v20.0.66 -> v20.0.67 in lockstep (also rolls `06-ghl-install-pages`'s markers per the script's own unconditional lockstep — both were already at `v20.0.67` from Unit 118's own G3 compliance work, so no diff landed there). This tag also finalizes the merge-train batch that landed on `main` since v20.0.66, including Unit 118 (funnels department — the 23rd mandatory floor department; `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt` + `SKILL.md` frontmatter independently bumped to `20.0.67` at merge time, per G3) — one batched tag for the accumulated landings, same convention as v20.0.63. ## [v20.0.66]  -  2026-07-16  -  U110 (E5-5/G2d) — provisioning caller-wiring for the U108 opt-out contract file

v20.0.66 — `skill6-v2/U110`: U108 built `department-optout-sync.py` (derives the machine-readable `provisioning/department-optout.json` opt-out contract from the interview's provenance-gated department decisions) but its own CHANGELOG entry (v20.0.61) named the residual explicitly: "provisioning caller-wiring ... routed, not fixed here (owned by U110)". With no caller, the script was referenced only by itself, its own test, and the CHANGELOG — `provisioning/department-optout.json` was NEVER produced on a real box, so the Command Center board (U110's own CC leg, `blackceo-command-center`) read a file nobody wrote. This IS that caller.

- **fix:** `resume-workforce-build.sh` — the ONE durable, recurring provisioning-flow driver on every box (its own header: "the ONLY autonomous-recovery layer in the workforce-build pipeline") — now invokes `department-optout-sync.py` unconditionally on every tick, BEFORE any of the terminal-state/parked/stuck-cap early-exit branches, so an opt-out recorded via `record-dept-decision.sh` at any point during or after the build is reflected promptly, not only at the very end. The workspace dir is `mkdir -p`'d first so the sync script's own OC_ROOT-style path resolution never falls back to a repo-relative path on a brand-new box. Fire-and-forget by design (exit 0 = synced, exit 1 = synced with >=1 unconfirmed anomaly — both are success; the call is never a build blocker). The wiring block is bounded by `U110-OPTOUT-SYNC-BEGIN`/`-END` sentinel comments for programmatic mutation-testing.
- **test:** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-u110-optout-caller-wiring.sh` (10 assertions) — runs `resume-workforce-build.sh` as a real subprocess in a hermetic `$HOME` sandbox and observes the contract file it produces on disk; never calls the sync function directly (that would only re-prove U108's own suite). Proves: the caller fires unconditionally even with no build yet started; a real provenance-gated, warning-acknowledged decline of a FLOOR department (`general-task` — declinable-with-warning per the ratified 2026-07-16 ruling that `general-task` is NOT structurally exempt like `ceo`/`master-orchestrator`) flows end-to-end into the written file (`floorStatus: floor-confirmed`, `lossWarningShown: true`); an UNCONFIRMED floor decline (missing `lossWarningAck`) lands in `unconfirmed`, never silently in `optedOut`; and a mutation guard programmatically excises the sentinel-bounded wiring block from a copy of the script and confirms the contract file stops being produced (deleting the call site turns the suite RED). Every assertion independently proven to FAIL on the pre-fix commit and PASS on this fix. Wired into `skill23-provisioning-tests.yml` CI (suite 11/11).
- **Consistency:** the `general-task` case in T2 above exercises, through the real caller (not a direct function call), the operator ruling that onboarding's reading governs — `general-task` IS declinable (with its mandatory functionality-loss warning), only `ceo`/`master-orchestrator` stay structurally exempt. Verified against `department-floor.py`'s `HARDCODED_MANDATORY` list (which includes `general-task` as a normal floor department) and `canonical_decline.py`/`department-loss-warning.py` (neither carries any `general-task` special-case): the ONB code already reflects the ruling; this unit builds on top of it without altering it.
- **CC leg** (`blackceo-command-center`, `skill6-v2/U110`): board-column projection reading the actual provisioned set (no orphan/ghost columns; catch-all honored) — see that repo's own CHANGELOG for the board-side leg.

No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic model added/removed/substituted; client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers. Branch pushed, not merged — the annotated tag is the merge-writer's step at actual merge time. Only `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt` + `SKILL.md`'s inline version marker were bumped to `20.0.66` (G3 compliance for this push touching `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/`) — the other 10 lockstep repo-version markers are intentionally NOT touched here; the atomic all-11-marker ripple is the merge-writer's batched step, same convention as U109 (v20.0.64). **Note:** this unit originally targeted v20.0.65, but U78 landed first and took that number (merge-train batch, same day) — renumbered to v20.0.66 before any tag existed for this unit; no collision, no race.

## [v20.0.64]  -  2026-07-16  -  U109 (E5-4/G2c) — interview provisioning merges onto the department floor, never replaces it

v20.0.64 — `skill6-v2/U109`: `build-workforce.write_chosen_departments_artifact()` — the durable "chosen department list" writer every downstream consumer (Skill 32 seeding, `department-floor.py`, Command Center) reads as authoritative — was a blind overwrite. A call with a SMALLER `selected_departments` set than a prior call (a second provisioning run, a partial/aborted interview, or a late single-department edit) silently WIPED every department the prior, larger call had already recorded, with no decline and no audit trail behind the loss. Fixed to MERGE (`floor ∪ derived`), never replace, honoring only an EXPLICIT, provenance-gated decline (the same mechanism U107/U108 already use).

- **fix:** `write_chosen_departments_artifact()` now resolves the full prior chosen-set from every source that can carry it — the on-disk `departments.json` artifact AND build-state's own `canonicalReconciliation.chosenDepartments.slugs` record — before writing, so neither a corrupt/truncated artifact nor a falsy `company_dir` on the production path (`build_from_config`'s two call sites never pass `company_dir` explicitly) can silently disarm the merge and wipe build-state's record. Both the on-disk artifact write and the build-state write are now ATOMIC (temp file + `os.replace`), closing the self-inflicted half of the bug: the prior non-atomic write could itself manufacture the exact truncated/corrupt read that defeated the guard. Dedup and decline comparisons now run on ONE normalized bare-slug identity throughout, so a legacy id-only entry (`{"id": "dept-marketing"}`, no `"slug"` key) collapses onto the same department as a bare-slug entry instead of surviving as a duplicate.
- **test:** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-u109-floor-wipe-regression.sh` (10 assertions) — reproduces the wipe against the unguarded function, then proves: a second smaller run and a partial/aborted-interview-shaped run both leave `floor ∪ prior set` intact; only an explicit decline drops a department; a corrupt/truncated artifact recovers from build-state instead of being trusted; a falsy `company_dir` on the production call path recovers build-state's prior record instead of wiping it; a legacy id-only prior entry dedups instead of duplicating. Every assertion independently proven to fail on the pre-fix code and pass on the fix. Wired into `skill23-provisioning-tests.yml` CI (suite 10/10).
- **CC leg** (`blackceo-command-center`, `skill6-v2/U109`): `reseedWorkspacesFromConfig` was already additive-only (upsert, never delete) — added a regression case to `tests/unit/floor-department-invariant.test.ts` proving a shrunk `departments.json` on a later reseed never removes a previously-provisioned department; verified non-vacuous by temporarily patching the function to prune absent-from-manifest workspaces (test correctly failed) and confirming a clean revert restored the pass.

- **Ripple:** `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.64` rolled all 11 tracked repo version markers v20.0.63 -> v20.0.64 in lockstep to satisfy G3 (this push touches `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/`) — also rolls `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` + `SKILL.md` + `browser_manager.sh`/`.py` version markers per the script's own built-in lockstep (unconditional on every repo-version bump, not conditional on this push touching that skill dir). No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic model added/removed/substituted; client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers. Branch pushed, not merged — the annotated tag is the merge-writer's step at actual merge time.

## [v20.0.63]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 merge-train batch: green-gate recovery + 6 QC-passed units landed (U79, U80, U100, U113, U114, U93)

v20.0.63 — Continues the serial merge-train. GREEN GATE FIRST: main was still RED
after v20.0.62's department-guides fix — fixing that step unmasked a second,
independent `QC static invariants` failure (the Skill 03 P3-06 regression suite's
"well-behaved close" fixture), because the earlier failure had short-circuited the
CI job before the Skill 03 steps ever ran. Root-caused and fixed
(`03-agent-browser/qc-agent-browser.sh` v6.6.2) before landing any unit — see below.
Then landed, in dependency-safe order, 6 independently QC-passed `skill6-v2/*`
units (all scored >= 8.5, all re-verified on the actual merged tree before push,
after re-running each unit's cited gate battery FOREGROUND with an explicit
timeout). U89/U117/U11/U98/U30/U106/U108/U39 were already merged as of v20.0.61
and correctly SKIPPED as already-ancestors of main.

- **fix (repo-wide, pre-unit):** `03-agent-browser/qc-agent-browser.sh` v6.6.1 -> v6.6.2 — the GK-27/U89 lattice-citation tripwire resolved its repo root as `SKILL_DIR`'s parent to locate `docs/tools/check_lattice_citation.py`. That is only the real repo root when the script runs from a plain checkout; run against a staged/installed copy of just the skill directory (the P3-06 regression fixtures' `cp -R` staging, and identically a real `~/.openclaw/skills/03-agent-browser/` install) the checker script itself does not exist, so the gate hard-FAILed unconditionally rather than reporting drift — a permanently-red condition for every such run, not just this test fixture. Fixed to WARN-only SKIP when the checker is absent (staged/installed context) and hard-assert exactly as before when it is present (a real repo checkout) — same "absent skips cleanly" convention as the neighboring GK-28/U90 on-box drift gate. ## [v20.0.59]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U71 (GK-09): clear the WAF/edge 403 on verify-imported (CODE tier); LIVE snapshot chain run still owed to the operator

v20.0.59 — Merges `skill6-v2/U71` (GK-09, QC-passed) into **59-anthology-engine**. Lands the CODE/offline tier of the fix for the WAF/edge 403 blocking `verify-imported`.

- **Fix (59-anthology-engine/scripts/anthology_registry.py):** `verify-imported`'s read of `services.leadconnectorhq.com` was hitting an HTTP 403 that does not match Convert and Flow's own scope-denial JSON signature — an edge/WAF block (`UpstreamBlockedError`), not a proven token problem. The request already carried a browser User-Agent, but it was a reuse of `cover_render.py`'s Kie-CDN `MOZILLA_UA` constant (a malformed 3-segment Chrome build string); `CAF_BROWSER_UA` is now its own dedicated constant, ported byte-for-byte from the proven-live Podcast gate string (`verify-podcast-ghl-workflows.py`).
- **Fallback (59-anthology-engine/scripts/anthology_snapshot.py):** if the edge block persists even with the corrected UA, `verify_imported`'s pipeline read now falls back to a Firebase-JWT internal rail (also ported from the Podcast gate's proven pattern), gated on the CLIENT's own Firebase refresh token (`ANTHOLOGY_GHL_FIREBASE_REFRESH_TOKEN` / `GOHIGHLEVEL_FIREBASE_REFRESH_TOKEN` / `GHL_FIREBASE_REFRESH_TOKEN`) — per the binding 2026-07-14 per-client/client-owned ruling on U69/U70/U71, never a shared operator token. Without that token configured, behavior is unchanged: HELD, retryable. The custom-fields read has no such fallback (no internal-API path proven live for it anywhere in this repo) and stays on the corrected-UA public v2 (PIT) surface only.
- **Test proof (merged tree):** `anthology_snapshot.py self-test` OK (now also covers the GK-09 WAF/edge-403 fallback ladder: no-token HELD unchanged / internal-rail-clears OK / internal-rail-also-blocked HELD); `anthology_registry.py self-test` OK (now also covers the GK-09 Firebase refresh-token alias resolution + internal-rail header shape + `InternalRailClient` mint/empty-token/HTTP-error/success+cache ladder); `python3 -m py_compile` clean on both scripts; adjacent suites `test_stage_s8_deliver.py` + `test_rewrite_preservation.py` (both import these modules) 12/12 PASS.

## [v20.0.57]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U53 (HL/U68) ONB leg: crown-executor single-path static regression lock (D12 decision + live loop-proof still owed)

v20.0.57 — Merges `skill6-v2/U53` (HL/U68, QC-passed) ONB leg into the self-updater guard set. Lands the openclaw-onboarding side of the both-repo U53 unit: a STATIC regression lock proving the "ONE crowned executor / thin wrapper" invariant. Clears the reconciler's fail-closed integrity alarm (U53's ONB leg was the unmerged-but-verified mismatch).

Extracts real source text by anchor (never a hardcoded line range) with loud vacuous-extraction guards; touches no live box / real git remote / pm2 / npm, and consumes only repo file content (no untrusted GitHub event fields — no injection surface).

## [v20.0.56]  -  2026-07-16  -  fix(ledger-reconciler): canonical git-tracked fail-closed core + regression test (verified-but-unmerged leg mismatch alarm)

v20.0.56 — Merges `skill6-v2/fix-reconciler-failclosed` into `shared-utils/`. Ships the canonical, git-tracked data/logic engine for the Skill 6 / Skill 62 ledger reconciler, whose fail-closed detector refuses to let a both-repo unit's shared "verified" status be re-printed, unqualified, next to a repo leg that git proves is NOT merged.

- **Feature (shared-utils/ledger_reconciler_core.py):** the canonical core the operator-local cron wrapper invokes (the wrapper itself stays OUT of this fleet-wide template — it carries box-specific absolute paths that must never ship). The module does no git writes; it reads git state (read-only subprocess against dedicated scratch clones the wrapper already fetched/reset) and (a) renders `recovery-state.md` from truth, (b) patches ledger/checklist rows IN PLACE but ONLY rows whose status cell is EXACTLY "pending" and ONLY with a proven direct merge-parent commit + a tag containing it (never verified/deferred/other), (c) appends timestamped session-log blocks (append-only, never truncates), and (d) detects + loudly surfaces "verified-but-unmerged leg" mismatches: a both-repo unit whose SHARED row reads "verified" while one repo's leg is provably not merged into that repo's main. That is a fail-closed ALARM (`detect_failclosed_mismatches()` + the Integrity Alarms section in `render_recovery_state()`), not a silent table cell — the reconciler still NEVER auto-corrects a verified/deferred/other row (fail-open-by-mutation would be worse); it only refuses to render the contradicted status silently.
- **Test (tests/unit/ledger-reconciler-failclosed.test.py):** fail-first regression proving the U53-shaped bug (ONB leg unmerged, no mergeSha/tag; CC leg merged, tag v6.0.36; one shared row reading "verified") — on the pre-fix module the detector function is absent (AttributeError) and the render prints bare "verified" next to the unmerged ONB branch row; after the fix that row carries "MISMATCH (fail-closed)".
- **Test proof (merged tree):** `python3 -m py_compile shared-utils/ledger_reconciler_core.py` clean; `python3 tests/unit/ledger-reconciler-failclosed.test.py` ALL PASS (fail-closed detection + render surfaces the alarm).
- **Gates green (merged tree):** `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` 11/11; `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (28/28 depts, 10/10 dimensions); `hash-content-manifest.py --check` 435 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas; `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check` 104 files; `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version.sh` PASS; `qc-assert-skill-version-newline.sh` PASS; agent-browser guard main-scan exit 0.
- **Ripple:** `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.56` rolled all 11 tracked repo version markers v20.0.55 -> v20.0.56. Merged via `git merge --no-ff`. ## [v20.0.55]  -  2026-07-16  -  fix(agent-browser-guard): exempt Skill 3's own raw-CLI backstop-conformance battery (exact-path) — main-scan greens (exit 0)

v20.0.55 — Merges `skill6-v2/fix-agent-browser-guard-red` into the agent-browser lifecycle guard. Clears the 4 PRE-EXISTING false-positive violations that had the `guard-agent-browser-managed.sh` main-scan exiting 1, so the main branch's agent-browser guard runs GREEN — unblocking clean gate batteries on every later merge.

- **Fix (scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh):** the managed-only scan (check 1) exited 1 on 4 raw-`agent-browser` calls in `03-agent-browser/scripts/lib-backstop-conformance.sh` (lines 94/105/116/128). These are FALSE POSITIVES: that file is Skill 3's OWN raw-CLI conformance battery — it must exercise the raw `agent-browser` binary to PROVE the underlying contract (open / ref-based snapshot / snapshot-ref stability / fill-by-ref / guaranteed close) that `browser_manager.sh` and Skill 44's Tier-4 fallback are both built on (GK-28/U90, commit 2057aefd). Routing it through 06's `browser_manager.sh` would invert the dependency (03-agent-browser is a lower-level primitive that 06/44 depend ON, never the reverse) and defeat the file's entire purpose. Exempts ONLY that exact path (never a directory-wide carve-out) in BOTH the bash `is_exempt()` and the batched-python hot-path `EXEMPT` set.
- **Test (tests/unit/guard-agent-browser-managed-backstop-conformance-exempt.test.sh):** new negative-fixture proving the exemption is exact-path only — any OTHER file under `03-agent-browser/` that plants a raw, unmanaged `agent-browser` call is still caught. Wired into `.github/workflows/agent-browser-lifecycle-guard.yml`.
- **Test proof (merged tree):** `guard-agent-browser-managed.sh` main-scan now **exits 0** (was exit 1, 4 violations) — SINGLETON gateway intact (managed-only, teardown+lock+breaker present, no per-run names, sentinel present, headless-only enforced); the new backstop-conformance-exempt fixture + the scan-roots + all-skill-dirs negative fixtures ALL PASS.

## [v20.0.54]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U31 (B-U17): GHL page inventory + staged lifecycle (flag -> operator card -> fail-closed execute) + evidence-root retention

v20.0.54 — Merges `skill6-v2/U31` (B-U17, QC-passed) into **06-ghl-install-pages** (Skill 6). Adds a page-inventory engine whose lifecycle actions run through a fail-closed staged approval ladder.

- **Feature (06-ghl-install-pages):** new `tools/ghl_inventory.py` (1,229 LOC) — a page-inventory + staged-lifecycle engine. Every potentially-destructive lifecycle action runs through a **flag -> operator card -> fail-closed execute** ladder: a page is first FLAGGED, then surfaced on an operator card for an explicit human decision, and only then executed — never auto-destructive, and absent an approval it fails closed. Inventory receipts are kept under an evidence-root retention policy.
- **Scheduling:** `schedule/skill6-page-inventory-lifecycle.cron.json` + `scripts/install-page-inventory-lifecycle-cron.sh` install a scheduled inventory sweep; `SKILL.md` (+68) documents the lifecycle doctrine.

## [v20.0.52]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U28 (B-U14): headless D6 coverage audit + stale-browser reaper proof (Mac REAL) + schedule-presence check; VPS + live-run legs OWED

v20.0.52 — Merges `skill6-v2/U28` (B-U14, QC-passed) into **06-ghl-install-pages** (Skill 6). Audits every browser-launch site for D6 headless-refusal coverage, fixes a real exit-code gap on the 5 live-build CLI entry points, and proves the stale-browser reaper + its schedule.

- **(a) Headless D6 coverage audit + exit-75 fix:** new `tools/headless_guard_audit.py` (AST-based, self-updating) enumerates all 8 browser-launch sites and re-verifies the `browser_session()` chokepoint invariant (`gap_count: 0`). **REAL GAP FOUND + FIXED:** the 5 live-build CLI entry points (community/course/pipeline via shared `ghl_run_state.cli_run()`, plus form/survey `main()`) were catching the D6 headed-mode `RuntimeError` with the same generic `except Exception` every build failure hits and returning exit 1 — silently breaking the ENV-MATRIX "headed = forbidden (D6, exit 75)" contract on all 5 sites (only `ghl_builder.py`'s 3 CLI fast paths were correct). Fixed via `ghl_run_state.is_d6_headless_refusal()` + one check before each builder's final return; also corrected a stale docstring claim in `browser_manager.py`.
- **(b)+(c) reaper + schedule proof:** hermetic seeded-orphan test against the REAL `scripts/agent-browser-reaper.sh` proves a fake orphan `.engine` + a dead-pid `ab.lock.d` are reaped in one pass while a live-leased descriptor/lease survive untouched; new `scripts/check-agent-browser-reaper-schedule.sh` (by-name via `openclaw cron list --json`, the same uniform Mac/VPS abstraction `ensure-pipeline-crons.sh` uses — never a native launchd/crontab probe) asserts the reaper is registered.
- **(d) zero-orphan measurement:** `tools/agent_browser_orphan_audit.py` is a read-only audit mirroring the reaper's own live/lease/scoped-Chromium matching logic so the two can never disagree.
- **Two-tier acceptance (like U22/U84):** the offline/hermetic tier + the Mac reaper leg are proven in-repo; the **VPS reaper leg and the live "run a real Skill-6 build against an authorized GHL test location, wait 10 minutes, run the orphan audit" leg are OWED to the operator** (need an authorized VPS box + real GHL credentials — not fabricated here). Both shipped scripts are platform-agnostic (env-derived paths / `openclaw` CLI), so the owed legs need only be run for real, no code changes anticipated.

## [v20.0.51]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U14 (A-U14): blueprint-generation reconciliation (D-A4 Option A) — _section-map.json crosswalk + Section-4 load-contract hazard fix + Skill 23 CHANGELOG backfill

v20.0.51 — Merges `skill6-v2/U14` (A-U14, QC-passed) into the blueprint-generation path. Reconciles blueprint generation against a committed section-map crosswalk (D-A4 Option A) and fixes the Section-4 load-contract hazard in both generation paths.

- **Feature (22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system):** new `personas/_section-map.json` — the crosswalk mapping every one of the 99 on-disk persona blueprints to its section structure — plus its generator + drift-lock `scripts/build-section-map.py`. `build-section-map.py --check` fails loudly if the committed map ever drifts from the 99 blueprints on disk, so the crosswalk can never silently rot.
- **Feature (shared-utils):** `persona_for_job.py` extended (+105) so blueprint generation reconciles against `_section-map.json` (Option A), with the **Section-4 load-contract hazard fixed in BOTH generation paths**. The never-naked contract (a job always gets a governed persona/blend, even degraded, with an honest warning) is preserved, including blend mode.

## [v20.0.50]  -  2026-07-16  -  Skill 6 U9 (A-U9): exemplar convention + write-time injection (CALIBRATION-ONLY clause + honest receipts)

v20.0.50 — Merges `skill6-v2/U9` (A-U9, QC-passed) into **06-ghl-install-pages** (Skill 6) + **35-social-media-planner**. Adds an exemplar convention and a write-time injection seam so a build can calibrate against a shipped gold exemplar without ever copying it.

- **Feature (shared-utils):** new `shared-utils/exemplar_injection.py` (381 LOC) + `shared-utils/test_exemplar_injection.py` (15 tests). An on-disk exemplar pack is `WHY-GOOD.md` + `gold-output.md` + `provenance.json`; the injector selects a matching pack by asset kind (Skill 6 lead / buyer / event; Skill 35 social packs) and injects the gold output into the generation prompt under a hard **CALIBRATION-ONLY** clause — the model is told to match the exemplar's quality bar and structure, never to reproduce its text. Every call writes an honest receipt: the sha256 of the exact exemplars injected, `injected=true/false`, and a no-match call still writes an honest `injected=false` receipt (never a silently fabricated one). Each shipped pack carries an `llm_content_review` provenance receipt rather than a name-grep.
- **Feature (06-ghl-install-pages):** the write-time injection seam is wired into `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/skill6_convergence.py` (+121 lines) and covered by `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/test_a_u9_exemplar_injection.py` (acceptance (a)-(d) against the actually-shipped packs). Exemplar packs shipped: `exemplars/lead/clarity-call-optin`, `exemplars/buyer/premium-mastermind-sales-letter`, `exemplars/event/...`, and `35-social-media-planner/exemplars/founder-story-carousel-caption`; `35-social-media-planner/references/playbook.md` documents the pack convention.
- **CI:** `.github/workflows/funnel-automation-libraries-guard.yml` extended to gate `06-ghl-install-pages/exemplars/**`, `35-social-media-planner/exemplars/**`, `shared-utils/exemplar_injection.py`, and `shared-utils/test_exemplar_injection.py`, plus a dedicated exemplar-injection selftest + acceptance-suite step — the packs, the injector, and its receipts can no longer drift silently.
- **Test proof (merged tree):** funnel-automation-libraries-guard full sequence GREEN — `check-funnel-automation-library-drift.py` OK (38 funnels, 0 broken/phantom refs), `persona_crosswalk.py --validate` 0 unresolved / all-canonical, `guard-fab-qc-gate.sh` PASSED, flex/cli/funnel_matcher_cli/persona_bundle_ladder/copy_persona_blend_seam selftests ALL PASS; matcher+fab+producer+convergence+U9 pytest suites **106 passed, 3 skipped**; `fab-qc.test.py` / `fab-artifact.test.py` / `persona-crosswalk.test.py` OK; `exemplar_injection.py` selftest ALL PASSED + `test_exemplar_injection.py` **15/15**; `skill6_convergence.py` selftest ALL PASSED; `prove_skill6_block_u22.py` ALL LANDED CHECKS PASSED.
- **Gates green (merged tree):** `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` 11/11; `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (28/28 depts, 10/10 dimensions); `hash-content-manifest.py --check` 435 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas; `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check` 104 files; `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version.sh` PASS; `qc-assert-skill-version-newline.sh` PASS.

## [v20.0.48]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 U85 (GK-23/D18): one-question-at-a-time UNFAKEABLE at the record layer

v20.0.48 — Merges `skill6-v2/U85` (GK-23/D18, QC-passed) into the presentation intake path. Moves the "ask exactly one question at a time, wait for the answer" guarantee from prompt discipline (fakeable) to a deterministic RECORD-layer gate.

- **Fix (23-ai-workforce-blueprint + 51-signature-presentation):** `scripts/deck-intake-driver.py` now writes a per-turn provenance ledger (turn stamp + signature) as intake proceeds, and `51-signature-presentation/scripts/prove_sp_intake.py` gains the `AF-SP-INTAKE-UNPACED` record gate. A batch-dumped multi-question turn after the grace window, a missing turn for an answered question, a bad/forged turn signature, or any single turn carrying multiple questions now deterministically fails (exit 2, `AF-SP-INTAKE-UNPACED`); a genuinely driver-paced intake — or an unpaced intake still within the grace window with no forged provenance — passes. The step can no longer be faked by an agent claiming it asked one at a time.
- **Test proof (merged tree):** `prove_sp_intake.py --self-test` ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED, including the six new GK-23/D18 turn-ledger provenance cases (driver-paced VALID; batch-dumped-post-grace, multi-question-turn, bad-signature, missing-turn-for-answered-q all -> `AF-SP-INTAKE-UNPACED`; within-grace-no-provenance VALID); `tests/unit/presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh` **92/92 PASS**; `python3 -m py_compile` clean. (Skill 56's separate `prove_sp_intake.py` copy already differed from Skill 51's on `origin/main` — independent provers, no lockstep; U85 correctly touches the Skill 51 copy only.)
- **Gates green (merged tree):** `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` 11/11 at v20.0.48; `hash-content-manifest.py --check` (435 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas); `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (10/10 dimensions); frontmatter + newline + no-client-names PASS; `check-docs-language.py` 0 new occurrences.

## [v20.0.42]  -  2026-07-15  -  Ollama Cloud routing doctrine: baseUrl gains the `/v1` OpenAI-completions suffix (docs + selector comment)

v20.0.42 — Fixes the Tier-1 Ollama Cloud `baseUrl` recorded in the model-routing doctrine so it carries the `/v1` OpenAI-completions path. `https://ollama.com` (no `/v1`) points the OpenAI-completions client at the marketing HTML site, which returns non-JSON and silently fails the turn; `https://ollama.com/v1` is the correct OpenAI-compatible chat-completions base.

- **Fix (`docs/MODEL-ROUTING.md`):** Universal Fallback Order table, Tier 1 — Ollama Cloud row — `baseUrl=https://ollama.com` -> `baseUrl=https://ollama.com/v1`.
- **Fix (`shared-utils/select_model.py`):** PREFERENCE CASCADE comment, TIER 1 line — `(baseUrl https://ollama.com)` -> `(baseUrl https://ollama.com/v1)`. The edit is inside a comment block; no selector logic changed.
- **Deliberately left unchanged:** the native `ollama` provider standard in `docs/OLLAMA-PROVIDER-BY-PLATFORM.md` and its enforcer `scripts/qc-assert-ollama-provider-platform.sh` use `api: "ollama"` (the native Ollama client, not the OpenAI-completions client), for which `https://ollama.com` with no `/v1` is correct. No provider config template hardcodes an OpenAI-completions ollama-cloud baseUrl. No live box runtime config touched — repo source only.

## [v20.0.41]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 62: Cinematic and Web Funnel Engine — new client-facing engine (U1–U23, ONB)

v20.0.41 — Merges `skill62/cinematic-engine` (the engine-build-QC'd Cinematic and Web Funnel Engine, build units U1–U23) as the new skill **62-cinematic-web-funnel-engine**. The engine turns ONE intake brief into a deployed, conversion-capable, scroll-controlled website/funnel whose visual story is AI-generated, driven by a deterministic P0–P16 fail-closed phase spine.

- **Feature (new skill 62):** `run_cinematic_web_funnel.py` manifest-driven no-skip orchestrator behind a run-scoped nonce front door (`cinematic-web-funnel-entry.sh`, ADR-6); every phase passes on artifacts + a deterministic gate-script exit code, never an agent's claim; a signed PROCESS-CERTIFICATE (`prove_certificate.py`, HMAC nonce) is issued only when every mandatory gate passes contiguously. Covers intake/brief-lock (P1), content-methodology router (P2/P3, delegated to Skill 49/56 — sacred copy never duplicated), budget forecast + hard spend cap + approval (`AF-CWFE-PAID-GATE`), visual-journey/scene planner (P4), concept board + anchor approval + scene-image generation (P6/P7), draft/final video + connectors (P8/P9), FFmpeg boundary-frame extraction/re-encode + seam QC (P10), Next.js/TypeScript scroll-scrub site template (P11), conversion/GHL wiring delegated to Skill 6/44 (P12), headless-browser mobile/reduced-motion/accessibility/performance QC gate (P13), Vercel + Blob deployment adapters (P14/P15), and a GHL whole-page iframe embed package (`frame-ancestors` allowlist, dynamic height, no nested-scroll traps), plus full unit/integration/e2e/adversarial suites.

Skill folders now 62 (01–62; 57 active + 5 archived); client-facing skills 28. Deployed output is a STATIC site; content authoring runs on the CLIENT's own providers, never Anthropic in any client runtime path.

## [v20.0.40]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 U92 (X/U-X2): docs-language CI guard + version-marker-tolerant history carve-out (QC 9.4, ONB)

v20.0.40 — Merges `skill6-v2/U92` (master unit U92, Section X id X/U-X2) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build: a documentation-language CI guard that fails the build when a NEW line in a changed doc file (`*.md`/`*.mdx`/`*.rst`/`*.txt`) introduces the operator-banned coded term that U91 verified scrubbed out of MASTER SPEC v2. Scope is ADDED lines only; three allowlist carve-outs (git-history resurfacing, third-party vendor literals, legacy filenames pending the U30/U93 renames) never touch the repo's existing footprint.

- **Feature:** `scripts/check-docs-language.py` (+ `scripts/docs-language-allowlist.json`, `.github/workflows/docs-language-guard.yml`, `scripts/tests/docs-language-guard.test.py`) — the guard, its allowlist, its CI workflow, and its hermetic unit-test battery (21/21 green).
- **Producer fix (guard-side):** the history carve-out now MASKS dotted version tokens (`v?X.Y.Z`) on BOTH the added line and the historical corpus before its byte-identity comparison, so a pre-existing term-bearing line whose ONLY change is an inline version marker being rolled during the version bump is recognized as that same historical line, not flagged as new writing. Real detection is unchanged (genuinely new prose differs from history by more than a version number); the guard-strength tests still pass and a new regression test pins the masked behavior (it FAILs the pre-fix guard, PASSes the fixed guard).
- **Producer fix (bump-side):** `scripts/bump-version.sh` now rolls ONLY the two TRACKED README version markers (`this repo at vX.Y.Z`, `Current Version: vX.Y.Z`) and no longer rewrites the inline `(vX.Y.Z)` embedded in historical `**NOTE (vX.Y.Z)**` release lines — those are not tracked version markers (absent from `scripts/version-markers.json`), and rewriting released prose violated the never-rewrite-history doctrine and was what turned two pre-existing lines into "added" lines that made the guard flag its own ripple.
- Single serial ONB merge-writer, Skill 6 Stage 2 Wave 4, unit U92. Fresh dedicated clone off `origin/main` in scratchpad — never the `~/openclaw-onboarding` clone. RED first reproduced on the unfixed bump (README.md:20 / README.md:22 flagged), then GREEN after the two producer fixes. Merged via `git merge --no-ff` commit `6fc4f2c817b3bbb53219be9573e3b6060f68b8fd` (2-parent, not a squash). Ripple: `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.40` rolled all 11 tracked repo version markers `v20.0.39`→`v20.0.40` (`--check` green before and after). The new guard re-run on its OWN ripple reports 0 new unexplained occurrences — PASS. `scripts/hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check` clean (unaffected). Annotated tag `v20.0.40`. Ledger U92 row set to `verified`. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic models.

## [v20.0.39]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 blended-persona U23/B-U9: decision-engine hardening -- signal derivation, site-level routing, regression corpus (QC 9, ONB)

v20.0.39 — Merges `skill6-v2/U23` (master unit U23, Section B id B-U9) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build. Hardens the Skill 6 method-decision engine (`06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_method.py`) per the three verified gaps in master spec B-U9.

- **Feature:** `derive_page_signals(html)` — a pure analyzer that infers `js_frameworks`/`third_party_js`/`interactive`/`css_fighting` from raw HTML (script-tag/library fingerprints, fetch/WebSocket/canvas/WebGL/routing usage, `!important` density, global selectors, `@font-face` blocks). `merge_derived_under_explicit` merges the derived signals UNDER any explicit page_spec (explicit always wins); `classify_page_from_html(html, explicit_spec=None)` wires both together.
- **Feature:** Site-level routing aggregation — `decide_site_method`/`decide_and_record_site`: when >=50% of a site's pages score ADVANCED, or a navigation-linked interactive core is declared, the whole site routes `VERCEL_EMBED` as one project (avoids split DIRECT+VERCEL hosting). Flag-guarded on `GHL_SITE_LEVEL_ROUTING=1` (additive; unset reverts to per-page-only behavior). Writes `routing/site-method-decision.json`. Skill-44 widget routing unaffected.
- **Feature:** Committed routing regression corpus (22 page specs, `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/fixtures/routing_corpus.json`) asserting DIRECT/VERCEL_EMBED/WIDGET outcomes against the real `classify_page`. `scripts/guard-ghl-method-decision.sh` gains a `--corpus` mode that hard-fails CI on any regression, wired into `qc-ghl-install-pages.sh` as an additional assert. New `tools/ghl_routing_drift_check.py` + `schedule/skill6-routing-drift-check.cron.json` + `scripts/install-routing-drift-check-cron.sh` add the monthly live-proof scaffold (classify golden fixture -> deploy to Vercel -> assert_embeddable -> dated receipt), offline CODE-MERGE tier only; the GHL-embed + render_check leg and live cron registration are honestly recorded as deferred to a live run (deferred to U22's own live-proof receipt), matching the established B-U10/B-U16 doctrine in this repo.

Only `README.md` changed on this point.

## [v20.0.36]  -  2026-07-15  -  D6 archival-rail token-kwarg defect fix: GitHub archival rail now live-functional (QC 9.3)

v20.0.36 — Merges `fix/archival-rail-token-kwarg-20260715-053412` into `openclaw-onboarding` main via a fresh dedicated worktree off the latest `origin/main` (rebased past the `ledger-reconciler` cron's advancing mechanical commits, never force-pushed). Fixes the D6/B-D2 archival-rail defect: `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_github_archive.py`'s `_http_request()` declares `token` keyword-only (`*, token`), but its 5 real-path callers (`resolve_authenticated_owner`, `ensure_repo` x2, `put_file` x2) passed it positionally as a 4th argument. On the live path (`requester=None`) every GitHub API call raised `TypeError: _http_request() takes 3 positional arguments but 4 were given` before the token was ever used — no repo was ever created, so the Option A GitHub archival rail never archived on the live path, despite the offline U24 mock-only suite staying green (its injected `requester` fixtures accept 4 plain positional args, masking the mismatch). Sibling `ghl_vercel.py` already used the correct `token=token` convention at all 3 of its call sites and served as the fix template.

- **Fix:** all 5 real callers now pass `token=token` (keyword), matching `ghl_vercel.py`. `_http_request`'s defensive keyword-only signature is unchanged.
- **Test coverage:** new `TestLivePathTokenKwarg` regression class in `test_ghl_github_archive.py` exercises the LIVE path (`requester=None`) end-to-end via `resolve_authenticated_owner` (direct) and `run_archive_task` (full pipeline, all 5 call sites), stubbing ONLY `urllib.request.urlopen` — never the `_http_request`/`requester` seam — so this class of defect is now caught offline. Proven to fail on the unfixed tree (captured `TypeError` / failed receipt containing the same `TypeError` text) and pass after the fix.
- Confirmed by U22 live proof, reproduced by two independent Opus instances. QC 9.3.
- Test proof re-run independently on the merged tree: `test_ghl_github_archive.py` 32/32 PASS; combined with `test_ghl_github_reconcile.py` + `test_ghl_archive_receipt_gate.py` + `test_ghl_vercel.py` = 91/91 PASS; `ghl_github_archive.py --selftest` PASS. Full `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/` suite: 1615 passed, 15 skipped, 3 failed — all 3 confirmed byte-identical on a fresh unmodified `origin/main` baseline clone before this change (a live cross-origin-iframe Playwright selftest needing a browser install; a pre-existing no-client-names guard false-positive on this repo's own already-committed U8 ledger evidence text; and a stale `v19.`-prefix assertion in `test_survey_builder_hardening.py`) — zero regressions introduced by this merge.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` updated — the D6 archival rail is now live-functional and regression-tested (was previously silently broken on the live path behind a passing offline mock suite).
- Ripple: `scripts/bump-version.sh v20.0.36` rolled all 11 tracked repo version markers `v20.0.35` -> `v20.0.36` (`--check` green after). Annotated tag `v20.0.36` (`git tag -a`) pushed to origin. Merged via `git merge --no-ff`; pushed to `origin/main`; remote HEAD re-read via a fresh independent clone AND `gh api repos/trevorotts1/openclaw-onboarding/commits/main`.

The **LIVE-PROOF tier** (the ONE end-to-end operator-box fixture funnel run, plus the live proofs deferred here from A-U4/A-U7/B-U7/B-U10) is this same unit's own separate deliverable and is NOT claimed by this ripple.

- **Feature:** new `06-ghl-install-pages/scripts/prove_skill6_block_u22.py` — the offline fixture proof chaining B-U1/U15 (persona-bundle-ladder receipt) → B-U2/U16 (per-page blend directives, guardrail-terminated) → B-U3/U17 (copy-stage selection log, voice match) → B-U5/U19 (FAB-QC D4 == 10.0, loaded via the real `load_inputs_from_evidence` disk path) → B-U6/U20 (declared-vs-used voice equality) against ONE fixture funnel written to a real evidence-root directory tree, plus a legacy no-bundle control run proven byte-identical to an in-memory golden baseline via the same disk-loading path; new `tests/test_prove_skill6_block_u22.py` wires it into CI.
- 3 new dispatcher-integration-level tests in `test_v2_dispatcher.py` (`TestPersonaBundleAcquisition`) close the CC-rung / local-rung / standalone-degradation gaps in the threaded/cc/local/absent × confirm-state matrix (the 4 pre-existing tests covered threaded confirmed/pending + absent + a raising ladder only).
- `scripts/guard-fab-qc-gate.sh` gains a bundle-receipt schema check (new section 8: asserts `persona_bundle_ladder.resolve_persona_bundle()`'s receipt carries all 10 required fields, mutation-self-tested) and confirms the copy-stage blend seam module is present. The branch's own forward-compat "7b" `copy_craft_pool` WARN hook was dropped at this merge as redundant — U18/B-U4 landed on `main` since this branch was cut and already added an equivalent HARD check (guard section 1d, `copy_craft_pool present + non-empty`).
- `.github/workflows/funnel-automation-libraries-guard.yml` wires `persona_bundle_ladder.py`, `cc_board.py`, `49-signature-funnel/scripts/copy_persona_blend_seam.py` (+ its own long-unwired `test_copy_persona_blend_seam.py`) and the two new U22 files into the CI path triggers and pytest/selftest steps — none of these had ever triggered any CI job before this change.
- **Honest scope note:** at the time the `skill6-v2/U22` branch was originally built, B-U4/U18 (`copy_craft_pool`) and B-U7/U21 (ingest parity) were still `pending` — the proof script printed an honest `[PENDING]` notice for each rather than fabricating their behavior. Both units landed on `main` in the interim (U18 verified 2026-07-15, U21 verified 2026-07-15); re-running `prove_skill6_block_u22.py` on this merged tree now shows both self-detected as `[INFO] ... appears LANDED` automatically — no code change was needed, confirming the script's own design intent.

Full `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/` suite: 1607 passed, 15 skipped, 3 failed — all 3 failures independently reproduced byte-identical on a fresh unmerged pre-merge `origin/main` baseline (a live cross-origin-iframe browser selftest, a pre-existing client-name-guard false-positive on this repo's own already-committed U8 ledger evidence text, and a stale v19-prefix assertion in `test_survey_builder_hardening.py`) — confirmed pre-existing, zero regressions introduced by this merge.

## [v20.0.32]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 blended-persona Wave One Stage B — U5/A-U5: per-page scoped persona blends (QC 8.9, ONB half)

- **Feature:** per-page blends were structurally impossible before this unit — `build_bundle` ran once per TASK. `build_bundle` gains an additive `scope_hint: dict = None` kwarg (`{page_role, page_slug, conversion_goal, part_id}`); `_resolve_scope_key()` derives a stable, persistable `scope` string; the bundle gains `scope` + a verbatim `scope_hint` echo + `rationale['scope']` when resolvable; omitted/`None`/`{}`/unrecognized-keys produce a bundle byte-identical to pre-A-U5 output. New `write_persona_selection_log_entry()` appends one `persona-selection-log.md` entry per page.
- **Merge note (real content conflict, hand-resolved):** this branch and U4/U6 (already on `main`) both inserted new code at the same location in `persona_blend.py` — U6's `validate_blend_invariant` block and U5's per-page scope-function block, plus U4's `conversion_goal`/`goal_source` params and U5's `scope_hint` param on the SAME `build_bundle` signature line. Resolved by keeping BOTH feature blocks (non-overlapping, both additive) and combining the signature to carry all three new params (`conversion_goal`, `goal_source`, `scope_hint`); `.github/workflows/persona-blend-match-quality-guard.yml`'s doc comment and path-filter conflicts resolved the same way (both units' checks and watched paths kept, U5's promoted to check (H) since U4 already claimed (G)). `py_compile` clean on the merged file; `build_bundle`'s body (unmarked, auto-merged hunks) already threads both `resolve_conversion_goal(conversion_goal, goal_source)` and `_resolve_scope_key(scope_hint)` correctly.
- **Ledger conflict (hand-resolved):** this branch's own copy of the ledger row for U5 collided with this merge-writer's U4 update and a pre-existing legacy duplicate placeholder U5 row baked into `main`'s own ledger copy; resolved by keeping this merge-writer's U4/U6 rows verbatim and removing the stale duplicate, leaving exactly one U5 row (updated to `verified` below).

## [v20.0.29]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 blended-persona Wave One Stage B — U8: book-to-persona embeddings wiring, root-cause bare-CI platform-detection fix (QC 8.9)

v20.0.29 — Merges `skill6-v2/U8` (master unit U8, Section A.10 id A-U8) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 section A.8. First unit landed by the Wave One Stage B serial merge train (U8 -> U24 -> U4 -> U5 -> U21).

- **Feature:** book-to-persona embeddings wiring — post-synthesis index write (client key, honest `embedding: deferred` receipt on missing/invalid key or credential-shaped mid-run failure), publish-time index-verify (`publish-personas-to-fleet.sh` exits non-zero on an unexplained disk-vs-index gap), and a scheduled operator-box drift check (`persona_embedding_drift_probe.py`) comparing personas-on-disk vs personas-in-index.
- **Root-cause CI fix (replaces an earlier CI-env-only band-aid):** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/gemini-section-indexer.py::main()` called `get_openclaw_paths()` (`shared-utils/detect_platform.py`) **unconditionally**, before checking whether `args.db` / `args.personas_root` were supplied — so any runner lacking `/data/.openclaw`, `~/.openclaw`, or `~/clawd` (bare CI included) `SystemExit(1)`s even when both explicit path args make platform detection unnecessary. Fixed by resolving platform paths **lazily** via a memoized closure consulted only where actually needed; behavior when either arg is omitted is byte-identical to before. Verified locally under `env -i` with a synthetic empty `$HOME` and no `OPENCLAW_PLATFORM` set — `tests/unit/embedding-credential-error-classify.test.py` (7/7, including both `TestIndexerExitCodeRouting` exit-4/exit-6 cases) passes without the env override. The defensive `OPENCLAW_PLATFORM=mac` CI-env addition is left in place (no longer load-bearing).
- **Merge-train note:** U8's own branch history carries two internal self-ripple stamps (`v20.0.26`, then re-stamped `v20.0.27`) that collided with versions `main` had already shipped independently (U6, U18) before this merge train ran. Those internal stamps are superseded entirely by this merge-writer's own ripple — the branch's feature commits are merged as-is; all 18 version-marker files (SKILL.md/skill-version.txt/README.md/cc-compat.json/install.sh/update-skills.sh/version/browser_manager.py+.sh/agent-browser-reaper.sh/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh/role-library templates) are resolved to `main`'s side at merge time and then rolled forward to `v20.0.29` in the single ripple commit that follows this merge, so there is exactly one version history, never two competing stamps.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U8 row set to `verified`.
- No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no Anthropic model id shipped in this code — client boxes run embeddings on their own Gemini key per standing doctrine.

be any of the 99 catalog personas; the copy-craft TASK slot itself draws from a single
machine-validated `copy_craft_pool` (the original five plus `edwards-*` plus the Brunson-family
crosswalk targets), defined once in `shared-utils/persona-crosswalk.json` and referenced by both
Standard Operating Procedures.

## [v20.0.27]  -  2026-07-15  -  Skill 6 blended-persona Wave One — U6: min-2/max-4 persona-count invariant (`validate_blend_invariant`, D2/D-A2 ratified, QC 9.0)

Merges `skill6-v2/U6`: `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/persona_blend.py` gains
`validate_blend_invariant(bundle)`, a pure function that counts the blend
directive's ROLE slots (voice/topic/task) and records the reading as
`bundle["rationale"]["invariant"]` on every CONTENT bundle assembled by
`build_bundle()`.

- **D2 (D-A2) ratified 2026-07-14 — role count, not persona-id count.** A
  COLLAPSED blend (one persona id filling BOTH the voice role and the topic
  role) satisfies the min-2 invariant by ROLE COUNT — `collapsed=true` (plus
  the reason) is recorded on the reading so every collapse stays auditable.
- **Bounds:** `MIN_BLEND_PERSONAS = 2`, `MAX_BLEND_PERSONAS = 4`. Below-min and
  above-max readings are honest audit signals (`reason` = `below-min`,
  `below-min (house-voice pending audience confirm)`, or `above-max`) — the
  validator RECORDS and ALERTS, it NEVER BLOCKS the write path.
- Non-content bundles (mechanical tasks, A.7) are exempt — always
  `ok=true, reason="exempt-non-content"`.
- Test proof: `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-persona-blend-matcher.py`
  57/57 PASS (11 new A-U6 cases, T13), re-run independently on the merged
  `main` tree post-merge.

## [v20.0.26]  -  2026-07-14  -  n8n/self-hosted webhook tunnel SOP: ingress-rule merge into the box's existing tunnel, kills the impossible cloudflared tunnel login + second-connector dead ends (SOP-N8N-TUNNEL-01, QC 8.7)

v20.0.26 — Merges `fix/n8n-tunnel-sop-round2`: a new universal SOP for any client agent operating a
self-hosted service (a self-hosted n8n instance receiving inbound automation triggers is the flagship
case) that needs to receive INBOUND webhooks over the public internet. Went through three QC rounds
(7.8 -> 8.2 -> 8.7); round-3 closed the last blocking defect.

- **Gap closed:** with no SOP for this, an agent's generic training knowledge reached for
  `cloudflared tunnel login` (opens a browser, requires a Cloudflare account the client does not have
  on the fleet's zone — a dead end that can never complete) or reached for provisioning a brand-new
  tunnel/connector/token, duplicating what the box already has.
- **`universal-sops/SOP-N8N-TUNNEL-01-SELF-HOSTED-WEBHOOK-INGRESS.md` (new, 527 lines):** the correct
  shape — Section 1 hard-guardrails `cloudflared tunnel login` outright; Section 2 documents the real
  architecture (ONE operator-owned tunnel per box, multiple hostnames on one ingress array, a new
  service gets one new ingress rule via `shared-utils/cc-tunnel-ingress.sh`'s GET->merge->PUT pattern,
  never a full-replace); Section 3 is the only client-agent action — classify the connector
  (`pm2 status cloudflare-tunnel`), prove the local origin (box-conditional: Mac `localhost`, VPS
  compose-service DNS name or host-reachable address — never assumed), then escalate the five asks
  (hostname, origin port+location, ingress-merge request, path-scoped Access bypass, root-app-stays-gated
  confirmation) through the Rescue Rangers webhook (the only channel that crosses the box->operator
  boundary; `AGENTS.md`'s cross-dept-request-template and bot-to-bot Telegram posts both explicitly do
  NOT reach the operator); Section 4 confirms the existing PM2 connector rather than installing a new
  one; Section 5 sets `WEBHOOK_URL` per install type (Mac launchd plist, VPS host-side Docker Compose —
  a structural dead end for a client agent inside the OpenClaw container, routed to the operator as a
  conditional ask #6, or VPS PM2 in the same container); Section 6 requires BOTH a webhook-path 404 (not
  an Access 302) AND a UI-path 302 (not an unauthenticated 200/401/404) — either alone is a defect.
- **Round-3 fix (this merge's HEAD):** closed a VPS-container dead end reintroduced inside the fix
  itself — Section 5's only VPS instruction previously assumed a `docker` CLI/socket reachable from
  inside the OpenClaw container, which does not exist there
  (`platform/mac/service-selfheal/gateway-health-watchdog.sh:66-69`'s own box-type detection). Section 5
  is now three explicit branches (Mac launchd, VPS host-side Compose service routed to the operator, VPS
  PM2-in-same-container the agent can act on directly) plus a corrected ask #2 (port AND location, not
  port alone) and a resurrect-hook persistence note for Docker-VPS `pm2 save`.
- **`AGENTS.md`:** new Rescue Rangers cross-reference ("provisioning asks use this same channel") and a
  new top-level guardrail section pointing at the SOP; one hardcoded operator name scrubbed (fleet-wide,
  no-human-names).
- **`32-command-center-setup/INSTALL.md` + `scripts/create-tunnel.sh`:** hardcoded operator name scrubbed
  fleet-wide (Phase 6b architecture note, webhook messaging, gate check); `create-tunnel.sh` gains
  explicit `ESCALATE:` guidance (never fall back to `cloudflared tunnel login`) on both its transport-
  failure and webhook-failure exit paths.
- **`38-conversational-ai-system/references/cloudflare-tunnel-troubleshooting.md`:** new section
  distinguishing Skill 38's own client-owned GHL-inbound tunnel from this SOP's operator-owned tunnel for
  a self-hosted service, pointing at the new SOP.
- **`universal-sops/_content-manifest.json`:** regenerated (`scripts/hash-universal-sops-manifest.py
  --check` PASSES, 104 files, all sha256 match).
- No client names, no human names, no secret values, no box identifiers. Cloudflare tunnel TOKEN is
  referenced only by its env-var name (`CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN`, distinct from Skill 38's own
  client-owned `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`) and its canonical storage path — never a value. No model
  added/removed/substituted; this is a pure documentation/SOP + escalation-plumbing change, no LLM/
  provider wiring touched.
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.25` -> **v20.0.26** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check`
  green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged.

## [v20.0.25]  -  2026-07-14  -  Skill-6 board reconcile: `cc_board.py reconcile` closes the SKILL.md:607-608 blindness (U27/B-U13, both-train, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2)

v20.0.25 — Merges `skill6-v2/U27` (master unit U27, Section-B id B-U13) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 section B. Both-train unit — this repo carries the producer/reconcile side; the companion advisory probe lands on the same branch name in `blackceo-command-center`.

- **Gap closed:** `SKILL.md:607-608` names the blindness verbatim — "`cc_board.py` fail-softs (the card just never lands / never moves) and the build continues unregistered" — with nothing that ever swept for it. `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/cc_board.py` gains `resolve_evidence_base()`, `list_evidence_runs()`, `_read_json()`, `ReconcileReport`, `reconcile()`, and the `cc_board.py reconcile [--base-dir DIR] [--json]` CLI subcommand.
- **Read-only, fail-soft, non-gating by design (mirrors the Anthology Engine's `mc_board.py reconcile` / the Command Center's `checkAnthologyBoardProjection()` clone):** compares each run's existing `routing/intake-receipt.json` against its `routing/board-ingest-receipt.json` (new — written by `ingest_task()` when called with `evidence_root=`) and reports DRIFT only for a run that completed intake but whose card never landed. A run with no board-ingest receipt at all (built before this unit, or a caller that hasn't threaded `evidence_root=` through yet) is reported as `unwired` — informational only, NEVER counted as drift, so shipping this file never turns an existing box red. `reconcile()`/`_reconcile_cli()` always exit 0; `ingest_task(evidence_root=None)` remains byte-identical legacy behavior.
- **Merge note:** branch forked before already-merged U20 landed `_persona_used_selftest()` at the same anchor point in `cc_board.py` (immediately after `_u9_selftest()`, before `_demo()`); both units independently appended non-overlapping, self-contained blocks at that line — rebased/unioned onto current `main` so both coexist (`--selftest` now runs 6/6 suites including both `persona-used-selftest` and the new reconcile coverage).
- **Tests (all green on merged `main`):** `cc_board.py --selftest` (6/6 suites, `persona-used-selftest` PASS); `pytest 06-ghl-install-pages/tests/test_cc_board_reconcile.py` (20/20 — includes both binary acceptance criteria: (a) a suppressed/failed ingest surfaces as drift, (b) a clean run shows zero drift across 3 probes — plus the legacy-unwired-never-drift guard and CLI JSON/human-output exit-0 checks).
- **Companion (`blackceo-command-center`):** `checkSkill6BoardProjection()` mirrors this file's evidence-base precedence exactly and is wired as a non-gating advisory (`advisory.skill6_board_projection`) at `/api/health/deep`, cloning the shipped Anthology A7 pattern, with a `BoardDriftBanner` component. 12/12 new tests; full suite 233/233 (deep-health 79/79 unchanged, zero regressions); `tsc --noEmit` clean; `next build` clean.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U27 row set to `verified` (QC 9.0, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.24` -> **v20.0.25** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check` green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted — pure health/reconcile application code, no LLM/provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.24]  -  2026-07-14  -  "any content" blend-governance proof — email, blog, newsletter named + proven; text-message NOT-FOUND routed to U116 (U111/E5-6, closes G4, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2)

v20.0.24 — Merges `skill6-v2/U111` (master unit U111, Section-E5 id E5-6, closes gap G4) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 section E5.

- **Gap closed:** per the D1 binding ruling (the blend GOVERNS voice + content-writing in every engine, never advisory, no exemptions), "Rewrite ANY content" was previously proven only for funnel copy — email/blog/newsletter/text-message were covered by inheritance, never a named per-engine proof. First-act read confirmed the gap was real: `50-email-engine/tools/persona_canonical.py` imported only `persona_for_job`'s helper functions for a style crosswalk and never called `persona_for_job()` itself (zero blend path); `57-social-media-in-a-box/scripts/persona_adapter.py` (the one canonical entry point behind both the blog and newsletter golden modes, plus social) called `persona_for_job()` but never passed `blend=True`.
- **`50-email-engine/tools/persona_canonical.py`:** new `blend_block()` call site (`persona_for_job(..., blend=True)`, `department="marketing"` — Skill 50's real seeded department).
- **`57-social-media-in-a-box/scripts/persona_adapter.py`:** `resolve()`'s "adapter" branch now honors an opt-in `cfg["blend"]` flag + `topic_hint` passthrough; default off, byte-identical to pre-U111 behavior when unset.
- **Text-message (SMS): NOT-FOUND**, recorded not silently skipped. Both SMS-adjacent surfaces read in full (`38-conversational-ai-system`'s Build-with-AI workflow spec; `44`'s `wf5-ht-interest-builder.py`) confirmed non-persona-consumers. Routed to U116 (E6-2, ADD-2), which already owns mandatory text/SMS comms governance.
- **`tests/unit/u111-any-content-blend-proof.test.py`:** the acceptance battery — fixture-driven governed-output proof + dynamic call-site proof (monkeypatched capture, not a string grep) per engine, plus the SMS NOT-FOUND check. Fail-first verified: 5/7 checks fail against the pre-U111 tree, all 7 pass post-build. 7/7 PASS on merged `main`.
- **`.github/workflows/u111-any-content-blend-guard.yml`:** new CI gate — fails the build if email/blog/newsletter regress off the U1 seam, or the SMS NOT-FOUND finding goes stale.
- **Scope boundary (recorded, not silently expanded):** does not flip `blend=True` to the production default, does not decommission any engine's local voice logic — that is U98/U114 (P3), which own the hash re-pin + judge sign-off discipline for that larger change.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U111 row set to `verified` (QC 8.9, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.23` -> **v20.0.24** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check` green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted. Client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers — this change is additive/opt-in application-layer Python plumbing, no provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.22]  -  2026-07-14  -  book-to-persona: Schema-1.4 enrichment — emotional_register / audience_resonance / conversion_style on all 99 personas + D6 pipeline stamping (U3/A-U3, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2)

v20.0.22 — Merges `skill6-v2/U3` (master unit U3, Section-A id A-U3) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 section A.

- **`persona-categories.json` schema 1.3 -> 1.4:** every one of the 99 personas gains `emotional_register` (string), `audience_resonance` (string), `conversion_style` (string, one of `story-close`/`stack-close`/`logic-close`/`invitation-close`/`challenge-close`) — synthesized from each persona's own already-catalogued `voice_style.tone[]/devices[]/cadence/avoid[]` and `domain[]`, never invented from the author's name. Adds top-level controlled-vocabulary arrays `emotionalRegisterTags` (14), `audienceResonanceTags` (12), `conversionStyleTags` (5, operator-pinned canonical closing-style set).
- **New one-time backfill:** `22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/pipeline/backfill-schema14-registers.py` (+ data module `pipeline/schema14_register_assignments.py`) — additive, idempotent, fails loud and writes nothing if the post-write catalog doesn't validate clean under `persona_blend.validate_catalog_tags`.

Fixed and proven fail-first in `tests/unit/persona-fleet-schema14-sync.test.py`.

Persona-SET count triad unchanged at 99/99/99/99/99 — additive fields only, no persona added/removed.

## [v20.0.21]  -  2026-07-13  -  ai-workforce-blueprint: Page-QC v2 semantic scorer — six-dimension, client's-own-judge, SKIP-not-fabricate (U25/B-U11, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2)

v20.0.21 — Merges `skill6-v2/U25` (master unit U25, Section-B id B-U11) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 section B.

- **`shared-utils/page_qc.py` (new) — the semantic scorer FAB-QC cannot be.** Six weighted dimensions summing to 100, threshold 8.5 (matching the standing FAB-QC gate). Runs the judge model the client already owns — never Anthropic, never a substituted model — and SKIPs honestly (never fabricates a score) when no judge key is configured on the box. Two-pass scoring with spread-triggered third pass, falling back to the median of two when the third pass itself is unparseable.
- **Wired into the build path.** `06-ghl-install-pages/qc-built-funnel.sh` gains the Page-QC v2 overlay (flag-gated); `scripts/guard-fab-qc-gate.sh` extended to assert the new scorer is present, its weights sum to 100, its threshold is 8.5, and the funnel wrapper actually wires it in.
- **Rubric.** `06-ghl-install-pages/references/funnel-automation-build-quality-rubric.md` documents the six dimensions and the 8.5 gate.
- **Adversarial re-pass (2 follow-up commits, same branch).** QC's adversarial pass found `_detect_broken_images()` could crash on externally sourced `images/manifest.json` evidence: (1) non-numeric `http_status` strings and non-string `dom_html` shapes raised uncaught `TypeError`/`ValueError`; (2) a sibling gap — `json.load()` decodes bare `Infinity`/`-Infinity`/`NaN` tokens to Python floats by default, and `int(float('inf'))` raises `OverflowError`, a distinct exception the first fix's except tuple didn't cover. Both fixed (except tuple now `(TypeError, ValueError, OverflowError)`), verified exhaustive against every shape `http_status`/`dom_html` can arrive as (None, non-numeric strings, NaN, ±Infinity, lists, dicts, tuples, sets, bytes, huge-but-finite ints/floats) — huge-but-finite values correctly still score as a real >=400 status, not silently swallowed by the widened guard.
- **Tests.** `tests/unit/page-qc.test.py` (21 tests, was 17 pre-adversarial-pass) + `tests/unit/page-qc-gate-guard.test.sh` (2 tests) — new. Full regression: `tests/unit/fab-qc.test.py` (21/21), `tests/unit/fab-artifact.test.py` (8/8), `scripts/guard-fab-qc-gate.sh` — all green on merged `main`. `py_compile` clean on `shared-utils/page_qc.py` + `tests/unit/page-qc.test.py`; `bash -n` clean on all three shipped shell scripts. Zero conflict markers found by `git merge-tree` pre-check and by post-merge sweep.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U25 row set to `verified` (QC re-pass 8.85, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.20` -> **v20.0.21** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check` green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted. Client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers — this change is pure application-layer Python (a semantic scorer module + its wiring/guard/tests), no provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.20]  -  2026-07-13  -  Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2: on-box P0 verification evidence — Social Media Planner unbroken-proof (or captured break) + P3-05 content proof (U81/U84, chainB)

v20.0.20 — Merges `skill6-v2/chainB` (master units U81 crosswalk GK-19, U84 crosswalk GK-22) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master spec v2 lines 1964-1968 (GK-19) and 1982-1986 (GK-22). Both units FAILED P0 QC (score 3.5) on first pass for having no archived proof; this merge lands the fix — real commands run on the operator box, real outputs archived verbatim, never self-certified.

- **U84/GK-22 — on-box CONTENT proof for P3-05.** Read the live deployed manifest (`~/.openclaw/skills/.onboarding-content-manifest.json`); adjudicated the `src_git_sha` != `origin/main` HEAD nuance (`git merge-base --is-ancestor` confirms it's a real ancestor, not stale/fabricated — every intervening commit touches skills 06/23/49, never 35/45). Content byte-match (the real acceptance bar) proven three ways: `git hash-object` on all 3 named files (`prompt-bands.json`, `prove_gip_prompt_floor.py`, `pregen_prompt_gate.py`) byte-identical across manifest-sha/main-HEAD/deployed-box; the repo's own `scripts/skill-content-hash.sh` run live against a clean main checkout and the deployed box (Skill 45 exact match; Skill 35 diverges only via a local `.pytest_cache/` artifact, root-caused, filed as non-blocking follow-up U84-F1); on-box `skill-version.txt` stamps as corroboration only. **Operator-box leg: PASS.** Fleet-roll leg explicitly NOT executed this pass — named **PENDING-P4** per the fix mandate (master spec line 137), never silently skipped.
- **U81/GK-19 — Social Media Planner proven unbroken against the graphics handoff, or the exact break captured.** All 3 gate legs driven live via `pregen_prompt_gate.py`: Leg 2 (receipt removed) exit 6 `AF-SM-INPUT-QC-GATE` exact match; Leg 3 (Nano Banana refused / Ideogram passes) exit 6 `AF-SM-MODEL-ROUTING` then exit 0 exact match; Leg 1 gate-check half exit 0 exact match. Leg 1 live-post half driven via the real `caf social create-post` rail (Skill 44) against the operator's OWN GHL sub-account (BlackCEO LLC, verified via `caf locations get` — not a client) — genuinely **BROKEN**: GHL's real API 422s on a malformed request body (`locationId` duplicated in body, missing required `type`/`userId`, wrong `skip`/`limit` types), corroborating `caf doctor`'s own SYNTHETIC_FIXTURE warning that this command was never validated live before. Root-caused by replaying the identical call with a corrected-shape payload through the same authenticated client — succeeded, proving the break is isolated to the CLI's body construction, not credentials/permissions/connectivity. Confirmed zero orphan writes by read-back (0 posts matching the fixture marker). Written reproduction (exact command/exit-code/stderr) archived per this unit's own binary-acceptance OR-branch; follow-up unit **U81-F1** filed (fix `gohighlevel_cli.py::social_create_post`/`social_posts` body construction — out of scope for this P0 verification unit).
- **Evidence.** `ledgers/evidence/U84-GK-22/` (9 files) + `ledgers/evidence/U81-GK-19/` (24 files) — every command, exit code, and stderr archived verbatim. Scanned for secrets before merge (none present — approval tokens redacted, account/location identifiers only, no credentials committed). No client lane touched; zero client-visible artifacts.

## [v20.0.19]  -  2026-07-13  -  ai-workforce-blueprint: bundle-acquisition ladder + per-page blend directive + copy-stage bundle consumption + FAB-QC D4 v2 bundle-aware voice grounding (U15/U16/U17/U19, chainA, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2)

v20.0.19 — Merges `skill6-v2/chainA` (master units U15/B-U1, U16/B-U2, U17/B-U3, U19/B-U5 — five commits, U17 carries two) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master-spec v2 section B. U17 and U19 are ledger-declared merge-paired ("merges ONLY with U19") and land together in this one train, per the ratified serial-merge-writer decision — never apart.

- **U15/B-U1 — `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/persona_bundle_ladder.py` (new).** Bundle-acquisition ladder wired into `v2_dispatcher`: threaded bundle (already resolved upstream) -> Command-Center fetch -> local `--blend` selector run -> absent (never blocks a build). A receipt is always written, confirmed or degraded, so downstream FAB-QC always has an honest acquisition trail to read.
- **U16/B-U2 — per-page `build_blend_directive` in `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/funnel_matcher.py`.** The template's baked-in persona is demoted to a crosswalk-resolved topic hint rather than a voice source; each page in a multi-page funnel gets its own directive from the acquired bundle. `copy_persona` stays fully back-compatible — a call site with no bundle reproduces the pre-U16 behavior byte-for-byte.
- **U17/B-U3 — the copy stage consumes the bundle.** `49-signature-funnel/scripts/copy_persona_blend_seam.py` (new) wires the acquired bundle into `SOP-FUNNEL-02-COPY.md` + `funnel-copy-prompts.md`; the persona-selection-log format stays back-compatible with the pre-U17 parser (verified by regex round-trip tests against both old and new log lines).
