- **U19/B-U5 — FAB-QC D4 v2 bundle-aware voice grounding (`shared-utils/fab_qc.py`).** D4 (voice/persona grounding) now scores against the bundle's blend voice when a bundle is active, instead of only the template's baked-in persona name — closing the v1 P0 gap where D4 could pass on template-persona-only naming even though the actual copy was blended. Legacy (no-bundle) scoring path is byte-identical; `universal-sops/_content-manifest.json` re-stamped after the `SOP-FUNNEL-02-COPY.md` edit.
- **Tests.** `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/test_funnel_matcher.py` (19 cases) + `06-ghl-install-pages/tests/test_v2_dispatcher.py` (54 cases, 3 environment-conditional skips pre-existing) + `49-signature-funnel/scripts/test_copy_persona_blend_seam.py` (10 cases) + `tests/unit/fab-qc.test.py` (21 cases) — 70/70 runnable PASS (3 skip) on the merged tree via `pytest`/`unittest`. `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/hash-content-manifest.py --check` green (433 roles + 132 SOPs + 19 personas, no drift, no token leaks).
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U15/U16/U17/U19 rows set to `verified` (QC 9.2, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.18` -> **v20.0.19** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check` green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted. Client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers — this change is pure application-layer Python (dispatcher ladder, directive builder, copy seam, QC gate), no provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.18]  -  2026-07-13  -  ai-workforce-blueprint: Blend Directive v2 — structured per-slot voice-attribute block (U2/A-U2, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2) — catalog-sourced only, byte-identical v1 degrade on pre-enrichment catalogs

v20.0.18 — Merges `skill6-v2/U2` (master unit U2, Section-A id A-U2) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`), master-spec v2 section A.3.

- **`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/persona_blend.py` — `build_blend_directive()` gains an optional `catalog: dict = None` kwarg.** When supplied and a populated voice slot's persona carries `voice_style{}` (schema >=1.3), a structured attribute block is appended per slot: a `LABEL — Name (pid)` header, then `tone` / `cadence`, `devices` (top 3) / `signature move`, and `avoid` lines — every value sourced verbatim from the catalog's `voice_style{}`, never model-invented, and a slot with no `voice_style` renders no block at all. A single `VOICE CONTRACT: echo one line into persona-selection-log confirming the register you wrote in.` instruction closes the block. `build_bundle()` now passes `catalog=catalog` through to `build_blend_directive()` so every bundle-producing call site gets the v2 block for free.
- **Byte-identical degrade, purely additive.** `catalog=None` (the default — every pre-A-U2 call site is unaffected), a schema-1.2 (pre-enrichment) catalog, or a slot persona with no `voice_style` all reproduce the exact pre-A-U2 (v1) prose-only directive, golden-diff empty. `GUARDRAIL_CLAUSE` remains the verbatim, non-removable TRAILING clause of the composed directive in every case (voice-only, collapsed, topic-only, non-content, and now the v2 attribute-block variant) — the `ensureBlendGuardrail` marker-detection contract this unit never touches.
- **Tests.** New `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-a-u2-blend-directive-v2.py` (16 tests: byte-identical v1 degrade on the 1.2 fixture incl. the collapsed branch, >=4 attribute lines per populated slot on the enriched catalog with catalog-sourced-verbatim tracing, guardrail-trailing + guardrail-text-untouched, and graceful degradation on house-voice/topic-only/non-content branches) wired into `.github/workflows/persona-blend-match-quality-guard.yml` as check (E), additive to the pre-existing 46-test W7 contract suite + 20-case match-quality regression corpus (100% >= 90% gate) + P4-02 four-slot synergy suite (10/10) — none weakened, all still green on merged `main` (72 combined tests across the four persona_blend.py suites, plus the match-score-distribution logger, the book-to-persona e2e round trip, and the D6 duality-tags pipeline shell suite, all re-run and green).
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U2 row set to `verified` (QC 8.9, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.17` -> **v20.0.18** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (`--check` green before and after). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted. Client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers — this change is a pure additive kwarg + catalog-sourced string-formatting seam in a shared Python module, no provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.17]  -  2026-07-13  -  shared-utils: persona_for_job carries the blend (U1/A-U1, Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2) — default-off `blend=True` seam through `_run_selector`, bundle superset returned verbatim

v20.0.17 — Merges `skill6-v2/U1` (master unit U1, Section-A id A-U1) of the Skill 6 Blended-Persona Kanban v2 build (`ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md`). This is the seam every downstream blend-consuming unit (A-U7 Skill 6 convergence, U111 any-content proof, U115 per-part governance, U116 audience-confirmation prompt) rides.

- **`shared-utils/persona_for_job.py`** — `_run_selector()` gains `blend: bool = False` and `topic_hint: str | None = None` kwargs; when `blend=True` it appends `--blend` (+ `--topic <hint>` when supplied) to the canonical selector's argv so the selector emits `persona-selector-v2.py`'s W7 branch (`persona_blend.build_bundle`) — the voice-first persona-BUNDLE superset — instead of a single-persona selection. `resolve_persona_for_job()` gains the matching `blend=False` / `topic_hint=None` kwargs: with `blend=True` it returns the bundle dict VERBATIM (`blend_directive`, `voice`, `resolved_audience`, `task_personas`, `rationale`, `fallbacks`, plus a back-compat `persona_id`/`persona_name` mirror) with `department`/`sop_slug`/`job_text`/`warnings` normalized on top so the shape matches the single-persona return's context fields.
- **Never-naked contract preserved for blend mode.** If the selector is unreachable, errors, or returns no `blend_directive` while `blend=True` was requested, the function fails closed the same way single-persona mode does — never returns a naked result, records an honest warning, and degrades to the single-persona fallback path.
- **Client sovereignty untouched.** An express `client_persona_id` + `persona_source` in `CLIENT_FINAL_SOURCES` is still FINAL and the selector never runs, blend or not — a client-named voice is never blended or judged.
- **Default-off, zero migration.** `blend` defaults to `False` on both `_run_selector()` and `resolve_persona_for_job()`; every existing caller (single-persona mode) is byte-identical in behavior — same code path, same return shape, as before this parameter existed.
- **Tests.** `shared-utils/test-persona-for-job.sh` extended with 7 new blend-mode contract cases (non-empty `blend_directive`/`voice`/`resolved_audience`, `persona_id` back-compat mirror present, governed, degraded-still-governed, honest degraded warning, blend-omitted shape unchanged) alongside the pre-existing 11 single-persona + 6 multi-slot cases — 24/24 PASS on merged `main`.
- Ledger: `ledgers/skill6-blended-persona-kanban-v2-2026-07-13.md` U1 row set to `verified` (QC 9.35, gate 8.5).
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.16` -> **v20.0.17** via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted. Client skills/engines still run only on the client's own providers — this change is a pure argv/return-shape seam in a shared Python module, no provider wiring touched.

## [v20.0.16]  -  2026-07-13  -  chore(release): re-establish CI guard G3 after the v20.0.15 fast-forward push

v20.0.16 — No functional change. The v20.0.15 release landed on `main` via a fast-forward push of four separate commits (feature, version bump, a small hardening fix, a docstring fix) rather than a single squash/merge commit. `version-consistency.yml`'s G3 guard (skill content change requires a skill-version.txt bump) diffs `HEAD` vs `HEAD^1` on a `push` event — a single-commit window — so it only examined the LAST of those four commits (the docstring fix), which touched `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_github_archive.py` without also touching `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` in that same commit (the bump happened two commits earlier, in v20.0.15's own release commit). G3 failed on `main` as a result (`https://github.com/trevorotts1/openclaw-onboarding/actions/runs/29264011166`), even though the actual content was already fully consistent (SKILL.md frontmatter and skill-version.txt agreed at v20.0.15, and `bump-version.sh --check` was green locally before every push). This release re-runs `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 lockstep markers + `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` + its SKILL.md frontmatter, all in ONE commit) so the very next push-diff window sees a clean, matched version bump and G3 passes going forward. Lesson for future releases: prefer a single squash/merge commit (or bundle every skill-content commit with its own skill-version.txt bump) when pushing directly to `main`, since G3's push-event diff only covers the most recent commit, not the full range introduced by a multi-commit push.

## [v20.0.15]  -  2026-07-13  -  Skill 6 (ghl-install-pages): VERCEL_EMBED now archives to GitHub, non-blocking, with a reconciliation sweep

v20.0.15 — Skill 6's VERCEL_EMBED path previously violated the operator's standing rule that a page's source code must ALWAYS also live in a GitHub repo: `tools/ghl_vercel.py` base64-uploaded the generated files straight to the Vercel deployments API with no GitHub step at all, and `SKILL.md` said so explicitly ("IS A DIRECT API UPLOAD — NOT GitHub"). This closes the gap without slowing the page down.

- **`tools/ghl_github_archive.py` (new).** After `ghl_vercel.run_pipeline`'s Vercel deploy is live and `assert_embeddable` has passed, this module stages the generated `index.html`/`vercel.json` to a stable path under the run's evidence root (independent of the possibly-ephemeral `project_dir`) and fires a DETACHED subprocess (new session — survives the parent process exiting) that pushes them to a per-page GitHub repo. The repo name is deterministic from `project_name` + `marker`, so a re-deploy always updates its own repo and can never create a second one or touch an unrelated repo. Every failure mode (missing `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN`, GitHub API error, network error, subprocess spawn failure) is caught and recorded as an honest F6 receipt (`ghl_receipts`, object_type `vercel_github_archive`) — NEVER raised into the Vercel pipeline, and never able to take a live page down.
- **`tools/ghl_github_reconcile.py` (new).** A straightforward, runnable sweep over one evidence root: list every `vercel_deploy` receipt (every page built via VERCEL_EMBED), confirm each has a verified `vercel_github_archive` receipt, and (`--retry`) re-run the archive job from its staged source for any that don't — or FLAG the page if no staged source remains (reconciliation never fabricates source code). `python3 tools/ghl_github_reconcile.py --evidence-root <dir> --retry` exits 0 when every page's code is confirmed in GitHub, 1 otherwise.
- **`tools/ghl_vercel.py::run_pipeline`** writes the `vercel_deploy` F6 receipt and calls the archive step when `evidence_root=` is passed — fully additive; a caller that omits it (every prior caller/test) behaves exactly as before.
- **`SKILL.md` / `INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `06-ghl-install-pages/v2-autonomous-build-sop.md`** corrected: the "NOT GitHub" doctrine is replaced with the Vercel-deploy-plus-non-blocking-GitHub-archive shape, plus a regression guard (`qc-ghl-install-pages.sh` + the new `.github/workflows/vercel-github-archive-guard.yml`) so the old doctrine can never silently creep back in. Root `CREDENTIALS.md` now lists `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN` as optional for Skill 6 (archival only, never a build blocker).
- 33 new mock-only tests (`tests/test_ghl_github_archive.py`, `tests/test_ghl_github_reconcile.py`, plus a `TestRunPipelineGithubArchival` class in `tests/test_ghl_vercel.py`) — no live GitHub/Vercel call, no real subprocess spawn, same injectable-seam discipline as the rest of Skill 6's GHL-agnostic test suite.
- Scoped out this unit (operator decision open, documented in `06-ghl-install-pages/CHANGELOG.md`): automatic fleet-wide discovery of every run-evidence root, wiring the reconciliation sweep into a periodic cron/fleet gate, and org-owned (vs personal-account-owned) archive repos.
- All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.14` -> **v20.0.15** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt`, so CI guard G3 stays green — its own independent per-skill CHANGELOG is at v19.59.0). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` unchanged at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added/removed/substituted.

## [v20.0.14]  -  2026-07-13  -  Skill 47 (movie-producer) v14.3.0 — provisions latest Remotion + HyperFrames (required) + Chromium/ffmpeg render deps via provision-render-deps.sh; Piper demoted to optional/opt-in (off by default, SKILL47_INSTALL_PIPER=1), Fish Audio 2.1 Pro primary narrator with Gemini/OpenAI/MiniMax cloud fallbacks; opt-in Dockerfile for VPS shared-base-image; Node-22 preflight

v20.0.14 — Skill 47 (movie-producer) v14.3.0 — provisions latest Remotion + HyperFrames (required) + Chromium/ffmpeg render deps via `provision-render-deps.sh`; Piper demoted to optional/opt-in (off by default, `SKILL47_INSTALL_PIPER=1`), Fish Audio 2.1 Pro primary narrator with Gemini/OpenAI/MiniMax cloud fallbacks; opt-in Dockerfile for VPS shared-base-image; Node-22 preflight.

- **Render runtime — Remotion + HyperFrames required, provisioned on macOS AND Linux/VPS.** New `47-movie-producer/provision-render-deps.sh` (arch/OS-aware, idempotent) runs unconditionally from `install.sh` Step 3.5: it installs the pinned latest-stable Remotion (`4.0.489`) + arch/OS-specific compositor + Chrome-Headless-Shell, cache-warms the pinned latest HyperFrames (`0.7.56`) CLI + bundled Chrome, and (Linux) `apt-get`s the Chromium system libraries + `ffmpeg` that headless Chrome needs to launch — closing the gap where the upstream OpenMontage `make setup` installs ZERO Chromium system libs and every VPS render fails. The free ffmpeg documentary-montage path and the Kie.AI remote path still need no browser.
- **Piper demoted to optional / opt-in — OFF by default.** The default install path installs NO Piper and can NEVER abort on Piper: `provision-render-deps.sh` §5 installs `piper-tts==1.4.2` + pre-stages the `en_US-lessac-medium` voice ONNX model ONLY when `SKILL47_INSTALL_PIPER=1`; with the env unset it prints "Piper not requested … skipping install + voice pre-stage" and moves on. The ONLY hard failure in the provisioner is a missing OpenMontage clone; every Piper install/download failure is WARN-only ("never fatal"). The **primary narrator is Fish Audio 2.1 Pro (`s2.1-pro`)**, with **Gemini TTS / OpenAI TTS / MiniMax (a.k.a. "Mimo") as the cloud fallbacks**; Piper is an offline-only last-resort fallback. With Piper absent, OpenMontage's TTS auto-discovery simply uses the cloud providers. `SKILL.md`, `INSTALL.md`, and `DEPENDENCY-MANIFEST.md` all state this voice order and the opt-in Piper status.
- **Opt-in Dockerfile for a VPS shared base image.** `47-movie-producer/Dockerfile` (base `node:22-bookworm-slim`, Remotion's own recommended Docker base) bakes in the Chromium libs + pinned Remotion/HyperFrames so the animated-render paths work on a VPS the same way they do on the operator's Mac. It is OPT-IN only — built by hand (`docker build -t openmontage-skill47 47-movie-producer/`) and NEVER auto-invoked by `install.sh`, `provision-render-deps.sh`, or `preflight.sh`. The client's own Kie key is injected at runtime, never baked into the image.
- **Node-22 preflight retained.** `47-movie-producer/preflight.sh` still fails closed when Node major < 22 (required by HyperFrames `engines: node>=22` and Remotion), with macOS + Linux remediation.
- Skill 47's own `47-movie-producer/skill-version.txt` is at **v14.3.0** (an INDEPENDENT skill version, deliberately NOT one of the 11 repo-wide lockstep markers). All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.13` -> **v20.0.14** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. Env-var names only — no client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.13]  -  2026-07-13  -  video: ai-video-generator-specialist -> Kie-first + Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) access model + capability-equivalence engine (role v3.1); Grok Imagine = Pika-equivalent; HeyGen BYO-key notify-if-missing; Sora hard-excluded

v20.0.13 — video: rewrite the AI Video Generator Specialist role how-to (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/video/ai-video-generator-specialist.md`) to the operator's corrected **Kie-first + client-supplied Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK)** access model and generalize its missing-capability handling into a real **capability-equivalence engine** (role Version 2.0 -> 3.0 -> **3.1**). Grounded in a live headless-browser proof of the Kie.ai catalog (2026-07-13, `KIE-CATALOG-PROOF.md`).

- **Access model — Kie-first + BYOK.** Kie.ai on the company's own `KIE_API_KEY` stays the default/preferred path for every tool Kie carries; client-supplied BYOK is a supported option for tools off Kie or where the client prefers their own vendor key. Every generative video/voice/music/image call routes through Kie first, with BYOK as a clip-level option for this role's own hand-driven generation only.
- **Capability-Equivalence Engine (v3.1).** Fires automatically whenever ANY needed tool / model / skill is missing (key NOT-SET, tool not installed, capability absent). Every time it (1) evaluates equivalents the client ALREADY has — scanning BOTH the client's own tool stack AND their kie.ai catalog for a DIFFERENT tool that delivers the SAME capability — and (2) presents an explicit THREE-WAY CHOICE: (a) give me the API key (you already have the account), (b) create it (set up / obtain the key or account), or (c) use this equivalent (the same-ability tool you already have). Never just stops, never silently substitutes; notifies the exact key needed. SOP 9.6 carries the capability->equivalent map (Pika -> Grok Imagine; HeyGen -> OmniHuman 1.5 / Kling AI Avatar / Infinitalk; Fish Audio -> Gemini TTS / OpenAI TTS; missing video model -> Veo 3.1 / Runway / Seedance / Happy Horse / Gemini Omni / Wan / Kling / Hailuo).
- **Catalog corrections (verified 2026-07-13).** Pika is NOT on Kie (0 market results; absent from Kie's 84-model sitemap) — the named Kie-native equivalent is **Grok Imagine / Grok Imagine Video 1.5**, plus BYOK Pika (`PIKA_API_KEY`). HeyGen is NOT on Kie (0 results, `/heygen` 404, absent from sitemap) — **BYOK HeyGen (`HEYGEN_API_KEY`), notify-if-missing**, plus the Kie-native avatar equivalents OmniHuman 1.5 / Kling AI Avatar / Infinitalk offered when no key is present. Runway + ElevenLabs confirmed ON Kie (Kie-first; BYOK optional). Seedance slugs corrected to the real catalog (`/seedance-2-0` … `/bytedance/seedance-v1`); added Grok Imagine, the Kie avatar/lip-sync family, and Topaz Video Upscaler.
- **Sora — hard exclusion.** Sora is never wired (Kie or BYOK) and is **never offered as an equivalent** by the capability-equivalence engine, per owner directive.
- **Manifest + neighbors.** Refreshed the stale Skills-You-Operate block (`stamp-skills-you-operate.py`) and re-stamped the content manifest (`hash-content-manifest.py`): role `content_version` 1.0.4 -> **1.0.5**, `sop_count` stays **6**, video department rollup `content_sha` refreshed. Skill 47 (Movie Producer / OpenMontage) intentionally left Kie-only; its `AF-VID-PROVIDER-AUDIT` / `AF-VID-NATIVE-PROVIDER` gate is not weakened.
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.12` -> **v20.0.13** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. Env-var names only — no client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.12]  -  2026-07-13  -  fix(skill-30): Fish Audio default model s2-pro -> s2.1-pro (paid, client/production default) + document the s2.1-pro-free operator-internal dev tier constraints

v20.0.12 — fix(skill-30): the Fish Audio API reference skill defaulted its text-to-speech model to the interim `s2-pro` label. Fish Audio's current production release is S2.1 Pro (model id `s2.1-pro`), so client/production content should target `s2.1-pro`. This release updates the client/production default to `s2.1-pro` across the skill and documents the separate `s2.1-pro-free` tier's constraints so it is never used for client production.

- **Fix — production default `s2-pro` -> `s2.1-pro`.** `30-fish-audio-api-reference/SKILL.md` now sets the `Model Header` to `model: s2.1-pro` and the `Default Model (client/production)` to `s2.1-pro` (PAID, always the client-facing default). The same swap is applied to INSTALL.md, QC.md, README.md, the voice SOP, and `references/fish-audio-api-reference.md` (curl examples, parameter tables, and the phone/podcast preset table). Model selection remains an HTTP `model` HEADER value on `POST /v1/tts`, never a request-body field.
- **Free-tier caveats documented (`s2.1-pro-free`).** Added a "Model Selection: Paid vs Free" section: the free tier carries no SLA / latency guarantee, has a data-retention risk (requests "may be used to improve model quality"), a $1M ARR commercial restriction, and its free access expires end of July 2026. It is operator-internal dev/prototyping ONLY and is FORBIDDEN for client production; it must never be hardcoded as a durable default. Sourced from Fish Audio's own announcement (https://fish.audio/blog/s2-1-pro-free-api/) and docs (https://docs.fish.audio/features/text-to-speech).
- Skill version `30-fish-audio-api-reference/skill-version.txt` bumped to `v6.5.10`. All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.11` -> **v20.0.12** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model provider added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.11]  -  2026-07-13  -  fix(updater): always write the content-manifest on a successful update (manifest-first, stamp-last) + verify shared-utils sync — root-cause of the fleet false-"done" (stamp over stale content)

v20.0.11 — fix(updater): `update-skills.sh` could write the `.onboarding-version` STAMP without ever writing the `.onboarding-content-manifest.json` MANIFEST, so a box LOOKED updated while running STALE skill content that no drift-detector could ever catch. This is the root cause of the observed fleet false-"done": boxes stamped `v20.0.10` with a missing manifest while carrying real skill-content drift — one affected box's Skill-58 (`scripts/webhook/aliases.json` missing 3 shipped alias entries plus an entirely missing test) hidden under a good-looking stamp; the same class hit other boxes with 4-7 drifted files each. The stamp is a "content is current AND recorded" certificate and must NEVER exist without a matching manifest. This release makes the manifest write MANIFEST-FIRST / STAMP-LAST and fail-closed, and verifies the `shared-utils/` tree that the A3 numbered-skill gate does not cover.

- **Root cause (`update-skills.sh`).** The `.onboarding-version` stamp was written UNCONDITIONALLY *before* the manifest, and the manifest write was (a) gated on a possibly-empty `$SRC_MANIFEST` — so it was silently SKIPPED on the "legacy path" whenever `scripts/skill-content-hash.sh` was unavailable in the source (the same condition that skips the A3 content-gate) — and (b) swallowed with `... 2>/dev/null && mv ... || echo WARN (non-fatal)`, so any `python3`/`mv` failure left the box STAMPED but WITHOUT a manifest. `check-updates.sh` (A4) then reads a missing manifest as "first install — not an error" and reports the box CURRENT on a version match, so the drift can never surface (A4 needs the manifest to compare against). Additionally, `shared-utils/` (incl. the whole `sop-embed-once/` tree) was `cp -r`'d with no post-copy verification and sits OUTSIDE the A3 gate (`skill-content-hash.sh` enumerates only numbered `[0-9]*` skill dirs), so a partial copy left a box missing an entire helper tree while still getting stamped.
- **Fix 1 (primary) — manifest always written, stamp withheld on failure (manifest-first, stamp-last).** The manifest is now built + validated to a temp file for BOTH paths, then committed, then re-read to assert `tree_sha` persisted, and ONLY THEN is the `.onboarding-version` stamp written as the LAST artifact. A build failure (`python3`), a commit failure (`mv`), or a post-write `tree_sha` mismatch is now FATAL (`exit 1`) — the stamp is withheld so a box is never reported "current" without a matching manifest. On the legacy path (empty `$SRC_MANIFEST`) a DEGRADED manifest is still emitted (`content_verified: "unavailable"`, empty `skills` map) so the stamp is never orphaned and A4 sees a degraded — not silently-current — box. A new `content_verified` field (`"true"` on the verified path) is added to the manifest schema. The pre-existing A3 content-gate (installed skill digests == source digests, `exit 1` on mismatch) is unchanged and still runs BEFORE the stamp on the verified path, so Skill-58-type content drift cannot hide under a good stamp.
- **Fix 2 — `shared-utils/` refresh is now verified and stamp-gating.** After the `cp -r`, the updater asserts every source top-level entry is present on the box (source ⊆ dest — missing source entries only; dest supersets are fine, so benign drift can't false-positive) via a new `_SHAREDUTILS_STATUS` latch, wired into the existing `_STEP_GATE_FAILS` stamp-gate. An incomplete `shared-utils/` (e.g. a missing `sop-embed-once/`) now WITHHOLDS the stamp instead of shipping a silently-partial helper tree.
- **Proof.** A hermetic before/after harness that EXTRACTS and RUNS the real manifest+stamp block from each version, on identical inputs, reproduces the exact defect on `main` (LEGACY-EMPTY and PY-FAIL both `rc=0 stamp=set manifest=ABSENT`) and proves it fixed on this branch (LEGACY-EMPTY -> `stamp=set manifest=PRESENT content_verified=unavailable`; PY-FAIL -> `rc=1 stamp=ABSENT` — withheld; NORMAL -> `stamp=set manifest=PRESENT content_verified=true`). A second targeted harness proves the `shared-utils` gate flags a partial copy (missing `sop-embed-once/` -> status=fail -> stamp withheld) and passes a complete one. `tests/unit/update-skills-resume-cron.test.sh` stays green (20/20). `bash -n` clean; `shellcheck` introduces zero new findings vs `main` (identical SC-code distribution, 0 error-level).
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.10` -> **v20.0.11** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.10]  -  2026-07-13  -  fix(onboarding): decouple workforce-provisioning completeness from the skills-content version stamp + idempotent/clobber-guarded secrets/.env writes

v20.0.10 — fix(onboarding): the completeness-gate was conflating "is this client's WORKFORCE fully built" with "is the skills CONTENT current" and WITHHOLDING the `.onboarding-version` stamp for the former. In the fleet convergence ~11 boxes installed the v20.0.9 skills content and PASSED the A3 content-gate, but stayed UNSTAMPED because the client had not finished the AI Workforce interview (empty departments -> floor-fill), carried a large artifact-refresh queue of OUT-OF-SCOPE items, or sat below the 95% department floor — all WORKFORCE-provisioning states, not skills-content problems. This release decouples the two so a box on current content stamps its content version, while the workforce is still driven to completion by the separate post-stamp qc-completeness run + onboarding-resume cron. Also hardens two secrets/.env writers that reordered the file and could clobber it.

- **Fix 1 (primary) — content-vs-workforce gate split (`update-skills.sh`).** The pre-stamp completeness-gate now WITHHOLDS the `.onboarding-version` stamp ONLY for SKILLS-CONTENT integrity misses and SURFACES workforce-provisioning incompleteness as a non-blocking advisory:
  - **STILL stamp-gating (unchanged protection):** the A3 content-gate (installed skill digests == source digests); `_U6B_PERSONA_FAIL` (persona-index content wiring — triad-divergent library, asset missing vectors, installed sentinel != manifest release_tag, or the provision helper did not run); `_D2_REFRESH_STATUS` (`refresh-stale-roles.py` rc 3 — an IN-SCOPE role/SOP content refresh that SHOULD have re-applied the new library content to an EXISTING artifact genuinely failed); and `_SHAREDCORE_STATUS` (`link_shared_core_files` wiring step errored). A real content failure in ANY of these STILL withholds the stamp and exits 1.
  - **No longer stamp-gating (newly decoupled — advisory only):** workforce floor-fill / workforce QC via `migrate-existing-workforce.sh` (its exit code is a WORKFORCE verdict — rc 2 = a dept below the 95% floor, rc 3 = a dept at zero materialization / no workforce yet for an interview-incomplete client) is split out of the old shared `_D2_REFRESH_STATUS` latch into a new `_D2_MIGRATE_STATUS` latch; and dept-agent activation via `materialize-dept-agents.sh` (`_D5_ACTIVATION_PASS`, agents.list[] below this box's computed department floor). Both now feed a `_WORKFORCE_INCOMPLETE_NOTES` advisory that is printed and left to the post-stamp qc-completeness run (`QC_COMPLETENESS_RC`) + the onboarding-resume cron, and NEVER withholds the content stamp. The A3 content-gate independently protects the shipped skills content, so decoupling the workforce QC (which measures the client's built departments, not the shipped library) loses no content protection.
- **Fix 2 — idempotent, order-stable, clobber-guarded `secrets/.env` writes.** Two helpers did an unconditional `grep -v "^VAR=" | mv | append`, which (a) MOVED the var to the bottom of the file on EVERY run so an unchanged run never left `secrets/.env` byte-identical (breaking hash-based revert / integrity checks) and (b) swallowed a failed `grep` (`|| true`) whose empty `.tmp` the `mv` then clobbered the whole file with. Fixed at both sites — `shared-utils/provision-persona-index.sh` `wire_ghl_funnel_catalog` (`GHL_FUNNEL_CATALOG` / `GHL_FUNNEL_INDEX`, via a new `_pidx_write_env` helper) and `install.sh` `inject_shared_operator_secrets` `_shared_write_env`: if the exact `VAR=val` line already exists the write is a byte-identical no-op (no reorder); otherwise the scoped replace backs up to a `.bak` first and only swaps in the rewritten `.tmp` when `grep` did not error AND the `.tmp` is non-empty (or a legit all-removed case), so a `grep` failure can never blank the file.
- **Proof.** A hermetic harness that EXTRACTS and RUNS the real edited gate block proves the stamp is STILL withheld on each content failure (persona content, in-scope refresh rc 3, shared-core wiring) and on a mixed content+workforce failure, and is WRITTEN on a workforce-only miss (migrate floor-fill and/or D5 dept-floor) with the advisory printed. A second harness proves both `.env` helpers leave an unchanged file byte-identical (var preserved mid-file), update on value change, preserve sibling vars, write a `.bak`, and never clobber. `tests/unit/d5-dept-activation-floor-gate.test.sh` (7/7), `provision-idempotency.test.sh` (14/14), `prebuilt-index-section-tagged.test.sh` (25/25), and `refresh-stale-roles.test.sh` (10/10) stay green; the D5 test's comments were updated to state the floor check now feeds the workforce advisory (not the content stamp), assertions unchanged.
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.9` -> **v20.0.10** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.9]  -  2026-07-12  -  SECURITY/PRIVACY: qc-completeness maintenance-silence gate (OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT) + --quiet on roll callers + operator-only durable routing

v20.0.9 — SECURITY/PRIVACY: qc-completeness maintenance-silence gate (OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT) + --quiet on roll callers + operator-only durable routing; stops the fleet roll leaking internal QC tables to client Telegram.

Closes a client-data leak in the fleet roll path. `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/qc-completeness.sh` resolved `channels.telegram.allowFrom[0]` — the CLIENT's own chat on a client box — and sent it the INTERNAL per-department QC gap table whenever it ran non-`--quiet` and workforce STATUS != PASS. The roll's own entrypoint reaches that send path (via `migrate-existing-workforce.sh` Step 5, which invoked qc-completeness with no suppression), so every convergence re-run could message the client. This already leaked to a real client. Per-box operator routing alone could not be trusted because some boxes have an operator-escalation chat mis-pointed at the client, so the primary fix is a HARD maintenance-silence gate independent of any box's chat/account config.

- **Fix 1 (primary) — maintenance-silence env gate.** `qc-completeness.sh` now suppresses the Telegram send when `${OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT:-0}` == `1`, in ADDITION to the existing `--quiet` / `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE` paths. When suppressed it emits a distinct audit log line (`maintenance-silent mode — QC alert suppressed (OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT=1)`) — QC is still COMPUTED, only the notification is gated. The gate is independent of `--quiet` and of any chat/account config.
- **Fix 2 (primary) — export the gate for the whole roll.** `update-skills.sh` `main()` `export`s `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT=1` at the top of the run so BOTH `migrate-existing-workforce.sh` and the embedded qc-completeness call inherit it. `shared-utils/fleet_refresh_runner.py` passes `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT=1` into the updater's subprocess environment (the runner executes ON the target box), so it crosses to remote fleet boxes.
- **Fix 3 (belt-and-suspenders) — `--quiet` on both roll callers.** `migrate-existing-workforce.sh` Step 5 and the `update-skills.sh` embedded qc-completeness capture now pass `--quiet`, so both known roll sends stay log-only even if the env gate ever regresses.
- **Fix 4 (durable correctness) — operator-only routing for normal qc runs.** The qc-completeness `!= PASS` send now resolves the OPERATOR escalation chat (`OPERATOR_ESCALATION_CHAT_ID`, then the known aliases; env first, then `openclaw.json` `env.vars`) via a new externalized helper `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/_qc_operator_chat.py`, and sends on `--account operator --session-key agent:main:operator`. When no operator chat is configured it is LOG-ONLY — it NO LONGER resolves `_qc_owner_chat.py` / `allowFrom[0]`, so even a normal (non-roll) qc run can never message the client. Three stale "Telegrams the operator" comments (qc-completeness header, update-skills embedded-qc comment, migrate Step 5) corrected to state the real behavior.
- **Regression lock.** `tests/unit/qc-maintenance-quiet-guard.test.sh` rewritten to prove, hermetically end-to-end: the send fires to the OPERATOR chat (never the client owner even when both are configured); an owner-only config sends ZERO times (log-only, never falls back to the client); `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE=1`, `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT=1` (independent of `--quiet`), and `--quiet` each suppress the send; and update-skills.sh statically wires both `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE=1` on the embedded call and the roll-wide `export OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE_SILENT=1`.
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.8` -> **v20.0.9** via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt` rolls in lockstep (skill-23 scripts changed, CI guard G3). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` UNCHANGED at `v6.0.2`; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.8]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(roll): pin Command Center to v6.0.2 (VPS container-portability fixes: sqlite3 CLI removed, mktemp portable, orphan-port guard python3-portable)

v20.0.8 — pin Command Center to v6.0.2 (VPS container-portability fixes: sqlite3 CLI removed, mktemp portable, orphan-port guard python3-portable).

Trivial pin bump so the fleet roll delivers three already-merged Command Center container-portability fixes instead of leaving them stranded behind the pin.

- **Problem.** `blackceo-command-center` merged and tagged `v6.0.2` (annotated tag at commit `c418785ffe4377ea2e231ea09f9597fb6b4b75c7`, all CI green) fixing three VPS-container defects: the `sqlite3` CLI dependency was removed, the `mktemp` call was made portable, and the orphan-port guard was made `python3`-portable. `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` was still `v6.0.1` — `resolve_cc_tag()` returns `pinnedTag` unconditionally, so `fleet-refresh.sh` would keep checking out the un-fixed `v6.0.1` on every roll, permanently stranding these fixes behind the pin on every Linux/VPS fleet box.
- **Fix.** `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` bumped `v6.0.1` -> `v6.0.2`. `minVersion` UNCHANGED at `v4.59.1` (permissive, so `assert_min_version` does not block a box still mid-update on an older CC); `maxVersion` UNCHANGED (`null`). `v6.0.2` >= `minVersion` and `maxVersion` is unbounded, so the schema contract (`pinnedTag >= minVersion`, `pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded`) holds; `python3 shared-utils/cc_compat.py --self-test cc-compat.json` resolves `cc_tag = v6.0.2`. No app code, no endpoint, and no `mission-control.db` schema changed in the pinned CC delta.
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.7` -> **v20.0.8** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` is bumped to **`v6.0.2`**; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.7]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(roll): bump Command Center pin v6.0.0->v6.0.1 (mktemp fix)

Trivial pin bump so the fleet roll delivers a already-merged Command Center fix instead of leaving it stranded behind the pin.

- **Problem.** `blackceo-command-center` merged and tagged `v6.0.1` (annotated tag at commit `d4abf9436ff47a2c1aaadb8b11bd335e9c5551b8`, PR #157): `scripts/atomic-deploy.sh` line 428 called `mktemp /tmp/atomic-build-exit-$$.txt` — a fixed filename with no `XXXXXX` template. GNU coreutils `mktemp` (every Linux/VPS fleet box) REQUIRES the trailing path component to contain a template of at least 3 consecutive `X`s and fails closed (`too few X's in template`) without one, so Phase 2 of the atomic deploy (`npm run build`) never ran on any VPS box. macOS's BSD `mktemp` is lenient about a missing template (falls back to the literal path), which is why this never surfaced on the operator Mac. `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` was still `v6.0.0` — `resolve_cc_tag()` returns `pinnedTag` unconditionally, so `fleet-refresh.sh` would keep checking out the un-fixed `v6.0.0` on every roll, permanently stranding the fix behind the pin.
- **Fix.** `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` bumped `v6.0.0` -> `v6.0.1`. `minVersion` UNCHANGED at `v4.59.1` (permissive, so `assert_min_version` does not block a box still mid-update on an older CC); `maxVersion` UNCHANGED (`null`). `v6.0.1` >= `minVersion` and `maxVersion` is unbounded, so the schema contract (`pinnedTag >= minVersion`, `pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded`) holds. Verified the `v6.0.0`->`v6.0.1` delta (`git compare`) is scoped to `CHANGELOG.md` + `package.json` + `package-lock.json` + `version` (release metadata, all 5 CC version locations) plus the single-line `atomic-deploy.sh` fix — no app code, no endpoint, no `mission-control.db` schema change, so this pin bump carries zero migration risk.
- All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.6` -> **v20.0.7** via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted.

## [v20.0.6]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(cc-installer): D6 update-only credential-mirror gate + D7 detached-HEAD-safe git sync

Two Skill-32 installer defects fixed together, both in `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh`.

- **D6 — credential-drift on a code-only roll.** `cc_write_env_local()` called `cc_mirror_api_auth_to_agent_secrets()` UNCONDITIONALLY, including during `--update-only` (the mode a code-only fleet roll drives via `update-skills.sh`'s Skill-32 "CC data/content refresh" step). The mirror WRITES into `$OC_ROOT/secrets/.env` whenever `MC_API_TOKEN`/`WEBHOOK_SECRET` are absent OR hold an empty placeholder there — exactly the store `scripts/fleet-roll/preflight-credential-guard.sh` fingerprints (label `openclaw-secrets-env`, content-hashed). On a box in either state, a code-only roll that never intended to touch credentials would mutate the guarded secrets store mid-roll; the guard's `do_verify` would see CONTENT CHANGED and the runbook would force-revert an otherwise-healthy box — and because the revert undoes the mirror's own write, the box would drift/revert on every subsequent roll too (the "in-place value rewrite, line count unchanged" signature matches the empty-placeholder case exactly: `cc_env_set_if_absent` drops the empty line and re-appends the filled value). Fixed by gating the mirror call on `UPDATE_ONLY`: it now runs ONLY on a full install (first-time provisioning); `--update-only` never writes the agent credential store. The existing loud post-condition (unchanged) still catches a genuinely under-provisioned update-only box and names the remedy (a full install) — a real gap is still surfaced loudly, never silently mutated mid-roll. The guard itself (`preflight-credential-guard.sh`) is untouched and stays exactly as strict: this removes the CAUSE of the drift, not the detector.
- **D7 — CC update was a silent no-op on a detached-HEAD checkout.** Both git-refresh call sites (the `--update-only` refresh path and the full-install already-cloned path) used a bare `git pull --ff-only`, which ABORTS on a DETACHED HEAD (`"You are not currently on a branch. Please specify which branch you want to merge with."`) — the exact shape of a client Command Center pinned to a version tag. The abort was swallowed to a `WARN` and the stale, pinned checkout was kept, so `npm install` / `db:push` / build / `update.sh` all ran against OLD code and the CC never advanced (observed on multiple client Command Center boxes). Fixed with a new `cc_git_sync_to_default_branch()`: fetches origin's default branch, then either fast-forwards (`git merge --ff-only`, already on a branch) or re-attaches (`git checkout -B <branch> origin/<branch>`, detached). Both call sites now route through it. Deliberately never uses `git reset --hard` / `checkout -f` / `git clean`, so gitignored files (`.env.local`, `*.db`) are untouched by construction (no git operation ever touches an ignored path) and a tracked local patch (e.g. `logo-config.json`) either carries across the switch cleanly or makes the helper refuse non-destructively on a genuine conflict — identical safety posture to the old ff-only abort, just no longer blind to the detached-HEAD case that was silently eating every update.
- **Tests.** New `scripts/test-cc-update-only-credential-and-git-sync.sh` (14 cases, self-contained bash + git + a hasher, no gateway/network). Extracts both fixed functions VERBATIM from the real installer via a function-name-anchored `awk` range (no reimplementation of the logic under test) and exercises them against sandboxed HOMEs / a real local git remote. D6: a fail-first TEETH case proves the OLD unconditional call really does mutate `secrets/.env` from an absent or empty-placeholder state; the FIXED `cc_write_env_local` is then run under `UPDATE_ONLY=true` against all three credential states (absent/empty-placeholder/present) and proven byte-identical after the call in every case; a full-install (`UPDATE_ONLY=false`) run is proven to still provision the agent secrets store, unchanged from pre-fix behavior; and the REAL `scripts/fleet-roll/preflight-credential-guard.sh` is driven snapshot→(fixed call)→verify and proven to see NO drift from this call site during a simulated roll, while a SEPARATE genuine external credential rewrite between snapshot and verify is STILL caught (nonzero) — the guard is not weakened. D7: a TEETH case proves a bare `git pull --ff-only` really does abort on a detached-HEAD checkout with HEAD left unmoved; the FIXED helper is then proven to (a) sync a detached checkout to latest `origin/main` while a non-conflicting local uncommitted edit survives, (b) fast-forward an already-attached branch cleanly (regression check), (c) refuse a GENUINE conflict non-destructively — nonzero return, HEAD unmoved, local edit not overwritten — and (d) leave a gitignored file byte-identical across a successful sync; plus a static check that the new helper's own source never invokes `reset --hard` / `checkout -f` / `clean -f`. 14/14 PASS against the fixed installer; independently confirmed the pre-fix installer lacks the `UPDATE_ONLY` gate around the D6 call site and lacks `cc_git_sync_to_default_branch` entirely (grep against `origin/main`), and that both `git pull --ff-only` call sites are bare there — the fix addresses a real, reproduced defect, not a hypothetical one.

`32-command-center-setup` content changed (`run-full-install.sh` + new `scripts/test-cc-update-only-credential-and-git-sync.sh` under the repo-root `scripts/`, which is NOT a numbered skill dir), so `32-command-center-setup/skill-version.txt` rolled `v12.9.37` -> **`v12.9.38`** in lockstep (CI guard G3). Skill 32's `SKILL.md` carries no top-level `version:` frontmatter field, so `skill-frontmatter-version-guard` reports it as skipped (not applicable), not failed. All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.5` -> **v20.0.6** via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` is UNCHANGED at **`v6.0.0`**; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.5]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(update-skills): correct D3 inverted-boolean gate + D5 magic-number gate + wire D1 double-gate into CI

Follow-up to v20.0.4's completeness-gate unification (PR #576), fixing three QC findings against that release before it merges.

- **F1 — D3 `_RECONCILE_OK` inverted-boolean gate (`update-skills.sh`).** `reconcile_persona_assets()` (`shared-utils/provision-persona-index.sh`) exports `_RECONCILE_OK=1` on success and `0` on failure — the INVERSE of the standard shell `0`-is-success convention. The v20.0.4 D3 completion re-assertion wrote its check as if `0` meant success (`[ "${_RECONCILE_OK:-1}" != "0" ]`), so a genuinely SUCCESSFUL reconcile (`_RECONCILE_OK=1`, which is `!= "0"`) tripped the FAIL branch and blocked the stamp on every healthy box, while an actual reconcile FAILURE (`_RECONCILE_OK=0`) satisfied `!= "0"` as false and could fall through. Flipped the comparison to `[ "${_RECONCILE_OK:-1}" = "0" ]`, matching the variable's real convention — a successful reconcile now correctly PASSES the gate, an actual failure still correctly FAILS it.
- **F2 — D5 dept-agent activation fixed `-lt 2` magic number (`update-skills.sh` + new `tests/unit/d5-dept-activation-floor-gate.test.sh`).** The D5 pre-stamp gate asserted `agents.list[] -lt 2` as its only completeness bar. A genuine interview-complete box carries the 28-department universal floor (`department-naming-map.json`: 22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary, net of any owner-declined department), so a box whose dept-agent activation genuinely failed for MOST departments but still kept >=2 `agents.list[]` entries sailed through as a false PASS — the exact "copied != live" defect class D5 exists to catch. The gate now derives a per-box `_D5_EXPECTED_COUNT` from `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/department-floor.py --json`'s `expected_floor_count` (the same source `qc-completeness.sh`'s own floor gate already imports, keeping D5 in lockstep instead of drifting) and gates on THAT instead of the fixed threshold. Graceful degradation preserved: when `department-floor.py` is missing or cannot resolve a verdict for this box (older bundle, no departments dir yet), the gate falls back to the prior `-lt 2` wiring-only check rather than introducing a new false-FAIL path. New hermetic CI test proves both halves: a partial-activation box (3 agents — ABOVE the OLD threshold, so it would have false-PASSED before this fix) is now BLOCKED; a fully-activated box (agents == the computed floor) is NOT blocked. 7/7 PASS against the patched `update-skills.sh`; 4/7 PASS (3 static checks FAIL) against the pre-fix original, confirming the test discriminates the fix.
- **F3 — D1 `update-skills.sh` double-gate had zero executing test coverage (`scripts/test-fleet-refresh.sh` + `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`).** `shared-utils/fleet_refresh_runner.py`'s `step_pull_onboarding()` (correct as shipped in v20.0.4 — not touched here) double-gates `pull-onboarding` success on BOTH `update-skills.sh` returncode `== 0` AND the active skills-dir stamp actually equalling the pinned tag post-run. The old Test 6 in `test-fleet-refresh.sh` never exercised this contract — it had no `update-skills.sh` fixture at all and only asserted the runner emitted syntactically-valid JSON. Test 6 now builds a mock `update-skills.sh` (new `build_mock_update_skills()` helper) and asserts `pull-onboarding=ok` when it exits 0 and stamps the pinned tag; two new cases, Test 6a (exit 0 but wrong stamp) and Test 6b (right stamp but exit 1), each assert `pull-onboarding` FAILS — proving neither half of the double-gate can be satisfied alone. `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml` gained a new executing step, "PRD 1.11 D1: fleet-refresh behavioral test suite (test-fleet-refresh.sh)", immediately after the existing static-only fleet-refresh-artefacts step, so a regression in this contract now fails CI instead of being provable only by code read. The step passes the workflow's auto-provided `GITHUB_TOKEN` through so `fleet_refresh_runner.py`'s Wave-5 GitHub Contents API preflight authenticates instead of hitting the unauthenticated-caller rate limit.
- **Proof.** `bash -n` clean on `update-skills.sh` and `scripts/test-fleet-refresh.sh`; `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(...)"` clean on `qc-static.yml`. New `tests/unit/d5-dept-activation-floor-gate.test.sh` run hermetically (sandboxed `HOME`, explicit `--departments-dir`) confirms `department-floor.py` reports `expected_floor_count=28` with no departments/no declines. `bash scripts/test-fleet-refresh.sh --verbose` run twice end-to-end with an authenticated `GITHUB_TOKEN`: 40/40 PASS both runs, including Test 6/6a/6b.

All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.4` -> **v20.0.5** in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` is UNCHANGED at **`v6.0.0`**; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.4]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(update-skills): unify the completeness gate — single stamp chokepoint, D1-D5 convergence

Closes a class of "silent partial roll" bugs where `update-skills.sh` could write its `.onboarding-version` stamp (declaring a box fully updated) even though persona provisioning, artifact refresh, shared-core linking, or dept-agent activation genuinely did not complete. All four defects converge on ONE gate contract keyed on the single stamp chokepoint at `update-skills.sh` (`echo "$ONBOARDING_VERSION" > "$SKILLS_DIR/.onboarding-version"`). Universal convention: PASS == fully completed OR a benign/legitimate skip (idempotent no-op, already-current, pre-interview no-op, nothing-to-do, out-of-scope); FAIL only when a completeness-critical action genuinely did not happen — so a healthy box always still reaches the stamp.

- **`update-skills.sh` (D3/D4/D5 convergence).** Adds a block of completeness-gate latches (`_U6B_PERSONA_FAIL`, `_D2_REFRESH_STATUS`, `_SHAREDCORE_STATUS`, `_D5_ACTIVATION_PASS`), all initialized to their PASS value before Step U6b (`set -u` safe). Persona provisioning (U6b) now flips its latch on a triad-divergent skip, an `asset_rebuild_required:true` skip, a missing manifest/helper, AND a new post-provision completion re-assertion that cross-checks the exported `_RECONCILE_OK` flag plus the on-disk `.prebuilt-index-version` sentinel against the manifest's `release_tag`. `migrate-existing-workforce.sh` and `refresh-stale-roles.py` non-zero exits now flip `_D2_REFRESH_STATUS` (the latter captured pipefail-correctly — the old `cmd | tee ... || echo` swallowed the real exit code because `tee`'s status, not python3's, terminated the pipeline). `link_shared_core_files` failure flips `_SHAREDCORE_STATUS`. A new PRE-STAMP `materialize-dept-agents.sh` check flips `_D5_ACTIVATION_PASS` on a genuine registration failure or an interview-complete run that still leaves `agents.list[]` under 2 entries (a pre-interview self-skip or Skill-32-not-yet-installed box is benign, not a fail). ONE unified gate block, inserted between the existing A3 content-gate and the stamp write, refuses the stamp and exits 1 (`rm -rf` temp dirs, print a consolidated banner) if ANY latch is FAIL — replacing three separately-proposed overlapping gate blocks with one. The companion `.onboarding-content-manifest.json` also gains an additive `activation` sub-dict (`deptAgents`, `deptAgentCount`, `skillVerifyGate`) for post-hoc observability.
- **`shared-utils/provision-persona-index.sh` (D3 leaf truth).** `reconcile_persona_assets()` now exports `_RECONCILE_OK` (1 by default, flipped to 0 on a missing Skill-22 source or an unreadable union-merge seed). Every existing `return 0` is preserved verbatim — bare callers under `set -euo pipefail` (e.g. `install.sh`) are never aborted; truth is carried out-of-band for `update-skills.sh`'s new completion re-assertion to consume.
- **`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/refresh-stale-roles.py` (D2: closes the sop/dept drain gap).** `.artifact-refresh-queue.json` had queue rows for `kind=="sop"` and `kind=="dept"` STALE items that this consumer never drained — only `kind=="role"` was ever handled (P2-08). Adds a `sop` branch (`refresh_sop()`: re-copies the canonical SOP file bytes from `templates/role-library/<dept>/sops/<fname>` over the client's existing copy) and a `dept` branch (a department's `content_sha` is a rollup over its member roles' hashes — there is no independent dept-level file, so draining a STALE dept row is a provenance restamp only, via the new `_apply_state_restamps()`, additive-only against `artifactProvenance.sops`/`.depts`, never touching `.roles`/`.personas`). New exit code 3 (in-scope sop/dept refresh incomplete — a real IO error, a malformed row, or a state-restamp write failure) alongside the existing 0 (drain complete/benign) and 1 (usage error); also writes a pipe-immune `.artifact-refresh-receipt.json` and prints `DRAIN_STATUS ok=<0|1> ...` on stdout as a cross-check for a caller whose stdout is piped through `tee`. The role branch is moved verbatim, unchanged.
- **`shared-utils/fleet_refresh_runner.py` (D1: runner read-path + entry-point correction).** `step_pull_onboarding()` now shells `update-skills.sh` (with `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_AUTO_SYNC=1`, 1200s timeout) instead of `force-update.sh --apply`, and double-gates success on BOTH `returncode == 0` AND the active skills-dir stamp (`paths["skills"]/.onboarding-version`) actually equalling the pinned tag post-run — never trusting a bare zero exit alone, since `update-skills.sh`'s own gate above can legitimately exit 1 without ever reaching the stamp. `step_detect()` is repointed from reading `repo_root/.onboarding-version` (never written by anything) onto the same active skills-dir path, so detect and verify can no longer disagree with the actual roll outcome. `force-update.sh` is untouched and remains the manual "machine was off" notifier; it is no longer the autonomous skill-sync entry point.
- **Proof.** All 4 files pass `bash -n` / `py_compile`; shellcheck introduces zero new warnings on the two shell files vs. the pre-existing baseline (7 pre-existing hits in `update-skills.sh`, 0 in `provision-persona-index.sh`, both unchanged). Locally reproduced against a fresh clone: a synthetic sop/dept-queue drain refreshes real content and restamps provenance additively (exit 0, `DRAIN_STATUS ok=1`); a genuine permission-denied write on an in-scope sop item correctly returns exit 3 (`DRAIN_STATUS ok=0`) with the receipt reflecting the failure. All directly-relevant existing regression suites pass unmodified: `tests/unit/{bounded-resume-cron,core-updates-all-skills-wired,cron-owner-chat-guard,onboarding-state-obs-api,persona-task-mode-wiring,prebuilt-index-section-tagged,qc-maintenance-quiet-guard,update-skills-resume-cron,wire-no-match-completes,refresh-stale-roles,asset-rebuild-required-gate,provision-idempotency,persona-asset-freshness,provision-preserves-local-personas,both-paths-zhe-delivery}.test.sh`, `scripts/test-config-injection-shapes.sh`, `scripts/test-gws-credential-preservation.sh`, `32-command-center-setup/scripts/test_guard_department_runtime_parity.py`, `tests/probe/test-p208-sop-role-provisioning-probe.sh`, and all 8 Skill-23 hermetic provisioning suites.

All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.3` -> **v20.0.4** in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green — `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`'s own `skill-version.txt`/frontmatter roll with the repo since `refresh-stale-roles.py` lives under that skill dir). `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` is UNCHANGED at **`v6.0.0`**; `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.3]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(ci): correct video-pipeline-lockstep fixture — free-path exit 2 + paid-keyless exit 4

Patch release. Greens the `Video pipeline lockstep (Movie Producer)` CI guard, which had been latently red since 2026-07-09 (run 28984750142). No production enforcement changed — the fix is CI/test-side only. `47-movie-producer/scripts/executive_producer.py` is unchanged and remains correct/stricter on purpose.

- **Root cause (stale test expectation, not a regression).** Commit `5aaf5303` ("fix(skill47/SK1-67): fail loud on a paid Kie job with no API key at phase-0") made the driver HARD-ABORT a paid Kie job that has no `KIE_API_KEY` at phase-0 (`AF-VID-KIE-BALANCE`, exit 4), BEFORE the phase-precondition check. The lockstep guard's BAD fixture was a PAID job (`kie-video.yaml`, `kie_in_scope:true`, cost 2.0), so it now hits exit 4 first and can never reach the `AF-VID-PHASE-SKIPPED` (exit 2) case the "BAD run MUST HARD-ABORT (exit 2)" step asserted. CI runners correctly carry no `KIE_API_KEY`, so the step went red. Production got *stricter*; only the self-test drifted out of lockstep with it.
- **The fix — a free-path BAD fixture (exit 2) + a paid-keyless assertion (exit 4).** `47-movie-producer/test-fixtures/make-video-fixtures.sh` now materializes a third fixture `bad-run-free`: the identical phase-skip bypass signature (no upstream attestations, no owner-authorized skip, `ffprobe_pass:false` render receipt) but on the FREE `documentary-montage.yaml` path (`kie_in_scope:false`, cost 0). Phase-0 is N/A there, so the driver reaches the phase-precondition check and HARD-ABORTS with `AF-VID-PHASE-SKIPPED` (exit 2) — the fixture that proves the phase-skip gate itself bites. The existing paid `bad-run` fixture is unchanged and now doubles as the SK1-67 fixture.
- **`.github/workflows/video-pipeline-lockstep.yml`.** The exit-2 step was repointed at `bad-run-free`, and a NEW step "BAD paid keyless run MUST HARD-ABORT (driver exit 4 — AF-VID-KIE-BALANCE)" runs the driver on the paid `bad-run` with `KIE_API_KEY` guaranteed unset and requires exit 4 — locking SK1-67 in at the driver level so it can never silently revert. Header + Summary updated to list both BAD assertions.
- **`47-movie-producer/qc-movie-producer.sh` (install-QC parity — the plan's third file).** The install QC embeds its own GOOD/BAD driver self-test that asserted the paid `bad-run` exits 2 — latently broken by the same SK1-67 change (it just never ran in the red CI job, which fail-stopped at an earlier step). Its BAD assertion was split the same way: `bad-run-free` -> exit 2, and `bad-run` with `KIE_API_KEY` unset (`env -u`, deterministic even on a box carrying a real Kie key) -> exit 4.
- **Proof.** Reproduced locally against a fresh clone: `bad-run-free` -> exit 2 (`AF-VID-PHASE-SKIPPED`), `bad-run` (keyless) -> exit 4 (`AF-VID-KIE-BALANCE`), GOOD run -> exit 0 through V-CONTROL. `video_sync_check.py`, `test_video_preflight.py`, `video_gate_integrity_check.py`, `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`, and `qc-movie-producer.sh` all pass (76 passed | 0 failed).

Changing files under `47-movie-producer/` requires a skill-version bump (CI guard G3), so `47-movie-producer/skill-version.txt` and its `SKILL.md` frontmatter rolled `v14.1.5` -> **`v14.1.6`** in lockstep. All 11 repo version markers rolled `v20.0.2` -> **v20.0.3** via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. `cc-compat.json` `commandCenter.pinnedTag` is UNCHANGED at **`v6.0.0`** (`resolve_cc_tag` -> `v6.0.0`); `minVersion` unchanged at `v4.59.1`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.2]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(roll): bump Command Center pin v4.69.1->v6.0.0 to prevent fleet-wide CC downgrade

Patch release. Corrects the Command Center pin in `cc-compat.json` so the fleet roll deploys the current CC major release instead of downgrading every box. No feature code, no skill-content change; repo-side only, touches no box.

- **The fix.** `commandCenter.pinnedTag` was stale at `v4.69.1`. `resolve_cc_tag()` in `shared-utils/cc_compat.py` returns `pinnedTag` unconditionally, so `fleet-refresh.sh` would `git checkout v4.69.1` and DOWNGRADE any box already on Command Center `v6.0.0`. Bumped `pinnedTag` `v4.69.1` -> **`v6.0.0`**; `resolve_cc_tag()` now resolves to `v6.0.0` (verified via `cc_compat.py --self-test`).
- **Constraint holds.** `minVersion` is UNCHANGED at `v4.59.1` (kept permissive so the roll's `assert_min_version` does not block boxes still on an older CC mid-update). `v6.0.0` >= `minVersion` (`v4.59.1`) and `maxVersion` is `null`, so the schema contract (`pinnedTag >= minVersion`, `pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded`) holds; the `cc-compat.json` self-test and schema/constraint gate pass.

All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.1` -> **v20.0.2** in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v20.0.x train.

## [v20.0.1]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(ci): green qc-static — restamp content manifest + pin pre-P3-06 fixture

Patch release. Greens the last red CI guard (`qc-static`, "QC static invariants") on `main`, cutting `v20.0.0` -> **v20.0.1**. No feature code, no fleet action, touches no box.

- **Content-manifest restamp (the regression fix).** The `v20.0.0`-train work edited four `rescue-rangers` role files (`diagnostician`, `qc-postmortem-specialist`, `structured-fix-operator`, `ticket-clerk`) but did not re-run `hash-content-manifest.py`, leaving STALE `content_sha` in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/_index.json`. The qc-static CONTENT-HASH sub-gate (`hash-content-manifest.py --check`) exited non-zero on the stale sha. Re-ran the generator: `content_version` `1.0.0` -> `1.0.1` for exactly those four roles, `content_hashed_at` refreshed; hashes were regenerated by the script, never hand-edited. `--check` now PASSes (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, every stored `content_sha` matches its live file, no token leaks).
- **Pre-P3-06 fixture pinned to a fixed SHA.** The qc-static "Skill 03 fail-first fixture" step derived the pre-fix `qc-agent-browser.sh` from the MOVING `origin/main` ref. Now that P3-06 (`73ff2975`) is on `main`, `origin/main` carries the drift gate, so the step's own sanity check failed permanently. Re-pinned to the last-blind commit `49b88cd5` (parent of P3-06) — the SAME SHA `agent-browser-lifecycle-guard` already pins — and set the checkout to `fetch-depth: 0` so the ancestor SHA resolves via `git show` with no network fetch. Verified locally: fixture captures 26 lines with no drift gate; the pack / drift-gate / headed-refuse / reaper-assert regression tests all pass against it.
- **Stabilized a flaky qc-static assertion (T4b).** With the two fixes above landed, the qc-static "Skill 41 agent-browser probe hard-timeout + child-tree-kill (AUD-19)" step exposed a pre-existing RACE in `41-build-with-ai-playbook/scripts/lib/browser-probe-timeout.test.sh` (masked earlier because qc-static fail-fasts at the CONTENT-HASH step). T4b asserted the probe wrapper was dead after a fixed `sleep 1.5` following `kill -TERM`, but the wrapper's EXIT/TERM trap runs `ab_kill_tree` cleanup BEFORE it exits, so under CI load it was still alive at the check (~2/3 flake locally). T4c/T4d — the assertions that prove the actual AUD-19 guarantee (child tree reaped, ZERO orphans) — always passed. Replaced the fixed sleep with a bounded poll (up to 20s) for the wrapper's actual death; 8/8 deterministic locally. `41-build-with-ai-playbook/skill-version.txt` bumped `1.5.7` -> `1.5.8` (CI guard G3). No production behavior changed — test-timing only.
- **Cleared a PRD 1.8 grep false-positive (next masked failure).** Once T4b was stable, qc-static advanced to the "PRD 1.8 embedding engine — single implementation" step, which greps `^GEMINI_MODEL\s*=` across all `*.py` to enforce a single model definition in `shared-utils/embedding_engine.py`. A test added in the v20.0.0 train, `tests/unit/sop-embed-once-hash-skip.test.py`, bound `GEMINI_MODEL = mod.GEMINI_MODEL` at module top level — a re-read of the single source, NOT a competing definition, but it tripped the grep. Renamed the test-local alias to `EMBED_MODEL` (reads the same `mod.GEMINI_MODEL`); the guard is unchanged and un-weakened. Test still passes (13/13).
- **Cleared a banned-model-token doc false-positive (next masked failure).** After PRD 1.8, qc-static advanced to the "No banned model tokens in GHL skill markdown prose" step. Its Anthropic-slug detector (PCRE `us\.anthropic\.[a-z0-9.-]*`, GNU-grep only — which is why a BSD-grep local sweep missed it) matched the bare `us.anthropic.` prefix inside `docs/E10-SHARED-SCRIPT-DRIFT-CLASSIFICATION.md`, where line 87 *documents* that exact detector shape as `us.anthropic.<id>` (a `<id>` placeholder, not a usable model). Reworded that one shape to prose (`the us-region cross-region inference-profile id form`); the other five shapes and the guard are unchanged. A GNU-grep local sweep of every qc-static step now reports zero real failures. NOTE: this chain (CONTENT-HASH -> T4b -> PRD 1.8 -> banned-token) was pre-existing on `main` since the v20.0.0 CAPSTONE — qc-static's fail-fast surfaced one failure at a time; only CONTENT-HASH was the branch-introduced regression, the other three predate this work.

All 11 version markers rolled `v20.0.0` -> **v20.0.1** in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and their two skill dirs, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASSes post-bump. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted. The Skill 38 doc self-count advisory (`INSTALL.md` protocol/reference counts) remains a pre-existing, non-fatal WARN carried unchanged from the v19.x train.

## [v20.0.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  CAPSTONE: SUPER SPEC campaign close-out (Phases 1-5) — v19.71.0 -> v20.0.0

**Capstone MAJOR release.** This closes out the SUPER SPEC campaign. It ships no new feature code of its own: every unit it closes was already merged, QC'd, and released across the `v19.50.0` -> `v19.71.0` train. The MAJOR bump marks the campaign boundary — the point at which persona selection, department routing, the content engines, and the fleet-roll machinery all became one coherent, gated system rather than a set of independently-drifting skills.

All 11 version markers rolled v19.71.0 -> **v20.0.0** in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (plus the four script-embedded browser-manager sub-markers and the two skill dirs those markers live in, `06-ghl-install-pages` and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint`, so CI guard G3 stays green). Role/SOP/persona CONTENT is unchanged by this release, so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required — verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS both pre- and post-bump (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, every stored `content_sha` matches its live file, no token leaks). Annotated tag `v20.0.0` cut on this release commit.

### What the campaign closed

- **Persona blending + matching (P4-01, P4-02).** Persona matching and the book-to-persona path were strengthened, and the synergy blend directive gained the TASK-persona slot — so a blend now carries the task-time persona rather than resolving it implicitly. Personas remain a SHARED library: embedded once, not re-embedded per client.
- **Department routing + embed-once (P1-04, P2-05, P2-07, P2-08, P4-03).** The trust-engine requester stamp means the CEO passes the originating client chat id when routing a client message. `mc_board.py` can no longer silently drop an unrecognized `department_slug`, and the general-task catch-all is probe-covered. The interview now correlates to departments with opt-out loss warnings, a net-new department path, and fail-closed sub-floor verification. The artifact-refresh-queue CONSUMER landed, so stale role docs actually refresh on update. The SOP embed-once pipeline (hash-skip incremental embedder, idempotent client-box import, real-vector hard gate) mirrors the proven persona corpus pattern, and the two disagreeing embedding health surfaces were reconciled against real row counts.
- **THE FLOOR-WIPE FIX (P2-06).** The department/role floor could be wiped on update; fixed, live-remediated, verify residue cleared, and locked behind CI wiring + join-verify hardening so it cannot regress silently.
- **Presentation, podcast, and anthology engines (P3-01, P3-02, P3-03, P3-05).** The presentation department tail enforces one-question-at-a-time intake doctrine, closes the prompt-gate side door, and reconciles the prompt byte-floor. The podcast engine's webhook alias remap and anthology snapshot doc-drift are closed. The anthology engine's last mile landed with a MISSION_CONTROL_URL regression lock and skill-version/frontmatter lockstep. Graphics now emulates the presentation pattern (prompt-floor wiring, pre-generation gate, routing, doctrine).
- **Agent-browser packaging (P3-04, P3-06).** The stale bundled archive is regenerated and held straight by a drift gate plus determinism hardening. The browser lifecycle gained an iframe failure taxonomy, a weekly iframe-survival check, unmanaged-spawn guard auto-discovery, and a per-box conformance probe.
- **Social-media planner gates + conversational-AI playbook engine (P3-07, P3-08).** The playbook engine's parser fixes shipped alongside a runtime tool-gating prover. Skills 6/44 now integrate with 35/38: DM-first CTA plus comment reader, a tier-gated workflow builder, and the unmanaged-spawn guard widened to cover the operator skill.
- **Fleet-roll runbook + credential guard (P6-01, HELD).** `docs/FLEET-ROLL-RUNBOOK.md` codifies in-place-update (never fresh-install) doctrine: model policy, two platform proving boxes with mandatory reboot proof, per-box pre-flight, platform-specific update paths, banned credential destroyers, an authenticate-and-survive-reboot health gate, resumable bounded waves with a persistent per-box ledger, and per-box snapshot rollback. `scripts/fleet-roll/preflight-credential-guard.sh` is fail-closed: it snapshots, verifies, and restores every credential store, recording SET/NOT-SET plus a hash only — never a secret value — and exits nonzero on any drift.

### Scope + safety

Repo-side only. This release executes no fleet roll and touches no box; the fleet-roll machinery above remains HELD pending an explicit operator go. No client names, no secret values, no box identifiers, no model added, removed, or substituted.

**Known pre-existing advisory (NOT introduced here, out of scope for a release-only unit):** `38-conversational-ai-system`'s SKILL.md/INSTALL.md self-counts (51 protocols / 25 references / numbered-script range through `33`) remain stale against disk. `bump-version.sh` surfaces this as a non-fatal WARN on every bump; it is flagged for a follow-up fix-unit and was carried, unchanged, through v19.67.0-v19.71.0.

## [v19.71.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(P6-RUNBOOK): P6-01 — Fleet Roll runbook + credential-preflight-guard (HELD machinery)

Merges `p6-01/fleet-roll-runbook-and-credential-preflight-guard` (1 commit) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Clean merge, no conflicts — the branch is entirely net-new files (`docs/FLEET-ROLL-RUNBOOK.md`, `scripts/fleet-roll/preflight-credential-guard.sh`, `tests/unit/preflight-credential-guard.test.sh`); `main` had advanced to v19.70.0 (P3-FIXUP-01) since branch-cut but touched none of the same paths.

- **Fleet Roll runbook, repo-side machinery only — does NOT execute a roll and touches no box.** `docs/FLEET-ROLL-RUNBOOK.md` codifies in-place-update (not fresh-install) doctrine: Opus-owns-judgment / Sonnet-mechanical / Fable-never model policy; two platform proving boxes with mandatory reboot proof; per-box pre-flight plus mounted-volume durable-state check; Mac launchd vs. VPS Docker paths (force-recreate, env_file, pm2-save, node-user chown); banned credential destroyers; authenticate-and-survive-reboot health gate; <=20-box resumable waves with a persistent per-box ledger; per-box snapshot rollback.
- **Fail-closed credential preflight guard.** `scripts/fleet-roll/preflight-credential-guard.sh` snapshots, verifies, and restores every credential store — records SET/NOT-SET plus a hash only, never a secret value — and exits nonzero on any drift.
- **Tests.** `tests/unit/preflight-credential-guard.test.sh`, a fail-first 13-check suite proving teeth (a no-op stub guard misses the wipe), wipe detection across 4 shapes, unchanged-passes, no-secret-value-in-output, fail-closed behavior on missing/corrupt snapshot, and byte-identical restore round-trip. All 13 PASS, Bash 3.2 clean, re-verified on this merge commit.

No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff, no canary, no model added/removed/substituted, no client box touched. This is repo-side doctrine + a HELD guard script only — nothing here executes a fleet roll.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.70.0 → **v19.71.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Role/SOP/persona CONTENT was NOT changed by this merge (the branch adds a runbook doc and a standalone guard script/test, no `role-library`/SOP/persona catalog content), so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required — verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS post-bump (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, all shas match, no token leaks). Annotated tag `v19.71.0` cut on the release merge commit. Pre-existing, unrelated advisory surfaced during this bump (NOT introduced by it, out of scope for a merge-only unit): `38-conversational-ai-system`'s SKILL.md/INSTALL.md self-counts (51 protocols / 25 references / script range through `33`) are stale against disk — flagged for a follow-up fix-unit, not touched here (same advisory noted in v19.67.0/v19.68.0/v19.69.0).

## [v19.70.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(P3-FIXUP-01): resolve unresolved merge conflict in skill-35 skill-version.txt

Merges `fix/p3-fixup-01-conflict-markers` (1 commit) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`.
