Clean merge, no conflicts — the branch's change is scoped to a single file (`35-social-media-planner/skill-version.txt`); `main` had advanced to v19.69.0 (P4-03) since branch-cut but touched none of the same path.

- **Literal git conflict markers left in a checked-in file, fixed.** The earlier P3-08 merge (`2b53bbf4`) left raw `<<<<<<<`/`=======`/`>>>>>>>` conflict markers in `35-social-media-planner/skill-version.txt` instead of the resolved value. The merge commit message itself documented the intended resolution as `v2.9.10` (branch's `v2.9.8`/`v2.9.9` renumbered above main's already-published `v2.9.8`, per diamond-dependency version-label collision handling), and both `SKILL.md` frontmatter (`version: v2.9.10`) and Skill 35's own `CHANGELOG.md` (top entry `v2.9.10`) already carried that value correctly — only `skill-version.txt` was left with the markers. ## [v19.69.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(sop-embeddings): P4-03 — SOP embed-once pipeline + health-surface reconciliation

The branch was cut before P4-02 landed; by merge time `main` had advanced to v19.68.0 (P4-02, synergy blend directive). Version re-bumped fresh to v19.69.0 (next free after P4-02's v19.68.0) via `scripts/bump-version.sh`.

- **"Embed once, push to clients" pipeline for CC's SOP/routing embedding system (System 2)**, mirroring the proven Corpus-1 persona pattern. `shared-utils/sop-embed-once/embed_sop_library.py` is a HASH-SKIP incremental embedder for the shared `sops.jsonl`, with a real-vector hard gate (`gemini-embedding-2` @3072) and embed-text construction kept in field-for-field parity with `sop-embeddings.ts::buildSOPEmbedText()`. `shared-utils/sop-embed-once/build-and-publish.sh` does the hermetic build + GitHub Release publish, mirroring `shared-utils/prebuilt-index/build-and-publish.sh`. `shared-utils/sop-embed-once/SOP-EMBEDDINGS-MANIFEST.json` ships as a seed manifest (`asset_rebuild_required:true` — no asset published yet, operator action required).
- **Idempotent client-box import.** `shared-utils/sop-embed-once/provision_sop_embeddings.py` wired into `ingest-sop-library.sh`: sha256 hard gate, model/dims mix refusal, scoped `INSERT OR REPLACE`, zero embedding API calls, and a new marker table `sop_embeddings_shipped_asset` so `backfill-sop-embeddings.ts` can detect shared-library coverage (the CC-side step 3 of this pipeline).
- **Two-health-surfaces-disagree bug fixed.** `shared-utils/embedding_health.py::check_cc_sop_index` leg-b now reads REAL row counts (`_read_cc_sop_row_counts`) instead of relying solely on a stamp table CC's own migrations never create — a box with SOP content loaded but no stamp row previously reported a false unhealthy/disagreeing status between the two health surfaces. `32-command-center-setup/HEARTBEAT.md` lines 50-52 rewritten to name the ACTUAL fix per corpus (shared library vs. client-delta vs. persona index) instead of the previous "re-run the SOP library ingest" wording, which never touched embeddings.
- **Tests.** `tests/unit/sop-embed-once-hash-skip.test.py`, `tests/unit/provision-sop-embeddings-idempotency.test.py`, `tests/unit/embedding-health-cc-sop-reconciliation.test.py`.
- **CI** — `.github/workflows/embedding-integrity-guard.yml` extended to cover the new hash-skip embedder and provisioning idempotency.

## [v19.68.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(skill23): P4-02 step 7 — synergy blend directive gains the TASK-persona slot

Merges `feat/p4-02-synergy-directive-task-slot` into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. The branch was cut at v19.65.0 and targeted v19.66.0, but by merge time `main` had already advanced to v19.67.0 (P3-06 first consumed v19.66.0, then P4-01 rolled to v19.67.0) — all 15 non-CHANGELOG version-marker files (`version`, `install.sh`, `update-skills.sh`, both `23-ai-workforce-blueprint` and `06-ghl-install-pages` skill-version markers, `_index.json`, `_qc-summary.md`, README.md, `cc-compat.json`, `DIRECT-TO-AGENT-UPDATE-MESSAGE.md`, and the browser-manager/reaper/guard sub-markers) conflicted as PURE version-number churn — verified line-by-line, no content collision. Resolved by taking `main`'s current state for those files and re-bumping fresh to v19.68.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` so every marker lands in lockstep. The real functional change (`persona_blend.py`) and the new test file both merged clean with zero conflict.

P4-02's dual-persona content system combines a PERSONA SIDE (audience voice + topic expertise) with a TASK SIDE (the persona matched to the work being performed), "combined synergistically." The task side was computed (`build_task_personas`) and returned in the bundle, but it NEVER reached the writer's actual instruction: `build_blend_directive` composed only voice + audience + topic. Step 7 closes that — the directive now composes up to FOUR synergy slots and degrades gracefully.

### `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/persona_blend.py`
- **`build_blend_directive` gains a fourth slot** (`task_persona_pid=`): after the voice/audience/substance body, it appends "The task-side persona is W — apply ITS process and decision method to execute the work." The slot is content-gated and only added when W is a GENUINELY DISTINCT persona (not already the voice or topic persona, which already doubles as task guidance) so the composed directive never repeats itself. All four slots populate when available and degrade gracefully to three/two/one (voice-only, topic-only, task-only, or the neutral house voice). The mandatory `GUARDRAIL_CLAUSE` remains the non-removable trailing clause.
- **`build_bundle` reordered**: `build_task_personas` now runs BEFORE the directive is built so the primary (first non-mechanical) task-side persona populates slot 4. No change to the bundle's shape, the confirm-gate, the audience resolution, or the back-compat `persona_id` mirror.

### Tests
- `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-p4-02-synergy-directive.py` — 10 fail-first checks (the whole file raises `TypeError: unexpected keyword argument 'task_persona_pid'` on the pre-P4-02 tree): the four-slot compose; graceful degradation with slot 4 omitted when the task persona is None or redundant (== voice/topic); topic-only and collapsed branches still carrying the task slot; non-content tasks never getting a voice/task blend; and the guardrail staying the non-removable trailing clause even with all four slots present, each paired with a NO-WEAKENING probe.
- The existing W7 contract suite (`test-persona-blend-matcher.py`) stays green at 46/46 after the reorder.

No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff, no canary, no model added/removed/substituted, no client box touched.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.67.0 → **v19.68.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers + the browser-manager/reaper/guard sub-markers in lockstep). Role/SOP/persona CONTENT was NOT changed by this merge (`persona_blend.py` is matcher logic, not catalog content; no `role-library`/SOP/persona files touched), so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required. Annotated tag `v19.68.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.67.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(persona): P4-01 — persona matching + book-to-persona (strengthen)

Merges `feat/p4-01-persona-match-strengthen` (1 commit) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Clean merge, no conflicts — the branch's changes are net-new files only (a persona-match regression corpus, match-score logging, a book-to-persona matcher-selectable e2e suite, and their CI workflow); `main`'s changes since branch-cut (P3-06, `03-agent-browser/` + version markers) touch none of the same paths.

- **Real match-quality defect found and fixed, proven with a regression corpus.** A new 20-case persona-match regression corpus (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/testdata/persona-match-regression-corpus.json`), grounded in the live 99-persona catalog, surfaced that `persona_blend.py::_tag_hit`'s substring-containment matching only length-gated the QUERY-side token — a short tag-side token (e.g. `a`/`b` from `a-b-testing`, `hr` from `hr-leaders`) trivially substring-matched unrelated long query words. 84 of 99 personas' audience/topic tags carry a <=2-char hyphen-split token, so this silently mis-matched in production. Fixed by gating both sides at >=5 chars. Pre-fix the corpus scores 17/20 (85%, below the 90% gate); post-fix 20/20 (100%), existing 46-test persona-blend suite unchanged at 46/46.
- **Match-score-distribution logging for drift observability.** `persona_blend.log_match_score()` (best-effort, append-only JSONL, never raises) wired into `build_bundle()` for both audience and topic dimensions; `match_score_distribution()` summarizes count/mean/min/max + low/mid/high buckets from the log tail. `tests/unit/persona-match-score-log.test.py` (10 tests, fail-first proven: 7 errors + 3 fails pre-fix, clean post-fix).
- **Book-to-persona matcher-selectable e2e closed.** `tests/unit/p4-01-book-to-persona-matcher-selectable-e2e.test.py` connects the existing orchestrator-WRITE half (D6 duality-tags pipeline) to the matcher-READ half for the first time: runs `_phase6_register_categories()` against a temp catalog, then proves `persona_blend.load_catalog()` / `match_topic_persona()` / `match_audience_persona()` actually SELECT the newly-synthesized persona — plus NO-WEAKENING cases (a pre-enrichment entry and a rejected/out-of-vocab entry are never selectable). All 5 cases pass; no gap found requiring further scoped work.
- **Cross-repo state confirmed, no unmerged gap.** `onb22-duality-tags` (D6) is merged on `main` (v19.29.0); CC's `cc-persona` line (D1-D4, `--blend` in `persona-selector.ts`, `/api/tasks/[id]/audience`) and `persona_reason` (P2-02) storage (migration 099, `buildPersonaReason`) are already on CC `main` — no queued train needed on either repo for this scope.
- **CI** — new `.github/workflows/persona-blend-match-quality-guard.yml` wires the new regression corpus, match-score-log suite, and e2e suite (plus the pre-existing 46-test persona-blend suite and the D6 shell test) into CI; no workflow previously ran `persona_blend.py`'s own test suite at all.

No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff, no canary, no model added/removed/substituted, no client box touched.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.66.0 → **v19.67.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Role/SOP/persona CONTENT was NOT changed by this merge (`persona_blend.py` is matcher logic, not catalog content; no `role-library`/SOP/persona files touched), so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required — verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS both pre- and post-bump (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, all shas match, no token leaks). ## [v19.66.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(skill3): P3-06 — regenerate stale bundled archive, drift gate, determinism hardening

Merges `fix/skill3-archive-drift` (4 commits) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Clean merge, no conflicts — the branch's changes are fully scoped to `03-agent-browser/`, which `main` had not touched since the branch cut.

- **Stale bundled archive, regenerated + made impossible to drift again.** `agent-browser.skill` was hand-packaged with no regeneration step and no drift check, so it silently shipped a STALE copy of `INSTALL.md` (missing the N24 TYP citation, the mandatory `--headed false` requirement, the guaranteed-close `trap ... EXIT` subshell, the "Lifecycle hygiene" section, and the entire "GATEWAY RESTART PROTOCOL" block) and a STALE `CORE_UPDATES.md`, right next to current loose files. New `scripts/pack-agent-browser-skill.sh` is the ONLY sanctioned way to produce/update the archive now (`--check` mode, wired into CI). Regenerated the archive from current on-disk source.
- **Determinism fix.** Two regenerations of unchanged source, on the same box/toolchain, now produce a byte-identical archive: the initial version normalized every file's mtime before zipping but left the `agent-browser/` directory's own zip-entry mtime at a live build-time value, so `--check` (and the packer's own determinism) could still drift on that one directory-entry byte even with zero content changes — fixed by normalizing the directory entry's mtime too, proven by `scripts/tests/pack-agent-browser-skill.test.sh`'s two-independent-regenerations assertion (failed reproducibly before this fix).
- **`qc-agent-browser.sh` drift gate.** Now unzips `agent-browser.skill` and diffs `INSTALL.md`/`SKILL.md`/`CHANGELOG.md`/`CORE_UPDATES.md` inside it against the on-disk copies — ANY mismatch is a hard QC FAIL naming the differing file (`scripts/lib-archive-diff.sh`, shared by the packer's `--check` and the QC gate).
- **Step-4 smoke test is now ASSERTED, not implied.** `qc-agent-browser.sh` re-extracts the exact fenced Step-4 code block live from `INSTALL.md` (the guaranteed-close trap, `--headed false`) and prints the command + flags it actually ran as evidence. An ambient `AGENT_BROWSER_HEADED` signal that would force a visible window is refused (exit-75 class, matching Skill 06's D6 convention) before the smoke test ever runs.
- **Post-smoke-test session-state assertion strengthened** — a Chromium process this run's own smoke test spawned and left alive after `agent-browser close` ran now FAILS QC (was: no check at all). A scoped session that predates the QC run stays `warn_only` (not this skill's fault). QC.md gained explicit line items for all three. `03-agent-browser/skill-version.txt` rolled to `v6.5.9`; full detail in `03-agent-browser/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **CI** — new `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml` wired in for the static/drift checks introduced above.

No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff, no canary, no model added/removed/substituted, no client box touched.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.65.0 → **v19.66.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers + the browser-manager/reaper/guard sub-markers in lockstep). Role/SOP/persona content was NOT changed by this merge (only `03-agent-browser/` + `.github/workflows/` touched, neither part of the role-library/SOP/persona content manifest), so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required — verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS post-merge (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, all shas match, no token leaks). Annotated tag `v19.66.0` cut on the release merge commit. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

## [v19.65.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(skill6): P3-04 — residuals: iframe failure taxonomy, weekly iframe-survival check, unmanaged-spawn guard auto-discovery, per-box conformance probe

Merges `fix/skill6-residuals` (2 commits) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. The branch was cut at v19.58.0; several trains landed on `main` before this merge (through P3-08 at v19.64.0), so 2 files conflicted — real diamond-dependency content collisions, not pure version-number churn: both `main` (via P3-08) and the branch made genuine, independent changes to the same unmanaged-agent-browser-spawn guard in the same release window.

- **`06-ghl-install-pages/CHANGELOG.md`:** both sides added a genuine, non-overlapping entry in the same window (`main`/P3-08: the gated Tier-4 Automations builder; branch/P3-04: iframe failure taxonomy + weekly iframe-survival check). Resolved by keeping BOTH entries, P3-08's (already-released, `[v19.58.1]`) first, followed by the branch's `[Unreleased]` P3-04 entry — no content dropped from either side.
- **`scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh`:** base (v19.58.0) scanned 3 hand-enumerated roots. `main` (P3-08) hand-added a 4th root (`44-convert-and-flow-operator/`) pre-emptively. The branch (P3-04/AUD-25) independently replaced the entire hand-enumerated array with AUTO-DISCOVERY of every top-level `NN-*/` skill directory — which already covers `44-convert-and-flow-operator/` by construction, making P3-08's manual 4th-root entry redundant. Resolved by keeping the branch's auto-discovery implementation (a strict superset — verified `44-convert-and-flow-operator` is matched by the `[0-9][0-9]-*` glob) and merging both sides' historical commentary into one narrative so the P3-08 pre-emptive-widening rationale is preserved in the comments even though its manual array entry is superseded. `GUARD_AGENT_BROWSER_MANAGED_VERSION` re-rolled to `v19.65.0` by this release's own `bump-version.sh` pass (below).

Executes the branch's P3-04 residuals scope (closing items left open from the original P3-04 pass):

- **Iframe failure taxonomy (P3-04 c4)** — `ghl_iframe_drag.py` gains `classify_board_reason()` / `board_note()`, mapping every `IframeDragError` code into `cc_board.py`'s existing 6-value taxonomy, so iframe problems post a diagnosable board card instead of a generic build-exception stall. `ghl_survey_builder.py` / `ghl_form_builder.py` carry the classified reason through their existing `StopAndReport`/dict-return contracts. New `tests/test_iframe_failure_taxonomy.py` (13 tests).
- **Weekly iframe-survival check (P3-04 c4, fix-loop)** — new `run_iframe_survival_check()` / `--iframe-survival` CLI wired into the existing selector-canary machinery (read-only, dependency-injected, no network of its own), checking that published pages/surveys/forms still embed their cross-origin iframe on a recurring basis rather than a one-time snapshot. New `tools/iframe-survival-targets.json` (ships EMPTY — no client URLs in this repo). New `tests/test_iframe_survival_canary.py` (12 tests).
- **Unmanaged-spawn guard widened to EVERY skill directory (AUD-25)** — `scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh` no longer scans a hand-enumerated root list; it auto-discovers every top-level `NN-*/` skill directory, closing the class of bug where a new skill silently ships unscanned. AUD-26 fix-loop performance pass: the widened scan (3 roots → ~1,022 files) is now ONE batched python3 process (comment/string stripping + argv-spawn AST pass + every regex check) instead of per-file/per-line process forking, restoring the guard to a runnable time budget. New `tests/unit/guard-agent-browser-managed-all-skill-dirs.test.sh`.
- **Per-box conformance probe (P3-04 c5)** — new `scripts/probe/p304-agent-browser-conformance-probe.py` + `tests/probe/test-p304-agent-browser-conformance-probe.sh`, a repo-level (not skill-06) probe proving the widened guard + gateway discipline hold on a real box.
- **CI wiring** — `.github/workflows/agent-browser-lifecycle-guard.yml` updated for the widened scan; `41-build-with-ai-playbook/scripts/06-verify-agent-browser.sh` rewritten in lockstep (`41-build-with-ai-playbook/skill-version.txt` bumped, its own CHANGELOG entry covers the detail).

No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff, no canary, no model added/removed/substituted, no client box touched. ## [v19.64.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(workforce): P3-08 — Skill 6/44 ↔ 35/38 integration (DM-first CTA + comment reader, Tier-4 gated workflow builder, unmanaged-spawn guard widened to Skill 44)

The branch was cut at v19.58.0; several trains landed on `main` before this merge (through P3-01 at v19.63.0), so 4 files conflicted — real diamond-dependency version-label collisions, not pure version-number churn: both `main` and the branch made genuine, independent content changes to the same two skills (35-social-media-planner, 38-conversational-ai-system) in the same release window and each side's own version bump landed on an overlapping label.

- **`35-social-media-planner/CHANGELOG.md` + `skill-version.txt` + `SKILL.md` frontmatter`:** base was v2.9.7. `main` independently bumped to v2.9.8 for P3-05 (pre-generation prompt QC gate, Ideogram routing for text-overlay images) while the branch independently bumped v2.9.7→v2.9.8→v2.9.9 for its own two rounds of work (P3-08 Gap B/C: DM-first CTA + comment reader, weekly-link automation; P3-08 QC-fix: CTA lockstep in example templates + comment-reader injection fencing) — both sides claimed the label "v2.9.8" for different content. `references/playbook.md` itself touched non-overlapping line ranges on both sides (main: Section 8/8a/8b/19/19a; branch: Section 12/12b) and merged clean automatically — verified by diffing both sides against the merge-base. Resolved by renumbering the branch's two entries to sit ABOVE main's already-published v2.9.8 (v2.9.8→v2.9.9, v2.9.9→v2.9.10) so no version label is reused for different content; final `skill-version.txt` = v2.9.10.
- **`38-conversational-ai-system/CHANGELOG.md` + `skill-version.txt`:** base was 1.8.2. The branch's real change is a small SKILL.md cross-reference addition (documentation only, no code/behavior change), bumped 1.8.2→1.8.3 at branch-cut time. `main` independently shipped a MINOR bump to 1.9.0 in the interim (P3-07: `playbook_engine.py` parser fixes + the runtime U-1 tool-gating prover) — a real functional change that supersedes the stale 1.8.3 patch label. Resolved by keeping the branch's cross-reference content but relabeling it 1.8.3→1.9.1 (the next patch above main's current 1.9.0), placed above the `[1.9.0]` entry as the most recent change.

`scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh` + its test auto-merged cleanly with no conflict (branch widened the unmanaged-spawn scan roots to include `44-convert-and-flow-operator/`; main only bumped the file's own version marker string on a non-overlapping line — both changes verified present in the merged file). `06-ghl-install-pages/`, `36-ghl-mcp-setup/`, and `44-convert-and-flow-operator/`'s own CHANGELOG/SKILL.md/skill-version.txt changes applied with no conflicts (main made no changes to those files since the branch cut, confirmed by diffing against the merge-base).

Executes the branch's P3-08 scope:

- **Skill 44 widened into the unmanaged-agent-browser-spawn guard, pre-emptively** — `scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh`'s scan roots gain `44-convert-and-flow-operator/` (the `caf` Tier-0 GHL operator) alongside the existing 06/41/03 roots. Skill 44's SKILL.md/INSTRUCTIONS.md document a "Tier 4 agent-browser" workflow-build backstop that had no implementation and no CI protection; adding it as a scan root now means the guard bites the FIRST careless unmanaged spawn, not a repeat of the 22-orphan/357MB incident.
- **DM-first two-channel CTA (Skill 35 playbook §12, §6 pitch examples, carousel/CTA templates)** — the primary call-to-action across every post/carousel/video template is now "send us a message / DM" (FB/IG DM → GHL Conversations → Skill 38's inbound pipeline), with "the link is in the comments" as the reach-preserving backup. Closes the dead-end where the highest-intent CTA pointed at public comments that nothing in the repo reads or replies to. §19 QC checklist updated in lockstep (a post with only the comment directive and no DM CTA now FAILS).
- **Comment reader (`35-social-media-planner/scripts/comment_reader.py` + tests)** — Phase-4 Engagement Monitor sub-task that surfaces prospect comment REPLIES as synthetic inbound handoffs into Skill 38's `conversational-logs/`, tagged with post/permalink context. Per-channel honesty: a channel with no comment-read API surface is ledgered + skipped, never fabricated. Injection-fenced (Category 6 hardening) — untrusted public comments cannot forge a Skill-38 `### Inbound` turn or inject a `- text:` field, proven with fail-first tests (spoofed-turn, code-fence breakout, field-injection).

## [v19.60.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(skill38): P3-07 — playbook_engine parser fixes + runtime U-1 tool-gating prover

Merges `feat/skill38-parser-runtime-prover` (1 commit) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Clean merge — every file in the branch's diff (`38-conversational-ai-system/tools/playbook_engine.py`, the new `scripts/33-runtime-tool-gating-prover.sh` + `qc-runtime-tool-gating.sh` + `.test.sh`, `scripts/04-register-crons.sh`, `scripts/11-run-qc-checklist.sh`, `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`, the new fixtures and `tools/tests/test_playbook_engine.py`, plus the skill's own `CHANGELOG.md`/`INSTALL.md`/`SKILL.md`/`skill-version.txt`) applied with no conflicts against the divergent bump history that landed between the branch cut and this merge (P3-02 at v19.59.0).

Skill 38 (`38-conversational-ai-system`) v1.8.2 → v1.9.0. Fixes two live bugs in `tools/playbook_engine.py` — the single canonical parser every machine-enforced QC gate trusts — and adds the missing RUNTIME proof for the tool-gating guarantee:

- **`###` heading closure** — the phase-block-closing check claimed a `#`/`##`/`###` non-Phase heading closes the phase's key:value scan but matched only `# `/`## `. A bare `### Notes` mid-playbook silently absorbed its lines (e.g. `tools:`/`max-attempts:`) into the preceding phase, corrupting the exact data the U-1 tool-gating gates rely on. Closing tuple now includes `### ` (Phase-N exemption + `#### edge` carve-out preserved). Regression test proven to FAIL against the pre-fix engine (fail-first law), PASS after.
- **Exit-rule order independence** — `_parse_exit_rule` assumed `closing:` precedes `target:`; a reversed order let `target` greedily swallow the closing message with no validation error. Rewritten to parse action/closing/target as named segments in any order; a missing action is now a loud validate defect, never a silent bad parse. Tests cover both orders plus the malformed case.
- **New `scripts/33-runtime-tool-gating-prover.sh`** — the tool-gating twin of `scripts/24-self-test-hook.sh`. Layer A (static, CI-safe) resolves the fixture playbook's active phase via the canonical engine and asserts it grants `check_availability` but NOT `book_appointment`. Layer B (live) POSTs a synthetic flat-23-key inbound engineered to tempt a `book_appointment` call, reads the session transcript, and asserts by ground truth the agent refused the ungranted tool. `--dry-run` = Layer A only; `--auto` degrades to Layer A when live params are unresolved (no schedule spam).
- **New `scripts/qc-runtime-tool-gating.sh` (+ `.test.sh`)** — the gate. Runs the engine against the shipped fixture to prove the gating boundary is real (not a grep), runs the prover Layer-A dry-run, and asserts the live-layer wiring + gate-set + cron wiring. Negative test proves the gate FAILS when the fixture's active phase is mutated to also grant `book_appointment`.
- Wired into `scripts/11-run-qc-checklist.sh` and `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`, and registered as the `skill38-tool-gating-selftest` box-level self-test cron in `scripts/04-register-crons.sh` (`register_command_cron` gained an optional script-args parameter; existing callers unchanged).

Full engine unit suite green (44 tests). Skill CHANGELOG + `skill-version.txt` + SKILL.md/INSTALL.md self-counts rolled by the branch per 2.6. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.59.0 → **v19.60.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep, verified). No role/SOP/persona content changed by this merge (only `38-conversational-ai-system/` and `.github/workflows/` touched, none of which are part of the role-library/SOP/persona content manifest), so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required — verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS post-merge (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas, all shas match). ## [v19.58.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(workforce): P2-06 — floor-wipe verify QC fix-loop round 2, CI wiring + join-verify hardening

Merges `fix/floor-wipe-verify` (2 commits) into `main` as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Clean merge — every file in the branch's diff (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/materialize-missing-departments.py`, the new `test-materialize-missing-departments-join.sh`, `.github/workflows/floor-wipe-fix-guard.yml`, `scripts/probe/p206-floor-short-probe.py` + its new test suite) applied with no conflicts against the divergent bump history that landed between the branch cut and this merge (P2-05 aggregate gate at v19.57.0); the 11 version-marker files and README/CHANGELOG resolved as pure version-number churn, re-rolled below.

Closes the P2-06 residue: `materialize-missing-departments.py` (shipped at v19.56.0) closed LAYER 2 (provisioned: real department dirs on disk) but never touched LAYER 1 (chosen: `<company_dir>/departments.json`) or LAYER 3 (displayed: the Command Center's `workspaces` table), and never invoked `prove-board-join.py`. This unit wires the missing three-layer join verification, then closes all 5 items a follow-up QC pass found against that first fix:

- **`sync_chosen_artifact()`** — append-only merge of newly-closed department id(s) into `departments.json` + build-state `canonicalReconciliation.chosenDepartments`, resolving dept-info from the same sources `build-workforce.py`'s own build path reads.
- **`verify_board_join()`** — re-runs `seed-workspaces.py` (idempotent, `INSERT OR IGNORE`) then `prove-board-join.py`, folding a DRIFT/CANNOT-VOUCH/GATE-ERROR verdict into the overall exit code even when the on-disk floor is met (`BLOCKING_JOIN_STATUSES`). `--skip-join-verify` escape hatch; `--db` / `$DASHBOARD_DB_PATH` / `$DATABASE_PATH` for explicit-signal-only DB discovery — the ambient install-path candidate list is never consulted, after a live incident during development where the original unsafe resolver silently found and wrote to the operator's own live `mission-control.db` (fixed before merge; one legitimate `app-development` workspace row left in place, flagged for operator visibility, no deletes/overwrites).
- **QC round 2** — CI wiring (`floor-wipe-fix-guard.yml` now runs the new join-verify suite as suite 3/4, path triggers added); new T5/T6/T7 headline-branch regression tests (a phantom CHOSEN department that can never be closed forces DRIFT and downgrades the overall exit code even though the on-disk floor is fully met); the sole production caller (`p206-floor-short-probe.py --remediate`) now threads `--db` and independently re-verifies `verify_board_join()` from source rather than trusting the remediator's self-report; doc-string/help-text drift describing the removed ambient DB discovery corrected to EXPLICIT-SIGNAL ONLY.

All pre-existing P2-06 suites re-run green (department-floor 12/12, materialize-missing-departments 14/14, naming-map-corruption-fail-closed 4/4, materialize-missing-departments-join 15/15 incl. new T5, p206-floor-short-probe 19/19 incl. new T6/T7), plus adjacent skill-23 provisioning suites unmodified and passing.

## [v19.54.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(workforce): P2-06 — THE FLOOR-WIPE FIX, live remediation + verify residue

Onboarding-side, repo-only fix. The root cause (`department-floor.py` /
`build-workforce.py` failing OPEN when `department-naming-map.json` is
unreadable, silently shrinking the enforced floor from 28 departments down to
22) was already fixed at build-time in an earlier unit. This unit closes
P2-06's remaining scope: verification tooling plus live remediation for boxes
that were built before that fix landed and are still missing whole
departments today.

- **New `scripts/probe/p206-floor-short-probe.py`** — per-box floor-short
  verdict via `department-floor.py`'s own `evaluate_floor()`, three distinct
  exit codes (0 ARMED / 1 DEGRADED / 2 UNRESOLVABLE), `--remediate` support,
  and it always re-verifies from the live tree rather than trusting the
  remediator's self-report.
- **New `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/materialize-missing-departments.py`**
  — for a flagged box, re-runs the fixed builder path additively: resolves
  each missing department's full role roster from
  `templates/role-library/_index.json` (handles both library layouts — flat
  `<slug>.md` files and per-role `<slug>/how-to.md` subdirectories) and
  invokes the already-shipped v16.0.2 `floor-fill-driver.py --apply` to
  materialize it. Additive-only, idempotent, honors client declines, never
  fabricates content, dry-run by default.
- **Regression lock + new permanent residue coverage** —
  `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-naming-map-corruption-fail-closed.sh`
  (new: corrupts a TEST COPY of the naming map — invalid JSON / missing file /
  valid-but-empty — and proves the enforced floor never silently shrinks to
  22; verified fail-first by reintroducing the actual defect before
  restoring), `test-materialize-missing-departments.sh` (new: additive-only /
  idempotent / decline-respecting / dry-run-never-mutates proofs, plus a
  fail-first regression lock for a library-layout bug hit during this unit's
  own development), and `scripts/probe/test-p206-floor-short-probe.sh` (new:
  ARMED/DEGRADED/UNRESOLVABLE contract, remediate-then-reverify, JSON-output
  integrity). `.github/workflows/floor-wipe-fix-guard.yml` (new) wires all
  three suites into CI, path-triggered on the files this unit touches. The
  pre-existing `test-department-floor.sh` re-runs green (12/12), unaffected —
  `department-floor.py` itself is unchanged by this unit.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.53.0 → **v19.54.0** via
  `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep; content-manifest
  `--check` already passing, no role/SOP/persona content touched by this

## [v19.53.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(23): P2-08 step 2 — the artifact-refresh-queue CONSUMER (stale role docs now actually refresh on update)

Onboarding-side, repo-only fix. `.artifact-refresh-queue.json` has had a PRODUCER since v12.27.0 (`detect-stale-artifacts.py`, invoked from `update-skills.sh` and `shared-utils/workspace-dept-refresh.sh`) but NEVER a consumer for the STALE-role case — a box that updates its skills kept its OLD role docs forever, because nothing ever read the queue and acted on it (Presentation spec Section 13.9 deploy trap; P2-08 (b)). The v16.0.2 `floor-fill-driver.py` closed the MISSING-role half of this gap (skip-existing/no-clobber by design — it never touches a role that already has a folder on disk); the STALE half stayed open until now.

- **New `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/refresh-stale-roles.py`** — drains ONLY queue items where `kind=="role"` AND `status=="STALE"`. For each: locates the role's EXISTING folder on disk (never creates one — that stays floor-fill's job; a role not already present is skipped loudly, not fabricated), re-runs the SAME `library_lookup()` + `try_library_fill()` machinery `create_role_workspace()` itself uses, and overwrites ONLY `how-to.md` with the freshly filled + re-stamped content (`IDENTITY.md`/`SOUL.md`/`MEMORY.md`/`HEARTBEAT.md` are role-specific and untouched). On success the item is dropped from the queue. A poisoned entry (nonexistent role path, corrupt JSON row, no library match, fill-below-floor) is skipped with a loud WARN and left queued — it NEVER aborts the drain. `kind=="sop"/"dept"/"persona"` and `status=="MISSING"/"ORPHAN"/"UNTRACKED"` rows are left untouched — out of this consumer's narrow scope. Dry-run by default; `--apply` required to mutate.
- **`update-skills.sh` wired**: immediately after the existing per-artifact staleness DETECT block (which writes the queue), a new best-effort step runs `refresh-stale-roles.py --workspace "$OC_WORKSPACE" --apply`, loudly logging `N artifact(s) refreshed` — a missing/broken consumer tool can never fail the update itself (`|| true`-equivalent handling).
- **P6-01 probe row shipped now, deployed at the final roll**: `scripts/probe/p208-sop-role-provisioning-probe.sh` — three independent checks (SOP library populated via `assert-sop-library-populated.py`, standalone; skill-version.txt drift vs the fresh-clone reference checkout; refresh queue drained of `kind=="role"`/`STALE` rows) feeding one PROVISIONED/DEGRADED verdict per box. `--min-total` has no default (fail-closed, same contract as the v19.50.0 gate it wraps).
- **Tests, fail-first proven against the pre-fix tree**: `tests/unit/refresh-stale-roles.test.sh` (10/10 with the fix; 0/1 with the script removed — real library content used throughout, never a fabricated fixture) and `tests/probe/test-p208-sop-role-provisioning-probe.sh` (10/10 with the fix; 0/1 with the script removed). The pre-existing `tests/unit/floor-fill-gap.test.sh` (compile/gap-map shape) and `32-command-center-setup/scripts/test-sop-library-phase-wiring.sh` (38/38) both re-run green, unaffected.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.52.0 → **v19.53.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). No role/SOP/persona content changed, so no `hash-content-manifest.py` restamp was required (verified: `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` CONTENT-HASH dimension OK post-merge). Annotated tag `v19.53.0` cut on the release merge commit. Merged `fix/sop-role-provisioning` (1 commit) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`, rebased over the concurrently-landed `fix/general-task-catchall` (P2-07, v19.52.0). No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

## [v19.52.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(routing): P2-07 — mc_board.py never silently drops an unrecognized department_slug

Fixes the shared `mc_board.py` family (Skills 49/50/53/54/55/56/57 — Signature
Funnel, Email Engine, Book Writer, Anthology Writer, Product Bio, Sales Page
Assets, Social Media In-a-Box) so a Command Center board-routing bug can never
silently swallow a bad department slug again. Repo-only; no live-box change
here beyond the shared script content each already carries.

- **`mc_board.py` — an UNRECOGNIZED `department_slug`** (a typo, a regressed
  hardcoded fake slug like the historical `funnels`/`books`/`email` family, or
  an empty string) is now caught client-side before the ingest POST, logged
  loudly to stderr, and RE-ROUTED to the `general-task` catch-all department
  with the original bad slug annotated on the card description and on
  `begin_run`'s initial board event note. Never silently dropped. Recognized
  slugs (the 22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary floor departments + known
  variant aliases, mirrored from
  `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/department-floor.py:116-158`) pass
  through unchanged. Applied identically across all seven `mc_board.py`
  copies (49/50/53/54/55/56/57).
- **`test_cc_contract.py`** — new regression cases per skill: an unrecognized
  slug reroutes to `general-task`, an empty slug reroutes, a known slug and
  `general-task` itself pass through unchanged, the reroute logs loudly to
  stderr, and `begin_run`'s initial advance note records the original bad
  slug as a board-visible event.
- **Per-skill CHANGELOG + `skill-version.txt` bumps** — 49→1.3.3, 50→1.1.3,
  53→1.1.5, 54→1.4.1, 55→1.0.11, 56→1.4.2, 57→0.2.10.
