## [v19.51.2]  -  2026-07-12  -  fix(interview): P2-05 QC fix — make the opt-out confirm-loss gate FAIL-CLOSED, and cap the net-new department slug length

QC fix on the P2-05 branch (`feat/interview-dept-correlation`). Repo-only; no live-box change here. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **PRIMARY — the `--confirm-loss` gate is now FAIL-CLOSED.** Before this fix, `record-dept-decision.sh` trusted `department-loss-warning.py`'s rc=3 ("non-floor, proceed") even when the naming map was UNREADABLE — because a corrupt map made a REAL floor department (e.g. `billing-finance`) look identical to a non-floor dept (empty map → no warning → rc3). Result: `record-dept-decision.sh --dept billing-finance --decision no` (no `--confirm-loss`) exited 0 and WROTE the floor decline with no warning and no confirmation, silently dropping a floor department — directly contradicting the feature's promise ("an opt-out is sovereign, but it is never silent") and the repo's own OQ-7 fail-closed doctrine (`department-floor.py` degrades to the FULL 28 floor on an unreadable map). Fix, two parts: (1) `department-loss-warning.py` now returns a NEW distinct exit code **rc=4 INDETERMINATE** when the naming map is unreadable/unparseable or structurally missing its populated `mandatory` floor section — so rc=3 now means CONCLUSIVELY non-floor against a READABLE map, and can be trusted. (2) `record-dept-decision.sh` treats a floor dept (rc0) OR any indeterminate result (rc4, reader missing, non-zero, crash) as REQUIRING `--confirm-loss`: without it the decline is REFUSED (exit 2, NOT written) with an explicit "restore the naming map or re-run with --confirm-loss" message; with the explicit override it still writes (auditable escape hatch). Isolated proof: with the map present a plain floor `--decision no` exits 2 and does not write; with the map corrupt the SAME command NOW exits 2 and does not write (was exit 0 + silent write); with `--confirm-loss` the override writes under either map.
- **SECONDARY — `net-new-department.py` now caps slug length.** A 5000-char `--slug` previously returned rc0 "net-new" in `--check-only` and could be passed to `add-department.sh` as a filesystem-hostile directory/board/runtime key. Added `MAX_SLUG_LEN = 64` (comfortably above every real canonical slug, safely under the 255-byte POSIX filename limit; `slugify()` already restricts the character set to `[a-z0-9-]`, so length was the remaining gap). A slug over the cap is rejected as bad usage (rc5) before any duplicate check or creation.
- **Regression tests (fail-first against this branch's pre-fix HEAD).** `scripts/test-opt-out-loss-warning.sh` grows Test 4 (now 22/22): corrupt/replace the naming map, attempt a floor decline without `--confirm-loss`, assert exit 2 + NOT written + the "CANNOT DETERMINE" message; assert `--confirm-loss` still writes under the corrupt map; assert the reader returns rc=4 (never a false rc=3); the real map is backed up and restored (repo left clean). `scripts/test-net-new-department.sh` grows Case 6 (now 16/16): a 5000-char slug is rejected (rc5), a 64-char slug (at the cap) passes, a 65-char slug (over the cap) is rejected. Existing suites stay green: repo-consistency 8/8, decline-provenance-guard PASS.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.51.1 → **v19.51.2** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). No content_sha restamp needed (only scripts + tests changed; the naming map is untouched and is not part of the role/sop/persona content manifest).

## [v19.51.1]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(interview): P2-05 interview → departments correlation — opt-out loss warnings, the net-new department path, and sub-floor verification

Closes the P2-05 tail (SUPER-SPEC-2026-07-11): the interview drives departments truthfully — a floor department can be declined but never silently, real estate is never forced, a genuinely new need can become a real department, and fewer-than-floor is legal and provably correct end to end. Repo-only; no live-box change here. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **Opt-out loss warnings (step 1).** Each of the 28 floor departments (22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary) now carries a one-line `loss_warning` in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/department-naming-map.json` (additive only — the map's structure and 22+6=28 floor arithmetic are unchanged). New single-source reader `scripts/department-loss-warning.py` surfaces a floor dept's warning (rc0 + text) and returns nothing (rc3) for a non-floor dept (an industry-gated extra like `listings`, or a custom) — so no confirmation is ever forced where nothing is lost. `scripts/record-dept-decision.sh` now ECHOES that warning and REQUIRES `--confirm-loss` before it writes a floor-department decline: a plain `--decision no` for a floor dept prints the warning and exits 2 without writing (the dept stays in the floor until the owner confirms). A confirmed decline is written in the same provenanced object form `canonical_decline.py` honors, plus `lossWarning` + `lossWarningAck:true` audit fields. A non-floor decline needs no confirmation. `INSTRUCTIONS.md` Phase 5.5 (Steps 3 + 3.6) now documents the show-the-warning-then-confirm loop.
- **Net-new department path (step 2).** New `scripts/net-new-department.py` is the interview-close "we uncovered a department we don't have" path. `--check-only` enforces the two hard rules offline: RULE 1 — a proposed slug that resolves to any existing canonical / universal-primary / variant / vertical-pack / role-library department is REJECTED (rc2, route there instead); RULE 2 — a slug that collides with a department already on disk is REJECTED (rc3). The duplicate logic is the SAME `department-floor.canonical_slug_for` + `_raw_department_dirs` resolver `reconcile-legacy-tree.py` uses (never re-implemented). Creation mode calls the FIXED `32-command-center-setup/scripts/add-department.sh` (board row + OpenClaw runtime wired together) and reports success ONLY after `guard-department-runtime-parity.py` passes (rc4 otherwise) — so a net-new dept can never ship a board card that sticks in `no_specialist_runtime`. Nearest role-library dept + 2–3 seed roles are proposed. `INSTRUCTIONS.md` Phase 5.5 Step 3.9 documents the prefer-route-then-create flow.
- **Sub-floor verification (step 3).** A build with 3 declined floor departments is proven legal and consistent: `department-floor.evaluate_floor` honors the 3 provenanced declines (floor MET, `expected_floor_count` = 25), the durable `departments.json` chosen artifact equals the 25 chosen (+ the CEO column), and `prove-board-join.py` returns rc=0 (chosen == provisioned == displayed, 26/26/26). The fail-closed OQ-7 side is re-locked: an unreadable naming map degrades to the FULL 28 floor (22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary), never fewer — a declines file can shrink the floor, a broken map cannot.
- **Tests (all fail-first where they cover new code).** `scripts/test-opt-out-loss-warning.sh` (15/15; 10 fail against pre-P2-05 HEAD) proves every floor dept carries a warning, the reader's rc contract, and the recorder's confirm-gate + honored-decline round-trip. `scripts/test-net-new-department.sh` (13/13; the tool is absent pre-fix) proves both hard rules, the read-only `--check-only`, and the creation-mode orchestration (success only when the parity guard passes; canonical dup rejected before any create). `scripts/test-sub-floor-build.sh` (11/11) is the sub-floor regression lock + the OQ-7 fail-closed proof, exercising the real `department-floor.py` and `prove-board-join.py`. Existing suites stay green: decline-provenance-guard, department-floor (12/12), chosen-departments-artifact (22/22), board-join-chain (65/65), ws4-departments, reconciliation-engine; repo-consistency 10/10 and floor-count-consistency (28) both PASS.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.51.0 → **v19.51.1** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Skill 23 `skill-version.txt` rolled with it; no Skill-32 file changed, so its independent version is untouched. No content_sha restamp needed (the naming map is not part of the role/sop/persona content manifest).

## [v19.51.0]  -  2026-07-12  -  feat(routing): P1-04 trust-engine requester stamp — the CEO passes the originating client chat id when routing a client message

Onboarding side of P1-04 (the #1 client-complaint "report-back loop"; the Command Center side is `blackceo-command-center` branch `feat/v5.18-trust-engine`). Repo-only; no live-box change here. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **`scripts/mc-route.sh` (the SIGNED task router) now threads the originating client chat id into the ingest payload.** Two new inputs — `MC_ROUTE_REQUESTER_CHAT_ID` and `MC_ROUTE_REQUESTER_CHANNEL` (default `telegram`, only used when a chat id is set). The compact JSON body gains `requester_chat_id` + `requester_channel` **only when a chat id is present** (an operator/internal route omits them entirely — the box never "reports back" to a phantom chat). The three KEEP-IN-SYNC copies of the helper (`scripts/mc-route.sh` and the stamped blocks in `apply-fleet-standards.sh` + `apply-routing-fix.sh`) carry byte-identical payload logic.
- **The on-box CEO doctrine now TELLS the agent to pass the chat id — wired into the installer blocks that actually assemble `workspace/AGENTS.md`, not just the repo-root file.** (The repo-root `AGENTS.md` is never copied to a box; the prior cut only edited that, so it was documentation-only.) The env-prefixed `mc-route.sh` invocation is added to (a) the `SKILL_INTENT_ROUTING_REFLEX` block in `apply-fleet-standards.sh` — which strip-then-reinserts every run, so it reaches already-onboarded boxes — and (b) the `CEO ROUTING — NO LOOPHOLES` block in BOTH `apply-fleet-standards.sh` and `apply-routing-fix.sh`.
- **Doctrine marker bumped `CEO_ROUTING_NO_LOOPHOLES_V1 → V2` (both installers) WITH a strip-V1 migration.** A stale V1 marker would no-op the block forever, so the ~30 already-onboarded boxes would never receive the new rule on the Sunday update. The bumped block strips any legacy V1 region (it has no END marker — it terminates at its first `---`) and re-injects V2 with the trust-engine chat-id rule + an explicit END marker. Proven end-to-end against a sandbox workspace: a fresh box injects V2 with the rule; an existing box carrying a real V1 block (produced by the origin/main installer) migrates to V2 (V1 count → 0, single block, no duplicate) and re-runs are byte-idempotent. `scripts/verify-routing.sh` G2 is now version-agnostic (`CEO_ROUTING_NO_LOOPHOLES_V[0-9]+`) so it accepts V1 or the migrated V2.
- **Tests.** `tests/unit/mc-route-requester-payload.test.sh` (new) drives the REAL `scripts/mc-route.sh` with a stubbed `curl` that captures the exact POST body: no chat id → fields omitted; chat id set → `requester_chat_id` + `requester_channel:telegram`; explicit channel passes through; whitespace-only chat id stripped to omitted. Fail-first against the pre-P1-04 helper. `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` MAP-CONSISTENCY (Layer-C reflex coverage) stays green; `test-ceo-tool-gate.sh` green under the V2 bump.
- **Fix-loop QC re-review:** wired the requester chat-id rule into the ON-BOX CEO doctrine (not just the repo-root `AGENTS.md`, which is never copied to a box) and bumped the `CEO_ROUTING_NO_LOOPHOLES` marker so already-onboarded boxes re-inject on their next Sunday update.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.50.1 → **v19.51.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.51.0` cut on the release merge commit. Merged `feat/trust-engine-requester-stamp` (2 commits) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`.

## [v19.50.1]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(32): close the Sunday CC-update rebuild gap — D5 now routes through CC's OWN update.sh (atomic-deploy + health-check + auto-rollback), never a bare `npm run build` + `pm2 restart` (P1-07)

**P1-07 — the version-update protocol.** Answers the operator's question
("does the Sunday check update the CC repo too, and does it actually
rebuild?") with a real fix, not just verification: it did pull, but the build
step it ran had NO health check and NO rollback wired to it — a broken build
could ship straight to `pm2 restart` with nobody checking. That is the same
BUILD-05 "dead client Kanban" defect class the CC repo's own
`scripts/atomic-deploy.sh` already exists to close on OTHER paths; this repo's
own D5 update-only step just never routed through it.

- **`32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh` — new
  `cc_route_update_through_canonical_path()`**, called from the Phase 6
  `--update-only` block in place of the old bare `cc_ensure_fresh_build` +
  `pm2 restart`/`pm2 start` pair. Three tiers, each strictly safer than the
  old path: (1) the freshly-pulled CC's own `update.sh` (owns
  `scripts/atomic-deploy.sh` — build into a temp dir, gate on a FRESH
  `.next/BUILD_ID`, atomic swap, restart, health-check, auto-rollback on a
  failed health check); (2) `scripts/atomic-deploy.sh` invoked directly, for
  a checkout that has it but predates `update.sh`; (3) a legacy fallback for
  the oldest boxes with neither file — snapshots `.next` before building and
  manually reverts it if the post-update assertion fails, so even the
  last-resort tier can never leave a half-updated CC standing. ALL THREE
  tiers end in the same independent post-update assertion: `.next/BUILD_ID`
  mtime newer than the pull timestamp AND `curl -fsS localhost:4000/api/health`
  returns `200` — stamped to the box's build-state as
  `commandCenterLastUpdateVerified` (true/false), the single source of truth
  for whether a given box's Sunday CC update actually took effect.
- **Coordinated CC-repo fix (branch `fix/sunday-cc-rebuild` there too):**
  `update.sh`'s own install-dir autodetection never matched this repo's
  documented clone target (`~/projects/command-center` /
  `/data/projects/command-center`) — a standalone invocation would have
  failed "Command Center not found" on every real box. Fixed there; this
  repo's new call passes `CC_APP_DIR` explicitly so it never depends on
  autodetection succeeding at all.
- **New: `scripts/probe/p107-sunday-update-probe.sh`** (ships in P6-01) —
  per-box EXACT schedule+command match against `crontab -l` (never a
  truncated-text grep, the v19.47.0 lesson) for both the Sunday 3:00 AM
  CC/onboarding updater cron and the Saturday 23:59 OpenClaw-CLI updater
  cron; on a Docker VPS box, verifies `/data` is a genuine persistent mount
  (structural proof the CC checkout survives a container recreate, N40).
  `--remediate` re-runs `scripts/setup-weekly-update.sh` to install whatever
  is missing and re-verifies; `--json` for the fleet ledger.
- **Tests (real, fail-first — proven to fail against the pre-fix tree before
  passing against the fix):**
  `tests/probe/test-cc-route-update-canonical-path.sh` — extracts the ACTUAL
  `cc_route_update_through_canonical_path()` body from `run-full-install.sh`
  (not a reimplementation) and proves tier 1 invokes the freshly-pulled
  `update.sh` with `CC_APP_DIR` pointed at the checkout, AND that tier 3
  reverts `.next` to its pre-update snapshot when the post-update health
  check fails (the exact defect class this closes — a broken build that
  would previously have shipped straight to `pm2 restart`).
  `tests/probe/test-p107-sunday-update-probe.sh` — proves the probe's exact
  schedule+command matching correctly rejects a line that would false-positive
  under a naive substring grep (wrong schedule, extra trailing args), reports
  ARMED only on an exact canonical match, and that `--remediate` converges a
  bare box to ARMED. Existing regression suites re-run clean against the
  changed file: `tests/unit/cc-tunnel-ingress-guard.test.sh` (21/21),
  `tests/unit/both-paths-zhe-delivery.test.sh`, `tests/unit/cc-done-degraded-retry-gate.test.sh` (20/20).
- **Docs:** `UPDATE-PLAYBOOK.md` gained a new "Automated Command Center
  Update — Sunday 3AM (P1-07)" section — what runs, which file, what proves
  success, how to roll back, one page — see also the CC repo's own
  `DEPLOYMENT.md` for the other half of the same chain.
- No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

### Fix-loop iteration (same branch, P1-07 QC round 2)

A prior QC judge FAILED this unit on three findings, all closed here without
touching the tier 1/2/3 build+restart logic itself:
1. **The final assertion's own `if` only checked `health_code==200`**,
   stamping `commandCenterLastUpdateVerified=true` regardless of whether
   `BUILD_ID` actually postdated the pull — contradicting the function's own
   comment, spec P1-07(c)2, and the "single source of truth" claim in
   `UPDATE-PLAYBOOK.md`/`DEPLOYMENT.md` (both already described the correct
   two-part contract; only the code hadn't caught up). Fixed:
   `verified=true` now requires `build_id_mtime -gt pull_ts AND health==200`;
   a box healthy-but-serving-the-prior-build (auto-rolled-back) now correctly
   stamps `false` with an honest log line distinguishing "rolled back, still
   green" from "update took effect" — it does NOT read as a half-updated CC.
2. **Scenario B's fake `npm run build` was a no-op** — it never touched
   `.next`, so the "old marker restored" assertion passed whether or not the
   tier-3 revert code actually ran (the judge proved this by neutralizing the
   entire tier-3 block into a no-op and watching the suite still pass 6/6).
   Fixed: the fake `npm run build` now clobbers `.next` (deletes the old
   marker + `BUILD_ID`, writes a fresh one) exactly like a real Next.js
   build would, so the revert assertion can only pass if the revert
   genuinely restores the pre-update snapshot. Re-run against the same
   neutralized tier-3 block: now correctly fails (4/12), where the pre-fix
   test suite passed 6/6.
3. **Tightened the tier-3 behavioral assertions** so a missing/renamed
   function (RC=127) cannot masquerade as a pass: added a `declare -F`
   guard immediately after sourcing the extracted unit, a static
   vacuous-extraction guard on the extracted file itself, and a requirement
   that the function's own tier-3 revert log lines are present in
   `$LOG_FILE` (not just "RC is non-zero" and "a file exists").
4. **New Scenario C** — a direct, fail-first regression lock for finding #1:
   tier 1, `update.sh` reports success and health answers 200, but
   `BUILD_ID` is deliberately left untouched (simulating `atomic-deploy.sh`'s
   own internal rollback to a still-healthy prior build). Proven to fail
   3/4 against the pre-fix `run-full-install.sh` (reproducing the exact bug)
   and pass 4/4 against the fix.

Full suite after the fix: `test-cc-route-update-canonical-path.sh` 12/12;
`test-p107-sunday-update-probe.sh` 6/6; `cc-tunnel-ingress-guard.test.sh`
21/21; `both-paths-zhe-delivery.test.sh` and `cc-done-degraded-retry-gate.test.sh`
(20/20) both re-run clean. No docs required changes — `UPDATE-PLAYBOOK.md`,
`DEPLOYMENT.md`, and this CHANGELOG's own P1-07 entry above already described
the two-part (freshness AND health) contract; the code now matches what was
already documented.

## [v19.50.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(32/C2): wire the SOP V2 library ingestion into the Command Center install, fail CLOSED, and close a preserved-empty ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH fail-open

Merges `fable-fix/onb32-c2-installer` (4 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.49.0) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Repo-only: the live ingest/converge RUN is operator-live and deferred; no live-box change here. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **C2 — every fresh install shipped a GHOST SOP library.** `INSTALL.md` documented a "SOP V2 Library Ingestion" step as automatic, but `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh` **never called** `ingest-sop-library.sh` and never triggered the Command Center's `converge(scope=sops)` role-library import (`grep -c ingest-sop-library run-full-install.sh == 0` before this branch). Every box shipped whatever the CC's own boot-seed (`autoSeedStarterSOPs`) wrote — a ~54-row stale/test set — instead of the real ~2,555-row V2 library, starving `dispatch_rules.sop_id` matching and Triad routing. New **PHASE 6i** (named 6i because this file's own Phase 6c is the unrelated sync step) runs `ingest-sop-library.sh` (direct JSONL upsert) + the CC `converge(scope=sops)` role-library import, then a fail-loud row-count gate. Idempotent (both writers are upsert-only); runs in full and `--update-only` mode.
- **Root cause of the ALWAYS-ZERO role-library import (established by a supervised converge against a throwaway CC, not by reading code).** `converge(scope=sops)` resolves its departments tree from the ZHC company roots → `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH` → `<OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_PATH>/departments`, whose built-in default is the **Docker-only** `/data/.openclaw/workspace`. On a Mac there is no ZHC tree and no `/data`, so `discoverRoleHowTos()` scans a missing dir, returns `[]` **without throwing**, and converge answers HTTP 200 `{"ok":true,"sops":{"imported":0,"updated":0}}` having written nothing — the box's real library (`$OC_ROOT/workspace/departments`) never in its search path. `cc_write_env_local` now **pins `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH`** at that tree (only when it really holds role `how-to.md`; existing operator values preserved), written before the board boots. Measured: unpinned → 0 role rows; pinned → 690 rows across 70 departments.
- **The gate fails CLOSED (two writers, two independent asserts).** `assert-sop-library-populated.py` enforces `--min-total` (all rows; **no default** — an absent floor exits 3 rather than rubber-stamp the boot-seed) AND `--min-role-library` (rows `WHERE source='role-library'`, default 1) — because a healthy 2,555-row JSONL ingest with a **failed** converge yields zero role-library rows and a bare `COUNT(*)` waves it through (the exact live C2 shape). Converge success is judged on **rows written** (`imported + updated`, parsed from the body), never on the HTTP status: a `200 {"ok":true, imported:0, updated:0}` is `zero-imported`, never `ok`. Gating on `imported` alone is rejected too — the idempotent re-run of a healthy library reports `imported=0, updated=912`, which would brick every re-install.
- **NEW in this reland — a preserved-empty `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH` fail-open (found in review, independently reproduced against the real gate before fixing).** The floor-0 "nothing to import" discriminator counted role `how-to.md` **only under the pinned `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH`**. When `.env.local` carried a **preserved-but-empty / mis-pointed** `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH` (an operator override, or a prior pin against a tree since moved/regenerated) while the box's REAL library sat at the `$OC_ROOT/workspace/departments` default, the discriminator counted 0, dropped the role floor to 0, and **PASSED GREEN** over a converge that imported nothing — the real library shipping as a ghost behind a green check, the exact fail-open `cc_write_env_local` step (5) promises to fail LOUDLY on. **Fix:** `SOP_ROLE_SRC_COUNT` is now the **UNION** of `how-to.md` under the pinned dir AND the box default; a real library under EITHER forces role floor 1, so a mis-pointed `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH` is a **LOUD rc=4** (the hint now names the mis-pointed key and the tree that actually holds the how-tos) instead of a silent pass. The genuine "no library anywhere" case still correctly drops to floor 0 and passes (no over-hardening).
- **Tests real, not rubber stamps.** `test-sop-library-phase-wiring.sh` stands up a FAKE Command Center answering a scripted converge body over real HTTP + a real DB + the real shared resolver. **New scenario 4h** reproduces the preserved-empty fail-open (real 12-how-to library at the default, `ROLE_LIBRARY_PATH` pinned to an empty dir, converge 0-imported): it **fails as a FAIL-OPEN against the pre-fix gate (37 passed, 1 failed)** and is fail-closed rc=4 with the fix (**38/38**). The pre-existing scenarios (4a healthy, 4b/4c/4d/4e ghost/spec-miss/converge-status repros, 4f no-brick, 4g re-run-not-bricked) are unchanged and green. Python gate suite **18/18**. Verified the whole suite fails on the branch's earlier commits (21 shell failures on the first cut, incl. the fail-open + spec-miss repros; the python suite cannot even load until the gate gains `EX_NO_FLOOR`) — the guards bite.
- **Gates green.** `bash -n` clean on `run-full-install.sh` + the shell test. `bump-version.sh --check` → **all 11 markers agree at v19.50.0**. Repo-consistency (`qc-assert-repo-consistency.py`) → **10/10 dimensions OK, 0 DRIFT**. Content manifest untouched (scripts + docs + INSTALL.md — no roles/sops/personas — **no `content_sha` restamp required**).
- **Skill version rolled (guard G3):** `32-command-center-setup` content changed → `skill-version.txt` **v12.9.36 → v12.9.37**.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.49.0 → **v19.50.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.50.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.49.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(23/C-series): stop the board-join prover false-failing a HEALTHY multi-company board whose company_id is not derivable from company-config

Merges `fix/onb-cseries-join-prover` (6 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.48.0) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Repo-only: no live-box change. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **THE DEFECT — a healthy multi-company Command Center board could hard-fail `done` FOREVER.** The C-series JOIN prover (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/prove-board-join.py`, the `chosen == provisioned == displayed` gate) scopes a multi-company `workspaces` table to ONE company by resolving `workspaces.company_id` from `company_slug_candidates()` — `{company-config.json .slug, the company dir name, slugify(config .name)}`. But `seed-workspaces.py` writes `company_id` from `company_info["slug"]`, which **derives from the company NAME** whenever a `COMPANY_NAME` override / interview-answers name is used, or the box has no `company-config.json`. On such a box NONE of the candidates reproduce the real `company_id`, so `resolve_company_slug()` returns a candidate that **is not on the board** (the phantom fallback), the display filter empties this company's REAL rows, and the prover returns `CANNOT-VOUCH` (rc=3). `scripts/qc-assert-workspace-departments-built.sh` maps that to rc=6, and `lib-onboarding-state.sh` maps ANY non-zero rc to "not materialized" — feeding `oc_overall_goal_check` AND the watchdog kill condition. A **perfectly healthy** board therefore blocks "done" indefinitely. This is the multi-company re-opening of the very single-company fail-safe asymmetry the prover's own header documents (it protects single-company boxes from a name-derived `company_id`, but the multi-company path did not).
- **VERIFIED, NOT ASSUMED (the lead's diagnosis re-proven from raw output).** Reproduced end-to-end with the REAL Skill-32 seeder: acme's 5 chosen==provisioned==displayed departments seeded under `company_id='acme-inc'` (`slugify("Acme Inc")`), globex's rows sharing the table → prover rc=3, **QC gate rc=6 on a healthy board**. The prior test T14 never covered this: its fixture puts acme's rows under `company_id='acme'`, which IS the dir-name candidate, so the resolver always matched — the no-candidate-match path was untested.
- **THE FIX (`prove-board-join.py`).** Before accepting the phantom slug, identify this company's rows by **CONTENT**: new `resolve_scope_by_content()` scopes to the `company_id` whose DISPLAYED departments overlap what the client CHOSE, **iff exactly one company_id dominates** the overlap. Fail-closed: no overlap (genuinely un-seeded) or a tie → the phantom stands and `CANNOT-VOUCH` correctly blocks. The orchestrator/CEO column is excluded from the overlap signal (every company has one). It can **never** manufacture a false PASS — it only swaps a phantom that already matched zero rows for a real `company_id`, and the join then verifies `chosen == provisioned == displayed` on those rows independently. `chosen` is now read before scoping; the keyer is computed once.
- **THE FIX (`scripts/qc-assert-workspace-departments-built.sh`).** The QC consumer stopped pre-resolving a `--company-slug` and handing it to the prover as *explicit* — the bare resolver returns the phantom, which `main()` would honour and skip the content disambiguation. `main()` now owns the full resolution (single source of truth for CI, the QC gate, and a bare operator run).
- **THE TEST (`test-board-join-chain.sh` T17).** The no-candidate-match case, reproduced with the real seeder + real gate: proves the phantom fallback is live and non-vacuous, then proves the healthy board passes (prover rc=0, QC gate not-rc=6), PLUS a fail-closed companion — a genuinely un-seeded company on a multi-company board still `CANNOT-VOUCH`es (prover rc=3 / QC rc=6). **Fails 3/3 against the pre-fix tree; 65/65 green with the fix.**
- **Also on this branch (the original prover work, unchanged by this reland).** The AF-BOARD-JOIN-DRIFT prover itself (`chosen==provisioned==displayed`, six drift classes, archive-aware, canonical join key); closure of the X.11 FAIL-OPEN in `scripts/qc-system-integrity.sh` (rc=6 now HARD-FAILS instead of routing to the WARN catch-all); the `companyDir`/`artifactWritten` reconciliation marker in `build-workforce.write_chosen_departments_artifact()` so a fail-soft artifact write can no longer buy a silent pass; `AGENTS.md` / `FLEET-STANDARDS.md §6` enumerate rc=6 in the gate's exit-code contract; and a `.github/workflows/board-join-chain-guard.yml` CI trigger.
- **Gates green.** `test-board-join-chain.sh` **65/65**. `bash -n` clean on the changed shell gate. `bump-version.sh --check` → **all 11 markers agree at v19.49.0**. Repo-consistency gate (`qc-assert-repo-consistency.py`) → **10/10 dimensions OK, 0 DRIFT** (VERSION-MARKERS + CONTENT-HASH included). Content manifest untouched (scripts + docs only — no roles/sops/personas — so **no `content_sha` restamp required**, same rationale as v19.48.0).
- **Skill version rolled (guard G3):** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint` content changed; its `skill-version.txt` is version marker #3 and rolls **19.48.0 → 19.49.0** with the repo.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.48.0 → **v19.49.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.49.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.48.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(32/updater): provision a sovereignty-clean QC judge per box, and stop the updater running a STALE Skill-32 installer

Merges `fix/onb-cc-qc-judge-and-stale-checkout` (2 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.47.0) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Two defects, one branch. Repo-only for the fleet: the ONLY live-box change is on the **operator's own box**, as an authorized canary, and it is the proof this works. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **A — the quality-review JUDGE had no model on any box, so no task could ever pass review.** `qc-scorer.ts`'s `resolveClientJudgeModel()` reads exactly two sources — the department QC agent's `model` column and `QC_JUDGE_MODEL` — and **both are empty on every box**, so it fails **CLOSED by design** (QC-08: the judge runs on the CLIENT'S OWN model and NEVER borrows a shared/operator key). Every review therefore fell to the heuristic `'no-key'` path, which **never auto-passes**; after 3 passes it escalates to a terminal `[QC-HEURISTIC-FINAL]` marker which the re-score sweep excludes **permanently**. The board looked alive and **completed nothing**. This was never a scorer bug — it was an **unprovisioned setting**.
- **A-fix — `cc_resolve_judge_model()` in `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh`.** At install/update the installer now selects a judge from **THIS client's OWN Ollama Cloud text models** (read from the box's own `openclaw.json` — the same sovereign store the existing `cc_resolve_sovereign_model` reads) and writes `QC_JUDGE_MODEL` into the app's `.env.local`. Family precedence is the operator's decision: **deepseek > glm > qwen3 > gpt-oss > mistral**; a `*-code`/`*-coder` model is **never eligible** (the judge must be a strong general reasoner). **⛔ It is always one of the client's OWN models** — there is no hardcoded fleet default that could point at another client's key. **No eligible own-model → `QC_JUDGE_MODEL` stays UNSET** (fail-closed is correct) and the installer logs `judge not provisioned — needs a client model`. We never guess, never borrow. Idempotent: an operator-set judge is preserved (`CC_QC_JUDGE_MODEL` overrides, and must itself be a client-owned non-code reasoning cloud model).
- **A-form — derived from real capability, not assumption.** The judge id is written **verbatim** as the client's own `<m>:cloud`, stripping only a leading `ollama/`/`ollama-cloud/` prefix. We deliberately do **NOT** rewrite `<m>:cloud` → `ollama-cloud/<m>`: that strips the `:cloud` tag the endpoint requires. Proven live — the box's Ollama endpoint **404s on the bare id, 200s on `<m>:cloud`**.
- **A-note — JUDGE != WRITER only fires when the WRITER is known.** The scorer skips the equality guard when `input.writerModel` is null. **Every `agents.model` column is blank fleet-wide** (measured on the canary: **0 of 290** agents carry a model), so the guard is skipped today and **any eligible client-owned reasoning model is a safe judge**. Recorded in code and here so a future reader does not "fix" a guard that is correctly inert.
- **A-PROOF (the canary, on the operator's OWN box — this is the deliverable, not "I set the var").** Two REAL Podcast-Engine tasks had been stuck in `review` at a heuristic 8.0 with the terminal `[QC-HEURISTIC-FINAL]` marker (sweep-excluded forever). With the judge provisioned to one of the operator's OWN DeepSeek cloud models and the app restarted, both were re-scored through the **real LLM path** and **advanced out of the stuck state**: events flipped from `[QC-HEURISTIC-FINAL]` to **`[QC-AUTO] … [path:llm] [scorer:QC Specialist — Marketing]`** with substantive, artifact-aware, model-stated verdicts — **1.0/10** and **2.0/10** — correctly identifying that each "draft" deliverable was a **96-byte placeholder file** containing none of the promised 2 emails + 2 SMS. The heuristic had been passing these at a false 8.0; the real judge caught them and routed both to `backlog` with actionable gap notes. The judge now scores, and the board now moves.
- **A-FINDING (open, for the owner — the judge NAME is provisioned, the ENDPOINT is a separate gap).** On the operator's box the CC Ollama-Cloud connector's default endpoint (`https://ollama.com/v1`) returned **401 Unauthorized** for **every** stored key. `GET /v1/models` is **unauthenticated** (a bogus key also returns 200 + the full 34-model catalog), which is why the `model_registry` looked healthy and masked this for so long — listing models never proved the box could *call* one. The working sovereign path is the box's **own local Ollama proxy**, which serves the client's `:cloud` reasoning models. The canary therefore also set `OLLAMA_CLOUD_BASE_URL` to that local proxy — **on the operator's box only**. The installer **does NOT** provision this base URL fleet-wide: that would be guessing another box's endpoint, and some boxes may legitimately hold a real ollama.com API key. **Decision needed from the owner** on the fleet-wide endpoint posture before any roll — provisioning the judge NAME alone will not make review pass on a box whose ollama-cloud endpoint cannot authenticate.
- **B — the token-mirror never reaches a box whose on-box checkout is old.** `update-skills.sh` **never `git pull`s the on-box onboarding checkout.** It clones `origin/main` fresh to `/tmp` and **copies skill folders** over `$SKILLS_DIR` — but the copy loop refreshes a skill **only when that skill is in this run's copy set**. The D5 block then invokes the **ON-BOX** installer (`$SKILLS_DIR/32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh`) **unconditionally, with no version guard**. So an `--only <not-32>` run (or a resume/closeout path calling the once-placed on-box copy) executes a **STALE** `run-full-install.sh` that predates `cc_mirror_api_auth_to_agent_secrets` (Skill-32 v12.9.31). That stale installer writes the token to the app's `.env.local` (**server**) but never mirrors it to `$OC_ROOT/secrets/.env` (**department agent**) — the box lands **half-provisioned: server fine, dispatch dead**, board silently stalled. This is exactly how two boxes "provisioned" today and stayed dispatch-dead.
- **B-fix — D5-PRE, in `update-skills.sh`.** Before either D5 branch runs the installer, the on-box Skill-32 folder is **refreshed from the freshly-cloned source** (`$ONBOARDING_DIR`), so the installer that actually executes always carries current provisioning logic. Version-gated on `skill-version.txt` (a no-op when already current), best-effort, never fatal. This is the **repo/process** fix — it makes the NEXT run correct on **every** box rather than hand-patching individual boxes. Belt-and-suspenders: `run-full-install.sh` now also asserts a **loud post-condition** — `MC_API_TOKEN` in the CC `.env.local` but **absent** from `$OC_ROOT/secrets/.env` logs an `ERROR` naming the stale-checkout cause and the remedy, so a half-provisioned box can never fail silently again.
- **B — what the two affected boxes will need (NOT done here, by instruction).** Their on-box Skill 32 is **v12.9.27** (repo detached at v19.1.0), predating the mirror. They need a **normal `update-skills.sh` run once this lands** — D5-PRE will refresh their Skill 32 to current *before* the installer runs, and the mirror will then execute and populate `$OC_ROOT/secrets/.env`. They must **NOT** be "fixed" by re-running their existing on-box installer directly: that is the very stale binary that caused the failure. No live-box remediation was performed on them in this branch.
- **Gates green.** `bash -n` clean on both changed shell files. `bump-version.sh --check` → **all 11 markers agree**. Judge-resolver validated against the operator's real `openclaw.json`: selects a DeepSeek cloud model and correctly **rejects** `kimi-k2.7-code:cloud`, `qwen3-coder:480b` (code models), `gemma4:31b-cloud` (not a `:cloud` model), and non-family models. Content manifest untouched (this branch changed only scripts — no roles/sops/personas — so **no `content_sha` restamp was required**).
- **Skill version rolled (guard G3):** `32-command-center-setup` v12.9.35 → **v12.9.36** (its content changed).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.47.0 → **v19.48.0** via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.48.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.47.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(39/38/23/install): fail-closed industry gate, truncation-safe cron idempotency, durable cron tombstone, and a loop-safe resume cron

Merges `fix/industry-gate-and-idempotent-crons` (11 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.46.0) as the serial onboarding writer, `--no-ff`. Independent judge **9.1 MERGE-READY**; every claim re-proven here from raw output rather than taken on the branch's word. **This branch is the hard prerequisite for any fleet roll — rolling the previous main would re-install all three defects on every box.** Repo-only: it deliberately performs NO live-box remediation. No client names, no secret values, no roster human names in the diff.

- **A — the real-estate playbook was installing on EVERY box, and now cannot.** Skill 39 (`39-real-estate-playbook`) had **no industry gate anywhere** in its provisioning path — `00-verify-prerequisites.sh` checked only Skill 38 + jq/curl, and `wire.sh` ran every step unconditionally. That is how a real-estate department landed on a non-real-estate client. A new canonical predicate (`shared-utils/industry-gate.sh`) keys on `.industryPack.slug == "real-estate"` in the box's build-state and **FAILS CLOSED** on every ambiguity — no OpenClaw root, missing/unparsable build-state, absent or non-matching slug — because absence of information is never permission to install. The gate is enforced at THREE independent choke points (`00-verify-prerequisites.sh` Section E, `07-register-crons.sh`, and `wire.sh`) so bypassing any one of them still installs nothing; `wire.sh` in particular now captures the prereq exit code **directly** instead of through its fail-soft `run_step()`, whose swallowing of non-zero exits is exactly what made the old "Skill 39 REFUSES to proceed" claim toothless. Exit code **2** is reserved for "not real estate — skip this vertical", kept distinct from exit 1 (a genuine missing prerequisite).
- **B — cron idempotency guards were text-grepping a table that truncates.** `openclaw cron list`'s text table truncates names past **~22 chars** (appending `...`). Every presence guard built on `cron list | grep -q "<name>"` therefore **false-negatived on any longer name** and re-registered a duplicate on every wire/update run — the confirmed root cause of the 6x-duplicate-cron incident. **Both** real-estate cron names exceed the limit (`re-open-house-followup-scan`=27, `re-post-close-anniversary`=25), and Skill 38 carried the identical defect on four more (`conversation-log-summarizer`=27, `proactive-suggestions-scan`=26, and two at 23). Replaced fleet-wide with `oc_cron_present()` (`shared-utils/cron-lib.sh`) — an exact JSON `.name ==` match against `cron list --json`, generalizing the fix `scripts/ensure-pipeline-crons.sh` already shipped for its own registrars in v13.0.2. It **never** falls back to a positive text-table match (that is the root-cause path); with no JSON parser available it fails OPEN, so a caller re-attempts registration rather than claiming a false presence.
- **B+ — the critical one: a DISABLED cron was invisible, so the next update silently RESURRECTED it.** On a live box `openclaw cron list --json` returned only **16 of 31** actual rows — the 15 missing were DISABLED. A disabled cron is therefore invisible to *any* presence check, which means **the entire fleet cron cleanup performed by disabling jobs would have been wiped out by the very next update run.** Because no `--all`/`--include-disabled` flag is documented (and none may exist), the fix cannot depend on list visibility at all: a **durable file-marker tombstone** (`oc_cron_tombstone` / `oc_cron_tombstoned`, plus the operator-facing `scripts/tombstone-cron.sh`) mirrors the already-proven `BOX_PARK_MARKER` pattern so a deliberate kill **sticks** across re-registration regardless of what the CLI does or does not show. Every registrar in the repo now checks the tombstone before adding. A best-effort full-visibility flag is *feature-detected* from `cron list --help` and never assumed to exist.
- **C — `workforce-build-resume` was burning a full LLM turn every 15 minutes to do nothing.** The cron was registered as a silent main-session **agent-message** job carrying the entire `resume-prompt.txt` as its payload, so every `*/15` tick spun up a complete agent turn merely to reach `resume-workforce-build.sh`'s own "nothing to resume" verdict — roughly **96 wasted turns/day/box**. That script is already a cheap, token-free bash+jq check that escalates to a real agent turn (via `openclaw message send`) *only* when there is genuinely pending work, so the cron is now registered in **command mode** (`bash resume-workforce-build.sh`), costing **zero LLM tokens per tick**, with a short run-the-script agent-message fallback for older CLIs that lack `--command`. Its dispatch path also gains **exponential rate-limit/error backoff** (429 / quota / overloaded / context-too-large → widen to `base × 2^n`, capped, cleared on success) instead of retrying an active rate-limit at the same fixed cadence.
- **Deliberately NOT done, and stated plainly.** No auto-dedup of the duplicate crons already present on live boxes, and no live-box remediation of already-disabled-and-invisible crons — both are mutating, box-wide cleanup actions with their own audit story, tracked outside this repo-only branch. The skill-specific cron registrars in **57 / 58 / 59** and **35-social-media-planner** still carry bespoke dedup logic and were **not** swept into `cron-lib.sh`; none of their cron names is currently known to cross the ~22-char truncation threshold, so this is a consistency/tech-debt item rather than a live defect, but it is the obvious next sweep.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC — re-run here, not quoted).** Full battery **88/88 passed, 0 failed** across 6 suites: `real-estate-industry-gate` **15**, `pipeline-cron-idempotency` **14**, `cron-tombstone-durability` **7**, `workforce-resume-cheap-command-mode` **8**, plus the two pre-existing suites at **zero regression** — `bounded-workforce-build-resume` **24** and `update-skills-resume-cron` **20**. Each new suite is **mutation-proofed**: defeating the gate, restoring the old text-grep, or removing the tombstone check each reproduces the original bug, proving the assertions are real rather than vacuous. `bash -n` + `shellcheck -S error` on all **16** changed shell files -> **clean**. `bump-version.sh --check` -> **all 11 markers agree**. `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` -> **PASS, 10 dimensions, 0 DRIFT**. `hash-content-manifest.py --check` -> **PASS** (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas; this branch touched only scripts, so no content_sha restamp was required). `qc-closeout-wiring.sh` -> **PASS**. `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` -> **PASS** (structural). `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh` -> **PASS**. `qc-assert-skill-version-newline.sh` -> **PASS** (61 files).
- **The judge's one open finding, closed and independently proven.** `install.sh`'s `install_presentation_deps_cron()` (cron name `reassert-presentation-deps` = **26 chars**, over the truncation limit) was missed by the original sweep, fixed in the branch's final commit, and never re-judged. Verified here against the repo's own fake-CLI fixture by extracting the **shipped** function straight out of `install.sh`: two consecutive runs register **exactly ONE** cron; restoring the old text-grep makes the same two runs register **TWO** (proving the check is non-vacuous); and a durable tombstone holds it at **ZERO**. It holds up.
- **Skill versions rolled for the three skills whose content changed** (guard G3): `38-conversational-ai-system` 1.8.1 -> **1.8.2**, `39-real-estate-playbook` 1.0.7 -> **1.0.8**. `23-ai-workforce-blueprint` tracks the repo version in lockstep and rolled with it.
- **Pre-existing, NOT introduced here.** `59-anthology-engine` carries a SKILL.md-frontmatter/`skill-version.txt` drift (0.1.5 vs 0.1.6) dating to the v19.44.0 roll; it is present on origin/main, this branch does not touch skill 59, and it is left for the owner of that skill rather than silently swept into an unrelated release.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.46.0 -> v19.47.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.47.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.46.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(fleet/06/59/install): Wave-A audit landings — per-box ledger, browser-probe hardening, broker preflight, survey resume, runRetries ceiling, n8n standing rules

The Wave-A merge train, landed by the serial onboarding writer off fresh origin/main (v19.45.0). SIX branches, each independently QC-judged, merged `--no-ff` one at a time. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff. (The train was interrupted by a host kernel panic after the fifth landing; it was resumed and completed from a re-verified origin — the five already-pushed merges were confirmed present on main BY CONTENT, not by `git cherry`, which lies on reworked relands.)

- **AUD-19/20/21 — `fix/onb-browser-probe-hardening` (QC 9.4).** Skill-41's CDP probe spawned `createBrowser({headless:true})` with NO timeout and a trap that deleted only the temp script, so a hung CDP session hung the calling script forever and a kill of that script orphaned node plus any Chromium it launched; a new shared lib (`41-build-with-ai-playbook/scripts/lib/browser-probe-timeout.sh`) adds a hard timeout clamped to ≤120s and a node-PID trap that recursively reaps the FULL descendant tree (`pgrep -P` walk, portable across BSD/GNU) on timeout or on INT/TERM/EXIT — proven against real OS process trees, not mocks, and a close-guarantee test plus a CI guard lock it.
- **AUD-24 — `fix/onb-runretries-wiring` (QC 9.0).** `git grep -c runRetries install.sh` was **0** — the outer-run-loop retry ceiling was never wired; `scripts/wire-run-retries.sh` now seeds `agents.defaults.runRetries.{base,perProfile,min,max}` with SET-IF-ABSENT semantics gated behind a PER-BOX schema grep of the runtime actually installed on that box, seeding the runtime's OWN constants (24 / 8 / 32 / 160, `ceiling = clamp(base + perProfile × profileCandidateCount, min, max)`) so the change is BEHAVIOUR-NEUTRAL everywhere, with a ceiling-guard workflow to keep it that way.
- **AUD-45 — `fix/onb-anthology-preflight-broker` (QC 9.3).** `59-anthology-engine/preflight.sh` carried **zero** broker references, so a client box's provisioning preflight never asserted that the n8n Drive credential broker is configured — leaving it free to drift toward the ONE thing `drive_adapter.broker_configured()`'s own docstring forbids, a client box holding the local Google service-account key; a thin, opt-in `--broker-check` mode now shells out to the already-shipped `drive-adapter broker-status` single source of truth and exits **2 (fail-closed)** unless `N8N_DRIVE_WEBHOOK_URL` **and** `N8N_DRIVE_WEBHOOK_TOKEN` both resolve, with the default RESOLVE and `--check` modes untouched.
- **AUD-52/53/54 — `fix/onb-skill6-survey-resume` (QC 9.7).** Three units on one branch because all three edit `ghl_survey_builder.py` (splitting them guarantees a conflict): **U6** stops scanning `a[href]`/"reading from the integrate-panel screenshot" and instead takes the survey id from `location.href` (falling back to the builder iframe `.src`), CONSTRUCTS the widget URL, GETs it, and stores a **fetch-200 receipt** — a non-200 or missing id raises `SurveyUrlCaptureStop`, fail-closed, screenshot fallback deleted entirely; **U8** adds phase-granular `--resume <run_id>` across the survey and form builders; **U10** gives every builder a uniform RUN REPORT.
- **AUD-58 — `fix/onb-fleet-perbox-ledger` (QC 9.5).** Closes the failure mode of a fan-out that reports GREEN on a BROKEN box — an aggregate exit code is the one number a partially-failed fan-out is worst at, and the roll previously left NO per-box evidence behind; `shared-utils/fleet_ledger.py` writes a persistent per-box ledger at the doctrine path `/tmp/<sweep>/<box>.json` (plus a `_sweep.json` rollup) with atomic writes, per-box `flock`, attempt history, and **expectations-hashed rows so `--resume` can never reuse a PASS recorded against a DIFFERENT roll target**, paired with a post-fan-out validation harness that is the hard gate in front of the ONE batched fleet roll.
- **AUD-65 — `fix/onb-n8n-standing-rules` (QC 8.7).** Ports the three standing rules learned from the n8n Anthology Drive Broker incident out of a spec doc that is about to be retired and into two durable in-repo homes before it disappears: the full rules restated in `59-anthology-engine/config/n8n/README.md` (the ops runbook for the deployment they actually govern) and a hyper-concise pointer in `AGENTS.md`, per the repo's own storage rule — summarize, don't paste, and point at the full doc — so every agent inherits them from its core bootstrap without re-deriving them.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** FULL skill-06 suite -> **1435 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed** (181s; up from 1392 at v19.44.0 — the +43 are this train's new survey-resume/run-report assertions, zero regression). The three directly-triggered skill-6 suites -> **105 passed**. `py_compile` on all 10 changed files -> **clean** (this train changed zero `.sh`, so `bash -n`/`shellcheck` are N/A for the final landing; the five earlier landings were shell-gated at their own merge). `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` -> **all 11 markers agree**. `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` -> **PASS** (structural). `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh` -> **PASS** (36/38 shipped SOPs, every secret-pattern grep existence-only).
- **`qc-system-integrity.sh` -> FAIL, and it is NOT this release (stated plainly rather than papered over).** It fails on **live-box environment** state — X.6 gemini persona smoke, X.7 legacy ZHC root, X.9 an Ollama `:cloud` maxTokens overshoot in the box's own config, X.11 phantom department tree, X.12 artifact drift, X.14 PLATFORM_FACTS. Run against the **already-shipped v19.45.0** (`origin/main`, db82fc8d) in a clean worktree it emits the **byte-identical** failure set (diff = a timestamp and a temp path). This release adds **zero** new integrity failures; the red is pre-existing box state and is tracked outside the merge train.
- **The defective Telegram chat-id CI guard stayed deleted** — `scripts/qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, its `qc-static.yml` step, and its fixture are all still absent, as intended since v19.45.0. No regression, nothing to re-remove.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.45.0 -> v19.46.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.46.0` cut on the release merge commit.

## [v19.45.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(platform/mac + ci): power-outage resilience for every provisioned Mac, and removal of the defective shape-based Telegram chat-id CI guard

Two landings by the serial onboarding writer, off fresh origin/main (v19.44.0). (a) Merges `fix/power-outage-resilience` (10 files, +2032/-35) hardening the Mac provisioner so a box comes all the way back after a power loss. (b) In its own labeled commit ahead of the merge, REMOVES the defective Telegram chat-id CI guard per the standing operator ruling that Telegram chat IDs are EXEMPT from the no-client-data rule. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **Every provisioned Mac now survives a power outage — FileVault is a HARD, fail-closed gate.** `scripts/fix-power-resilience.sh` REFUSES to remediate a FileVault-ON box (exit 78), prints exactly what a human must do on site, and does not pretend an SSH rescue can unlock the disk — because it cannot. FileVault-ON is the one state no amount of remote automation can auto-unlock at boot, so the tooling stops loud instead of shipping a false green.
- **pmset auto-restart + tunnels promoted from login-gated LaunchAgents to root LaunchDaemons.** `harden-mac-tunnel.sh` Layer D now sets `pmset autorestart` (it never did), and the cloudflared tunnel installs into `/Library/LaunchDaemons` (was `LaunchAgents`) so it comes up as a root system daemon with NO GUI login session required. The daemon conversion is guarded by a session-coupling probe: a box running a session-coupled plugin (iMessage enabled, or a HEADFUL browser) correctly REFUSES the conversion because TCC would break it; a headless box converts.
- **Tunnel token is never in cleartext on a command line.** cloudflared is resolved with `command -v` (no hardcoded `/opt/homebrew/bin` path; `/usr/local` Intel-prefix fallback), the token is written to a mode-600 file and the rendered plist uses `--token-file` — never a bare `--token`, and the token VALUE never appears in the plist. pm2 launchd naming drift is collapsed to one canonical label.
- **Ships its own proof surface.** A 49-assertion gate test (`tests/unit/power-resilience-gate.test.sh`) and a CI guard workflow (`.github/workflows/power-resilience-guard.yml`) lock the above so a future regression fails loud.
- **Removed the defective shape-based Telegram chat-id CI guard.** Deleted `scripts/qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, its `CRITICAL-1b` step in `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`, and its now-orphaned non-vacuity self-test fixture (`tests/fixtures/telegram-chat-id-leak/`). The guard flagged ANY 8-10 digit integer purely by SHAPE and could not distinguish a synthetic placeholder from a real ID; it false-positived on the obvious fake `11111111` in skill-60 test fixtures and sat RED on main, jamming the merge train. Per the standing operator ruling that Telegram chat IDs are EXEMPT from the no-client-data rule, the guard is removed. Every OTHER QC gate (CRITICAL-1 client-names, FLEET-STANDARDS, secret-printing, version markers, etc.) remains intact; historical CHANGELOG references to the guard are left untouched as records of past releases.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `power-resilience-gate.test.sh` -> **49 passed, 0 failed**. `bash -n` + `shellcheck -S error` on all 6 changed shell scripts -> **clean**. `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` -> **all 11 markers agree**. `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` -> **PASS** (structural). `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh` -> **PASS**. `qc-static.yml` parses as valid YAML after the CRITICAL-1b removal.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.44.0 -> v19.45.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.45.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.44.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  feat(59/06): Anthology pipeline PL1.land poll-with-deadline + browser-control AUTOCREATE + rich diagnostics (Skills 59, 6)

Merges `feat/anthology-pipeline-browser-autocreate` off fresh origin/main (v19.43.0) as the serial onboarding writer — the largest unlanded feature, merged LAST so nothing had to rebase over it. Judge 9.0. No client names, no real secrets, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff (both flags investigated and CLEARED — see below).

- **The Anthology pipeline can now be AUTO-PROVISIONED, fail-closed.** Convert and Flow exposes NO public API to CREATE a pipeline (the UI is the only create surface). `provision-pipeline` now finds-and-binds BY EXACT NAME and, when the pipeline is ABSENT, attempts ONE Skill-6 browser-control creation (`ghl_pipeline_builder.py`, exact-name mode), RE-READS, and binds ONLY what the API read surface confirms. A failed or unavailable walk STOPS with an operator surface (`AF-AE-PIPELINE-UI-CREATE`) — never a silent fallback, never a faked success (lying-builder STOP, verify-read HELD, dry-run-no-attempt, opt-out, missing-builder receipt).
- **Conflict reconciliation — main was 342 commits ahead, AND this branch overlapped `fix/skill6-ghl-form-iframe-drag` (landed as v19.41.0).** `anthology_registry.py`: main had evolved the field inventory 19 -> 28 (4 G10 rewrite + 5 U8 cover-style/SINGLE_OPTIONS) while the branch built browser-create on the OLD 19-field world. Resolved by KEEPING BOTH — and the file's OWN self-test now reports OK for exactly that combination, which validates the resolution rather than assuming it. `field-map.json`: took the branch's `$note`, a strict SUPERSET that RETAINS the `AF-AE-PIPELINE-UI-CREATE` STOP and adds the autocreate path; field count re-verified **28 -> 28** (no regression to 19).
- **Fleet-wide doctrine improvements taken from the branch.** `scan-no-json-exports.sh` and `persona-matching-protocol.md` were resolved in the branch's favour because it REMOVES hardcoded operator paths (`$HOME/clawd/...`, bare `$HOME`) from this fleet-wide repo.
- **Skill-local CHANGELOG version COLLISION resolved (both entries preserved).** Two parallel branches independently claimed skill-local `v18.1.12` for DIFFERENT changes (F4 remove-control vs pipeline EXACT-NAME mode), and `v18.1.13` was already taken by PL1.land. The EXACT-NAME entry was renumbered to **v18.1.14** (next free) with an inline note. Nothing truncated; 0 duplicate headers.
- **FALSE-POSITIVE FLAGS, INVESTIGATED AND CLEARED (not rubber-stamped).** The secret-shaped strings in `caf_credential_gate.py` / `scan-no-secrets.sh` are the DETECTORS' own regexes and their obviously-fake self-test fixtures (sequential-alphabet/hex literals; a variable literally named `fake_sk`); the engine's own `scan-no-secrets.sh` reports **CLEAN**. Every `claude-`/`anthropic` hit is ENFORCEMENT — the model ladders mandate the client's OWN **NON-Anthropic** chain and the manifest asserts `"no_anthropic_identifiers": true`.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `anthology_registry self-test` -> **OK** (28-field inventory + browser-control create). `qc-strike-gate --self-test` -> **25/25**. `test_ghl_pipeline_builder.py` -> **41 passed**. `scan-no-json-exports` -> CLEAN. `scan-no-secrets` -> CLEAN. `qc-assert-repo-consistency` -> **PASS, 10 dimensions, 0 DRIFT**. `hash-content-manifest --check` -> **PASS**. **FULL skill-06 suite -> 1392 passed, 15 skipped, 0 FAILED** (up from 1379 at v19.41.0 — the two previously-conflicting branches now coexist with ZERO regression).
- **Skill 59 rolled 0.1.5 -> 0.1.6** (its content changed here; `bump-version.sh` does not cover skill 59). Repo rolled v19.43.0 -> v19.44.0 (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.44.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.43.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(23, C5+C7): phantom duplicate department-tree gate + reconcile, and a durable chosen-departments artifact with a real reader (Skill 23)

Merges `fable-fix/onb23-tail` (2 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.42.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Wave-5 findings C5 + C7; independent adversarial judge **8.7**, re-proven here from raw output. Scoped to `23-ai-workforce-blueprint` + the workspace/system QC scripts. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **C5 — phantom DUPLICATE department-tree materialization is now GATED, not silently tolerated.** A legacy tree could collide with the chosen department set and materialize a phantom duplicate; the gate now ENFORCES (QC X.11, rc=5) and `reconcile-legacy-tree.py` gained a real reconcile path instead of leaving the collision in place.
- **C7 — the chosen department list is now a DURABLE artifact with a real reader.** Downstream consumers previously had no authoritative record of which departments the client actually chose (they re-derived it, which is exactly how a phantom tree survives). The chosen-list is now written as a durable artifact and `build-workforce.py` / `department-floor.py` read it authoritatively.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `test-phantom-dept-collision-gate.sh` -> **PASS=24 FAIL=0**. `test-chosen-departments-artifact.sh` -> **PASS=22 FAIL=0**. `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` -> **PASS, 10 dimensions OK, 0 DRIFT**. `build-workforce.py` / `department-floor.py` / `reconcile-legacy-tree.py` all compile; `qc-assert-workspace-departments-built.sh` + `qc-system-integrity.sh` pass `bash -n`. Both new tests are CI-wired into `skill23-provisioning-tests.yml`.
- **⚠️ OPERATOR-LIVE TAIL — NOT EXECUTED HERE (by design).** The code is merged, but running the legacy-tree RECONCILE against a live box is an operator-live step and was deliberately NOT run by the merge train. Boxes carrying a legacy/phantom tree still need that reconcile executed before the C5 gate will report clean on them.

## [v19.42.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(51/23, E5): enforce the signature-presentation turn-gated intake — the optional signature path is now REQUIRED (Skills 51, 23)

Merges `fable-fix/onb51-e5` off fresh origin/main (v19.41.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Wave-5 finding E5; independent adversarial judge **9.2**, re-proven here from raw output. Scoped to `23-ai-workforce-blueprint` (deck-intake-driver + the presentations role-library entry/SOP) + one unit test. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **E5 — the turn-gated signature path in the presentation intake was OPTIONAL when it should be ENFORCED.** The signature-presentation intake could be satisfied without walking the turn-gated path, so a deck could be produced without the eight-questions / one-block / frame-selection discipline that E2/E3/E4 already enforce elsewhere. It is now REQUIRED, making the intake consistent with the rest of the gated family.
- **Role-library restamp is legitimate, not churn.** The `_index.json` diff is dominated by 619 `content_hashed_at` re-stamps following the edits to `signature-presentation-architect.md` and `SOP-SIGPRES-01`. Verified by the content-manifest gate rather than assumed.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `tests/unit/presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh` -> **92 passed, 0 failed** (clean-main baseline was 90 passed — the 2 new assertions are the E5 enforcement). `hash-content-manifest.py --check` -> **PASS** (433 roles + 132 sops + 19 personas all carry content_sha/content_version; every stored sha matches the live file; no token leaks). `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` -> **PASS, 10 dimensions OK, 0 DRIFT**. `deck-intake-driver.py` compiles.

## [v19.41.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  feat(06/skill6): cross-origin-iframe drag primitive + F4 remove-control TIERED acquisition + icon-pill rebuild (Skill 6)

Merges `fix/skill6-ghl-form-iframe-drag` (13 commits) off fresh origin/main (v19.40.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Judge 9.0. Scoped to `06-ghl-install-pages`. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **F4 remove-control rebuilt on evidence, not on a false claim.** The §6 `role=link 'Remove field'` claim was CONTRADICTED live; F4 is rebuilt around the evidenced icon-pill mechanism with tiered acquisition + rich failure diagnostics — the root-cause fix for the 3x-failed `STOP@F4.delete:Phone`.
- **New shared primitive: `ghl_iframe_drag.py` (2797 lines).** agent-browser 0.27.0 cannot LOCATE a non-interactive drag-source row across a cross-origin iframe boundary, so the drag step is delegated to a frame-scoped coordinate-drag primitive driven by Playwright over the SAME agent-browser CDP session (one browser, one login). Fail-closed: raises rather than faking success when the primitive/CDP is unavailable.
- **Conflict reconciliation — main was 359 commits AHEAD of the branch (5 conflicts).** `ghl_survey_builder.py` was resolved by KEEPING BOTH SIDES: main's REUSED adapters (`ghl_ab_executor`, `ghl_iframe_dragdrop`, `ghl_survey_rest` + their binders) AND the branch's new work (`_get_cdp_url`, `_perform_iframe_drag`, `_xpath_text`, the v18.1.3 TEXT-targeting verbs). The `_run_cmd` glue follows git's OWN reference resolution of the sibling `ghl_form_builder._ab`: main's `stdin` mechanism (`eval --stdin`) and the branch's `shlex.quote(arg)` are COMPLEMENTARY and both retained. An earlier take-main-wholesale resolution was REJECTED — it silently dropped all 144 of the branch's additive survey lines, which the branch's own test caught (missing `_xpath_text`).
- **Skill-local CHANGELOG: both histories combined, nothing truncated** (32 entries, main's newer entries first, no duplicate headers).
- **Test-quality fix (added here, not in the branch).** The live selftest was guarded only on `PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE` (the PACKAGE) — but the browser BINARY can be absent, so it HARD-FAILED on any box that had not run `playwright install`, a fleet-wide false red. The guard now also verifies the browser executable and skips cleanly when it is missing. Environment check only: with a browser present the test still runs, so it cannot mask a code regression.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** FULL skill-06 suite -> **1379 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed** (122s). The live cross-origin-iframe drag selftest was REPRODUCED for real (chromium-headless-shell installed, test RUN, **1 passed** in 11s) — not skipped, not assumed.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.40.0 -> v19.41.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.41.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.40.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  feat(fleet-standards): per-turn token-burn family — toolSearch directory mode + reserved ollama prompt-caching slot + core-bootstrap size guard

Merges `feat/toolsearch-directory-default` off fresh origin/main (v19.39.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Judge 9.0. Scoped to `FLEET-STANDARDS.md` + `scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh` (no skill dirs → no G3 obligation). No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **On-demand MCP tool loading (`tools.toolSearch.mode="directory"`) becomes a fleet standard.** The fleet was paying "schema-every-turn": every OpenClaw / plugin / MCP tool's full JSON schema was injected into context on EVERY turn. Most acute on the GHL community MCP (~hundreds of tools), which `update-skills.sh wire_ghl_mcp` re-registers on every pass. Directory mode compacts the standing catalog behind a searchable names+descriptions index that the model hydrates on demand — so re-registration can never reintroduce the full-schema cost. Client-provided run tools stay directly visible; only the standing catalog is compacted.
- **Reserved (NO-OP) slot for ollama prompt caching — deliberately does not guess.** Prompt caching is the MEASURED dominant per-turn burn (`cacheRead=0` fleet-wide), but the config key is not yet verified, so the slot is reserved and inert until the verified key lands. It writes nothing. This is the correct fail-closed posture: no invented config keys.
- **Core-bootstrap SIZE GUARD (warn-only, ~150K target).** Flags oversized compiled AGENTS/MEMORY/TOOLS/SOUL/IDENTITY bootstraps that are re-billed every turn. Never edits content — it only warns.
- **Safe to re-run.** The enforcement is idempotent and override-preserving: `deep_merge()` recurses into an existing `toolSearch` block and enforces ONLY `mode`, leaving per-box tuning (`codeTimeoutMs` / `searchDefaultLimit` / `maxSearchLimit`) untouched. An `openclaw config validate` gate is the backstop + auto-rollback if a gateway version ever rejects the key.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `bash -n scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh` OK; all 3 embedded python heredocs (PRESDETECT_PY / PYBT / SKILLINS_PY) compile; `tests/unit/both-paths-zhe-delivery.test.sh` PASS (all dimensions deliver on both paths).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.39.0 -> v19.40.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.40.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.38.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  fix(FDN-7 residual): mirror the persona-SET 5th member (embedded_persona_count) into qc-assert-repo-consistency.py (Skill 23)

Lands the one piece the FDN-7 RELAND (PR #535, `f1c73bf7`) left behind. Serial onboarding writer, off fresh origin/main (v19.37.0). Judge 9.0. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **The persona-SET 5th-member contract was ONE-SIDED on main.** The reland shipped `embedded_persona_count` into `persona_fleet.py`, `build-and-publish.sh`, `provision-persona-index.sh`, `update-skills.sh` and the guard workflow — but OMITTED the mirror in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/qc-assert-repo-consistency.py`, even though the code requires both checkers to stay in lockstep. Result: on a STALE published asset, `persona_fleet.py triad` correctly exited 5 while the repo-consistency checker reported PASS on the same tree. A counted-but-vector-less persona could clear repo QC.
- **Fix, scoped to that gap.** `_persona_set_triad_failures()` now reads `embedded_persona_count` + `asset_rebuild_required` and fails when the published asset is stale (embedded != SET count while `asset_rebuild_required=false`). Carve-out when `asset_rebuild_required=true` (a `--no-asset` staging bump); an absent field is tolerated additively (legacy manifest).
- **Regression-locked, and the lock is real.** New `tests/unit/persona-asset-freshness.test.sh` covers both checkers across stale / staged-carve-out / fresh / legacy / provisioning-refusal: **14/14 pass**, and reverting ONLY the qc-assert change drops it to **13/14** ("qc-assert did not report a failure on stale asset") — so the test genuinely fails without the fix. Three of its assertions were re-pointed at the reland's ACTUAL shipped strings (it emits `asset_rebuild_required:true`, not `=true`); the pre-reland test asserted the old wording.
- **No regression (raw, this QC).** The reland's own `asset-rebuild-required-gate.test.sh` stays **15/15**; `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` on the real repo is **PASS — 10 dimensions OK, 0 failures**; real-repo triad shows all 5 members = 99.
- **Branch `fix/FDN-7` was NOT merged — it is SUPERSEDED.** A wholesale merge conflicts in 6 files and its copy of `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` is 100 lines behind main (1475 vs 1575), so merging it would have REVERTED main's newer QC dimensions. Only the genuinely-missing residual was landed.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.37.0 -> v19.38.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.38.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.37.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  security: scrub live Telegram bot token from the install.sh TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN example comment

Merges the Telegram-token security-scrub branch off fresh origin/main (v19.36.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Security fix (judge 9.0). Scoped to a single comment line in `install.sh`. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the merged tree.

- **A real, live Telegram bot token was embedded in `install.sh` as the TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN format example.** The `looks_like_real_key()` validator documented its expected `<bot_id>:<token>` shape using an ACTUAL working bot credential rather than a fake one. It is replaced with an obviously-fake, format-valid placeholder (`123456789:AAFake-Example-Token-Not-A-Real-Secret00`, 40-char secret part) that still satisfies the `^[0-9]{8,12}:[A-Za-z0-9_-]{30,40}$` regex on the following line, so validator behavior is unchanged.
- **Whole-tree re-verified, not just the diff.** `git grep` across the full post-merge tree confirms the bot id and the token secret are absent from EVERY file — this was the only occurrence in the repo. `bash -n install.sh` passes.
- **Commit message scrubbed.** The incoming branch's commit message named the owning bot handle; it was rewritten before merge so no client/roster-identifying name lands in this fleet-wide repo. The diff is unchanged.
- **⚠️ THE TOKEN IS NOT YET NEUTRALIZED — OPERATOR ACTION REQUIRED.** This scrubs HEAD only. The credential remains in this repo's prior git history and is fully recoverable by anyone with clone access, and it has NOT been rotated. It stays LIVE until the token is rotated via BotFather (and, if the history is to be cleaned, a history rewrite is performed). Rotation is the operator's call and was not executed here.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.36.0 -> v19.37.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.37.0` cut on the release commit.

- **Founder alerts actually DELIVER instead of only spooling.** `route_alert()` + `credit_queue.py` now invoke `raise --failure-class` with VALID severities — the prior code called a nonexistent `notify` subcommand, so founder alerts silently spooled and never delivered. With a founder-target env set the alert now dispatches through the real `raise` path; without one it fails-closed to NO_FOUNDER rc=4 and the alert stays spooled (never a silent drop).

## [v19.28.0]  -  2026-07-11  -  feat(furnace-wiring): wire Skill 60+61 loop/furnace protection into onboarding + updater (HELD by gate) + operator-box canary (Skill 61)

REPO-ONLY: the DEFERRED operator-live steps (operator-box canary install/arm on Trevor's box, the 7-day DRY_RUN burn-in, and the fleet roll) are flagged for the operator and NOT executed here. Scoped to `61-loop-protection-system` (HOW-TO-USE, config/rollout.json, CHANGELOG, skill-version) + root `install.sh` / `update-skills.sh` (activation wiring) + `scripts/` (activate-loop-protection.sh, loop-protection-canary.sh, test-loop-protection-wiring.sh). No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **Skill 60+61 loop/furnace protection wired into onboarding + updater — HELD BY GATE.** `install.sh` and `update-skills.sh` invoke `activate-loop-protection.sh --role client` and persist the activator + canary, but `config/rollout.json` ships `fleet_rollout_enabled: false`: client-box activation runs ONLY when the operator flips the gate (or sets `OPENCLAW_LOOP_PROTECTION_ROLLOUT=1`). Per the wiring test, the gate defaults HELD and — critically — neither the gate nor the wiring EVER arms a box: install leaves DRY_RUN observe-only (`armed=false`); Tier-1 arming is the operator's separate post-burn-in action. This honors SKILL.md law 8 (CANARY, THEN HOLD) + the ratified 7-03 repo-only HOLD + the batch-rollout doctrine.
- **Operator-box canary (ungated by design).** `scripts/loop-protection-canary.sh --role operator` must prove FIRST on the operator's own box; it is deliberately not gated (the canary precedes the hold). Running it is a DEFERRED operator step, not done here.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `scripts/test-loop-protection-wiring.sh` -> 9 passed / 0 failed (W1-W4 install/updater wiring + persistence; W5 fleet gate present and defaults HELD; W6-W9 activate / canary / Skill-60 install / Skill-61 install self-tests); `61-loop-protection-system/verify.sh` -> VERIFIED (all offline self-tests / scanners / drills PASS — offset rewind, orphan, idle-burn, backoff-across-restart, healer-loop, offline-escalate no-secret, dry-run byte-identical, armed-park, revert). `bash -n` clean on all changed shell.
- **Version markers.** The branch bumped `61-loop-protection-system/skill-version.txt` 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 (the new wiring); this release syncs 61's SKILL.md top-level frontmatter `version:` to 0.2.0 (the branch left it at 0.1.0) so `skill-frontmatter-version-guard` + CI guard G3 both pass.

No client names, no secret values (the D-S8 drill proves a caught secret's value never lands in an alert), no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **Signal S3 context-usage now computed LIVE.** `_context_usage()` reads the real trajectory to compute window utilization (e.g. 86% of a 128000 window) and feeds `sig_s3()` a real S3/handoff finding, replacing the prior call-site that could not produce a percentage; it never guesses a pct on an already-broken ceiling.
- **Two-lane delivery with an operator-only D5 boundary.** The context-usage notice routes to the box agent via the D5 self-notice lane, and Lane 2 fires on the OPERATOR's OWN box — and NEVER on a client box (the D5 boundary is drill-enforced), so client boxes are never notified.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `60-zhc-early-warning-system/verify.sh` -> VERIFIED (every self-test / scanner / drill PASS, incl. all six new D-CONTEXT-USAGE drills + the client-box D5-boundary drill; the 4/4 live D-ALERT pipeline SKIPs offline — a deferred operator step, proven live at the canary). `py_compile` + `bash -n` clean.
