## [v19.24.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(anthology-engine): A1 S0 card-created-before-Drive + fail-soft mc_board mirrors + A2 daily reconcile (Skill 59)

Merges `fable-fix/onb-anthology-s0` off fresh origin/main (v19.23.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Wave-1 functional fix (judge 8.5). Scoped to `59-anthology-engine` (mc_board.py, the S0-S8 stage runners, provision-anthology-client.sh, ENGINE-MANIFEST.json + a new S0 test) plus the lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls and Skill 59's own skill-version.txt + SKILL.md frontmatter (0.1.2 -> 0.1.3). No client names, no secret values (the Command Center HMAC WEBHOOK_SECRET + Bearer MC_API_TOKEN are env-resolved, never literals), no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **A1 — the board card is mirrored at S0 BEFORE the holdable Drive step.** `stage_s0_intake.py` now runs the `mc_board ensure` card mirror ahead of the Drive-tree-provision step, fully fail-soft, so a held/dark Drive never suppresses the participant card. Every stage's TERMINAL `sync` mirror is likewise fail-soft (a board outage/refusal never holds a stage). Proven by the new `tests/test_stage_s0_card_before_drive.py`.
- **A2 — daily `reconcile` safety net.** `mc_board.py` gains a `reconcile` subcommand that re-projects EVERY ledger subject onto its board card (fail-soft per subject) so a card a stage's fail-soft swallow missed self-heals; it doubles as the one-time backfill. Registered on the daily tick (anthology-smoke-test run) and via `provision-anthology-client.sh` step 8 (`--no-deliver`). `mc_board` still NEVER emits 'done' (review->done stays owned by the QC scorer at >=8.5).
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `pytest 59-anthology-engine/tests` -> 122 passed; `anthology-smoke-test.py self-test` -> 46/46 passed (one non-blocking WARN: alert-dedup.py W2.4 not yet integrated — the alert is written to disk, gateway send deferred); `provision-anthology-client.sh --self-test` -> PASS (every provisioning failure mode force-observed, incl. the A2 cron single-tick + the 0600 secret non-leak). `py_compile` + `bash -n` clean.

## [v19.22.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(51): presentation intake-trace scanner no longer autofails compliant transcripts (E2, Skill 51)

Merges `fable-fix/onb-presentation` off fresh origin/main (v19.21.1) as the serial onboarding writer. Wave-1 functional fix (judge 9.0). Scoped to `51-signature-presentation/scripts/intake_trace_check.py` + a repo-root regression test (`tests/unit/presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh`) plus the lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls and Skill 51's own `skill-version.txt` (1.0.5 -> 1.0.6, G3). No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **E2 fix — the AF-INTAKE-BATCH scanner stops flagging compliant intake transcripts.** `intake_trace_check.py` now (a) EXEMPTS a canonical bank prompt asked verbatim (one long question is not a "batch"), and (b) only fires the generic BATCH-BY-QMARKS multi-question fallback when the turn matched ZERO bank prompts — so a legitimate one-question-per-turn signature intake no longer autofails. The real batch anti-patterns still fire: 8Q-style dumps -> BATCH-IN-TURN, the banned "ask a few quick questions" phrasing, a 2-questions-in-one-turn turn with no bank match, and a missing choice-first opener.
- **Gates green (raw, this QC).** `intake_trace_check.py --self-test` -> ALL PASS (7 cases, incl. all 49 canonical bank prompts passing verbatim one-per-turn AND the BATCH-BY-QMARKS fallback still catching a real 2-in-1 turn); `tests/unit/presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh` -> 88 passed / 0 failed; `51-signature-presentation/verify.sh` -> PASS (4 P-SP provers + engine wire-presence + library register-check). `py_compile` clean.

## [v19.20.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(podcast-engine): functionalize the 4 Facebook-ad workflows + bulletproof SMIQ capture + per-client FB-connect provisioning (Skill 58)

Rolls repo v19.19.1 -> v19.20.0. Merges `feat/podcast-fb-smiq-functional` off fresh origin/main (v19.19.1) as the serial onboarding writer. Scoped to `58-podcast-production-engine` (two new scripts, one new provision step, manifest SECTION K, skill-version 0.1.6 -> 0.1.7 with its frontmatter drift closed 0.1.4 -> 0.1.7) plus the lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls. No other agent's files touched; no operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values — the Firebase Web API key is the PUBLIC GHL SPA identifier already committed across Skills 06/44/58 (`verify-podcast-ghl-workflows.py`), and the Firebase refresh token is read from env and never printed. Both live gates exit 0: `verify-podcast-ghl-workflows.py` (required 4 still published/active) AND the new `verify-podcast-smiq.py`.

- **Ops pipeline built + every `create_opportunity` remapped OLD -> NEW.** Created `Podcast Interview System Pipeline` (5 stages: Lead Form Ad, Did Not Complete Interview, Completed Interview Survey Form, Podcast Episode Is Ready, Made A Purchase). Every `create_opportunity` step in the FB workflows (03 Lead Form Ad, 02/02a Did Not Complete, 07 Completed Survey) was remapped from the absent OLD pipeline to the NEW one; zero OLD pipeline id remains anywhere (re-GET verified, and asserted by the new gate).
- **Dead webhooks removed from 07 (17 -> 13 steps).** Removed the `hook.us1.make.com` node carrying the stray legacy `{"black woman focused":"yes"}` payload (flagged NOT-to-replicate in SECTION H), the two `n8n.apptime.me` per-style production webhooks (box-side production is owned in v2 by the required `02-Podcast Intake Submitted (Personal)` posting to the client-box Cloudflare tunnel), and 07's per-client FB add-to-custom-audience step (01a owns that). No `make.com` / `apptime.me` string remains; 07 stays published with both triggers retargeted and active.
- **FB workflows at the honest per-client template boundary (nothing fabricated).** The four FB-ad workflows (01a / 02 / 02a / 03) ship STRUCTURALLY CORRECT but DRAFT with the OLD Facebook ids BLANKED (`facebook_account_id` / `facebook_custom_audience_id` / `pixel_id` / `access_token` = "") so no other account's Facebook ids ship in a fleet template. Draft is required and correct here — GoHighLevel refuses to PUBLISH a workflow whose Facebook custom-audience/CAPI step is unconfigured, and these cannot run until a client connects Facebook. This is DELIBERATE per-client-activation state, NOT the original inactive-shell bug (the required 4 intake workflows remain published + active + committed, still gated by `verify-podcast-ghl-workflows.py`).
- **New per-client FB-connect activator + provision STEP 7.** `scripts/activate-podcast-fb-workflows.py` (READ then PUT): given the client's connected FB ids (`--fb-account` / `--fb-audience` / `--fb-pixel` / `--fb-token`) it fills the blanked FB steps and PUBLISHES the four workflows; with no ids it runs a DRY report. `provision-podcast-client.sh` STEP 7 (`fb-ads-connect`, opt-in via `PODCAST_FB_ADS_READY=1`) records the connect checklist and delegates to the activator, defaulting to a documented PENDING ledger step (never a provision blocker). The Facebook OAuth connection + Lead Form / Audience selection is done by the client in the GHL UI (inherently per-client), and the FB Lead Form trigger for 02/02a/03 is added in the builder because it must bind a live connected form.
- **SMIQ Tracker BULLETPROOFED (operator: "very important").** The recreated SMIQ Answer Tracker triggered on `contact.smiq_answers` — a field the podcast flow never writes — and its accumulator read `{{contact.smiq_answer}}`, a key present in NEITHER OLD nor NEW (a real typo), so a just-changed answer was dropped. Fixed live: `SMIQ Answer Tracker` now fires `contact_changed` ACTIVE on the canonical `contact.podcast_interview_smiq` (id `pTkurBfVPJOuiAv7HELI`), accumulator reads `{{contact.podcast_interview_smiq}}`, still self-appends and writes `contact.smiq_history` (id `i08poncoJMo6abdET5nI`) — no re-trigger loop (writes a different field). `05 Create Note SMIQ` gained a 2nd trigger so it fires on BOTH the interview and personal surveys. Both published + committed.
- **New live QC gate — `scripts/verify-podcast-smiq.py` (READ-ONLY).** Re-runnable fail-loud gate over the SMIQ capture, the FB-workflow template boundary, and the pipeline: asserts the SMIQ Tracker is published/committed and triggers on the canonical field with no dead `{{smiq_answer}}`; asserts 05 fires on both surveys; asserts the NEW pipeline exists with 5 stages; asserts each FB workflow is draft and carries NO other-account Facebook id / NO OLD pipeline id; asserts 07 carries no dead endpoint. Exit 0 = all PASS. `py_compile` clean.
- **SECTION K manifest record + open-decisions.** `PODCAST-SNAPSHOT-BUILD-MANIFEST.md` SECTION K documents the live after-state (SECTION J-2 marked SUPERSEDED, its pre-functionalization gap list retained as the record) and flags four OPEN DECISIONS for the operator (07 vs 02 personal-survey collision; 02/02a completion-tag semantics; unset `{{custom_values.podcast_survey_podcast_title}}`; 04a assigned-user) rather than guessing. This work lands AFTER the v1 golden snapshot and ships only on a v2 re-cut.
- **Skill 58 frontmatter/skill-version drift CLOSED + trailing-newline hygiene.** SKILL.md frontmatter `version:` (was 0.1.4) and `skill-version.txt` (was 0.1.6) are now both `0.1.7`, so `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version.sh` PASSES. `58/skill-version.txt` was also missing a trailing newline (a pre-existing defect inherited from origin/main that would have failed `skill-version-newline-guard` on this push and risked the documented `1.0.1v17.0.25`-style version-glue corruption) — fixed to end with `\n`.

## [v19.19.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(snapshot-provision): same-agency snapshot push + cross-agency share-link wiring — LIVE + verified end-to-end (Skill 58/59)

Merges `feat/snapshot-provision-automation`. Wires the n8n "Snapshot Provisioner (Podcast + Anthology)" webhook into onboarding and amends the Skill 59 snapshot doctrine from the old blanket "agency → subaccount auto-push is REJECTED" to the two-branch rule Trevor ordered 2026-07-10: SAME-AGENCY (the client's Convert and Flow location is a sub-account under BlackCEO's agency, companyId `DAD7unnJpNUFc36952Xp`) = AUTOMATED push; CROSS-AGENCY (client owns their own agency) = share link + manual import. No client names, no secret values, no Anthropic runtime identifiers in the diff.

- **`shared-utils/fire-provision-snapshot.sh` (new).** Best-effort, NON-BLOCKING helper that POSTs `/webhook/provision-snapshot` with the shared token resolved BY LABEL (`PROVISION_SNAPSHOT_TOKEN`, never printed) and location ids masked on the operator surface. Fail-open: on an unconfigured / unreachable / non-2xx webhook it prints the MANUAL-IMPORT fallback and records it in the per-client ledger, so onboarding is never worse than the pre-automation manual path. Always exits 0 (advisory); genuine completion stays box-side gated.
- **`59-anthology-engine/scripts/provision-anthology-client.sh` STEP 7.5a.** Fires `engine=anthology --tenancy same_agency` before the existing verify → custom-value-fill → version-stamp completion gate (kept as the real gate), with a 7.5b manual-import fallback for cross-agency clients.
- **`58-podcast-production-engine/scripts/provision-podcast-client.sh`.** Mirrored snapshot step fired before the box-side STEP 0 credential/field gate; fail-closed until `PODCAST_SNAPSHOT_ID` is set in n8n (webhook 409 → manual fallback recorded).
- **Doctrine (contract + guide).** `config/anthology-snapshot-contract.json`: new `provisioning_branches_note` + `authorized_mechanisms` (same-agency PUT push with `snapshot.override`, cross-agency share link); `rejected_mechanisms` rescoped to the CROSS-agency case only (still REJECTED). `references/anthology-snapshot-guide.md` Section 6 rewritten to the two-branch model. `qc-snapshot-contract.sh` still PASSES (the agency → subaccount cross-agency push remains recorded as REJECTED) and cleanly co-merged with main's v19.18.x `workflows.release_notifications` (eight release-notification workflows) additions.
- **GO-LIVE COMPLETED + VERIFIED (this release).** n8n workflow `ol9YLeCpvYdNsbsg` is ACTIVE and no longer fail-closed. Env set the same way the Anthology Drive-broker token was set — inline env on the `n8n-main` Deployment (namespace `n8n-stack`, native-Kubernetes n8n on the Hostinger host, NOT docker-compose): `PROVISION_SNAPSHOT_TOKEN` (256-bit, shared with the operator box) and `PODCAST_SNAPSHOT_ID` = `IEmFFkIngiskcfJk9MH6` ([SNAPSHOT] ZHC Podcast Engine); operator box holds `PROVISION_SNAPSHOT_TOKEN` + `PROVISION_SNAPSHOT_WEBHOOK_URL`. The Anthology snapshot id is hardcoded in the workflow (`QHJSnt13nIKcsXc5GNHL` = [SNAPSHOT] ZHC Anthology Engine July 10th, 2026) and verified current against the agency snapshot list. Raw proof: bad-token fire → HTTP 401; good-token podcast fire → HTTP 200 `accepted:true` (run 84494) with the push branch executing `HTTP — PUT Push Snapshot` → GHL 200 (previously 409 snapshot_not_configured); prior live anthology run (exec 84478) status=success, 23/23 nodes, ~21-min settle.

## [v19.18.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(podcast-engine): live QC gate for GHL workflow activation + build-state manifest §J (Skill 58)

Rolls repo v19.17.0 -> v19.18.0. Merges `feat/podcast-ghl-workflow-activation` off fresh origin/main (v19.17.0) as the serial onboarding writer. Scoped to `58-podcast-production-engine` (a new read-only QC gate script + manifest Section J) plus the lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls. No other agent's files touched; no operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values — the Firebase Web API key is the PUBLIC GHL SPA identifier already committed across Skills 06/44, and the Firebase refresh token is read from env and never printed (the gate prints only the env VAR NAMES on a miss).

- **New live QC gate — `58-podcast-production-engine/scripts/verify-podcast-ghl-workflows.py` (READ-ONLY).** Re-GETs the four required podcast workflows (01/02 survey-submission intake, 04 contact-changed episode-url, 06 contact-tag episode-ready) in the podcast TEMPLATE sub-account via the GHL internal rail (Firebase JWT — the same `backend.leadconnectorhq.com` rail Skill-44 `caf` uses) and ASSERTS each is `status=published`, has exactly one `active` trigger of the expected type, and carries at least one action step. Exit 0 = all four PASS; exit 1 = any FAIL/auth error. `--location` / `--token-env` overridable for per-client re-verification. WHY: the first podcast build shipped the four workflows as inactive shells with no triggers wired and nothing detected it — the operator had to add the triggers by hand; this gate makes that failure loud and re-runnable before a snapshot is cut. `py_compile` clean.
- **Manifest Section J build-state record.** `PODCAST-SNAPSHOT-BUILD-MANIFEST.md` gains SECTION J documenting the four live workflow ids/triggers (verified 2026-07-10), the proven recreation mechanism (GHL internal API — there is no public create-workflow endpoint), the activation subtlety (the `triggersChanged` commit `PUT` must re-assert `status:"published"` or it silently demotes the workflow to draft and de-activates every trigger — the exact defect that shipped the original inactive shells), and the per-client post-snapshot caveat (re-run the gate after each provision; snapshot imports frequently land workflows in DRAFT).
- **Skill 58 skill-version 0.1.5 -> 0.1.6** (G3 skill-content/version lockstep — bumped in the merge commit alongside the changed Skill-58 content).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.17.0 -> v19.18.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (all 11 version markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.18.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.17.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(06-ghl-survey): branch-convergence primitive for conditional intake surveys + token-seed live-nav fixes (survey builder v1.2.0 -> v1.3.0)

Rolls repo v19.16.1 -> v19.17.0. Scoped ENTIRELY to `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_survey_builder.py` (plus the lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls). No `58-podcast-production-engine` file touched (no overlap with the concurrent podcast-preflight work); no other agent's files; no operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

- **Branch-convergence primitive (the survey capability gap — CLOSED, proven offline).** A router survey (a radio "which style?" -> one branch slide per option) previously emitted ONLY forward router jumps. A respondent who entered one branch then fell through into every OTHER branch — all required — so the survey was UNSUBMITTABLE. New task-level `converge_slide` (a slide ref) makes the builder auto-derive ONE Jump-To rule per branch, OWNED by that branch's EXIT slide and conditioned on the earlier router field's value, jumping forward to the shared converge slide. New per-rule `owner_slide` override lets a rule live on a slide OTHER than the one hosting `if_field` (the cross-slide branch-exit jump). The topology validator now honors `owner_slide`, resolves `converge_slide`, and HARD-STOPs (preflight P4) any branch that falls through with no convergence jump. Fully backward compatible: with no `converge_slide` the emitted click-list is byte-for-byte identical to v1.2.0 (a dedicated self-test asserts it). 6 new self-tests including a respondent PATH-SIMULATION that proves each chosen style visits only its own branch + the shared tail + capture and CAN submit (never a sibling branch). `--selftest` PASS; F1 suite 16/16; py_compile clean.
- **Dry-run proof.** Both authored podcast-intake task-jsons re-run through `--dry-run`: preflight PASS, `blocked=False`, topology_valid. The Interview survey now emits 8 branch rules (4 router on the Style slide P2 + 4 convergence jumps on P3-P6, all -> P7 "Visual, SMIQ and Details"); the Personal survey emits 4 (2 router + 2 convergence P3/P4 -> P5). Every branch slide now carries a branch-exit jump to the shared converge slide.
- **Two token-seed live-path bugs fixed (discovered + proven LIVE).** Attempting the live build surfaced two pre-existing defects in the survey builder's browser path (repo-marked PENDING-LIVE-RUN, never previously executed). (1) Phase A did a full `open()` of the app URL — a full load re-runs GHL's SPA boot gate and bounces a TOKEN-SEEDED session to the login form, so Part 2 fail-softs through a login page and builds nothing. Ported the community/form builders' proven pattern: `_assert_logged_in` + `$router.push` in-app navigation, NEVER a full open/reload (inject-ghl-auth.sh "DO NOT RELOAD" doctrine). (2) `_eval` passed JS as a CLI arg, which `ghl_builder.browser_cmd` space-joins and `_run_cmd` `shlex.split`s — shredding any multi-token JS body (a `$router.push` payload returned no-response). Ported `eval --stdin` (JS piped via stdin), mirroring `ghl_form_builder._eval`. BOTH fixes proven live against the podcast-engine location: the seeded session now reaches the AUTHENTICATED location dashboard (screenshot evidence), and complex eval returns correct results.
- **LIVE end-to-end survey build honestly DEFERRED (not faked).** With auth + eval fixed the seeded session lands on the real authenticated dashboard, but the survey-builder-v2 Part-2 UI walk (Sites -> Surveys -> Add survey -> canvas -> object-field drag -> conditional-logic modal -> capture) was NEVER live-captured and does not match this location's live DOM (its left rail has no "Sites" item; every `wait` times out). Building the two surveys live requires a survey-builder-v2 selector-capture bring-up pass — a separate effort. REST verified the location's surveys total stayed 0 -> 0 (nothing partial created; fully clean). No selector was invented, no live "done" fabricated, no visible browser window opened (headless `--headed false` throughout), the agency subaccount was never touched.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.16.1 -> v19.17.0 via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (version markers in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.17.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.16.1]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(podcast-engine): install.sh credential-discovery no longer flags Podbean client_id/secret as a prerequisite — broker pair + Channel ID only

CREDENTIAL PROTECTION follow-up to v19.16.0 (Skill 58). v19.16.0 reframed the Podbean DOCTRINE (SKILL.md, PRD.md, both onboarding SOPs, podbean_publish.sh) and added broker-pair injection to `install.sh`, but it did NOT update the readiness/env-check that enumerates what a box must have. `install.sh :: discover_all_credentials()` still listed `PODBEAN_CLIENT_ID` + `PODBEAN_CLIENT_SECRET` (labeled "shared") in its `CRED_LIST`, so every broker-mode box (which correctly holds NO Podbean app secret) reported them as "Not configured yet" — the source of the operator-visible "Prerequisites … Podbean client_id, client_secret, podcast_id — Not found in your env" ask that violates the Channel-ID-only rule.

- **`install.sh` `discover_all_credentials()` CRED_LIST fixed.** Removed `PODBEAN_CLIENT_ID:Podbean App ID (shared)` and `PODBEAN_CLIENT_SECRET:Podbean App Secret (shared)` from the discovery/prerequisite scan. Added the broker-based requirement the client box actually needs: `PODBEAN_BROKER_WEBHOOK_URL` + `PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN` (broker mode). `PODBEAN_PODCAST_ID` (the per-client Podbean Channel ID) is retained — it is the ONLY Podbean value the client supplies and is not a secret. Stale `v10.13.10` comment replaced.
- **Operator-OWN-box path untouched.** The client_id/secret *injection* (from `OPENCLAW_PODBEAN_CLIENT_ID`/`_SECRET`, kept as the operator/legacy local fallback), the alias map, the value-shape validation regex, and `podbean_publish.sh`'s local `client_credentials` fallback all remain — the operator's own box can still mint locally. Only the client-facing readiness/discovery no longer *demands or reports* client_id/secret.
- **Item 7 (GHL field-map check) left correct.** `ghl_credential_gate.py` already builds the intake-field check against base `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com` with `Authorization: Bearer <PIT>` and `Version: 2021-07-28` (DEFAULT_API_BASE / API_VERSION_HEADER, `default_http_get`). The 403 pairing error is a token/pairing-validity matter, tracked separately — not a request-construction bug — so no change here.
- No secret value is printed, echoed, or grepped by any of the above.

## [v19.16.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(podcast-engine): Podbean credential broker - client is NEVER asked for client_id/secret; n8n token-broker injection; Channel-ID-only intake

CREDENTIAL PROTECTION (Skill 58). The Podcast Engine intake/onboarding no longer treats the Podbean OAuth app client_id/client_secret as the client's OWN credentials. BlackCEO HOSTS every client's show under his ONE Podbean account, so those app credentials are his SINGLE shared app - never the client's, never asked from the client, injected server-side by his n8n broker. The ONLY per-client Podbean value is the Podbean Channel ID (podcast_id), captured at onboarding and not a secret.

- **Root cause (named): pre-existing doctrine, NOT the field cleanup.** This session's field cleanup deleted 11 style/tone/thesis contact fields - none Podbean. No Podbean client_id/secret custom field or survey question ever existed. The "ask" lived only in the doctrine (SKILL.md, PRD.md, both onboarding SOPs, podbean_publish.sh), which mislabeled the shared app as "the client's OWN."
- **Doctrine reframed** across `58-podcast-production-engine/SKILL.md` (Step 15 + Per-client credentials), `project-prds/podcast-engine/PRD.md`, `universal-sops/podcast-craft/SOP-PODCAST-01-ENGINE-RUNBOOK.md`, and `SOP-PODCAST-02-CLIENT-ONBOARDING.md`: the client supplies ONLY the Channel ID; client_id/secret are the shared app, brokered by n8n, never on a client box.
- **`scripts/podbean_publish.sh` gains broker mode** (backward-compatible). When `PODBEAN_BROKER_WEBHOOK_URL` + `PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN` resolve (fleet default) it mints a Channel-scoped Podbean token from the n8n broker and keeps NO app secret on the box; otherwise it falls back to the local client_credentials mint (operator's OWN box only). `PODBEAN_PODCAST_ID` (Channel ID) is required in both modes. Secret values are never printed (broker token added to the redactor).
- **n8n token-broker asset shipped (dormant):** `58-podcast-production-engine/config/n8n/podbean-broker.workflow.json` + `config/n8n/README.md`, modeled on the Anthology Drive broker WITH a shared-token auth gate (`X-Podbean-Broker-Token` / `$env.PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN`) and a vault-stored Podbean Basic-Auth credential. Contract: `POST {action:"mint_token", podcast_id}` -> `{ok, access_token, expires_in, podcast_id}`. Manual import + one-time setup (the fleet n8n management API cannot write); stays inactive until the operator wires it.
- **`install.sh`:** injects the broker pair (`OPENCLAW_PODBEAN_BROKER_URL` + `OPENCLAW_PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN` -> `PODBEAN_BROKER_WEBHOOK_URL` + `PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN`, chmod 600) for client boxes so no Podbean app secret lands on them; the raw client_id/secret injection is kept as the operator/legacy fallback only.
- **Operator follow-ups (out of repo; need Trevor's own Podbean secret, not handled here):** import + activate the token-broker in n8n and set `PODBEAN_BROKER_TOKEN`. Separately, the existing LIVE full-publish workflow `create podcast episode from openclaw` (`/webhook/podbean-publish`) lacks an auth gate and stores creds in plaintext - harden in n8n.
- Verified: webhook suite 33/33 pass; four functional `--test` checks prove broker mode needs only the Channel ID (no app secret), local mode is the operator fallback, missing-both hard-stops, and no secret value is echoed.

## [v19.15.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(podcast-engine): E1 webhook mapper TRUE positional value-routing of survey answers by style

## [v19.14.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(cc-writeback): Command Center task-API write-back auth (401 fix) — onboarding docs + templates, CI guard, cc_board payload

Merges `integration/cc-writeback-fleet-train1` as the serial single onboarding writer, off fresh `origin/main` (v19.13.0). Closes the onboarding half of the Command Center "carded-but-trapped" 401 defect: dispatched department/persona agents finish work and write results back to the task API (`POST .../activities|deliverables|events|return-to-orchestrator`, `PATCH .../status`), but the CC is fail-closed and 401s any non-`ingest` write-back that omits `Authorization: Bearer $MC_API_TOKEN`, freezing finished work `in_progress` until a sweep discards it. Companion to the `blackceo-command-center` v5.1.0 server-side train (dispatcher auth injection + fail-loud preflight + sweep recovery + namespace fix).

### fix — write-back auth in every remaining example
- `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/presentations/SOUL.md` + `universal-sops/CLIENT-WEBINAR-DECK-SOP.md` (already landed at 474c1a45): `Authorization: Bearer $MC_API_TOKEN` on the deliverables/activities/status write-backs + a 401-trap note.
- `universal-sops/cross-dept-request-template.md` (`POST .../events`), `master-orchestrator-dept/SOP-01-Blocked-vs-Return.md` (`POST .../return-to-orchestrator`), `master-orchestrator-dept/SOP-07-Full-Funnel-Build-Orchestration.md` (`PATCH .../status`; the sibling `POST /api/tasks/ingest` is explicitly labelled HMAC-signed, NOT Bearer), `references/BLOCKED-IS-GATED.md`, and `AGENTS.md` N36 — bearer line + note added; the `ingest` HMAC path is never given a bearer.

### fix — installer secret mirror (474c1a45)
- `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh`: `cc_mirror_api_auth_to_agent_secrets()` mirrors `MC_API_TOKEN` + `WEBHOOK_SECRET` from the CC `.env.local` into the dept-agent runtime secrets env (0600, idempotent, preserves any existing value) so a dispatched agent's `$MC_API_TOKEN` resolves and its write-backs authenticate.

### enforcement — CI guard
- `scripts/guard-taskapi-auth-docs.sh` (NEW) + `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`: scans tracked `.md`/`.sh`/`.py` for write-back EXAMPLES an agent would copy and fails when the surrounding block carries no `Authorization`/`Bearer` mention. Exempts `/api/tasks/ingest` (HMAC), `CHANGELOG.md`, server source-path citations (`route.ts`/`[id]`), and `.py`/`.sh` files that delegate to the signed board client (`_cc_board`/`_post_json`/`mc_route`). A negative-fixture CI step proves the guard bites (planted unauthenticated example → exit 1, passes once removed).

### fix — cc_board.py deliverable payload drift
- `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/cc_board.py` `register_deliverable()`: the old `{"url","meta"}` body 400s against the CC deliverables Zod schema; now sends `{"deliverable_type":"url","title":<meta.title|slug|type|"Artifact URL">,"path":<url>}` with `meta` folded into `description`. Signature/auth/returns unchanged; new selftest case 13 captures + asserts the schema-correct payload (`--selftest` PASS).

### bundled
- `scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh` + `scripts/apply-routing-fix.sh` (474c1a45): drop the over-broad bare `present`/`pitch` triggers from the presentation routing reflex (`pitch deck`/`presentation deck` still route correctly).

Scope: CC-401 onboarding auth files + the guard + cc_board payload — ZERO overlap with the Area-5 survey-builder train (`ghl_survey_builder.py`, landed at v19.12.0) or the podcast E1 train (v19.13.0). Repo-only merge; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary the operator's own box first). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

## [v19.13.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(podcast-engine): E1 webhook mapper answer-key alias-map fill (survey_answer_keys_by_style)

Merges `fix/podcast-e1-answer-key-aliases` as the serial single onboarding writer, off fresh `origin/main` (v19.12.0). Scoped ENTIRELY to `58-podcast-production-engine/scripts/webhook/aliases.json` (data), `58-podcast-production-engine/scripts/webhook/mapper.py` (self-test coverage only, no algorithm change), and the skill's own `SKILL.md` + `skill-version.txt` version stamp (plus the standard lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls repo-wide). No other department, no CC-401 onboarding auth/SOUL files, no Area-5 survey-builder files touched; ZERO overlap with the other in-flight trains on this branch (CC-401 onboarding, Area-5 survey branching already landed at v19.12.0, live-capture selectors). Repo-only merge; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

- **Companion gap E1 closed** — `scripts/webhook/aliases.json` shipped with `survey_answer_keys_by_style` empty for all four writing styles. Filled with the REAL legacy GHL custom-field keys (`PODCAST-SNAPSHOT-BUILD-MANIFEST.md` Section A Group 2, byte-for-byte, double underscore after `survey`), verified against `config/questionnaires/*.json` (`path_shape.per_style_content_fields` plus each question's `survey_position`/`ghl_internal_label`) and against the mapper's only consumer (`required_missing()`), in the exact positional order: Counter Intuitive `podcast_survey__barry_q1, podcast_survey__barry_q6`; Vulnerable `podcast_survey__brene_q1, podcast_survey__brene_q6`; Provocative `podcast_survey__dan_q1, podcast_survey__dan_q2, podcast_survey__dan_q7`; Passionate `podcast_survey__jia_q1, podcast_survey__jia_q6, podcast_survey__jia_q7`. These are the real API field keys, not the Barry/Brene/Dan/Jia internal labels, which never appear in any output.

- **Required-slot gate now correctly sized per style** — `required_missing()` sizes the required-answer-slot gate from `len(survey_answer_keys_by_style[style])`; previously every style silently fell back to the 1-slot `required_answer_slots_default` because the lists were empty, under-requiring Vulnerable/Counter Intuitive (really 2 slots) and Provocative/Passionate (really 3). Filling the real keys corrects the required count per style; the existing last-slot transparency-answer fallback is unchanged and still covers the final slot.

- **Tests** — 8 new `mapper.py --self-test` assertions pin the exact per-style key lists and prove the corrected required-slot behavior (partial answers correctly `needs_input` the right missing slot; complete answers map; the last-slot transparency fallback still works). 2 pre-existing self-test fixtures (Make.com/vulnerable, n8n/provocative) that were only supplying 1 of the style's real answer count, and had been silently passing `mapped` only because of the under-requiring bug, are corrected to supply a complete submission for their style.

- **Discrepancy found and reported, not silently patched** — `required_missing()` uses ONLY the list length, not the key strings, to size the required-slot gate. No code path (`mapper.py` or `intake_handler.py`) yet routes an inbound field literally named e.g. `podcast_survey__barry_q1` into the canonical `q1_answer` slot via `field_aliases`/`resolve_fields()` — there is no `field_aliases` entry for any of these real GHL keys. True positional VALUE routing (not just correct required-count sizing) needs a two-pass mapper resolution (resolve style first, then pull only that style's N keys) to avoid colliding with a blank same-position field from an unselected sibling style; that is a `mapper.py` algorithm change, out of scope for this `aliases.json` data-fill task (E1) and NOT attempted here. Flagged as a follow-up.

- **Offline gate battery GREEN** — `mapper.py --self-test` 33/33 (25/25 plus 2 regressions surfaced and fixed by the correct required-count, now 33/33); full `scripts/webhook/run-selftests.sh` (mapper/job_key/ledger/flow_client/intake_handler self-tests, `py_compile`, `aliases.json`/`route-template.json5` structure) ALL PASS; independent `tests/webhook` pytest suite (`spec_reference` oracle, unaffected) 33 passed; `py_compile` clean; `qc-assert-no-client-names` structural PASS; `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version` PASS (58 now `0.1.3` == `0.1.3`); `qc-assert-skill-version-newline` PASS.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.12.0 -> v19.13.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`, all agree). Skill 58 rolled `0.1.2` -> `0.1.3` (`SKILL.md` frontmatter + `skill-version.txt` in lockstep). Annotated tag `v19.13.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.12.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(ghl-skill6): task-driven survey branching topology + per-branch slide layout (Area 5)

Merges `fix/area5-survey-branching-topology` as the serial single onboarding writer, off fresh `origin/main` (v19.11.0). Area 5 of the FLEET-FIX master spec. Scoped ENTIRELY to `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_survey_builder.py` (plus the standard lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls repo-wide). No Anthology (58/59/60/61), Podcast, Presentation, or Command-Center-auth file touched; ZERO overlap with the in-flight CC-401 onboarding train (auth templates / SOULs / run-full-install). Repo-only merge; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary the operator's own box first). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

- **Root defect closed** — the survey builder could only ever build the ONE hardcoded 2-rule "attended events" demo (`CONDITIONAL_LOGIC_RULES`): the THINK-phase plan embedded the constant regardless of task, branching was applied only to a hardcoded "Slide 2", the layout was rigidly one-field-per-slide, and `main()`/CLI never threaded task branching/layout. Branching + slide layout are now TASK-DRIVEN end-to-end.

- **Task-driven branching (5.1)** — new `task['conditional_logic']` (a list of `{if_field, if_value, then_slide}` jump-to rules) OVERRIDES the demo constant ENTIRELY; the constant is retained only as the documented no-task fallback (`_resolve_conditional_rules`). A new `P4:topology_valid` preflight HARD STOP validates every `if_field`, every `then_slide`, and every slide placement (unknown / duplicate / orphan field) BEFORE any browser action — a dangling reference emits ZERO browser commands, never a mid-build failure.

- **Per-branch slide layout (5.2)** — new optional `task['slides']` layout: a slide may carry ONE OR MORE fields and a branch may target ANY slide (no longer one-field-per-slide, no longer Slide-2-only). Omit it for the linear default, which is byte-for-byte backward compatible with every existing caller.

- **Generalized live phases (5.3)** — the slide model + normalized rules thread through the THINK plan (`_plan_slides` / `_normalize_conditional_logic` / `_validate_topology`) AND the live phases: Phase G (`_p2_conditional_logic`) groups rules by OWNER slide (the slide that hosts `if_field`) and navigates there instead of a hardcoded "Slide 2"; Phases E/F/H/J (`_p2_pull_object_fields` / `_rename_question_slides` / `_required_toggles` / `_capture_slide`) drive off the planned slide model (multi-field-per-slide capable, real capture-slide index); the dry-run `_emit_click_list` reflects the same planned slides + normalized rules. Consistent with the merged Skill 6 v19.11.0 topology work — `ghl_object_router.py` rails and `ghl_receipts.py` reducers untouched; the F1/F2 GET-first / map-only field-reuse posture (`field_creation` modes) untouched. `SURVEY_BUILDER_VERSION` v1.1.0 -> v1.2.0.

- **CLI threaded (5.4)** — `main()` gains `--task-json <file>`: a full task payload (survey_fields + conditional_logic + slides + copy_persona + business_name + campaign + welcome_copy) merged OVER the CLI flags. This is how a multi-branch survey is built from the CLI. Unblocks the two podcast conditional surveys (Interview Q1 -> 4-branch, Personal Q1 -> 2-branch).

- **Tests + proof (5.5)** — 5 new `_selftest()` cases: task rules land normalized in the plan (canonical `if_field`, owner slide, `Pn -` target; `conditional_logic_source=task`); dangling `if_field` and dangling `then_slide` each fail preflight at `P4:topology_valid`; a multi-field slide layout emits the right drag/branch click-list; `--task-json` round-trip. The pre-existing demo-constant path stays green. Dry-run proof against a 4-branch fixture: the plan's `conditional_logic` == the task's 4 rules, all owned by `P2 - Stage`, targeting P3/P4/P5/P6; the negative dangling-`then_slide` fixture HARD-STOPs at preflight and writes ZERO browser commands.

- **Offline gate battery GREEN** — full `06-ghl-install-pages` pytest **1138 passed / 15 skipped** (no regression); `ghl_survey_builder.py --selftest` PASS (18 cases); `py_compile` clean; no `.sh` touched (`bash -n` N/A); no-client-names + no-secret-printing clean (structural); repo-consistency gate PASS (28 depts, 10 dimensions, VERSION-MARKERS + CONTENT-HASH OK at v19.12.0 — no role-library / universal-sops content touched, so no restamp).

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.11.0 -> v19.12.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`, all agree). Annotated tag `v19.12.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.11.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(ghl-skill6): integrate the Skill 6 GHL browser-control BULLETPROOF build (7 units)

Merges `skill6-bulletproof-build` as the serial single onboarding writer, off fresh `origin/main` (v19.10.0). Integrates the seven Skill-6 units built in isolated worktrees against the `SKILL-6-BROWSER-CONTROL-BULLETPROOF-SPEC-v1` foundation, scoped entirely to `06-ghl-install-pages/` (plus the standard lockstep version markers `bump-version.sh` rolls repo-wide). No Anthology (58/59/60/61), Podcast, or Presentation file touched; no other agent's files. Repo-only merge; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary the operator's own box first). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

- **The bulletproofing units, all landed as gate-green CODE** — (U1/F1+F2) the survey builder's #1 defect closed: killed in-browser custom-field auto-create; new `field_creation` posture defaults to `api` (Skill-44 GET-first dependency plan, reuse-or-create-missing), Part-2 binds map-only against the read-back key list, plus a per-field `key_policy` contract (`zhc` idempotent vs `verbatim` engine-owned, create-REFUSED, blocks loudly). (U7) new `ghl_object_router.py` encodes the §3 rail matrix as tested data+code — 18 object types, ordered fallbacks, token-context routing (F9), 429-never-tier-hops (F8), mandatory `[GHL tier used]` disclosure. (U5/F6) new `ghl_receipts.py` per-object receipt store+reducer — "no receipt = not created", `assert_consistent` anti-fabrication guard closing the "30/30 PASS next to 1/6 live" shape. (F4) route-aware asymmetry — `REST_IN_BROWSER` browser-only enforcement, `services.*` real-UA retry on 403 (Cloudflare-1010 false-403). (U2/F3) new `ghl_selector_canary.py` + `selectors-live.json` — 63 anchors with ordered fallback chains, a STOP-with-snapshot resolver (never invents CSS), and a read-only weekly drift scan. (F5) uniform 30-min keepalive + pre-phase token re-mint wired across the survey AND form builders (was a dead import). (§4) `ENV-MATRIX.md` + `browser_manager.py` durable-root Python mirror, and a REAL live-reproduced bash-3.2 `mapfile` crash fixed in `parallel_saves.sh`. (U9/§7) Command Center / Kanban tightenings — multi-deliverable driver, QC-score emission into the card, failure-taxonomy prefixes, queue visibility. (U11-U15) new `ghl_community_builder.py` / `ghl_course_builder.py` (+channels) with per-object/per-lesson receipts and QC gates, written D8-safe: complete live code that STOPs at the first un-captured selector rather than inventing one.

- **Integration hardening (auto-fixed at merge)** — `qc-built-funnel.sh` made bash-3.2 parseable: a pre-existing (shipped on `origin/main`) SEO/media pre-gate captured a python heredoc INSIDE `$(...)` command substitution with nested double-quotes — the classic bash-3.2 command-substitution parser bug, the only `.sh` in the skill dir to fail `/bin/bash -n`. Restructured to run the heredoc as a plain command writing stdout to a temp file, then capture via a simple command substitution (behaviour-identical; the python signals only via `*FAIL*` stdout text). Now parses clean under `/bin/bash` 3.2.57, bash 5.x, and zsh.

- **Offline gate battery GREEN** — full `06-ghl-install-pages` pytest **1138 passed / 15 skipped** (the 2 `test_browser_manager_singleton` "failures" are this operator box's real durable `agent-browser-abortloc.BLOCKED` park marker, proven identical on pristine `origin/main` and proven to PASS under a clean `$HOME` — environmental, not code); all module + QC-gate selftests PASS; `py_compile` clean; `bash -n` clean under bash 3.2 + bash 5 + zsh; no-client-names PASS (structural); no-secret-printing PASS; repo-consistency gate PASS (28 depts, 10 dimensions, VERSION-MARKERS + CONTENT-HASH OK at v19.11.0).

- **LIVE capture-then-build honestly deferred (not faked)** — the §9/§5 live proof of communities/courses/channels (create scratch object → walk → capture selectors → DELETE, zero residue) and the VPS live run are externally blocked on this box: no refresh token (`auth_path:none`, so no GHL session can be seeded at all), an operator-set durable `workforce-build.parked` marker (un-park is operator-only), and 39 concurrent agent-browser processes saturating the serial singleton. The new builders ship as D8-capture-pending-safe code with the exact resume procedure documented in `SELECTORS-LIVE-community.md` / `SELECTORS-LIVE-course.md`; no selector was invented, no live "done" was fabricated, and no client production object was touched.

## [v19.10.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(presentations): integrate the Presentation Department end-to-end fix (7 units)

Merges `presentation-dept-build` as the serial single onboarding writer, off fresh `origin/main` (v19.9.0). Integrates the seven Presentation Department fix units built in isolated worktrees against the reconciled foundation: **promptgate, charcount, notes, intakegate, typepicker, ccstatus, miniapp**. Scoped entirely to the Presentations department — `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/presentations/**` + its `scripts/`, the presentation `universal-sops/` (PIPELINE-MANIFEST, CLIENT-WEBINAR-DECK-SOP, the new PRESENTATION-MASTER-DOCTRINE), Skill 51 (signature presentation), Skill 46 (kie relay image path), and Skill 07 (kie-setup model default). No Anthology (58/59/60/61) or Podcast file touched; no other agent's files. Repo-only merge; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary the operator's own box first). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

- **The six DONE-definition items, all landed as CODE** — (1) the extracted shared `prompt_gate.py` (9,000–18,000-char floor + structural/negative/spelling/density blocks + real ENGLISH pin + mode-consistency + aspect verification + OCR readback) is now on EVERY image path: always-on full-floor in `build_deck.py` (the canonical renderer) and imported by the shared side-doors `kie_generate.py` (both copies) + `46-kie-callback-relay/kie-slide-submitter.js` (full floor opt-in via `KIE_PROMPT_GATE=presentations` to preserve Skill 06/47/49/59/video behavior); standalone `prove_pres_prompt_floor.py` prover wired into `presentations-lockstep.yml`. (2) The first-ever code-enforced per-slide copy char band — `_chk_copy_density` / `AF-COPY-BAND` (HEADLINE 12–60, SUBHEAD 20–110, KICKER ≤40, BULLETS ≤3 each 8–30, SLIDE TOTAL 40–180 / hook-exempt), floor AND ceiling, first-party preflight (no `QC_FOREIGN_SIGNATURES` collision); the 3-way bullet conflict reconciled and the dangling c18–c24 QC enumeration defined. (3) Speaker notes reordered — new `P9.5-NOTES-SYNC` phase re-injects the QC-passed presenter speech AFTER it exists, plus a real `AF-EMPTY-NOTES-PANE` delivery gate (`_chk_notes_pane`) and unified speech filenames. (4) One-question turn-gate proven in BOTH standard and signature modes (signature `--signature --next/--answer` walks 10 questions one at a time into a separate ledger; `AF-INTAKE-BATCH` given a real `intake_trace_check.py` scanner; the 27-question prose bank migrated into the driver). (5) One canonical `presentation_type` type-picker at intake order 0 derives all four legacy axes (deck_type / creation_mode / presentation_mode / audience_mode) via `derive_legacy_fields()`, closing the AF-MODE-UNSET signature ambiguity. (6) The producer now STOPS at `review` (never self-closes `done`) and carries real per-gate QC scores into the Command Center payloads, handing `review→done` promotion to the CC-side QC scorer / Devil's-Advocate gate every sibling department already respects; strict-server 422 fallback keeps the transition alive. Plus the hosted one-question-per-screen intake mini-app (Cloudflare Pages + Worker + D1) as a pure-addition front-end to the existing state machine.

- **Integration reconciliation (auto-fixed at merge)** — the `typepicker` and `intakegate` units both rewrote `deck-intake-driver.py` + `deck-intake-questions.json`. Reconciled into ONE conditional-skip entry point (`auto_skip_all_conditionals`, covering both the `conditional_on` type-picker follow-ups and the `ask_if` question-bank follow-ups, disjoint and idempotent) wired at all four command sites; merged both file descriptions; kept the `legacy_field_mapping` table with the migrated 40-question bank (order-0 picker, no orphan `deck_type` question). The locked quartet (`build_deck.py` + `PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json` + `SOP-SLIDE-00` + `test_preflight.py`) was 3-way-merged additively (manifest v24: 25 phases / 152 autofails / 37 roles), the `CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN.sha256` regenerated ONCE over the integrated `build_deck.py`+`run_signature_deck.py`, and the stale miniapp payload `deck_type`→`presentation_type` order-0 assertions corrected.

## [v19.6.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(loop-protection-system): land Skill 61 (Loop Protection System) with both QC-blocking defects fixed

Merges `skill-61-loop-protection-v2` as the serial single onboarding writer. Adds the **Loop Protection System (Skill 61)** under `61-loop-protection-system/`: the fleet's deterministic, ZERO-model-call, host-level reflex arc against crash-loops and idle token furnaces — it runs OUTSIDE every OpenClaw session so it survives the very wedges it treats, adds the RESPOND / PROTECT / HEAL layers Skill 60 deliberately omits, consumes Skill 60's ledger read-only, and contributes nothing client-visible. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary on the operator box first, then HOLD for operator timing — a repo merge is not a roll). Additive: the only paths touched outside the new skill dir are the 11 lockstep version markers rolled by `scripts/bump-version.sh`. No operator PII, no client names (fixtures synthetic), no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values. Independent adversarial re-QC scored 9.2/10 (pass threshold 8.5) with both blocking defects empirically fixed.

- **Defect 1 fix — the flagship RESPOND act is now wired (`loop_watchdog.py`).** The armed watchdog tick called `KC.apply(..., executors={})`, so an armed tick over an LP-B1/LP-B2 restart storm hit "no executor → refused+escalate" instead of parking the unit. The tick now wires the ONE safe in-tick mechanical act — the process-breaker trip+park (LF-6: STOP + park, visible-red, never respawns) — for a CONFIRMED P1 loop only: armed ⇒ `lf6_park_process` trips the process breaker AND parks the unit in one tick; DRY_RUN ⇒ plans and mutates nothing (the D-DRYRUN byte-identical invariant still holds). Every config-touching kill card (LF-1/2/4/5/7) stays plan-only in the unattended tick; escalation stays an ADD-ON (the P1 operator alert + Tier-3 / healer-breaker), never a substitute for the park. New drill **D-ARMED-PARK** runs a full armed tick over the restart-storm fixture and asserts `parked_units` non-empty AND the process breaker tripped (the whole tick pipeline, not the isolated predicate).

- **Defect 2 fix — the operator one-line revert is now real (`loop_breaker.py`, `loop_killcards.py`, `loop_common.py`, `loop-companion.sh`, `loop_ledger.py`).** `loop_breaker.py` had no CLI beyond `--self-test`, the companion dropped the `park`/`unpark` verb, `revert_command_for` emitted a no-op, and `fix`/`approve` were print-only stubs (spec 4.2: "a fix that cannot be reverted in one line does not ship"). Now: `loop_breaker.py` has a real CLI (`park <unit>` / `unpark <unit>` / `unpark --finding <id>` / `status`) with finding→unit resolution via the new `ledger.get_finding()`; the companion passes the verb and routes `fix`/`approve` to `loop_killcards`; `run_fix()` executes the config-FREE process park (LF-6) for real against the ledger and honestly PREPARES (never auto-applies off-box) every config-touching class — documented as a deliberate scope in `SKILL.md`, not a stub claiming success. `revert_command_for` now emits a command that actually reverts. New drill **D-REVERT** records a real finding, `fix`es it (parks for real), then executes the EMITTED one-line `unpark --finding <id>` through the real companion entry and asserts the unit is unparked.

- **Gates green (fresh worktree off `origin/main`, local run):** `py_compile` on all 9 `.py` + `bash -n` on all 8 `.sh` clean; every per-script `--self-test` PASS (aggregate ALL PASS, incl. the new `run_fix` case); `verify.sh` drill battery VERIFIED — 11 drills incl. the two new (D-ARMED-PARK, D-REVERT); the four merge-gate scanners (`guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py`, `scan-no-secrets.sh`, `scan-no-client-identifiers.sh`, `scan-no-json-exports.sh`) exit 0 over the tree; `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py --only artifact` rc 0 (10 dimensions OK, Skills 60 AND 61 registered, 61 total). Preserves the DRY_RUN-first / disable-never-delete / park-never-kill / refuse-root / never-print-secret doctrine intact.

- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.5.0 -> v19.6.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`, verified). The role-library content-manifest was untouched (no `_index.json`-tracked file changed — Skill 61 edits are self-contained), so no content-hash restamp was needed. Annotated tag `v19.6.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.5.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(anthology-engine): Convert and Flow snapshot import mechanism + CI drift gate (Skill 59)

Touches only `59-anthology-engine/` (disjoint from every other train, so it merged clean onto v19.4.0). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values. Independent adversarial QC scored 9.4/10 (pass threshold 8.5) with zero blocking defects.

- **Docs-truth fix (`c2a16962`)** — corrects the false "auto-provisioned pipeline" claims across every Skill 59 surface (`INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `MASTERDOC.md` §11, `REPAIRS.md` D9, `config/field-map.json` pipeline `$note`, `config/engine-config.template.json` `auto_provision_pipeline:true` → `pipeline_find_and_bind:true`, `scripts/anthology_registry.py` header/argparse, `ENGINE-MANIFEST.json` rows n:3 and n:30). GoHighLevel exposes NO API to create a pipeline — pipelines are UI-only — so the standard `Anthology Engine` pipeline must pre-exist (snapshot-shipped or hand-built once in the UI); onboarding FINDS it BY EXACT NAME through the client's OWN private integration token and BINDS to it, STOPPING setup with `AF-AE-PIPELINE-UI-CREATE` if absent.

- **Snapshot import mechanism + drift gate (`0e995142`)** — new `scripts/anthology_snapshot.py`, a Skill-38-style MANUAL-IMPORT path: `verify-imported` proves READ-ONLY that the operator's import landed (the pipeline exists BY NAME with all 9 stages, and all 28 custom fields exist BY KEY); `provision-custom-values` idempotently fills the 4 location custom values (GET-check → create-only-missing / update-in-place), with the intake hook secret resolved BY LABEL and NEVER printed; `stamp-version` / `plan` / `self-test` run fully offline. New CI drift gate `scripts/qc-snapshot-contract.sh` cross-checks the shipped fixture `config/anthology-snapshot-contract.json` against `field-map.json` and `engine-config.template.json` — 28 fields (27 LARGE_TEXT + 1 SINGLE_OPTIONS), cover options, the 9 exact stages, 8 tag slugs (3 LIVE), the forms contract, and the standing-REJECTED agency→subaccount auto-push — and is proven to go RED on injected drift. The provisioner gains step 7.5 (verify-import → fill custom-values → stamp-version) plus `--producer-email` / `--public-hostname` flags; the stale "19 fields" banner is corrected to 28. Adds `references/anthology-snapshot-guide.md` and CafClient location custom-VALUE methods. The live network paths (cut / import / verify against a real location) remain correctly deferred to the operator canary.

## [v19.4.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(zhc-early-warning-system): land Skill 60 (ZHC Early Warning System) — the fleet's deterministic, zero-model sentinel

Adds the **ZHC (Zero Human Company) Early Warning System (Skill 60)** under `60-zhc-early-warning-system/`: a deterministic, ZERO-model-call sentinel that runs on every fleet box and alerts the OPERATOR (never the client) the moment the machine breaks or drifts. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision (canary on the operator box first, then HOLD for operator timing — a repo merge is not a roll). Additive: the only paths touched outside the new skill dir are the 11 lockstep version markers rolled by `scripts/bump-version.sh`. No operator PII, no client names (all fixtures synthetic / example.com), no Anthropic runtime identifiers, no secret values.

Built to the nine LOCKED operator decisions D1–D9: one skill dir holding BOTH the per-box sentinel and the companion audit/install/verify/troubleshoot surface (D1); safety-cap enforcement is ALERT-ONLY fleet-wide, auto-undo (`enforce_caps`) ships as code but defaults OFF everywhere as a per-box opt-in only (D2); a 15-minute per-box tick and hourly operator-box aggregator, zero token cost (D3); an unacknowledged P1 escalates to Rescue Rangers after 30 minutes and a box silent for 2 aggregator cycles is a P1 "sentinel dark" (D4); the context running-low warning routes to the box's OWN agent to self-handle while the operator is alerted ONLY for the broken-config crash case (D5); install is a normal skill + one cron with no deep gateway plugin coupling (D6); snapshot retention is the larger of 60 snapshots or 45 days (D7); the self-update cadence starts WEEKLY and stays PINNED weekly, never auto-downshifting — the recommender surfaces an advisory note only (D8); and the furnace/idle-burn signal is on-box-first with a read-only provider-balance escalation and ingests each box's billing model to frame wasted-token impact correctly in BOTH subscription/allowance (usage consumed) and pay-per-token/metered (dollars spent) billing (D9).

- **Ten detection signals** — S1 model/provider config drift; S2 runtime fallback from trajectory ground truth; S3 context-vs-compaction with the SUBTRACTIVE `softThresholdTokens` arithmetic (broken-config P1 to the operator, running-low to the box's own agent); S4 the never-silently safety-cap raise (pinned baseline + approval stamp); S5 furnace/idle-burn (billing-aware); S6 config-write hygiene + root-owner freeze detection; S7 dark surfaces; S8 secret-shape leak (value-free, file:line + class only); S9 skills integrity / stale downgrade; S10 cron/delivery drift. Full catalog in `docs/SIGNAL-CATALOG.md`.
- **Reuse, not rebuild** — tails the native `~/.openclaw/logs/config-audit.jsonl` event stream; reuses the Skill 58 alert-dedup + guard-cron-inventory patterns, the Skill 59 four-scanner merge-gate family (`guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py`, `scan-no-secrets.sh`, `scan-no-client-identifiers.sh`, `scan-no-json-exports.sh`, 0/1/2/3/4 exit contract), the single-writer SQLite-WAL ledger pattern, and `scripts/skill-content-hash.sh` (Skill 60 is auto-picked-up as a hashed dir).
- **Backed-up and reversible** — every config write is snapshotted (0600, outside any repo, never quoted) with a platform-aware one-line revert command (`-u node` on VPS) placed in the operator's hand; `ews_revert.py` restores AS THE BOX USER (hard-refuses root), validates the subtractive arithmetic BEFORE writing, and reads back byte-for-byte.
- **Operator-only, client-silent, structurally** — all sends route through the box's own gateway to the operator account with dedup + storm cap; a client chat is never a recipient or fallback; the operator-box aggregator ships a dead-man switch for boxes too broken to self-report.
- **Gates green (fresh local run)** — every per-script `--self-test` PASS; the four merge-gate scanners exit 0 over the tree (each with its own self-test proving the detector fires); `verify.sh` drill battery VERIFIED (D-S1/S2, D-S3 with the D5 routing split, D-S4 stamp-honor, D-S6 root-owner, D-S8 value-free, D-S10, D-REVERT byte-identical, D-DEADMAN); frontmatter version `0.1.0` == `skill-version.txt`; content-hash picks up `60-zhc-early-warning-system`.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.3.0 -> v19.4.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`, verified `--check`). Annotated tag `v19.4.0` cut on the release commit.

## [v19.3.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  fix(cc-tunnel): shared-tunnel wrong-port / CF-1303 no-route guard on the Command Center ingress (Skills 32/38 + shared-utils)

Merges `fix/cc-tunnel-wrong-port` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values. Touches only Skills 32/38, `shared-utils/`, `n8n-workflows/` docs, and `tests/unit/` — disjoint from the concurrent Anthology Engine (Skill 59) train, so it merged clean onto v19.2.0.

Root-cause fix for "the Command Center public link 502s, and some boxes throw Cloudflare **1303 no-route**." The fleet runs ONE cloudflared tunnel per box carrying MULTIPLE hostnames on a SINGLE ingress array (CC dashboard -> `:4000`, OpenClaw gateway `…-hooks` -> `:18789`, podcast `…-podcast` -> `:4010`). Cloudflare's `PUT /cfd_tunnel/<id>/configurations` REPLACES the whole array, so any writer that emitted a freshly-built array (a "full-replace") silently DELETED every sibling rule it did not re-list — dropping the CC's `:4000` rule (=> CF 1303) or re-pointing the CC host at the gateway `:18789` (=> 502, the "wrong localhost port" symptom).

- **New shared library `shared-utils/cc-tunnel-ingress.sh`** — the single authoritative source for the CC ingress port (`CC_INGRESS_PORT=4000`, overridable only for tests) plus the anti-clobber helpers every ingress writer must use: `cc_ingress_merge` (GET -> merge -> PUT: preserve every existing hostname rule, (re)write only our host, keep exactly one `http_status:404` catch-all last) and `cc_ingress_assert_cc_port` (fail-loud guard that refuses a config in which the CC host is absent — the 1303 signature — or on a non-`:4000` port — the 502 signature). Pure bash + jq, network-free, bash-3.2-safe.
- **Skill 38 `13-create-cloudflare-tunnel.sh` — full-replace -> GET/merge/PUT** — the gateway tunnel writer now GETs the current `/configurations`, merges its own `…-hooks -> :18789` host while preserving all sibling rules, and runs an inline fail-loud guard (`CC-INGRESS-GUARD FAIL`, exit 7) that refuses to PUT any config leaving a Command Center host on a non-`:4000` port. CC hosts are identified by EXCLUDING the known sibling suffixes (`-hooks`/`-podcast`), so hyphenated client slugs are never false-flagged.
- **Skill 32 `create-tunnel.sh` — loud wrong-port/no-route diagnosis** — after provisioning it probes the local CC port (`:4000`) and the public URL; when the dashboard is UP locally but the public link returns 502/1033/1303/530/503 it now fails LOUD (exit 7) naming the wrong-port/no-route condition and the operator repair, instead of a soft "not live yet" warning. A warm-up retry/backoff on the public probe prevents a transient post-`tunnel run` edge blip from false-flagging.
- **`n8n-workflows/command-center-register-v4.md` — v5 merge recipe (doc only, HELD FOR OPERATOR GO)** — documents the same GET/merge/PUT + `:4000` guard for the live n8n registration workflow. Explicitly NOT applied to the live workflow (`i0P3OWCEsXZxVo0N` still runs v4) until the operator elects to roll it.
- **Regression lock `tests/unit/cc-tunnel-ingress-guard.test.sh`** — hermetic (jq-only) suite proving merge preserves siblings, the guard fails loud on both the dropped-rule (1303) and wrong-port (502) signatures, the Skill-38 writer no longer emits a bare full-replace, the shipping inline guard jq does not false-flag hyphenated slugs or sibling hosts, and the CC port literal (`4000`) is identical across the lib and every writer (`run-full-install.sh` `DASHBOARD_PORT`, `create-tunnel.sh`, the Skill-38 guard). All assertions pass.

Annotated tag `v19.3.0` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).
