## [v19.2.0]  -  2026-07-10  -  feat(anthology-engine): S9 finale+transitions, Gate B envelope, editable-doc delivery, n8n Drive credential broker (Skill 59)

Integration release that forward-merges `integration/anthology-engine` onto `main`, completing the Anthology Engine (Skill 59) feature set first landed at v19.0.0. Clean forward-merge (no conflicts) onto v19.1.0; the branch was strictly ahead by 26 commits. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values. Sibling Skill 53 (Book Writer) is byte-for-byte untouched (`scan-skill53-untouched` CLEAN); Skill 54 (Anthology Writer) authoring core unchanged except its baked cover-prompt pin (`aw-11`, re-pinned + `guard-prompt-pins.py` CLEAN across 22 pins). All 11 version markers roll in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh` and the annotated tag `v19.2.0` is cut on the release commit (G1 guard).

- **S9 assembly — inter-chapter transitions + Grand Finale (`stage_s9_assembly_logic.py`, prompts `ae-05`/`ae-06`):** the manuscript compile now writes producer-ordered N-1 inter-chapter transitions and a brand-new titled Grand Finale, gated by `s9_ready` -> producer "Confirm the finalized set & order" (triggers the Finale write) -> `s9_producer` sign-off. New provers `prove_transitions` (each seam names the next locked title, zero em dashes, frozen chapters byte-unchanged) and `prove_finale` (references every chapter, action-steps section, 14pt floor, zero em dashes). `confirm_order` uses the confirmed order, never re-curates. Engine-owned prompts `ae-05`/`ae-06` are sha256-pinned in `ENGINE-MANIFEST.json` and byte-verified.
- **Gate B envelope contract (`gate_engine.py`, `qc-tier1-anthology.py`):** S9 assembly Gate B is wired to qc-tier1's envelope contract so a passing assembly QC unblocks delivery (was previously blocked); `gate_engine` gains assembly-status passthrough + a `confirm_order` board action.
- **Editable-doc delivery (`drive_adapter.py`, `stage_s7`/`stage_s8`):** per-document view/edit share + `pull_doc_text` for Tier-1 pullback revalidation; S8 reads the real Google Doc link key from the adapter result; cover image shared view-only. Four named cover styles with sample fields (`cover_render.py`, `stage_s7_cover.py`).
- **n8n Google-Drive credential broker (`config/n8n/anthology-drive-broker.workflow.json`, `drive_adapter.py` broker mode):** an operator-hosted n8n workflow brokers the BlackCEO-owned delivery service-account credential so the engine never holds the raw Drive key; `drive_adapter` gains a broker mode. Per-client BlackCEO-hosted Shared-Drive delivery root (never anyone-can-read).
- **Chapter-rewrite preservation (`stage_s6_rewrite.py`, field-map):** S6 rewrite preserves unchanged chapter content; field-map rewrite fields aligned to LARGE_TEXT; caf routing + strike-gate.
- **create-a-book minter (`anthology_book.py`):** mint -> ledger -> pipeline -> intake link, pointed at the LIVE universal form; `intake.forms_base_url` config slot. Credential gate `--require-anthology-hook-secret`. The four CI merge-gate scanners (secrets / json-exports / client-identifiers / skill53-untouched) all CLEAN; 107 pytest cases green.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.1.0 -> v19.2.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` green; auxiliary browser-manager / agent-browser version markers kept in sync). Skill 59 `skill-version.txt` at 0.1.2. Annotated tag `v19.2.0` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).

## [v19.1.0]  -  2026-07-09  -  feat(persona-blend): voice-first AUDIENCE+TOPIC blend matcher (Skill 23) + additive schema-1.3 catalog enrichment (Skill 22)

Integration release that lands the operator-approved (2026-07-08) voice-first persona BLEND design across the two coupled skills. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values. Additive only — the N38 persona-set count triad stays green at 99 (no persona keys added or removed) and all 11 version markers roll in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. Persona voices are STYLE-INSPIRED, NEVER impersonation.

- **Skill 23 — persona-blend matcher (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/persona_blend.py`, +selector wiring):** for any content/communication job the matcher decides the VOICE first — it selects an AUDIENCE persona blended with a TOPIC persona (write in the audience voice, carry the topic expertise), COLLAPSES to one persona when it covers both, decomposes the job into UP TO 10 task personas via the existing per-sub-task matcher, and resolves the audience from the client onboarding ICP with an ALWAYS-confirm / ASK-when-unsure gate (`confirm_required` gates the downstream write; never fabricates an audience). It reasons over the WHOLE catalog via `audiences[]`/`topics[]`/`voice_style{}`/`usable_as[]` (no static voice library) and emits the persona-bundle SUPERSET — a strict superset of the single-persona selector result, so existing consumers keep working. Every `blend_directive` carries a mandatory, non-removable "STYLE-INSPIRED, NEVER IMPERSONATION" guardrail clause. Back-compatible on a schema-1.2 catalog (degraded-but-honest topic-only bundle) and for mechanical/non-content tasks (never-naked governance pointer). Locked by a hermetic 42-assertion contract suite (`test-persona-blend-matcher.py`).
- **Skill 22 — schema-1.3 catalog enrichment (`22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/persona-categories.json`):** all 99 personas gain `audiences[]`/`topics[]`/`voice_style{}`/`usable_as[]`; top-level controlled-vocab `audienceTags` (450) + `topicTags` (752) added (union of used tags, so membership validation passes by construction); `schemaVersion` 1.2 -> 1.3. The Skill-22 tag-validator (`pipeline/persona_fleet.py::_validate_entry`) is extended to validate the new fields against the vocabs + enum, and the publish path (`CANONICAL_ENTRY_FIELDS` + `sync_categories`) is made enrichment-safe so `publish-personas-to-fleet.sh` can never strip the layer. Skill-22 wrapper rolled v6.16.3 -> v6.17.0 (independent of the repo /version).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v19.0.0 -> v19.1.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` green; auxiliary browser-manager / agent-browser version-comment markers kept in sync). Annotated tag `v19.1.0` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).

## [v19.0.0]  -  2026-07-08  -  feat(anthology-engine): land Skill 59 (Anthology Engine) on main + forward-merge v18.1.2 — capstone release

Capstone release that integrates the **Anthology Engine (Skill 59)** onto `main` and forward-merges the concurrent `main` release train (v18.1.2) into the staging branch, then rolls the repo v18.1.2 -> v19.0.0. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values. Sibling of Skill 53 (Book Writer) and Skill 54 (Anthology Writer); it deprecates neither.

Skill 59 is the per-client **orchestrator** that turns ONE participant's Convert and Flow intake submission into ONE gated, quality-controlled 2,000–3,500-word anthology chapter (never twelve), delivered as BOTH a Google Doc and a designed PDF with no font below 14 point, with links pushed to standardized Convert and Flow contact custom fields keyed by `contact_id`. It owns ALL external input/output and the durable state, and **calls Skill 54 (Anthology Writer) as its authoring core** — it never re-authors the chapter pipeline. Four layers: a deterministic intake router; a durable multi-participant ledger (`contact_id` + `anthology_id`); the Skill 54 authoring core extended with the Skill 52 avatar handoff; and Google Drive + PDF + Convert and Flow delivery, plus producer/participant surfaces inside the client's own Command Center (a seeded Anthology department board). Every stage (S0 intake → S9 assembly) is an idempotent, resumable job against the ledger, run through one sanctioned entry (`anthology-engine-entry.sh`). Runtime is NEVER an Anthropic-family model; move-in-silence throughout (only the three sanctioned nudge templates ever reach a client).

- **Skill 59 integration** — lands the engine build units under `59-anthology-engine/` (orchestrator + ten stage dispatchers `stage_s0`…`stage_s9`, `ENGINE-MANIFEST.json` stage/gate/autofail tables, config templates, install/preflight/verify, runbook docs) at `skill-version.txt` 0.1.0. Applied the staged v19 patch set: gate-strip, model-map, PyMuPDF/fitz, department-runtime rework, webhook, manifest, title-truncation, PRD-sync, and the W4.10 doc residual.
- **model-map fail-closed** — `preflight.sh --check` is a PRE-GATE that reads the resolved `OUT_DIR/model-map.json` and fails closed if it still carries `<CLIENT_*>` placeholders (`AF-AE-UNRESOLVED-MODELMAP`) or any Anthropic-family id; it NEVER writes an Anthropic-family id or an operator key. Five tiers, `ollama-cloud` baseUrl slotting.
- **PyMuPDF (fitz) hard dep** — the output-side 14-point font-floor gate (`scripts/guard-font-floor.py`) HARD-requires PyMuPDF to inspect the rendered PDF; `verify-deps.sh` fails when it is missing so the font-floor gate can never be silently dead.
- **webhook intake transform** — the S0 intake path and its `verify-webhook-t1-t9.sh` coverage transform the inbound anthology intake submission into a ledger job keyed by `contact_id` + `anthology_id`.
- **department-runtime wiring** — the Anthology department agent is verified wired EXACTLY ONCE (idempotent-by-id on both `add-department.sh` and the provision path).
- **Forward-merge v18.1.2** — merged `origin/main` (v18.1.2) into the staging branch. Conflicts resolved: `universal-sops/_content-manifest.json` re-hashed via `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py` (102 files, both content manifests `--check` green); `CHANGELOG.md` union (v18.1.2 on top of the branch entries); `update-skills.sh` auto-merged in disjoint regions.
- **Artifact / consistency reconciliation** — reconciled the static skill inventory + department maps for Skill 59 so the artifact-coverage gate returns rc=0 (`role-library/_index.json`, marketing department docs, `README.md`, `install.sh`, `universal-sops/_content-manifest.json`). Post-merge CI-gate reconciliation added the missing v18.1.0 CHANGELOG entry, reconciled the 52/49/54 SKILL.md frontmatter ↔ `skill-version.txt` drift, and fixed the chronically-red `test-gws-credential-preservation.sh` T8 perms helper (GNU `stat -c` first; test-harness only).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.1.2 -> v19.0.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`, verified `--check`; plus the auxiliary browser-manager/agent-browser version-comment markers kept in sync). Annotated tag `v19.0.0` cut on the release commit (G1 guard). This `## [v19.0.0]` entry was added in a follow-up commit on `main` after the tag existed (on the PR the tag did not yet exist, so G2 passed then), greening the Version-consistency gate post-merge.

## [v19.0.3]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(anthology-engine): scrub Airtable base-id-shape token from the Skill 59 intake-transform comment (guard-prompt-pins W5.8)

Definitive v19 re-cut of the published `v19.0.0` content. `/version` is UNCHANGED (`v19.0.0`); this tag is a re-cut that carries the A3 content-hash exclusions plus the guard-prompt-pins scrub. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

- **Airtable base-id-shape scrub (guard-prompt-pins W5.8)** — `59-anthology-engine/config/hooks/transforms/anthology-intake.mjs` carried, in its CONTRACT documentation comment, an incidental camelCase code-identifier (a gateway hook-mapping function name) whose shape matched the Airtable base-id pattern (`app` + 14 base62 chars). It was NEVER an Airtable base id — real, agency, or example — and never a runtime value (the transform resolves its router purely from `ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_ROUTER` / `ANTHOLOGY_SCRIPTS_DIR`, and the engine reaches its state base by the `ANTHOLOGY_STATE_BASE_ID` label only). The shipped runtime-surface scan in `guard-prompt-pins.py` (check 4a, `AF-AE-PROMPT-PIN`) matched the shape and failed W5.8. The comment was reworded to describe the same `dist/hooks-*.js` hook-mapping applier without the matching token; runtime behavior is byte-identical (comment-only). Skill 59 bumped `0.1.0` → `0.1.1` (skill-version.txt + SKILL.md frontmatter + ENGINE-MANIFEST `skill_version` + skill changelog, G3/frontmatter-guard lockstep).
- **A3 content-hash exclusions** — folds in the intermediate re-cuts `v19.0.1` (exclude rendered `model-map.json`) and `v19.0.2` (exclude both install/runtime-mutated files) from the A3 content hash so the install-time content-gate does not false-mismatch on install/runtime-mutated artifacts. Those two intermediate re-cut tags are superseded by this tag (`v19.0.2`'s commit is this tag's direct parent) and were deleted.

## [v18.1.2]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(command-center-setup): wire the OpenClaw agent runtime in add-department.sh (Skill 32)

Cherry-picks `e1c1b142` (with `-x` provenance) from `fix/add-department-runtime-wiring` as the serial single onboarding writer. Only the fix commit was taken, NOT a merge of the branch: that branch is stacked on the in-flight Anthology Engine (`59-anthology-engine/`, which is not on main), so a full `--no-ff` merge would have dumped the entire in-flight engine and hit a real conflict in `run-full-install.sh`. This is the same cherry-pick-not-merge pattern used for the sibling Skill 32 parity-guard fix (v18.1.0, cherry-picked `-x` from `d768dc29`). `add-department.sh`'s parent blob is byte-identical to main's, so the cherry-pick applied with zero conflict. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

Fixes a pre-existing shipped defect in the SHARED `add-department.sh` tool. Its own header claimed it does "the full chain in one shot," but it never actually wired the OpenClaw agent RUNTIME — the `openclaw.json` `agents.list[]` entry (`id=dept-<slug>`) plus the `$OC_ROOT/agents/dept-<slug>/` directory. The workspaces/agents DB rows it inserts make a department SHOW UP on the Command Center board, and `register_routing_dept()` writes only the routing-sidecar bookkeeping file (`extension-registry.json`); neither wires `agents.list[]`. Without the real runtime entry, Command Center dispatch resolves NO specialist for the department, and every card lands and immediately STICKS in "Blocked" with reason `no_specialist_runtime` — forever. This is the exact defect that hit the Anthology (Skill 59) department: that skill's own caller was patched, but the SHARED `add-department.sh` — used by every other skill and by any operator adding a department by hand — was not.

- **Root cause:** `add-department.sh` seeded the board/DB rows and the routing sidecar but never emitted the OpenClaw runtime (`agents.list[]` entry + `agents/dept-<slug>/` directory), so dispatch had no specialist to resolve.
- **Fix:** adds `wire_department_runtime()` (delegating to the already-landed, byte-identical `materialize-dept-agents.sh`, with an inline fallback when the materializer is absent) and CALLS it on BOTH the fresh-create path AND the idempotent "already exists" healing path — so re-running `add-department.sh` self-heals a previously-broken (pre-fix) department. Also adds a root guard: config writes must not run as root.
- **New regression coverage** — `32-command-center-setup/scripts/test_add_department_runtime.py` (8/8), a HERMETIC BLOCK-before / RELEASE-after proof: a synthetic Command Center dispatch check BLOCKS with `no_specialist_runtime` before the department is wired and RELEASES after, against a mocked `openclaw.json` + `mission-control.db` (no live box, no real credential, no Anthropic identifier; HOME redirected to a throwaway temp dir, with a guard that refuses to run if `/data/.openclaw` exists). Covers fresh-wire, idempotency (no duplicate), sibling-agent preservation, inline fallback when the materializer is missing, incomplete-interview deferral, re-run healing of a pre-fix department, the simulated-root guard, and a no-Anthropic-id / no-secret-value assertion on the wired config.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.1.1 -> v18.1.2 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). `32-command-center-setup/skill-version.txt` is deliberately NOT bumped, matching the sibling parity-guard fix: the change adds scripts under `32-command-center-setup/scripts/` in a NON-tip commit, and G3 (skill-content-without-version-bump) evaluates only the release-bump TIP commit vs its parent — whose sole skill-dir changes are under `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/` and `06-ghl-install-pages/`, each with its own `skill-version.txt` rolled in lockstep. Annotated tag `v18.1.2` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).
- **Gates green (fresh clone, local run):** `test_add_department_runtime.py` 8/8; the existing Skill 32 suite — `test_register_routing_sidecar.py` + `test_seed_workspaces_normalize.py` + `test_guard_department_runtime_parity.py` (28 passed via pytest), `test-add-sop.sh` (6/6), `test-converge.sh` (7/7); security scanners run against the 2 changed files — `scan-no-secrets.sh --strict` (CLEAN), `scan-no-client-identifiers.sh` (CLEAN), and `guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py` (PASS — no Anthropic id/provider/package/key/host in runtime), each with its own self-test proving the detector fires; and `scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` (structural PASS — roster-specific scan requires the operator's external client-roster file). Working tree hermetically clean after the whole suite (no tracked file, including `_index.json`, modified by the tests). The Anthology-Engine-only suites (`test_department_runtime.py`, `test_model_map_resolution.py`, `test_webhook.py`, golden replay) live under `59-anthology-engine/`, which is not on main, and are N/A for this cherry-pick — they belong to the Anthology Engine's own future train and were validated on the branch by a prior run.

## [v18.1.1]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(signature-funnel): correct fabricated Command Center department slug (Skill 49)

Merges `fix/skill49-signature-funnel-department-slug` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

Fixes a pre-existing shipped defect, identical in shape to Skill 53's `department="books"`, Skill 56's `department="funnels"`, and Skill 50's `department="email"` defects: `run_signature_funnel.py`'s `_mc_board_begin` posted every Signature Funnel task card with a hardcoded `department="funnels"`, but "funnels" is not a seeded department anywhere in the fleet (confirmed against `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/department-naming-map.json`'s canonical department set and `department-floor.py` — "funnels" appears only as prose inside the web-development department's `one_liner`, never as a department key). `scripts/mc_board.py` forwards the slug verbatim and does not validate it, so every Signature Funnel Command Center card has been silently misrouted or dropped since the skill shipped.

- **Root cause:** the shipped code used a standalone `"funnels"` slug that was never actually seeded as a canonical department.
- **Fix:** `department="funnels"` → `department="web-development"`, the REAL canonical department that `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-department-map.json`'s authoritative skill-49 entry already declares (`"departments": ["web-development", "marketing"]`, with `web-development` as the `primary: true` role `signature-funnel-specialist`), matching sibling skills 50/52/53/55/56.
- **New regression coverage** — `49-signature-funnel/scripts/test_sf_department.py` statically extracts the `department=` literal from `_mc_board_begin` and asserts it (a) is a member of the canonical department set, (b) is never a known-broken slug (`"funnels"`/`"books"`/`"email"`), and (c) matches `skill-department-map.json`'s authoritative skill-49 binding. Wired into `49-signature-funnel/verify.sh` (check 4b). Proven as a real behavioral guard (fails 2/5 tests red when reverted to `department="funnels"`, passes clean with the fix restored). Purely Command-Center-board metadata (fail-soft, never a gate): the golden certificate signature / SACRED invariants are unaffected.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.1.0 -> v18.1.1 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; `49-signature-funnel/skill-version.txt` bumped 1.3.0 -> 1.3.1). Version rolled from v18.1.0 rather than v18.0.8 because the concurrent Skill 50 (Email Engine, v18.0.8) and Skill 32 (department-runtime-parity-guard, v18.1.0) merges landed on `main` while this gate suite was running; this merge was rebuilt on the post-Skill-32 tip with zero file overlap against either concurrent change (confirmed: no touch to `49-signature-funnel/`, `department-naming-map.json`, or `skill-department-map.json` from either). Annotated tag `v18.1.1` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).
- **Gates green (fresh clone, local run, re-verified after the rebuild onto the post-Skill-32 tip):** `49-signature-funnel/verify.sh` full PASS (7 provers + orchestrator/entry self-tests + new check 4b + golden reproduce + broken-variant rejections; golden bundle re-issues the SHIPPED signature `67a5738a2a62…`, byte-identical to the pre-fix committed certificate — confirming zero effect on funnel content), the new `49-signature-funnel/scripts/test_sf_department.py` (5/5, proven to fail on the reverted `"funnels"` string and pass on the fix), `49-signature-funnel/scripts/test_cc_contract.py` (shared board contract, 11/11), `scripts/qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh`, `scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` (structural — roster-specific scan requires the operator's external client-roster file), and the no-Anthropic-runtime guard (zero NEW findings vs. the current main baseline — the 4 pre-existing findings in this skill's own enforcement code, e.g. `prove_sf_cert.py`'s denylist literals documenting the never-Anthropic rule, are expected and byte-identical before/after).

## [v18.1.0]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(skill32): per-department Command Center runtime parity guard (Skill 32)

Cherry-picks the Skill 32 per-department runtime reconciliation guard (`-x` provenance from `d768dc29`) off `fix/skill32-department-runtime-parity-guard` as the serial single onboarding writer — only the fix commit was taken, not a full branch merge, because the source branch was cut from the in-flight Anthology-Engine tip (not on `main`); this is the same cherry-pick-not-merge pattern the sibling entry v18.1.2 documents. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

Closes the `no_specialist_runtime` (AGENT_COUNT) blind spot: a Command Center department could carry a board/DB row with no matching OpenClaw runtime and still pass because runtime coverage was validated only as an aggregate count, not per department.

- **Root cause:** department runtime coverage was checked against a total-count floor, so a single department missing its `agents.list[]` runtime entry was masked by the total.
- **Fix:** `run-full-install.sh` Phase 6e2 now hard-fails the install if ANY Command Center department has a board row with no matching OpenClaw runtime, checked individually per department (not just against a total-count floor). `update-skills.sh` carries the same check as a warn-and-continue belt-and-suspenders on the update path.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.8 -> v18.1.0 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). Annotated tag `v18.1.0` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).

## [v18.0.8]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(email-engine): correct fabricated Command Center department slug (Skill 50)

Merges `fix/skill50-email-engine-department-slug` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out is a separate operator decision. No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

Fixes a pre-existing shipped defect, identical in shape to the Skill 53 (Book Writer, v18.0.6) and Skill 56 (Sales Page Assets, v18.0.7) fixes: `run_email_engine.py`'s `_mc_board_begin` posted every Email Engine task card with a hardcoded `department="email"`, but "email" is not a real, seeded fleet department anywhere in this repo (not in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/department-naming-map.json`'s canonical set, not in `department-floor.py`'s mandatory set). `scripts/mc_board.py` fails SOFT on an unrecognized `department_slug` (caught, logged to stderr, run continues — never a gate), so this never threw a visible error: every Email Engine Command Center card has been silently stranded/unrouted since the skill shipped.

- **Fix:** `department="email"` → `department="marketing"`, matching Skill 50's own authoritative declared owning department (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-department-map.json` skill-50 entry: `"departments": ["marketing","crm"]`, PRIMARY role `email-campaign-strategist` in `marketing`).
- **New regression coverage** — `50-email-engine/test_department_routing.py` statically extracts the `department=` literal from `_mc_board_begin` and asserts it (a) is a member of the canonical fleet department set, (b) is never the historic broken `"email"` slug, and (c) matches `skill-department-map.json`'s authoritative skill-50 binding. Proven as a real behavioral guard (fails with 3/5 tests red when reverted to `department="email"`, passes clean with the fix restored). Wired into `50-email-engine/verify.sh`. ## [v18.0.7]  -  2026-07-08  -  fix(sales-page-assets): correct fabricated Command Center department slug (Skill 56)

Wired into `56-sales-page-assets/verify.sh` (section 7). Purely Command-Center-board metadata (fail-soft, never a gate): the golden `certificate_sha` / SACRED invariants are unaffected.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.6 -> v18.0.7 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; `56-sales-page-assets/skill-version.txt` bumped 1.4.0 -> 1.4.1). Annotated tag `v18.0.7` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).

## [v18.0.5]  -  2026-07-07  -  fix(comms): Telegram chat_id resolver + phone-number-target doctrine — never send to a raw phone number again

Merges `fix/telegram-phone-target-doctrine` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD (operator rolls to the fleet separately, as its own decision). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

Closes a class of Telegram send failures where a phone number was passed as the send target instead of the numeric Telegram `chat_id` the gateway actually requires — the two are not interchangeable, and a phone-number target silently fails or misroutes.

- **New `shared-utils/resolve-client-chat-id.sh`** — a runtime lookup that resolves a client's Telegram `chat_id` from the fleet registry by name (case-insensitive) or box-slug. Returns the confirmed `chat_id` on stdout with exit 0; empty stdout + a distinct non-zero exit on every failure mode (not-found = 1, matched-but-unconfirmed = 1, ambiguous substring match = 2, missing roster = 3, usage error = 4) so a caller can branch on exactly why resolution failed rather than guessing.
- **New doctrine — `universal-sops/telegram-target-resolution.md`** — codifies the rule fleet-wide: never pass a phone number as a Telegram send target; always resolve `chat_id` from the fleet registry first via the new resolver; escalate to the operator (never silently guess or fall back to a phone number) on a resolution miss. Folded into the universal-sops content-integrity manifest (`_content-manifest.json`).
- **Communications department how-to-use-this-department.md appendix** — documents the doctrine for the owning department so client-facing agents in that department see the rule in the same guide they already consult for department operating instructions.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.4 -> v18.0.5 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). Annotated tag `v18.0.5` cut on the release commit (G1 guard).
- **Gates green (fresh clone, local run):** `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh`, `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` (structural — operator path/placeholder scan; roster-specific scan requires the operator's external client-roster file, independently diffed by the operator against the live 34-client roster with 0 hits), `qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, the no-Anthropic-model-string scan (22/23/shared-utils scope, mirrors `qc-static.yml`), the new `tests/unit/resolve-client-chat-id.test.sh` (10/10 passed), `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check` (97/97 files, sha256 clean), `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-how-to-use-docs.sh` (7/7), and the full `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` + artifact-coverage gate (28/28 departments OK, 10/10 dimensions OK, all 11 version markers agree).

## [v18.0.4]  -  2026-07-07  -  fix(gws): close the ENTIRE headless self-wipe class fleet-wide — force the file keyring backend on every box + install the `gws-as` wrapper + off-box credential backup

Merges `fix/gws-headless-selfwipe-guard` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD (operator rolls to the fleet separately, as its own decision). No operator PII, no client names, no Anthropic models, no secret values.

v16.1.9 fixed the ONE bare `gws auth status` in skill-14's QC, but the underlying **class** was still latent on any box that runs `gws`: a bare `gws` invoked in a **headless / non-interactive** shell defaults to the OS "keyring" backend, cannot unlock it, and gws's OWN failure mode then rewrites `~/.config/gws/credentials.enc` to `credential_source:"none"` — wiping every account. The QC pin only protected the QC's own process; a bare `gws` from an EXAMPLES/INSTRUCTIONS doc step, an agent shell, a future script, or a cron was still exposed. This change makes a headless `gws` **unable to self-wipe on any box**, and makes any wipe recoverable.

- **Fleet-wide runtime resilience — new `scripts/harden-gws-credential-resilience.sh`** (idempotent, additive, box-user, never root; run from both the install and the update paths). It (1) writes an **append-only, sentinel-guarded** managed block to `~/.zshenv` (+ `~/.bashrc`, `~/.profile`) that exports `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_KEYRING_BACKEND=file` — so **every** shell gws runs in (including non-interactive zsh, which always sources `~/.zshenv`) uses the on-disk file key and can never fall back to the OS keychain; the block preserves the user's existing content and is never duplicated on re-run. (2) installs the **`gws-as`** wrapper on the agent PATH (`~/.openclaw/bin`) that FORCES the file backend for scripted/cron/multi-account calls (proven to override even a caller that pre-set the dangerous `keyring` backend) and routes named accounts to their per-account config dir. (3) writes an **off-box encrypted snapshot** of the default store (`credentials.enc` + the file-keyring key) under `~/.openclaw/secrets/backups/<box>-gws/<ts>/` (700/600), copy-only (the live store is never mutated), change-gated, last-10 retained — so a wipe by any path is restorable. The reference canary box already carries this runtime hardening and is live-verified.
- **Wired into both paths** — `install.sh` (new Step 8c) and `update-skills.sh` (roll path, plus the hardener is added to the persistent-scripts copy list so every box keeps a standalone copy for manual rescue). Both call sites are best-effort (`|| note`/`|| true`) and can never change the install/update exit status; the hardener refuses to write as root (re-drops to the box user where possible) so config is never root-owned.
- **Docs guarded** — skill-14 `SKILL.md`, `INSTALL.md`, `INSTRUCTIONS.md`, and `EXAMPLES.md` gained a prominent "CREDENTIAL SAFETY" callout: the file-keyring requirement, that the install sets it for you, the `gws-as` pattern for scripts/cron, and "never `gws auth logout/remove/revoke` to fix a headless auth error." The many bare-`gws` example commands are now safe because the environment guarantees the file backend.
- **Regression guard extended** — `scripts/test-gws-credential-preservation.sh` gains **T6–T8** (drive the REAL hardener headless): T6 the file backend is forced in `~/.zshenv`/`.bashrc`/`.profile` append-only + idempotent + user content preserved; T7 the installed `gws-as` forces `file` even when the caller pre-set `keyring`, routes accounts, and only ever `exec gws` (no self-clearing verb); T8 the off-box snapshot is byte-identical + copy-only + private 600 + change-gated. **29/29 PASS** (18 pre-existing invariants unchanged, incl. "exactly one guarded `gws auth status`", append-only dotfile writes, and no destructive op against `~/.config/gws*`). The CI workflow now also triggers on the hardener path.
- **Scope note** — non-interactive **bash** shells do not source `~/.zshenv`/`.bashrc`; those are covered by the per-script keyring pin (skill-14 QC) and the `gws-as` wrapper. The `~/.zshenv` export is the load-bearing guard for the Mac fleet (zsh agent shells).
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.3 -> v18.0.4 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). Annotated tag `v18.0.4` cut on the merge commit (G1 guard).
- **Gates green (fresh clone, local run):** `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`, `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh`, `qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, `qc-assert-skill-version-newline.sh` (58 files), `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version.sh`, `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` (11/11 markers), `scripts/test-gws-credential-preservation.sh` (29/29), and the full `qc-static.yml` static-invariant suite (107/107 steps).

## [v18.0.3]  -  2026-07-07  -  fix(cc): durable Command Center converge guards so a rebuild can never re-break the Kanban again (Skill 32, v12.9.31)

Merges `fix/cc-converge-kanban-durable-guards` as the serial single onboarding writer. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD (the 17 already-affected boxes were remediated live and separately; this merge lands only the permanent source fix). No new operator PII; no client names; no Anthropic models in runtime.

Root cause (proven on a client box): Phase 6 of `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh` pulled fresh Command Center source and ran `npm install` (whose `postinstall` only `npm rebuild`s the better-sqlite3 native addon) then restarted pm2 — but **never ran `next build`**. `next start` therefore kept serving the pre-existing `.next` bundle, which predates the pulled source; the Next.js instrumentation hook that registers the intake-advance + backlog-redispatch dispatch sweeps is compiled INTO `.next`, so a stale bundle silently drops them and Kanban cards stick in Backlog (`dispatch_attempts` stays 0). Three runtime env vars were also never provisioned, so even a fresh build could fail closed: an empty `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN`, an unset `SOVEREIGN_DEFAULT_MODEL`, and unset `MC_API_TOKEN`/`WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

- **Four durable, idempotent, additive Phase-6 guards** (both full-install and `--update-only`), reading only THIS box's own config — never a shared/Anthropic value: (1) `cc_ensure_fresh_build` rebuilds `.next` only when it is stale vs source (folded into the FINAL no-false-done degraded gate); (2) `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` provisioned from the box's own `gateway.auth.token` when `auth.mode=token`; (3) `SOVEREIGN_DEFAULT_MODEL` provisioned from the box's own primary TEXT model (free/Anthropic ids rejected; per-client override `CC_SOVEREIGN_DEFAULT_MODEL`); (4) `MC_API_TOKEN` + `WEBHOOK_SECRET` generated (never rotated) by default, with a Cloudflare-Access opt-out via `CC_ALLOW_INSECURE_OPEN_API`. `.env.local` is written 0600; existing operator values are always preserved; secret values are never logged.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.2 -> v18.0.3 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; `32-command-center-setup/skill-version.txt` bumped v12.9.30 -> v12.9.31, satisfying the skill-content-change gate). Annotated tag `v18.0.3` cut on the merge commit (G1 guard).
- **Gates green (fresh clone, local run):** `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`, `qc-assert-no-secret-printing-grep.sh`, `qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, `qc-assert-skill-version-newline.sh` (self-test + full, 58 files), `qc-assert-skill-frontmatter-version.sh`, `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` (11/11 markers), and the full `qc-static.yml` static-invariant suite (107/107 steps, including the CC-specific `AF3 local-candidate-loop guard`, `Command Center done-degraded / retry gate regression lock`, `Skill 32 register-routing-dept sidecar` gate, and `chmod-600 coverage`).

## [v18.0.2]  -  2026-07-06  -  fix: updater↔runtime cron-flag skew + remove hard `jq` dependency from the nudge/watchdog crons (two fleet-wide reliability fixes)

Two automation-reliability fixes landed together as the **serial single onboarding writer** (folded into ONE PR/version because the cron-skew branch had not yet merged). Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD. No new operator PII; no client names; no Anthropic models in runtime.

- **fix/cron-flag-skew — the self-heal / onboarding-resume cron installs on rolled boxes again.** The installed runtime `openclaw 2026.6.11` **removed `--session-target`** ("does not recognize option --session-target") and now **requires `--system-event`** for main-session cron jobs (a `--message` main job is rejected with "Main jobs require --system-event"). The updater + installers only ever emitted `--session-target main --message`, so on a rolled 2026.6.11 box the silent main-session crons — `onboarding-resume`, `workforce-build-resume`, `weekly-onboarding-update`, `reassert-presentation-deps`, `watchdog-onboarding-loop` — **silently failed to install** (the QC self-heal / onboarding-resume loop never armed). New shared helper **`_oc_cron_silent_main`** feature-probes `openclaw cron add --help` and emits the form the INSTALLED runtime accepts (`--session main --system-event` on 2026.6.11+, `--session-target main --message` on 2026.6.8/5.x), falls through the other known-good forms (incl. the docs-canonical positional form) so a mis-probe still recovers, and degrades to a SILENT isolated agent-message job (`--message … --no-deliver`) — so a cron ALWAYS lands under its name and the updater NEVER hard-fails. Every path stays silent (no `--channel`/`--to`/`--announce`). Defined in `lib-onboarding-resume-cron.sh` (canonical) and re-guarded self-contained in `update-skills.sh`, `install.sh`, and `scripts/ensure-pipeline-crons.sh` so each standalone-executed script has it on the guaranteed path. Cron INTENT and the `*/30`/`*/15`/`*/20` cadences are unchanged; boundedness/self-removal untouched.
- **fix/jq-hard-dep — the interview-nudge + closeout-readiness watchdog crons no longer die when `jq` is missing.** `jq` is **not shipped in the OpenClaw container image** and vanished on a container recreate around the v18.0.1 roll; both cron scripts guarded on `command -v jq … || exit 1` and so **hard-aborted every cycle** ("jq not found - aborting"), degrading owner interview nudges and the ZHC closeout escalation lane (an owner could sit stalled with no nudge). Both scripts now parse/mutate build-state with **python3** (guaranteed present in the container) — `state_get` faithfully mirrors jq's `<path> // empty` (null AND boolean-false yield empty), and the closeoutBlockers append / stuckEscalations throttle / nudge-UUID clear are done in atomic python writes. The hard `jq` guard is replaced by a `python3` check that degrades to a graceful no-op (exit 0), never a hard abort. Files: `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/interview-nudge-cron.sh`, `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/closeout-readiness-watchdog.sh`. The existing `test-closeout-watchdog.sh` T1–T5 (blocker class, throttle, QC-fail gating) pass unchanged against the python rewrite, proving byte-for-byte equivalent state mutations.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.1 -> v18.0.2 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-version.txt` is coupled to `/version`, satisfying the skill-content-change gate for the two skill-23 script edits). Annotated tag `v18.0.2` cut on the branch HEAD before merge (G1 race guard). Gates green locally: `update-skills-resume-cron.test.sh` 20/20, `bounded-resume-cron.test.sh` 21/21, `cron-owner-chat-guard.test.sh` 102/102, `test-closeout-watchdog.sh` T1–T5. Already-rolled boxes need a re-roll (`update-skills.sh`) to pick up these script fixes.

## [v18.0.1]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(skill23): operator-triggered AI Workforce Interview start/resume link delivery (send-interview-link.sh)

Lands the clean "when you're ready, start here" trigger for the AI Workforce Interview as the **serial single onboarding writer**, immediately after the podcast-engine v18.0.0 merge train cleared. Held behind that train (single-writer discipline), now rebased onto v18.0.0 and rolled to v18.0.1. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD. No new operator PII; no client names; no Anthropic models.

- **`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/send-interview-link.sh`** — the operator runs this ON the client's box (**never a cron**); the box resolves its OWN owner chat via `shared-utils/resolve-owner-chat.sh` (full S0->S1->S2 chain, operator ids rejected on every source — the script never pre-empts the resolver with a raw env read) and sends ONE Telegram message through the OpenClaw gateway: **START** `{OPENCLAW_DASHBOARD_URL}/interview` (nothing answered yet), **RESUME** `{OPENCLAW_DASHBOARD_URL}/onboarding/resume/{slug}` (interview underway; matches the nudge worker's P0-7 slug contract), or a **reply-here invitation** when no dashboard URL is configured (the interview is fully conductable in chat; never emits a placeholder/broken link).
- **Guardrails** — gateway-only (no direct Bot API, no fallback); refuses when `interviewComplete`/`buildCompletedAt` is set (exit 3); 30-minute re-send guard via a non-canonical per-box ledger (`FORCE=1` bypasses, exit 7); operator env-pin rejection (exit 4); canonical files read-only; no hardcoded chat ids; resolved id masked to last 4 in all output; `--dry-run` for verification. `bash -n` clean. The link send is **operator-triggered, never auto-fired** — the invitation is not consent.
- **`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/INSTRUCTIONS.md`** — documents the mechanism next to the nudge cadence, including the Command Center counterpart `POST /api/interview/send-link` and the invitation-is-not-consent rule.
- **Version roll** — repo rolled v18.0.0 -> v18.0.1 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`) to clear the G3 skill-content-change gate for the skill-23 edit (`skill-version.txt` is coupled to `/version` in lockstep). Annotated tag `v18.0.1` cut on the branch HEAD before merge (G1 race guard). Gates green: G3 (23 + 06 skill-version.txt bumped), version-markers, chat-id-leak scan, no-client-names, Skill-23 provisioning (6 hermetic suites), cron owner-chat guard, both-paths delivery, QC static invariants.

## [v18.0.0]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(podcast): Podcast Production Engine (Skill 58) capstone - serial merge-train integration of 31 slice branches

The v18.0.0 capstone lands the new **58-podcast-production-engine** skill: a per-client engine that turns ONE podcast intake survey into ONE fully produced, published episode end to end, with zero human editing between the AI and the listener. Integrated as the single serial merge train from 31 `feat/podcast-*` slice branches off `feat/podcast-engine-v18-base`. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD. No new operator PII; no client names; no Anthropic models in runtime.

- **Pipeline** - OpenClaw inbound webhook intake (flat body, deliver false) -> Research Assistant stage -> script authored in the client's Style Engine (Counter Intuitive / Vulnerable / Provocative / Passionate) and Production Mode (Personal / Interview) -> Fish Audio `s2.1-pro` render selected by header with the client's OWN `reference_id` (the free tier is forbidden) -> Kie.ai GPT-image cover art -> Episode Package + Speech Script documents -> Podbean publish -> Convert and Flow custom-field write-back. The Convert and Flow data plane is **Skill 44 caf + Skill 29 REST only, never MCP in the pipeline**.
- **Department wiring (existing, never duplicated)** - wires into the EXISTING `podcast` department (universal floor). `skill-department-map.json` registers Skill 58 as client-facing under the `podcast` department (client-facing skills 25 -> **26**); the four `podcast` owning-role "Skills You Operate" blocks (`stamp-skills-you-operate.py`), the `podcast` department DEPT_SKILLS guide (`stamp-dept-skill-guides.py`), and the `SKILL_INTENT_ROUTING_REFLEX_V1` catalog row (`scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh`) are all stamped from the ONE map; content_sha re-stamped for the four touched role templates. The intake webhook binds `sessionKey podcast:intake:<client-slug>` to the department agent; kanban pickup is via the state-ledger enum.
- **Two SEPARATE QC gates** - the 8.5 build rubric (Gate A), and the 16-Tier1 + rubric>=8 + three-strike EPISODE gate (Gate B). They are distinct scripts and never conflated.
- **Runtime model routing (never Anthropic)** - `config/models.json` routes content in strict priority Ollama Cloud Kimi 2.6 -> Ollama Cloud GLM 5.2 -> OpenRouter equivalents -> Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite, with high reasoning reserved for the primary Ollama Cloud lane (fallback lanes run default thinking; high reasoning on a fallback is its own furnace). `guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py` (furnace Guardrail 5) hard-fails any Anthropic id, provider, package, key, or host in any runtime file and asserts the routing tier; it honors the explicit per-entry `provider` field for tier detection and only fails the read-only dashboard on an actual pipeline invocation (not a documented-boundary comment or error string). Guard self-test and full scan PASS.
- **Cloudflare + furnace** - per-client Cloudflare dashboard (`<slug>-podcast`, Access allow-by-email) and hooks (`<slug>-hooks`, WAF POST-only) provisioning with a full 9-step revocation runbook and edge-only emergency mode; furnace-bounded worst-case daily spend with an alert-dedup guardrail. The provision/revoke scripts source the Cloudflare account id, zone id, Access team host, and operator Access emails from the environment (generic placeholder defaults) so no operator PII ships.
- **Integration reconciliation (this merge)** - version rolled to v18.0.0 (11 markers via `scripts/bump-version.sh`); README + install.sh skill inventory de-staled to 58 folders / 53 active + 5 archived with the Skill 58 inventory row added; PRD/design docs and runtime scripts scrubbed of operator home paths, personal emails, and Cloudflare account/zone ids. **Gates green (fresh local run):** `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (all 10 artifact dimensions OK: SKILLS-COUNT, VERSION-MARKERS all 11 at v18.0.0, CONTENT-HASH 426 roles/124 sops/19 personas, MAP-CONSISTENCY 26 client-facing skills in lockstep), `guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py` rc=0 (self-test + full scan), `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` rc=0, `qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh` rc=0, `hash-content-manifest.py --check` PASS. Annotated tag `v18.0.0` cut on the integration HEAD before merge (G1 race guard).

## [v17.0.45]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(blueprint): Departments-That-Use-Skills — Wave 1.3 owner-facing dept guides + craft self-description; how-to-use renderer reconciled with the DEPT_SKILLS overlay (rebased onto the v17.0.44 persona landing)

Closes the two remaining Wave-1 gaps of the **Departments-That-Use-Skills** PRD, all generated from the ONE source of truth `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-department-map.json` (Layer D) so they can never silently desync from the role "Skills You Operate" blocks or the CEO front-door reflex. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD. No new department; no new role. No Anthropic models; no client names.

- **Wave 1.3a — owner-facing department guides** (`scripts/stamp-dept-skill-guides.py`, landed on the branch). Stamps a marker-guarded `<!-- DEPT_SKILLS_V1 -->` "Skills This Department Can Operate For You" block into all 13 owning departments' `templates/role-library/<dept>/how-to-use-this-department.md` — the OWNER-facing twin of the specialist "Skills You Operate" block — so the guide and the `SKILL_INTENT_ROUTING_REFLEX_V1` reflex speak the same plain language ("make me some ads" → paid-advertisement). Idempotent + byte-stable; no `{{TOKEN}}` leak.
- **Wave 1.3b — craft-cluster Intent-triggers headers** (`scripts/stamp-craft-intent-triggers.py`, landed on the branch). Stamps a marker-guarded `<!-- CRAFT_INTENT_TRIGGERS_V1 -->` "Intent triggers" header into every map-referenced craft cluster README under `universal-sops/<cluster>/` (8 existing updated, 5 scaffolded: fb-ad-craft, presentation-slide-craft, presentation-image-library, presentation-design-system, video-pipeline-craft) so each cluster is self-describing to the specialist and the map generator. `universal-sops` content manifest re-generated (`hash-universal-sops-manifest.py`; 90 files, `--check` clean).
- **Renderer reconciliation (this version's fix)** — the shared owner-guide renderer `how_to_use_department.render_how_to_use` is now **DEPT_SKILLS-block-aware**. Wave 1.3a stamped the block into the committed templates AFTER the renderer's `_sanitize_block` pass, which left two pre-existing gates in `test-how-to-use-docs.sh` red: (1) the block carried two **em dashes** that survived the sanitizer and tripped the guides' em/en-dash hygiene check, and (2) `generate_how_to_use_docs.py --check` re-renders each guide and byte-compares to the committed file, so every stamped guide read as **STALE** because the renderer did not emit the block. Fix: the em dashes are removed at source in `stamp-dept-skill-guides.build_block` (the ONE block builder the MAP-CONSISTENCY gate validates), and `render_how_to_use` now injects the DEPT_SKILLS overlay itself from the SAME `build_block`/`stamp_text` functions. The renderer, the committed template, the build-time client copy, and the MAP-CONSISTENCY gate are now byte-consistent — and **live clients receive the block at build time**, not just the repo templates (closes the Wave-1.3a feature gap). The overlay is owning-dept-only and degrades gracefully (a map/module load failure renders the guide without the block and never blocks a build). 13 owning guides regenerated; only the two em-dash lines changed (everything else byte-stable).
- **Enforcement (no rot)** — `scripts/qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` MAP-CONSISTENCY dimension gained two sub-checks: **(d) DEPT-GUIDES** (every owning dept guide carries a current `DEPT_SKILLS` block) and **(e) CRAFT-READMES** (every referenced craft README carries a current Intent-triggers block), both rc 6 on drift. `how-to-use-this-department.md` is a build-time-rendered guide (NOT tracked by `hash-content-manifest.py`), so no content-hash re-stamp was required.
- **Gates green (fresh local run):** `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (**MAP-CONSISTENCY** map↔role↔guide↔craft↔reflex in lockstep — 25 client-facing skills, 40 owning roles, 13 dept guides, 13 craft READMEs; **CONTENT-HASH** current 426 roles/124 sops/19 personas; **VERSION-MARKERS** all 11 at v17.0.45), `test-how-to-use-docs.sh` 7/0, `check-skill-department-map.py` rc=0 (25 client-facing → live dept+role, 57/57 coverage), `stamp-dept-skill-guides.py --check` rc=0, `stamp-craft-intent-triggers.py --check` rc=0, `hash-content-manifest.py --check`, `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check`, `test-repo-consistency.sh` (8/8).

Repo version rolled to v17.0.45 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). **Rebased onto the v17.0.44 persona landing** (`feat/persona-set-v2.4.1-99`, merge `a429c273`) which claimed v17.0.44 first while this branch was held behind it (single onboarding writer); re-stamped from v17.0.44 → v17.0.45 to resolve the version-marker/CHANGELOG/README collision. The skills-wiring content (`skill-department-map.json`, role how-tos, AGENTS.md reflex, SOPs, stamp/qc scripts) and the persona content do not overlap — both sides kept; the persona set is undisturbed at 99. Annotated tag `v17.0.45` created before the merge (G1 race guard). Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD. No client names.

## [v17.0.43]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(blueprint): Departments-That-Use-Skills — native skill invocation (Layers B/C/D wired repo-side, map-consistency gate)

Integrates the **Departments-That-Use-Skills** implementation (PRD `Departments-That-Use-Skills`): skills become **native department capabilities** — a client benefits from a skill even when they never name it or type its slash command. The specialist reaches for the owning skill from the client's plain-language intent, dept-scoped. Repo-only; fleet fan-out HELD (existing content-hash refresh flow carries it for free on release). No new department; no new role (every client-facing skill assigned to an EXISTING owning role, PRD §6). No Anthropic models; no client names.

- **Layer D — source of truth already landed** (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/skill-department-map.json`, Wave 0): binds all 59 skills → owning department(s) → owning specialist role(s) → plain-language intent triggers → craft-cluster execution SOP. 27 client-facing skills carry intent triggers + live owners; 32 infra/build-time skills are `client_facing:false` with a `dept_owner` and no triggers. [count corrected W4.12 — Skill 58's landing (v18.0.0) moved this 57→58 total / 25→26 client-facing and the prose here was missed at the time; recounted live against `skill-department-map.json`. Moved to 59 total / 27 client-facing when W4.1 landed the Skill 59 map entry.]
- **Layer B — durable role knowledge** (`scripts/stamp-skills-you-operate.py`, NEW generator/validator). Stamps a marker-guarded `<!-- SKILLS_YOU_OPERATE_V1 -->` block into every owning role's `how-to.md` §8 "Tools You Use" (40 owning role templates, generated FROM the map): the skill, the client phrasing that should trigger reaching for it, the on-box path, and the `universal-sops/<cluster>/` playbook pointer. Idempotent + byte-stable (strip-then-insert; the `--check` mode feeds the gate). No canonical `{{TOKEN}}` in any block (neutral-render token-leak invariant preserved).
- **Layer C — front-door reflex** (`scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh`, NEW `SKILL_INTENT_ROUTING_REFLEX_V1`). A compact, normal-strength intent-cluster→department catalog (generated from the map, all 13 owning departments) injected into the workspace `AGENTS.md`: on a plain-language intent the CEO's FIRST action is `mc-route.sh <owning-department>` + ack — never self-intake, never "which skill?". Sits ALONGSIDE the strict presentation `PRESENTATION_ROUTING_REFLEX_V2` (REFLEX 0, fires first). Marker-guarded + **byte-idempotent** (strip-then-insert after the presentation reflex END marker; proven stable across 3 consecutive applies; `bash -n` clean; PA-only boxes skip it).
- **New SOP** — `universal-sops/native-skill-invocation.md` (the doctrine + the four-layer map; there was no existing SOP stating "a specialist reaches for the owning skill from intent, dept-scoped, before authoring by hand"). **Updated (not duplicated):** `answering-how-to-use-questions.md` (surface skill-backed capabilities, not just the roster), `master-orchestrator-dept/SOP-00-Owner-Task-Routing.md` (routing table now points at the reflex catalog), `ADDING-DEPARTMENTS-ROLES-SOPS.md` (new **Scenario E — wiring a skill to a department/role**).
- **AGENTS.md** — new `NATIVE_SKILL_INVOCATION_V1` doctrine block + **N39** rule-index row (pointer, not paste — detail lives in the SOP + map).
- **Enforcement (new map-consistency assertion)** — `scripts/qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` gains a **MAP-CONSISTENCY** dimension (rc 6 on drift): proves (a) STRUCTURE — every client-facing skill resolves to a live dept + owning role, exactly-one-primary, map↔disk skill-folder coverage, infra ownership, execution-SOPs resolve (reuses `check-skill-department-map.run_checks`); (b) LAYER B — every owning role carries a CURRENT Skills-You-Operate block; (c) LAYER C — the reflex catalog routes every department that owns a client-facing skill. `check-skill-department-map.py` refactored to expose `run_checks()` for reuse.
- **Content-hash re-stamp (MANDATORY)** — `hash-content-manifest.py` re-run over the 40 edited role templates (40 `content_version` bumps); `universal-sops` manifest re-stamped for the new + edited SOPs (85 files). So `detect-stale-artifacts.py` will flag exactly the affected clients on release.
- **Gates green (fresh local run):** `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` rc=0 (six-source + 9→10 artifact dims incl. **MAP-CONSISTENCY** OK; CONTENT-HASH current 426 roles/124 sops/19 personas; VERSION-MARKERS all 11 at v17.0.43), `check-skill-department-map.py` rc=0 (25 client-facing → live dept+role; 57/57 coverage), `stamp-skills-you-operate.py --check` rc=0 (40 roles), `hash-content-manifest.py --check`, `hash-universal-sops-manifest.py --check`, `register-library-additions.py --check`, `test-repo-consistency.sh` (8/8), `bash -n scripts/apply-fleet-standards.sh`.

Repo version rolled to v17.0.43 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). Annotated tag `v17.0.43` created and pushed BEFORE this release PR (G1 race guard). ## [v17.0.42]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(presentations): teleprompter three-mode upgrade (Manual / Auto / VOICE-tracking) + Skill-51 frame visual-identity seed craft + kie_generate drift re-unification + renderer model-truth fix

Presentation-department improvement pass (Skill 51 Signature Presentation + the Presentations engine's teleprompter and image pipeline). One review→fix→QC→verify train; no floors lowered, no SOP structure changed, no Anthropic models anywhere, no client names.

- **Teleprompter — THREE selectable scroll modes (`build_teleprompter.py`, Presenter's Speech Writer / SOP 9.2).** The mode control is now a segmented **Manual / Auto / Voice** selector (existing behaviors kept: Auto IS the previous "Traditional" fixed-speed engine, Voice IS the previous "Spoken" engine, both unchanged at their core; legacy persisted `traditional`/`spoken` localStorage values migrate automatically). **Voice-tracking hardened into a genuinely usable mode:** (1) an **adjustable sensitivity slider** (persisted) maps one control onto the aligner's fuzz tolerance + lookahead window + backtrack (strict word-for-word ⇄ loose paraphrase-friendly; the midpoint reproduces the previous fixed tuning); (2) the fuzzy aligner's lookahead **window now SLIDES with the matches** inside one utterance, so a long final recognition result advances the full distance actually spoken instead of at most one fixed window; (3) **manual-jump resync** — the rail / arrow keys / clicker RE-SEED the aligner cursor at the target slide's first token and move the tween target with the jump (previously the voice tween yanked the view back to the pre-jump position, fighting the presenter); (4) **hardened recognition restart** — deferred re-arm (an immediate `start()` inside `onend` can throw and silently kill listening), recognizer recreation on a failed restart, a 15s no-event watchdog, and a bounded consecutive-hard-failure fallback to Auto with a notice; (5) entering Voice mode **seeds at the current slide** (can start mid-deck) and Space becomes **Follow/Hold** (hold freezes the scroll while recognition + the spoken-word highlight keep tracking); (6) the current-word highlight tint now **derives from the locked brand accent** via `color-mix` (amber rgba retained as the older-engine fallback) instead of a hardcoded house color. Manual mode: Space = next slide (clicker-style paging), Play control hidden. Mic-denied / unsupported still falls back gracefully; the Voice option stays hidden where the Web Speech API is absent. **Verified end-to-end in a real browser** (mode switching, manual paging, auto-scroll motion, aligner exact/paraphrase/garbage-rejection/resync/sensitivity assertions all pass); the Python-side contract (`parse_speech`, `build_html`, `read_design_system_accent`, placeholders, self-contained + 20,000-byte AF-BUNDLE-COMPLETE floors) is unchanged and the M5 preflight tests stay green.
- **Skill 51 — per-frame VISUAL IDENTITY + suggested-image seed craft (images markedly better, by construction).** Each of the four frame templates (The Rulebook / The Vault / The Quest / The Original) now carries a **"Visual identity & suggested-image seed craft (Directive 4)"** section: the six seed anchors (archetype A1–A5 + subject/emotion + setting + light + locked-STYLE-BLOCK palette + baked-copy composition zone), the frame's **controlling visual motif** (journey-in-light / one-metaphor-escalating-light / editorial-rule-plate-series / derive-then-lock), per-phase visual direction, and series discipline (anti-template archetype variation with palette+motif held constant; logo/likeness ride image-to-image references per SOP-IMG-01 — the seed never re-describes the mark). `SKILL.md` points seed authors at the contract. The 9,000–18,000-char rich-prompt floor and all SP provers are untouched (seeds feed the Prompt Author; they never replace the floor). Skill 51 `1.0.3 → 1.0.4`.
- **`kie_generate.py` drift re-unification (the verified Kie.ai pipeline, `gpt-image-2-image-to-image` default, 16:9, 2K).** The two shipped copies (presentation-render template + presentations scripts) had EACH gained a fix the other lacked: the scripts copy had the HIGH-3 `$OPENCLAW_SECRETS` override but not FIX-IMG-03 (optional per-entry `aspect_ratio`/`resolution`) nor the runtime dead-endpoint guard in `_http_json`; the render-template copy had the reverse. Both copies now carry the UNION of all three fixes plus an explicit two-location LOCKSTEP note; logic is byte-identical between them (doc/usage strings differ only by location).
- **`build_deck.py` model-truth fix.** The render banner, the process-manifest record, and the final JSON summary now report `gpt-image-2-image-to-image` when a `--logo` URL is composited via `input_urls` (previously they claimed `gpt-image-2-text-to-image` unconditionally, so the audit trail misstated the model on every logo-composite run). No consumer parses `modelUsed` (verified across prove-deck / delivery_gate / sync_check / test_preflight), so this is a pure truth fix. `CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN.sha256` re-stamped to the new `build_deck.py + run_signature_deck.py` hash.
- **Doc truth + hashes.** `presenters-speech-writer.md` SOP 9.2 tool description rewritten to match the shipped teleprompter (content_sha re-stamped, content_version `1.0.5 → 1.0.6`, dept aggregate re-hashed). Verified (not assumed) that TOOLS.md's "deterministic pipeline is t2i-only" claim is still TRUE for the canonical-entry path (`presentation-canonical-entry.sh` passes no `--logo`), so it was deliberately left unchanged.
- **Gates green (fresh local run):** `sync_check.py` (manifest 23, 24 phases / 150 autofails / 37 roles IN SYNC), full `test_preflight.py` battery, `gate_integrity_check.py` (86/87 codes covered), `runner_gate_integrity_check.py`, `doctrine_residual_check.py`, `presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh` (85/85), `hash-content-manifest.py --check`, and `51-signature-presentation/verify.sh` (all provers self-test PASS).

Repo version rolled to v17.0.42 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). Annotated tag `v17.0.42` created and pushed BEFORE this release PR (G1 race guard). No client names.

## [v17.0.41]  -  2026-07-06  -  feat(persona): land blackceo-house-voice fallback (FDN-1/F3.1) + hunt-thomas CODE-craft specialist (DEP-6/F3.8) — 81->83 personas, prebuilt-index v2.4.0

Reconciled landing of the two remaining persona-matching-overhaul library additions on top of the published **prebuilt-index-v2.4.0** embedding asset (83 personas, `gemini-index.sqlite.gz` sha256 `25f8933c…`). Supersedes and closes PRs #532 (FDN-1) and #539 (DEP-6): neither persona alone yields a manifest consistent with the single 83-persona published asset (FDN-1 alone = 82), so both land in ONE mergeable change that takes the fleet library `81 -> 83` with the count triad and the `persona-set-asset-consistency-guard` green.

- **FDN-1 / F3.1 / Q1+Q2 — guaranteed no-naked-task fallback.** New hand-authored, brand-neutral **`blackceo-house-voice`** persona seeded as a permanent fleet fallback (`fallback:true`, domains `["communication"]`, no perspective/specialty tags — excluded from the normal scoring funnel so it never out-scores a real specialist). `persona-selector-v2.py` pins `DEFAULT_PERSONA_FALLBACK="blackceo-house-voice"`, drops `fallback:true` personas from `list_available_personas`, and adds `_resolve_default_persona_id` (client-sovereignty order: `company-config.default_persona_id` -> first real seed key -> pinned constant) so SELECT mode never emits `persona_id:null` unless `no_persona_required` is set. Mechanical tasks keep the truthful `no_persona_required:true` flag AND carry a `governance_persona_id` oversight pointer. `company-config.template.json` gains `default_persona_id` / `governance_persona_id`.
- **DEP-6 / F3.8 / Q3 — CODE + IMAGE craft-slot coverage.** New **`hunt-thomas-pragmatic-programmer`** (The Pragmatic Programmer) as the first true software-craft specialist, plus the new controlled-vocab domainTag **`software-craft`** wired in one change across `persona-categories.json.domainTags`, `DEPT_DOMAIN_TAGS['engineering']`, `_CATEGORY_DOMAINS['code']`, and `CRAFT_PRIMARY_DOMAINS['code']`. IMAGE slot surfaced via `visual-communication` joining the design craft-primaries (budelmann + opara earn the image craft bonus; rohde not displaced). `build-workforce.py` `dept_to_domains` kept byte-identical (mirror invariant).
- **Asset / manifest.** `INDEX-MANIFEST.json` -> `persona_count`/`canonical_persona_count`/`embedded_persona_count` = **83**, `release_tag` `prebuilt-index-v2.4.0`, `asset_rebuild_required:false`; `persona_set_md5` re-stamped to the shipped 83-key categories (which additionally registers `software-craft` — a metadata-only re-pin, persona membership unchanged, NOT an asset rebuild). INSTALL.md counts re-baselined to 83 / ~1,189 section rows.
- **Tests:** `persona-fallback-invariant.test.py` (8/8), `dep6-craft-slot-coverage.test.py` (21/21), `prebuilt-index-section-tagged.test.sh` re-baselined to 83 / 1,189 / v2.4.0 (25/25). Repo-consistency + version-markers + content-hash + asset-consistency triad all green. No client names.

## [v17.0.40]  -  2026-07-06  -  chore(release): Skill 38 v1.8.0 CloseBot-alignment release tag (PR #551, cards U-1..U-17)

Consolidated ANNOTATED release tag for **Skill 38 (conversation playbook) v1.8.0 — the CloseBot-alignment upgrade**. This is the single skill-behavior change merged to `main` since the v17.0.39 Wave-2 consolidation (PR #551, merged as `5f882b15`), which rolled its own code + `38-conversational-ai-system/skill-version.txt` → `1.8.0` but was merged WITHOUT an individual release tag. This version stamps that train as ONE annotated release so the fix-ledger, CHANGELOG, and git tags agree end-to-end. This release itself touches ONLY the 11 version markers (via `scripts/bump-version.sh`) + this CHANGELOG entry + the README NOTE — there is NO new skill-behavior change beyond what already shipped in PR #551. No client names.

Covered (Skill 38 v1.8.0, PR #551 — cards U-1..U-17, GROUP-0 setup through GROUP-6 integration/release):

- **U-1..U-7 — Foundations & conversation-flow alignment** (GROUP-1/GROUP-2 waves): CloseBot-pattern conversation-flow and behavior alignment for the playbook.
- **U-8/U-10/U-15 — Model-chain wiring & visuals** (CARD-12/CARD-13/CARD-11): model-chain wiring plus the model-chain/visual card set.

## [v17.0.36]  -  2026-07-05  -  feat(persona-selector): SOP-aware matching — persona-selector-v2.py consumes the governing SOP + sops.persona_hints (Persona-Matching-Overhaul DEP-1 / F3.4-selector + F4.2)

Persona-Matching-Overhaul Phase-2 dependent train DEP-1, relanded on `main` after the Wave-1 consolidation (v17.0.35). Skill 23's `persona-selector-v2.py` now makes the persona match a function of **Task + SOP + Persona**: new optional `--sop-slug` / `--sop-name` / `--sop-steps` inputs are folded into ONE composite match query (category inference + a single shared Stage-C / Layer-5 embed — no extra embed call), and `--sop-hints` (the previously write-only `sops.persona_hints`, killing F4.2) are UNIONed department-agnostically into the candidate pool and granted a bounded, task_fit-coupled additive bonus (capped 0.30, strictly below the specialty cap). With no `--sop-*` flags the selection is byte-identical to the pre-SOP path (inertness). New hermetic regression guard `test-persona-selector-sop-aware.sh` (8/8): inertness, poison-hint bound, pool union / never-to-zero, tiebreak, SOP-name-fold-drives-recall, non-installed-hint-ignored. Selector-side foundation feeding DEP-2 (Command Center wiring, already merged), DEP-4 and DEP-7.

This is a skill-23 content change, so per the merge-train doctrine it rides a repo-wide `/version` roll to v17.0.36 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`) to clear the skill-23 content gate G3 — `skill-version.txt` is one of the 11 lockstep markers. It is a **version roll, not a repo release**: no annotated tag is cut here; the tag rides the next consolidated wave release (the intermediate marker bumps 17.0.32 → 17.0.34 landed the same way before the v17.0.35 consolidation). No client names. Fix IDs: F3.4 (selector half), F4.2.

## [v17.0.32]  -  2026-07-05  -  feat(Skill 38 conversational-ai-system v1.8.0): CloseBot Alignment Upgrade (17 cards U-1..U-17)

This train ships the Skill 38 CloseBot Alignment Upgrade as its OWN unified release. Skill 38's independent `38-conversational-ai-system/skill-version.txt` rolls 1.7.13 to 1.8.0; the 11 repo version markers roll v17.0.31 to v17.0.32 in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`. No client names. All changes are scoped to `38-conversational-ai-system/` plus the shared QC-gate wiring (`.github/workflows/qc-static.yml`) and one operating SOP under `universal-sops/`.

The upgrade closes the 11 CloseBot feature-parity gaps (U-1 per-phase tool gating THE GATE, U-2 tag-driven workflow exits, U-3 smart FAQ closed learning loop, U-4 objective metadata on phases, U-5 persona registry, U-6 client test mode, U-7 standard lifecycle status tags, U-8 model fallback chain, U-9 machine-readable playbook declares block, U-10 per-workflow model tier override, U-11 workflow visual on every build), adds four GHL-coverage updates (U-12 multi-calendar routing, U-13 opportunity and pipeline stage sync, U-14 human task creation on handoff, U-15 Convert and Flow install snapshot), ships the canonical playbook parser (U-16 `tools/playbook_engine.py`), and lands the organizational operating SOP (U-17 departments, roles, and `universal-sops/SOP-CONV-AI-01-OPERATING-THE-CONVERSATIONAL-AI-SYSTEM.md`).

Every new surface is OPERATOR-ONLY with no customer-invokable path (a customer naming a tag, tool, calendar, pipeline stage, or persona does nothing); `escalate_to_human` is always granted and can never be gated off. All new JSONL sinks are PII-free and seeded only via `scripts/25-seed-round3-feature-files.sh`. Every new QC gate is wired into BOTH `scripts/11-run-qc-checklist.sh` AND `.github/workflows/qc-static.yml` and ships with a negative `.test.sh`. Skill 38 SELF-COUNTS recounted on disk (protocols 45 to 51, scripts 70 to 88, references 22 to 25). `references/v6.0-source-playbook.md` is untouched. Full per-update detail in `38-conversational-ai-system/CHANGELOG.md` under [1.8.0].

## [v17.0.30]  -  2026-07-05  -  fix(Skill 45 design-intelligence-library): coded DIU enforcement gates, QC clobber-safety test, embedding honesty, SOP-twin dedup (T-45-design-intelligence, Wave-0 merge-train)

Wave-0 hardening pass on the Design Intelligence Unit (Skill 45) and its materialized graphics-department SOPs (Skill 23). Turns three prose-only "binding gates" into MECHANICAL gates, makes the QC prove its own two-zone clobber-safety claim, stops the docs from advertising an embedding index that does not ship, and removes seven byte-identical duplicate role-SOP twins. No behavior change on a healthy governed run; every change fails louder or fails closed. Repo version rolled to v17.0.30 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`; Skill 45's independent line rolled 1.2.3 → 1.3.0). No client names. Fix IDs: FIX-XC-03h, FIX-XC-11c, FIX-S36-29, FIX-S36-30.

- **FIX-XC-03h (P2)** — new `45-design-intelligence-library/scripts/diu_validator.py` (Python stdlib, zero third-party deps) makes three binding gates mechanical, mirroring Skill 47's deterministic-gate pattern: `prompt-caps` enforces the MODEL-SPECS SHORT ≤ 500 / MEDIUM ≤ 2,800 / LONG ≤ 18,000-char tier table (over-cap ⇒ hard exit 3, `AF-DIU-PROMPT-CAP`); `route-check` is the SOP-DIU-611 §D.1 routing interlock as code (audience/webinar/funnel/virtual-event decks cannot run on the Rotation Engine ⇒ exit 2, `AF-DIU-ROUTING-INTERLOCK`); `fidelity` writes an append-only TEST-PROTOCOL §5 receipt (avg ≥ 4.0, no dim < 3, zero hard-rule violations) plus a per-(card,dimension) 3-strike counter ⇒ CDO escalation (exit 5, `AF-DIU-3-STRIKE`). SKILL.md gains a "Binding enforcement — coded gates" section + a `scripts/` inventory entry.
- **FIX-S36-29 (P2)** — `qc-design-intelligence-library.sh` gains an INDEX clobber-safety test (seeds a sentinel INDEX.md in a temp home, re-runs the documented `cp -n` seed, and asserts the populated copy survives byte-for-byte — proving the two-zone contract the SKILL.md claims); corrects the `_system` protocol-file count to `-maxdepth 1` so the documented total resolves to **19** (the nested `_system/templates/NAMED-STYLES.md` seed template is not a protocol file); the version banner now reads `skill-version.txt`. SKILL.md/CHANGELOG "16 library files" corrected to 19.
- **FIX-XC-11c (P2)** — semantic embedding search is marked NOT YET AVAILABLE across INSTRUCTIONS.md, CORE_UPDATES.md, and the SKILL.md read-order: the `index-style-cards-embedding.sh` companion script ships nowhere, so the activation command, `.embedding-index.json` references, and the Registrar embedding-refresh duty are removed. INDEX.md lookup (exact ID / friendly Sig # alias / category scan) is documented as the available surface until the index ships.
- **FIX-S36-30 (P3 bundle)** — wired the DIU production lifecycle onto the Command Center board (CDO SOP 9.12, reusing Skill 48's fail-soft `cc_board.py`): Gate 4 lives in the **review** column and the `review → done` transition is the auditable evidence Gate 4 occurred, closing the "Gate 4 skippable with no evidence" gap. Deleted seven byte-identical non-`SOP--` role-SOP twins under `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/graphics/sops/` (chief-design-officer, deck-systems-specialist, fidelity-tester, generation-operator, healer-graphics, photo-shoot-director, style-analyst) — the `SOP--` variant is canonical; re-pointed references in `deck-systems-specialist.md` and `SOP-DIU-502.md`, and re-stamped `_index.json` (dropped the seven duplicate `sops[]` entries + re-hashed the three edited artifacts), verified with `hash-content-manifest.py --check`.

No client names. Fix IDs: FIX-XC-02a, FIX-XC-02b, FIX-XC-04a, FIX-XC-04b, FIX-XC-04h, FIX-XC-09e.

- **FIX-XC-02b (P2)** — `full-funnel-pipeline/funnel_rubrics.py`: de-hardcoded the `hormozi-100m-offers` persona award in R-COPY (`persona_grounded`) and R-STRUCTURE. Both now read the ACTUAL selected persona from `persona-selection-log.md` (both the `- selected_persona:` harness form and the `"applied_persona":` live-run form) and credit the matched slug — a correct non-hormozi match is no longer punished; a miss fires only when the log is absent or the slug is unechoed in `copy.md`.
- **FIX-XC-04a (P1)** — `shared-utils/fab_qc.py` D2 now enforces lengthClass-keyed copy floors (body slots ≥ 40 words; short slots — headline/subhead/CTA/button — exempt at ≥ 4; page-level whole-funnel floors keyed to the matched template's lengthClass: short ≥ 350 / medium ≥ 700 / long-form ≥ 1,800). Below-floor is a HARD MISS on a LIVE artifact that cannot be averaged away; it triggers the bounded re-author loop (verifier ≠ author; ≤ 5 attempts). Automation-step copy keeps the light 4-word floor (funnel-scope train). Consumer fixtures re-authored (`06-ghl-install-pages/tests/test_v2_dispatcher.py`, `tests/unit/fab-qc.test.py`). Numbers are proposed defaults (RULING R4 tunes the constants table).
- **FIX-XC-04h (P2)** — `funnel_rubrics.py` R-COPY gains a load-bearing `length_vs_funnel_type` sub-check (weight 3.0 → hard-miss below floor) keyed to the per-funnel-type depth table (squeeze/opt-in 300 / 2-step-application 400 / webinar-VSL 800 / long-form-sales 1,500) — an approved-but-thin 150-word `copy.md` can no longer score 10/10 for a long-form funnel. `conversion-copywriter.md` Gate 1/Gate 2 + `qc-specialist--marketing.md` SOP 9.1 gain depth criterion (f): under-band or thin-section copy is returned as a revision. (Two new pipeline tests: 45 → 47 collected; CI asserts the count.)
- **FIX-XC-04b (P1)** — `conversion-copywriter.md` §8 `copy.md` contract expanded to the full direct-response section inventory keyed to the funnel-spec `pageStructure`, with a §8a per-funnel-type depth-band table and SOP 9.2 steps 10a/10b (section-by-section authoring + one expansion pass before PENDING-QC).
- **FIX-XC-02a (P2)** — copywriter-persona Step-0 grounding wired end-to-end: Skill 49 (`prove_sf_intake.py` → `AF-FUN-INTAKE-PERSONA-LOG`) and Skill 56 (`prove_sp_intake.py` → `AF-SP56-INTAKE-PERSONA-LOG`) fail CLOSED when the run dir carries no `persona-selection-log.md` naming a REGISTERED persona slug (mirrors FAB-QC D4). Goldens (`golden-daybreak`, `golden-momentum`) + harness now carry a real selection log; the Section-4 Task-Mode seam is documented in `SOP-FUNNEL-02-COPY.md`, `SOP-SALESPAGE-01-DR-ASSET-STACK.md`, `funnel-copy-prompts.md`, and `PROMPT-SEAMS.md`.
- **FIX-XC-09e (P2)** — Skill 49 (`signature-funnel-entry.sh`) and Skill 56 (`sales-page-assets-entry.sh`) resolve the CLIENT's own execution/content-tier authoring model, write `routing/model-content-receipt.json`, and gate it via `prove_sf_cert.py --model-receipt` / `prove_sp_cert.py --model-receipt` (`AF-*-MODEL-TIER` + `AF-*-MODEL-NOANTHROPIC`): a run cannot certify without recording that the client's own strongest model wrote the copy, and Anthropic is hard-banned by the provider FIELD. Prover pins re-minted; manifests carry the new AF codes.

## [v17.0.27]  -  2026-07-05  -  fix(Presentation department — the gold-standard template): T-presentation-dept hardening pass (FIX-PRES-01..09)

Wave-0 hardening pass on the presentation department — the gold-standard enforcement template that every other department's QC discipline is modeled on. Nine ledger items (FIX-PRES-01 … FIX-PRES-09) close silent-bypass, stale-state, and board-invisibility seams in the governed deck pipeline (materialized under Skill 23; methodology layer is Skill 51). No behavior change on a healthy governed run; every change fails louder or fails closed. Repo version rolled to v17.0.27 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`). No client names.

- **FIX-PRES-01 (P1)** — `QC_SKIP_PRESENTATION_DEPS=1` no longer silently bypasses the GATE-1 runtime-deps hard gate on a live run (`presentation-canonical-entry.sh`): a LIVE run may skip ONLY via a logged `owner_skip_approval` token; the env var is honored ONLY with a `.test-context` run-dir marker; every honored bypass appends a `dep_gate_bypassed` audit record to `process_manifest.json`. Mirrored (loud, audited) in `qc-completeness.sh`. The `tests/unit/presentation-deps-gate.test.sh` harness now drops the `.test-context` marker so its GATE-2 functional cases run on a dep-less CI runner.
