- **FIX-PRES-02 (P2)** — `update-skills.sh` runs an idempotent, fail-soft presentation-deps converge step (mirrors install.sh Step 6.5) with a hard end-of-update warning when any of the four deps is still missing.
- **FIX-PRES-03 (P2)** — new GATE 1b asserts the Skill-48 `ghl_media` module is importable BEFORE any paid Kie render (exit 8 `PRESENTATION_GHL_MODULE_MISSING`, owner-token skippable).
- **FIX-PRES-04 (P2)** — `create_role_workspaces.py` scripts-copy filter now includes `.sha256` and `.pdf` (always-overwrite) so `CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN.sha256` + the presenter-layout PDF reach the materialized department; GATE 3's hash-pin no longer silently disarms.
- **FIX-PRES-05 (P2)** — `test_cc_contract.py` replaces a hardcoded operator-machine session path with env-driven `CC_VALIDATION_TS_PATH`; `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` bans the dash-separated session-path spelling of the operator home (the form that evaded the slash-only ban).
- **FIX-PRES-06 (P2)** — `cc-compat.json` Skill-51 note rewritten to current scope (methodology layer that DOES card via `cc_board.py` and depends on the CC presentations done-gate, guaranteed at the pinned minVersion v4.59.1).
- **FIX-PRES-07 (P3)** — `run_signature_deck.py` fails CLOSED when `phase_verifiers.py` is missing beside the runner for a governed phase; a degraded pass is allowed ONLY under an explicit test/CI marker.
- **FIX-PRES-08 (P3)** — CC card opened at the runner's Phase-0 pre-flight (idempotent; render-begin reuses the stamped `cc_task_id`); new `cc_board.py --reconcile` + in-process `reconcile()` replay a transport-failed advance so a delivered deck is never stranded at `in_progress`.
- **FIX-PRES-09 (P3 bundle)** — Skill-51 prover root discovery for a materialized dept tree; presentations dept agent gets a per-dept tools allow-list WITHOUT the three generate tools (imagery flows through the governed Kie pipeline); the reassert-presentation-deps cron dropped from a `*/15` furnace to a daily `0 4 * * *` backstop with event-shaped self-heal on the GATE-1 deps-missing path; stale comments corrected to the 9,000-char prompt floor / manifest v23 and the retired 5,000-char floor added to `retired-doctrine-patterns.json`.

## [v17.0.26]  -  2026-07-05  -  fix(skill-43-graphify): stop the semantic map 404-ing on every install (/v1 base_url), kill the always-red install QC, correct the false "free + local" model claim, and pin the re-map backend (canary merge-train T-43-graphify)

Single-skill, low-blast-radius canary for the skills-analysis fix campaign — mostly deterministic doc/config/QC fixes to `43-graphify-knowledge-graph/`, no shared files touched. Bundles five ledger items (FIX-S36-21, FIX-S36-22, FIX-S36-23, FIX-S36-24, FIX-XC-09d) into one release to exercise the full merge-train mechanics (multiple fixes → one PR + version bump + annotated tag + CHANGELOG + README) before the wider Wave-0 fan-out. No client names.

- **FIX-S36-21 — semantic map 404 on every install (P1).** INSTALL.md / CORE_UPDATES.md / SKILL.md set `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434`, which graphify passes verbatim as the OpenAI-style base_url → `POST /chat/completions` 404s → every install's semantic map failed. Pinned all three to the `…:11434/v1` form and added install-QC assertions on the `/v1` form (plus a guard that no bare `:11434` base URL remains).
- **FIX-S36-22 — install QC always-red (P1).** `qc-graphify-knowledge-graph.sh` hard-asserted `skill-version.txt == 1.0.0` while the file was `1.0.2`, so QC failed on every correct install and agents learned to ignore it. The assertion now checks valid semver AND agreement with the SKILL.md frontmatter `version:` field (no hardcoded literal to drift). Backfilled the missing 1.0.1 / 1.0.2 skill CHANGELOG entries (pairs with FIX-XC-13e).
- **FIX-S36-23 — false "free + nothing leaves the box" claim (P2).** The docs defaulted `deepseek-v4-pro:cloud`, but a `:cloud` model runs on Ollama Cloud off-box and bills the client. Default is now a genuinely-local model (e.g. `qwen2.5-coder:7b`); every doc discloses that `:cloud` models are off-box + billed and owner-opt-in only.
- **FIX-S36-24 — doc/reference gaps (P3).** INSTALL Step 6 now passes the mapped folder to `verify-graphify-install.sh` (graph + hook checks are advisory/WARN-only); `references/GRAPHIFY-COMMANDS.md` gains the canonical `graphify extract . --backend ollama` row and an `OLLAMA_BASE_URL`/`OLLAMA_MODEL` env-var table.
- **FIX-XC-09d — bare re-map sovereignty leak (P2).** A bare `/graphify .` re-map lets graphify's `detect_backend()` auto-pick a resident API key and can route the client's corpus to a paid/Anthropic backend. INSTRUCTIONS.md / CORE_UPDATES.md / INSTALL.md now require the explicit `graphify extract . --backend ollama`, and CORE_UPDATES carries a binding BACKEND-PIN rule flagging the bare re-map as a violation.
- Skill 43 version rolled 1.0.2 → 1.0.3. Repo version rolled to v17.0.26 (11 markers in lockstep via `scripts/bump-version.sh`).

## [v17.0.25]  -  2026-07-05  -  fix(book-pipeline): make the workspace↔repo persona divergence STRUCTURALLY impossible — ONE atomic "publish personas to the fleet" command + early/pre-roll divergence guards

Closes the recurring persona-set divergence for good. Every time books were added, the persona INDEX asset + manifest could advance while the repo persona LIBRARY (the `22-…/personas/<slug>/` blueprint dirs + `persona-categories.json` seed) was NOT committed — so `main` could not go green at the new count and a fleet roll silently shipped the OLD count until someone hand-caught the repo up (65 vs 81 was the latest instance). The N38 count-triad only ran at CI / PR / build-preflight, so the divergence was always caught LATE. This release moves all four coupled artifacts together in ONE atomic run and adds guards that fail loud EARLY (pre-commit) and on the roll path. Everything new is hermetic and stdlib-only; no client names or ids appear on any changed line; the shipped 81-persona SET and its published `prebuilt-index-v2.3.0` asset are unchanged (triad stays green at 81).

### Verified root cause
The Skill-22 book pipeline (`add-persona-from-source.sh`, `pipeline/orchestrator.py`, `scripts/persona-inbox-watcher.sh`) is a **workspace-only writer**: it registers a persona under `~/.openclaw/workspace/data/coaching-personas/` (VPS: `/data/.openclaw/…`) and never writes the repo checkout. `shared-utils/prebuilt-index/build-and-publish.sh` automated only artifacts (c) manifest + (d) release asset — it *reads* the repo blueprint dirs / categories to compute counts and refuses to publish on a mismatch, but never *writes* them. So (a) blueprint dirs + (b) `persona-categories.json` were a hand edit that lagged, and the triad `blueprint dirs == categories keys == manifest.persona_count == canonical_persona_count` was enforced only in CI (`persona-set-asset-consistency-guard.yml`, `qc-static.yml`) and build-start preflight (`lib-onboarding-state.sh:oc_repo_consistency_ok`) — never on the roll path (`update-skills.sh` Step U6b provisioned without it).

### The ONE atomic command — `pipeline/publish-personas-to-fleet.sh`
From the current workspace persona set it brings ALL FOUR into lockstep in one re-runnable pass: (a) copies each `persona-blueprint.md` into the repo blueprint dir **sanitized** of operator-local absolute paths (only the shippable file); (b) merges the workspace entries into `persona-categories.json` with **controlled-vocabulary** tag validation (`domain` ⊆ `domainTags`, `perspective` ⊆ `perspectiveTags`, kebab-case `custom`); (c)+(d) delegates to `build-and-publish.sh` (HASH-SKIP delta embed — no furnace — recompute counts/sha256/`persona_set_md5`, bump the manifest, publish the asset); then **refuses to complete** unless the count triad AND the published asset's sha256 all equal the same **N**. Fully **atomic**: it snapshots every repo file it may touch and **rolls back** on ANY failure (vocab violation, asset-build failure, a triad that does not agree) — a failed run leaves **no half-committed state**. **Idempotent**: nothing new ⇒ no-op (no re-embed, no re-tag). `--no-asset` (hermetic counts-only) and `--dry-run` modes for tests / cheap proofs.

### Guards that make it un-forgettable (fail loud EARLY, not just at roll time)
- **Terminal pipeline phase** — `add-persona-from-source.sh` now writes a `.fleet-publish-pending.json` marker + prints a LOUD banner after every add ("in the WORKSPACE but NOT published to the fleet — run publish-personas-to-fleet.sh").
- **Standalone divergence guard** — `pipeline/assert-personas-published.sh` compares workspace ↔ repo ↔ manifest (and the pending marker) and errors with the exact remediation command; `--repo-only` runs the hermetic triad.
- **Pre-commit** — `.githooks/pre-commit` blocks a commit that stages a persona artifact while the triad disagrees.
- **Pre-roll** — `update-skills.sh` Step U6b now runs the repo triad (fail-closed) BEFORE provisioning: a roll off a divergent/stale library REFUSES to ship the persona set instead of silently shipping the old count.
- **Shared core** — `pipeline/persona_fleet.py` (stdlib-only) is the single implementation of enumerate / sanitize / sync-categories / triad, reused by all of the above.

### Tests + docs
- **Regression lock:** `tests/unit/publish-personas-to-fleet.test.sh` (12 assertions, hermetic, bash 3.2 + 5.x): (a) a mock workspace persona propagates so repo dirs + categories + manifest all move to N together and the triad passes (and the blueprint is sanitized); (b) an artificial index-at-N / repo-at-N-1 divergence makes the guard fail with the actionable message; (c) a mid-run out-of-vocab tag leaves the repo in its EXACT pre-run state (rollback proven — no orphan blueprint dir).
- **Docs:** `22-…/PIPELINE.md` gains "Adding books → publishing personas to the fleet (the ONE command)"; `23-…/ADDING-PERSONAS.md` now leads with the single command.

## [v17.0.24]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(book-pipeline): make the retry-storm STRUCTURALLY impossible (fail-fast/preflight/circuit-breaker/global-budget/per-model clamp) AND stop detached builds from orphaning (parent-liveness watchdog + process-group reap + targeted reaper + single-run lock)

Two structural safety layers for the book-to-persona build, on top of the v17.0.22/23 provider-reliability bug fixes. v17.0.23 removed the *causes* of the recent ~1300-request all-4xx storm; this release makes such a storm — and a detached build that keeps billing `:cloud` after its launcher is gone — impossible **by construction**, not merely less likely. Everything is hermetic: the two new test suites inject a fake `aiohttp` / use a sandbox HOME and mock child processes; no live workspace, no network, no real model calls, no real key. No client names or ids appear on any changed line.

### Anti-storm structural guardrails (orchestrator.py)
Even with the bugs fixed, an unforeseen deterministic failure could in principle still fan out across phases, books, and fallback tiers. These five guardrails bound total request volume regardless of which provider misbehaves:
- **Fail-fast, never retry a deterministic 4xx.** `400/401/402/403/404/408/422` raise a `_DeterministicHTTPError` on the FIRST response (no per-provider retry, no fallback-tier amplification); only `429` and `5xx` take the bounded exponential backoff, hard-capped at **2 retries** per provider (`PROVIDER_MAX_RETRIES`). Applied uniformly in `call_ollama_cloud`, `call_openrouter`, and `call_codex`.
- **Preflight auth/credit probe.** Before a build, `preflight_providers()` fires ONE tiny probe per distinct phase model; a deterministic auth/credit failure (a dead key, no credit) aborts the whole build immediately with a clear reason instead of discovering it 1000 calls in. Transient/5xx probe errors do NOT abort (the real call keeps its retry budget).
- **Circuit breaker.** A shared `_StormGuard` trips after **3** consecutive same-error-class failures (`CIRCUIT_BREAKER_TRIP`) — an all-4xx provider aborts the build after 3 requests, not thousands. Mixed/rotating errors do not trip the breaker (they are bounded by the budget instead).
- **Global request budget.** `init_storm_guard(expected)` sets a hard ceiling of `REQUEST_BUDGET_MULTIPLIER` × expected calls (3×) for the ENTIRE build; every provider call `charge()`s the shared counter and the build aborts with `_BuildAbort` (a `BaseException`, so an `except Exception` cannot swallow it) the moment the ceiling is crossed. A 5-book build is bounded to ~180 requests vs the ~1300 storm.
- **Per-model max_tokens clamp.** `_clamp_max_tokens()` caps every request to the model's real output ceiling (e.g. `deepseek-v4-pro:cloud` = 65536) in all three call paths, so an over-large ask can never deterministically 400 on every attempt.
- **Regression lock:** `tests/unit/storm-guard.test.sh` (8 assertions, hermetic) — headline T4 asserts an ALL-401 provider ABORTS after ≤3 requests; T2/T5 assert the global budget caps a rotating-error run; T6 asserts each deterministic 4xx = exactly one request while `429/500` still retry to the cap; T7 asserts preflight aborts a dead-401 provider and passes a healthy 200; T8 asserts a 5-book budget stays far under the old storm.

### Orphan-process prevention (fleet-wide) — new `pipeline/orphan_guard.py` + launcher + reaper
ROOT CAUSE: a build is often launched DETACHED (an agent copies `orchestrator.py` → `orchestrator_<slug>.py` and runs it in the background). Stopping the launching agent reaps the agent but NOT the detached Python child — it reparents to launchd/init and keeps making `:cloud` calls until done, billing the client. Four self-defenses, armed automatically by `orchestrator.py main()` for both modes and degrading gracefully if the module is absent:
- **Parent-liveness + max-runtime watchdog** (`start_watchdog_thread`, a THREAD so it fires even during a long provider await): self-terminates the instant the recorded `OPENCLAW_PARENT_PID` is gone, the `OPENCLAW_RUN_LOCKFILE` is removed, or the hard wall-clock ceiling (`OPENCLAW_MAX_RUNTIME_SEC`, default 6h) is exceeded.
- **Process-group cleanup.** On a detached launch (`OPENCLAW_ORCH_DETACH=1`) the orchestrator becomes its own session/group leader via `os.setsid()`, so `self_terminate` reaps the WHOLE tree (child + its Phase-5 indexer subprocess) as a unit — and only signals the group when it actually OWNS it, so an interactive foreground run can never signal its shell's group.
- **Run-id tagging + TARGETED reaper.** Every run stamps `OPENCLAW_RUN_ID` and writes a pidfile (`pid`/`pgid`/`parent`) under `OPENCLAW_RUN_DIR`; `reap-orchestrators.sh --run RUN_ID` kills exactly that run's recorded process group and `--sweep` reaps only runs whose parent pid is dead — **never a blind `pkill`** that could hit an unrelated run.
- **Single-run-per-slug lock** (`acquire_slug_lock`, flock `LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB`): a second orchestrator for the same slug is refused (`--single-book` exits 4), preventing duplicate/overlapping builds.
- **`pipeline/run-orchestrator.sh`** is the sanctioned reapable launch path (own group via the child's `os.setsid()`, no dependency on a `setsid` binary absent on macOS; writes the liveness lock; on exit/interrupt runs a targeted reap). **`pipeline/reap-orchestrators.sh`** is the safe stop-hook/cron payload.
- **Regression lock:** `tests/unit/orphan-process-prevention.test.sh` (5 tests, hermetic, mock child linking the REAL `orphan_guard`) — headline T1: kill the coordinator and assert the orphaned child AND its grandchild are GONE and the `:cloud`-call counter is STABLE (no further calls); T2 lock-removal self-terminate; T3 `--run` reaps exactly one run and leaves an unrelated run untouched; T4 `--sweep` reaps a parent-dead run and spares a parent-alive one; T5 the slug lock refuses a second acquire. Both suites wired into `.github/workflows/embedding-integrity-guard.yml`.

Operator docs: `22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/PIPELINE.md` gains an "Orphan-safe launching" section (always launch detached builds through `run-orchestrator.sh`; reap with `reap-orchestrators.sh --sweep`).

## [v17.0.23]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(book-pipeline): three confirmed provider-reliability bugs in the book-to-persona orchestrator — local signed-in Ollama daemon routing (dead cloud key no longer 401-storms), max_tokens clamp to the model output ceiling, and fail-fast on deterministic 4xx (no retry-storm)

Completes the book-to-persona provider-reliability sprint (task-64). v17.0.22 fixed the two config-layer bugs (secrets/.env value dequoting + the Ollama→OpenRouter fallback model-id mapping); this release fixes the three transport-layer bugs that were the acute cause of a recent run failing 10 of 15 books with ~1300 all-4xx requests and zero tokens produced. No live workspace is touched — the new test is fully hermetic (sandbox HOME + injected fake `aiohttp`). No client names or ids appear on any changed line.
- **Bug 3 — Ollama route via the LOCAL signed-in daemon (the primary failure).** The pipeline called `https://ollama.com/api/chat` with the box's `OLLAMA_API_KEY`, but that key is a dead 21-char placeholder that returns a bare 401 there — so EVERY Ollama call (the primary route for all three phases) failed and fell through. The route now DEFAULTS to the local daemon `http://localhost:11434/api`, which is authenticated by `ollama signin` (`~/.ollama/id_ed25519`) and transparently proxies `*:cloud` models to Ollama Cloud — the path that actually works on the operator box. `call_ollama_cloud` sends NO bearer header in local mode (the daemon needs none); a bearer key is used, and then required, ONLY when an explicit `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` points at a non-local base. The module-load provider guard now counts a reachable local daemon as a usable route (no API key required), and a cheap TCP probe (no API call, no tokens) backs it. Documented trap in the code: `ollama.com/api/tags` is PUBLIC and returns 200 for ANY key, so a dead key masquerades as valid — never validate an Ollama key against `/api/tags`.
- **Bug 4 — clamp `max_tokens` to the model's real output ceiling.** Ollama Cloud hard-caps output tokens per model; `deepseek-v4-pro`'s ceiling is 65536, but the synthesis phases requested `max_tokens=120000` → a deterministic 400 on every attempt. `_clamp_max_tokens()` caps requests to the model's known ceiling (map-driven, both the `:cloud` and `deepseek/…` id forms; 65536 default — above every non-synthesis call this pipeline makes) in BOTH `call_ollama_cloud` and `call_openrouter` (the deepseek OpenRouter fallback shares the ceiling).
- **Bug 5 — fail-fast on deterministic 4xx (kill the retry-storm).** 400/401/403/404/422 are client errors that cannot succeed on retry, yet each phase retried them `MAX_RETRIES` times AND across every fallback tier — the mechanism behind the ~1300 wasted all-4xx requests. `call_ollama_cloud` and `call_openrouter` now raise a `_DeterministicHTTPError` immediately on those statuses (aborting the current provider so the caller's next-tier fallback runs at once), while 408/429/5xx/network errors keep the normal bounded backoff retries.
- **New hermetic regression lock + CI.** `tests/unit/pipeline-provider-routing.test.sh` (9 assertions, offline, injects a fake `aiohttp` and a sandbox HOME so CI needs no network stack or real key): the Ollama base defaults to the local daemon and sends no Authorization header; `_clamp_max_tokens` caps deepseek-v4-pro (both id forms) + the default at 65536 and leaves in-range asks untouched; `call_ollama_cloud` AND `call_openrouter` make EXACTLY ONE call on every deterministic 4xx and raise, while a 500/429 still retries the full `MAX_RETRIES`; plus a bug-1 (dequote) / bug-2 (fallback mapping) regression re-assert. Wired into `.github/workflows/embedding-integrity-guard.yml`.

## [v17.0.22]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(embeddings): end-to-end embedding-subsystem hardening — one canonical index path, real-3072-or-fail-loud hard gate (fake-768 fallback deleted), hermetic prebuilt publisher with real-vector gate, book-pipeline provider-reliability fixes (key dequote + OpenRouter fallback model ids), universal-sops integrity manifest, docs/EMBEDDINGS.md source of truth + CI guard

Embedding-subsystem hardening across all four corpora (coaching personas, runtime persona matching, role library, SOP libraries). Three classes of silent failure are eliminated by construction, the fleet prebuilt-index pipeline gains a publish-side real-vector gate, and the book-to-persona build's two acute provider-reliability defects (which failed 10 of 15 books on a recent run) are fixed. No live workspace is touched by any of this — the publisher is now hermetic by design and the new tests run in sandboxed temp HOMEs. No client names or ids appear on any changed line.
- **EMBED-1 — ONE canonical index path.** `detect_platform paths["gemini_index"]` (BOTH copies: `shared-utils/` + `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/lib/`) pointed at `workspace/data/gemini-index.sqlite` while the search path (`embedding_engine.DB_PATH`), the prebuilt installer (`provision-persona-index.sh`), and `semantic_task_fit` all use `workspace/data/coaching-personas/gemini-index.sqlite` — so `gemini-section-indexer.py` WROTE embeddings into a DB the runtime never reads (observed live: an orphan index absorbing new-persona section rows invisible to search). Both authorities now resolve `coaching-personas/gemini-index.sqlite`; a `legacy_gemini_index` key exists for detection only, and the indexer WARNS loudly when the orphan file is present.
- **EMBED-3 — real vectors or FAIL LOUD (the fake-768 fallback is DELETED).** `gemini-section-indexer.py` used to silently write deterministic hash-derived 768-dim vectors whenever `GOOGLE_API_KEY` was missing from the process env (it never read secrets/.env) OR on ANY Gemini exception — and stamped those rows `provider='gemini'/model=gemini-embedding-2/dim=3072` (lying metadata that made drift detection blind). It now embeds exclusively through `embedding_engine.get_embedder()/get_embedding()` (all canonical secret stores, pinned `output_dimensionality=3072` + `RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT`, quota retry/backoff, dimension assertion) and exits 4 with a clear message when no embedder resolves. Fake vectors exist ONLY behind an explicit `--allow-fake-embeddings` + explicit `--db` (tests/plumbing), stamped TRUTHFULLY `provider='fake'/model='deterministic-hash-768'/dim=768`. Every persona write is post-verified (blob length == dim*4 == contract) and aborts the run on violation. `embedding_engine` gains `verify_index_integrity()` + a `--verify` CLI (rc 0/4) as the machine-checkable assertion, dimension hard-asserts on every embed/query vector, a post-index blob-shape check, and `--db`/`--personas-dir` args; the chunk indexer is converge-aware (a blueprint whose md5 already exists as SECTION rows is skipped — no accidental full re-embed / mixed-unit index).
- **EMBED-2 — sandbox is EXPLICIT (defensive).** All resolution is HOME-relative on Mac, so a faked `$HOME` silently redirects a whole build. New `detect_platform.assert_live_workspace_for_write()`: writers targeting the DEFAULT live DB under an overridden `$HOME` exit 4 unless `OPENCLAW_SANDBOX=1` is exported (tests sandbox on purpose; real builds land live). Wired into the section indexer + engine indexer entrypoints only — read paths and CI static checks are untouched.
- **task-64 — book-pipeline provider reliability (the bugs that actually failed 10/15 books).** (a) `orchestrator.get_keys()` now DEQUOTES values (`KEY="…"` in secrets/.env produced a malformed `Authorization: Bearer` → OpenRouter 401 "Missing Authentication header"), tolerates `export `-prefixed lines, skips comments, and probes all three canonical secret stores. (b) The Ollama→OpenRouter FALLBACK built `model.replace("ollama/","openrouter/").replace(":cloud","")` and passed the result UNstripped to `call_openrouter` → 400 "not a valid model ID" (the direct route strips the prefix; the fallback did not, and it never inserted the vendor segment). All 8 conversion sites now route through one `_openrouter_fallback_model()` helper (`ollama/deepseek-v4-pro:cloud` → `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, `ollama/kimi-k2.6:cloud` → `moonshotai/kimi-k2.6`, vendor pairs mirroring `select_model.py`'s chain patterns) — functionally verified. Orchestrator Phase 5 now PREFERS the section-level indexer per persona (`--persona-id <slug>`; the canonical index is section-converged and `--mode` retrieval needs the section tags) with the chunk wrapper as fallback, keeping its fail-loud contract.
- **EMBED-6 — hermetic prebuilt publisher + real-vector publish gate.** `shared-utils/prebuilt-index/build-and-publish.sh` used to gunzip the base asset OVER a live-workspace file and reconcile blueprints INTO the live coaching dir as side effects. It now stages everything under `mktemp -d` (staged personas tree keeps the `coaching-personas/personas/` shape the search LIKE-filter requires), takes blueprints straight from the repo Skill-22 source, preflights that a Gemini key RESOLVES (SET/NOT-SET only — value never printed), and adds step 3.5: REFUSE to publish unless EVERY row passes `verify_index_integrity` (gemini/gemini-embedding-2/3072, blob=dim*4) — a keyless/broken delta build can no longer ship fake vectors fleet-wide. `file_count` is now recomputed into the manifest.
- **INDEX-MANIFEST reconciled to the published v2.2.1 asset (verified by download).** The asset was re-downloaded and verified: sha256 matches the manifest, 935 section rows / 65 personas / 65 files, every vector 3072-dim float32, single `(gemini, gemini-embedding-2, 3072)` triple. Stale prose fixed: `delta_provider_backstamp.status` PENDING→PUBLISHED, "4574 rows" and "54 personas / 4413 chunks" wording replaced with the converged 65/935 reality, `file_count` 68→65 (pre-converge leftover), `published_at` + `asset_verified` recorded. The structured count triad (65/935) that CI asserts is unchanged.
- **EMBED-7/8 — runtime matching + health close their silent holes.** `semantic_task_fit.py` (the selector's PRIMARY semantic path) cosine-scored ANY row that matched the query dimension; it now excludes rows whose provider/model is definitively not the current gemini model (fake rows, stale slugs, openai rows) while keeping legacy NULL-provider compatibility. `embedding_health.py` leg-b read stamp files in `gemini-index/` directories that don't exist on modern installs (a silent no-op for the persona index); it now reads provider/model/dim from the ACTUAL `coaching-personas/gemini-index.sqlite` embeddings table and FLAGS RE-INDEX when any row's blob length disagrees with its stamped dim (fake/corrupt detection), with the stamp-file search kept as legacy fallback.
- **Corpus 4 — universal-sops finally integrity-covered.** The role-library dept SOPs (131) were CONTENT-HASH-covered but `universal-sops/` (75 files across the craft clusters) had NO manifest and NO hash — edits/corruption were invisible to every gate. New `scripts/hash-universal-sops-manifest.py` writes/checks `universal-sops/_content-manifest.json` (sha256 over CRLF→LF-normalized bytes; additive, deliberately SEPARATE from `_index.json` whose count triads are scoped to the role library). Stale hardcoded "439 role-library files" comments in `create_role_workspaces.py` now defer to `_index.json total_roles`.
- **docs/EMBEDDINGS.md — the single source of truth** (all four corpora: canonical paths, invariants EMBED-1..8, the per-corpus procedures, the delta-landing runbook including the publish-before-provision ordering trap, sandbox policy, quick-reference commands).
- **New hermetic regression lock + CI.** `tests/unit/embedding-hard-gate.test.sh` (13 assertions, sandboxed temp HOME, fully offline): keyless run exits non-zero and writes ZERO rows; fake mode is explicit-only + truthfully stamped + refused against the default live DB; `--verify` fails rc=4 on a fake DB AND on a LYING gemini/3072 stamp over a 768-dim blob, passes a clean 3072 DB; the sandbox write-guard blocks default-DB writes under an overridden HOME (and names the `OPENCLAW_SANDBOX` opt-in); both detect_platform copies + `embedding_engine.DB_PATH` agree on the ONE index path. Wired as `.github/workflows/embedding-integrity-guard.yml` (also runs the existing section-tagger/indexer agreement test and the universal-sops manifest check).

## [v17.0.21]  -  2026-07-04  -  feat(onboarding): self-healing roll-time skill activation (update-skills.sh installs the SILENT bounded onboarding-resume cron) + fix the latent install-time seed no-op (reversed oc_state_seed args)

Onboarding-honesty completeness fix. v17.0.19 restored the `obs_*` namespace so the roll-time seed/gate/verify chain runs again; this release closes the two remaining gaps that kept new numbered skills (e.g. the 49-57 band) from actually being DRIVEN to `qc-passed` after a fleet roll. GAP 1: only a fresh `install.sh` run installed the autonomous `onboarding-resume` cron — a box hot-patched via `update-skills.sh` got the new skill files + the UPDATE-PENDING activation flag but NO resume cron, so activation waited on a human instead of self-healing. GAP 2: `install.sh`'s own install-time state seed called `oc_state_seed "$ONBOARDING_VERSION" "$SKILLS_DIR"` but the signature is `oc_state_seed <src_skills_dir> [version]` — the args were REVERSED, so the version string was treated as the source directory and the seed discovered ZERO skills (a silent no-op that leaned entirely on the roll-time `obs_*` seed to backfill). Nothing about what the verification gate CHECKS changed. Fully verified in a hermetic `mktemp -d` HOME sandbox; the live `~/.openclaw` was never touched. No client names or ids appear on any changed line, and the installed cron is SILENT — it can never message a client.
- **NEW `lib-onboarding-resume-cron.sh` — one canonical `install_onboarding_resume_cron()` sourced by BOTH `install.sh` and `update-skills.sh` (no copy-paste drift).** The battle-tested installer (previously inline in `install.sh`) is now a shared lib: it registers the `*/30` `onboarding-resume` cron as a SILENT main-session self-ping (`--agent main --session-target main --light-context --message <prompt>`; NO `--channel`/`--to`/`--announce`), is IDEMPOTENT (if a cron already exists it is left in place — never a stacked duplicate), and resolves its prompt/guard files from a candidate list (`$ONBOARDING_DIR` → `$OC_PERSISTENT_SCRIPTS_DIR` → `$OC_CONFIG` → self-dir) so it works from the fresh-install tree AND from the post-cleanup persistent copies on the roll path. It self-provides guarded `step/success/warn/note` + `_cron_create_positional` fallbacks so it runs unchanged in `update-skills.sh` (which has no such helpers). All boundedness — `MAX_RUNS_BEFORE_ESCALATE`, 402/429 backoff, Rescue-Rangers escalation to the OPERATOR, and HARD self-delete on gate-pass — is UNCHANGED and still lives in `scripts/resume-onboarding.sh`, which the cron's prompt invokes.
- **`update-skills.sh` — a roll that leaves activation pending now installs the resume cron (CONDITIONAL + IDEMPOTENT + SILENT).** After writing the UPDATE-PENDING flag, if there is pending activation — the verification gate did NOT pass (`ONBOARDING_GATE_OK=no`) OR new numbered skills were copied (`NEW_SKILLS_CSV` non-empty) — it calls the shared `install_onboarding_resume_cron`. When the gate is green AND no new skills landed, it installs NOTHING (no furnace on a no-op roll; gate-unknown with no new skills also installs nothing). The updater sources the shared lib from the freshly-pulled tree BEFORE the temp-clone cleanup (so the function persists in-shell) and persists `resume-onboarding-prompt.txt` to `$OC_PERSISTENT_SCRIPTS_DIR` (a `.txt`, so it was not covered by the existing `.sh` persistence loop) so the installer can still read it after cleanup.
- **`install.sh` — the inline `install_onboarding_resume_cron()` is deleted in favor of the shared lib (sourced near the top, with a no-op fallback for older bundles), and the reversed `oc_state_seed` call is corrected to `oc_state_seed "$SKILLS_DIR" "$ONBOARDING_VERSION"`** so the install-time seed actually discovers the numbered skills. Behaviorally identical resume-cron registration; strictly more robust file resolution.
- **NEW hermetic regression lock + CI wiring.** `tests/unit/update-skills-resume-cron.test.sh` (20 assertions, sandboxed `mktemp -d` HOME): proves the seed arg-order statically AND functionally (correct order discovers the non-archived numbered skills; the reversed order discovers ZERO — the bug demo is non-vacuous); drives the REAL installer against a stubbed `openclaw` to prove IDEMPOTENCY (two calls register exactly ONE cron) and NO-CLIENT-CHANNEL (the recorded `cron create` carries `--session-target main` and NO `--channel`/`--to`/`--announce`); exercises the updater's exact install-decision for the pending/no-pending cases; and confirms a graceful no-op when `openclaw` is absent. The existing `tests/unit/cron-owner-chat-guard.test.sh` (SILENT-cron invariant) and `tests/unit/bounded-resume-cron.test.sh` (interval/boundedness) now also assert the shared lib where the cron registration lives. Wired into `.github/workflows/bounded-resume-cron-guard.yml` (new run step) and both guards' path triggers include the lib.

## [v17.0.20]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(skill-57): close the 35->57 double-post hole — gateway-store cron registrar v2 + mechanized atomic/idempotent/config-gated migrate-35-to-57.sh + CI atomicity guard (Skill 57 v0.2.2)

Fleet-safety fix that makes activating Skill 57 (social-media-in-a-box) safe on a box still running Skill 35 (social-media-planner). ROOT CAUSE of the double-post risk: Skill 35's live weekly cron (`skill35-weekly-theme`, Saturday 08:00) is registered in the OpenClaw GATEWAY cron store (`openclaw cron add` — 35's own registrar explicitly refuses the crontab), but the v1 of 57's `register-social-cron.sh` was ported to the SYSTEM CRONTAB: its "retire legacy" sweep grepped `crontab -l`, a store the live 35 cron never lived in — so applying it armed 57's trigger while leaving 35's fully active (both fire Saturday 08:00 = double-posting to the client's own GHL location and connected accounts). Secondary defects fixed: fleet VPS Docker containers have no cron daemon, so 57's crontab entry would NEVER fire there (a silently dead weekly engine after "migration"); no state carry (35's same-week `weekISO` marker was ignored, so the transition week could run twice); and no config gate (activation could proceed on a box lacking the client's own provider config). Activation remains an EXPLICIT OPERATOR TRIGGER per box — fleet rolls only refresh files and never run the migration. No live client was touched by this build (hermetic sandbox verification only); no client names or ids appear on any changed line.
- **`57-social-media-in-a-box/scripts/register-social-cron.sh` — v2, gateway-store registrar (the faithful port of 35's proven registrar that the merge plan §5.1 intended).** Registers the ONE weekly-theme cron `social-media-weekly-theme` (`0 8 * * 6`, sessionTarget=main, light-context, cheap-model guidance — the furnace-safe Skill-35/38 pattern) in the OpenClaw gateway cron store; retires the legacy `skill35-weekly-theme` IN ITS ACTUAL STORE and CONFIRMS it gone BEFORE registering (retire-then-register: never a moment with both weekly triggers armed); sweeps legacy `weekly-batch`/skill35 crontab lines AND self-heals any stale v1 `# oc-skill57` crontab line; carries 35's persistent `weekISO` marker so the transition week cannot run twice; CLIENT-CONFIG-GATED on `--apply` (fail-closed: required fields + secrets-SET imported from `preflight_gate.py` — one source of truth; missing/failing config = clean SKIP exit 8, NOTHING changed, field names only, never values); Saturday 07:45-08:15 fire-window guard (exit 9) so the swap can never straddle the fire instant; dry-run by default; `--check` asserts the cross-store invariant (exactly one weekly-theme cron, zero legacy anywhere).
- **NEW `57-social-media-in-a-box/scripts/migrate-35-to-57.sh` — the mechanized per-box 35->57 migration (merge plan §8 steps 2-6).** Config gate -> pre-change snapshot (cron stores + 35 marker + a sha256 of the client config, never the config itself, so no secret value is duplicated) -> state re-point BEFORE any cron change (`weekISO` marker; `themeOfWeek` and `plannerSheetId` adopt-existing into the client config, never overwriting) -> atomic retire-then-register flip via the v2 registrar -> independent cross-store invariant re-check -> machine-readable receipt (`data/skill57/migration-35-to-57.json`: MIGRATED / FAILED_NEEDS_RERUN / ROLLED_BACK, what was carried, invariant). Idempotent (re-run until exit 0; check-then-act at every step), SILENT (never posts, never probes a live endpoint, never messages anyone), `--check` and `--rollback` (re-arms exactly one `skill35-weekly-theme` via 35's own registrar — 35's files/state are never deleted; per-client archive of 35 stays a separate operator step after the independent live parity verify). A missed-fire note is recorded if migrating on a Saturday after 08:00 with no run for the current week.
- **NEW hermetic regression lock + CI guard.** `tests/unit/social-cron-migration.test.sh` (43 assertions) drives the REAL scripts against a sandboxed HOME with stubbed `openclaw` + `crontab` where every store mutation appends a snapshot of BOTH stores to a timeline, then proves: the config gate skips an unconfigured/incomplete box untouched (never half-migrated, the live 35 cron stays authoritative); ATOMICITY — no snapshot across migrate/re-migrate/rollback ever shows a 57 trigger and a 35 trigger armed together; IDEMPOTENCY — re-running `--apply` keeps exactly one entry and preserves the receipt; the fire-window guard refuses inside Saturday 07:45-08:15 and stores stay untouched; `--check` flips to exit 2 on a re-injected legacy cron and a re-run heals it; `--rollback` re-arms exactly one 35 cron (full circle re-migrate included). Wired as `.github/workflows/social-cron-migration-guard.yml` (path-triggered on the registrar, migration script, preflight gate, 35's registrar, wire script, and the test itself).
- **`cc-compat.json` (skill 57) + `SKILL.md` + `MASTERDOC.md` — the supersede/cron contract now states the mechanized reality.** The cron block records the store (`openclaw-gateway`), the migration entrypoint, the config gate, the operator-explicit trigger rule, and the cross-store exactly-one invariant; the supersede note replaces "fleet operation requiring operator direction — NOT rolled from this build" with the shipped, still operator-TRIGGERED `migrate-35-to-57.sh` path. Skill 57 -> v0.2.2 (`skill-version.txt` + SKILL.md frontmatter in lockstep). The engine hash pin is untouched (no pinned file changed) and `verify.sh` stays ALL GREEN.

## [v17.0.19]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix: update-skills.sh now robustly sources its onboarding-state lib so the internal verification gate runs (was: obs_seed_state command not found -> file-sync-only fallback)

Onboarding-honesty regression fix. On EVERY box during the v17.0.18 roll, `update-skills.sh` printed `obs_seed_state: command not found` and its internal verification gate fell back to "verification gate unavailable / file-sync-only." ROOT CAUSE: the PRD-2.1 unify (commit `2c798c72`) renamed the onboarding state-machine + verification-gate implementation from the `obs_*` namespace to `oc_*` (into `lib-onboarding-state.sh`) and rewrote `scripts/onboarding-state.sh` into a thin lib-sourcing shim — but it never migrated the RUNTIME callers and never added an `obs_*` compatibility layer. `update-skills.sh` seeds via `obs_seed_state` and gates on `command -v obs_verify_skill`; `install.sh`'s compat-fallback branch and `resume-onboarding.sh` also call `obs_*`. So sourcing the shim defined only `oc_*` — `obs_seed_state` was undefined ("command not found") and the gate's `command -v obs_verify_skill` guard failed, silently degrading every roll to file-sync-only. No client names or ids appear on any changed line. What the gate CHECKS is byte-for-byte unchanged — this only makes it REACHABLE again.
- **`scripts/onboarding-state.sh` — restored the self-contained `obs_*` compatibility API.** The shim still sources the canonical `oc_*` lib, and now ALSO re-provides the original `obs_seed_state` / `obs_set_status` / `obs_get_status` / `obs_verify_skill` / `obs_gate_summary` / `obs_resolve_workspace` implementation (pure bash + python3, idempotent, guarded by `__OBS_SOURCED`), restored verbatim from the pre-unify implementation so the legacy callers behave EXACTLY as designed (same `.onboarding-state.json` schema, same `GATE-HUMAN:` summary line the updater greps for). Both namespaces now coexist: `oc_*` for the migrated callers (watchdog, check-skill-prereqs), `obs_*` for update-skills.sh / install.sh-fallback / resume-onboarding.sh.
- **`update-skills.sh` — sources the state shim ROBUSTLY by resolved absolute path + self-verifies.** The seed block now resolves the shim from the freshly-pulled tree (`$ONBOARDING_DIR`) with a fallback to the updater's own dir (`$_SCRIPT_DIR`, via `${BASH_SOURCE[0]}`), sources it by absolute path, and SELF-VERIFIES `command -v obs_seed_state` before invoking it — so a bundle mismatch now degrades LOUDLY with a clear message instead of emitting a raw "command not found." The verification-gate block (guarded by `command -v obs_verify_skill`, calling `obs_gate_summary`) is untouched; it simply reaches its intended path now. The v17.0.18 maintenance-quiet guard and the TTY guards are untouched.
- **New hermetic regression lock + CI wiring.** `tests/unit/onboarding-state-obs-api.test.sh` first proves the bug is still real (sourcing `lib-onboarding-state.sh` ALONE does NOT define `obs_seed_state`, so the shim assertions are non-vacuous), then proves the shim defines the full `obs_*` API, that `obs_seed_state` seeds `.onboarding-state.json` with exactly the non-archived numbered skills (ARCHIVED excluded), and that `obs_gate_summary` emits the `GATE-HUMAN:` line — plus static assertions that the updater resolves+sources the shim by absolute path, guards the seed with `command -v`, and keeps the gate wiring. Fully sandboxed under `mktemp -d` HOME. Wired into `qc-static.yml`.

## [v17.0.18]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(fleet-safety): suppress qc-completeness owner-chat Telegram alerts during maintenance/rolls so the embedded QC step can never message a client; update-skills.sh now runs QC in maintenance mode

Silent-maintenance hardening. During a fleet roll, `update-skills.sh` runs `qc-completeness.sh` inline; on a box with an owner chat configured, that gate sent a one-time "workforce QC status" Telegram to the **owner** whenever status was not PASS — so routine maintenance messaged the client. This release adds a maintenance/quiet guard to the QC gate and runs the embedded QC call in maintenance mode, so no roll can ever notify a client. No QC computation changed — only whether it notifies. Normal, non-maintenance operation is unchanged: alerts still fire in real use. No client names or ids appear on any changed line.
- **`qc-completeness.sh` — owner-chat alert now suppressible (log-only) under maintenance/quiet.** Exporting `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE=1` (or passing `--quiet` / `--no-telegram` / `--no-notify`) forces quiet mode and SKIPS the owner-chat Telegram send entirely, emitting an explicit "owner alert suppressed" log line instead. The send path itself (default, flag-unset) is byte-for-byte unchanged, so real-use alerts on `!= PASS` still fire. The guard gates NOTIFICATION only — what QC measures/returns and its exit codes are untouched.
- **`update-skills.sh` — embedded QC call always runs in maintenance mode.** A roll is inherently maintenance, so the inline `qc-completeness.sh` invocation now runs with `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE=1`; the embedded QC step can never message a client owner. The operator still gets the workforce QC status folded into the existing OPERATOR-ROUTED update note (`send_telegram_progress`), so no visibility is lost. Audited the updater for any other owner/client send path: the only other Telegram path is `send_telegram_progress`, which is already operator-routed and no-ops (log-only) when no operator escalation chat is configured.
- **P1 (re-roll safety) — TTY guard on the updater's interactive prompts.** The two `read -p` prompts (UPDATE-PENDING continue, and same-version force-reinstall) are now wrapped in a `[ -t 0 ]` check: on a non-interactive stdin (cron / `curl | bash` / SSH pipe) they auto-decline instead of hanging on stdin. Interactive behaviour is unchanged. Both prompts already exit 0 on decline (the same-version "Already up to date" path was already idempotent), so re-rolls are now non-interactive-safe.
- **New hermetic regression lock + CI wiring.** `tests/unit/qc-maintenance-quiet-guard.test.sh` drives the REAL `qc-completeness.sh` end-to-end against a sandboxed fake non-PASS workforce with a stubbed `openclaw`: it first proves the owner-chat send DOES fire without the flag (non-vacuity), then proves `OPENCLAW_MAINTENANCE=1` and `--quiet` each invoke the send ZERO times and log the suppression note, and statically asserts the updater wires the maintenance flag. Wired into `qc-static.yml`.

## [v17.0.17]  -  2026-07-04  -  security(fleet-safety): scrub a pre-existing client Telegram chat-id leak (2 tracked files) + add a telegram-chat-id shape-rule CI gate

Security hardening. A pre-existing real-client Telegram chat-id had leaked into two tracked files (a test-fixture session key and a docstring example); it is scrubbed to the synthetic placeholder, and a new repo-safe CI gate now makes that class of leak impossible to reintroduce. No production logic changed — the scrub touches only example/test data, and the new gate is static QC. No client names or ids appear on any changed line.
- **Client chat-id scrub (2 tracked files → synthetic placeholder).** A real-client Telegram chat id that had shipped inside an `agent:main:telegram:direct:<id>` session key (test fixture) and a docstring example is replaced with the synthetic placeholder `1234567890`. Behaviour is unchanged — both sites are illustrative/test data, not live identity.
- **New telegram-chat-id shape-rule CI gate (`scripts/qc-assert-no-telegram-chat-id-leak.sh`, wired in `qc-static.yml`).** The existing client-name denylist only knows the three non-client operator ids, so a real client chat id (just a 9–10 digit integer) sails straight through — exactly how the leak shipped. The new gate INVERTS the denylist: it treats the operator ids plus one sanctioned placeholder as the ONLY allowed real ids and FAILS CI on any other chat-id-shaped integer that appears in a `telegram:direct:` session key or a telegram-identity context (allowFrom / ownerChatId / `*CHAT_ID` / …). It hardcodes NO client id, never fails open, and ships with a hermetic non-vacuity self-test that plants a fake id, proves the gate bites, then proves it does not false-positive. Wired into CI as `CRITICAL-1b`.
- **Operator absolute path redacted from a tracked working ledger.** An operator machine absolute path in a tracked working-ledger entry is redacted to `<redacted>`.

## [v17.0.16]  -  2026-07-04  -  fix(provisioning): onboarding follow-ups — floor 3-tier relabel + canonical-floor drift-guard, broken-install floor fallback, Skill-32 shell-first flip, WG-6/WG-10c CI

Onboarding follow-ups batch. Four disjoint fixes, each independently verified with no client-name leaks on any changed line: the department-floor docs are relabeled into three unambiguous tiers with a new CI drift-guard so they can never go stale again; a broken (unreadable) naming map now degrades to the full floor instead of silently shipping a short install; Skill 32 flips to shell-first provisioning with a lock-before-reachable guarantee; and the ZHE web-build parity harness plus the no-web-only-store assertion are wired into CI.
- **OQ-7 — floor docs relabeled to 3 tiers + canonical-floor drift-guard.** The department counts are now stated as three never-conflated tiers, all derived live from `department-naming-map.json` (v2.6.1): **28 = guaranteed FLOOR** (22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary), **34 = built departments** (`templates/role-library/_index.json`), **45 = full catalog union** (naming map). No client gets all 34/45 — each gets the 28 floor plus keyword-matched industry verticals minus declines. The stale "22 + 7 = 29" prose (from before naming-map v2.6.1 demoted `listings` to a real-estate-only vertical) is corrected across `build-state-schema.json`, `SKILL.md`, `_qc-summary.md`, and `ZHC-BUILDOUT-EXPERIENCE.md`. New guard `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/check-floor-count-consistency.py` **derives** the floor fresh from the naming map (hardcodes no integer) and asserts every registered doc/comment that quotes a floor count equals the derived value — failing CI on any future drift (11 assertions across 4 files pass; frozen historical CHANGELOG entries are exempt via optional sentinels). Wired into CI.
- **Broken-install fallback fix — map-unreadable degrades to the full 28 floor, never a 22-only install.** Previously, if `department-naming-map.json` could not be read, the universal-primary layer silently dropped and the install shipped only the 22 mandatory departments (6 universal-primary departments missing) with no error. `department-floor.py` / `build-workforce.py` now fail closed: an unreadable/parse-failed naming map degrades to the **complete canonical floor of 28** and **refuses** to proceed with a short (22-only) department set rather than silently dropping the 6 universal-primary departments.
- **Skill 32 — shell-first provisioning flip with lock-before-reachable guarantee.** Command Center provisioning flips to shell-first: the interview `/interview` lock is established **before** the surface becomes reachable, closing the window where a freshly-provisioned board could be reached in an unlocked state ahead of closeout. `32-command-center-setup/skill-version.txt` → v12.9.27; `INSTALL.md`, `PREREQS.json`, and `scripts/run-full-install.sh` updated for the shell-first sequence.
- **WG-6 + WG-10c — CI coverage for web-build parity and no-web-only-store.** WG-6: `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/prove-zhe.py` gains a web-build parity harness (`prove-web-parity.sh` + `prove_zhe_web_parity_fixture.py`) proving the web build path produces the same zero-human-workforce result as the reference path. WG-10c: `test-no-web-store.py` + `_qc_no_web_store.py` + the `qc-interview-completion.py` assertion prove no web-only store is left behind after provisioning. Both suites are wired into CI.

## [v17.0.15]  -  2026-07-04  -  docs(skill-32): Command Center ships locked to /interview until closeout (ratified OQ-1 locked-mode doctrine)

Skill 32 — Command Center now ships locked to `/interview` until closeout (ratified locked-mode doctrine, OQ-1); gating docs + comments; no behavior change to the provisioning sequence (the shell-first flip is deferred until P0-5 lands). Documentation + gating-comment clarity ONLY — no provisioning logic changed in this skill; the redirect/unlock enforcement lives in the CC middleware (P0-5), a separate deliverable.
- **OQ-1 (ratified 2026-07-03) — Locked interview-mode is BY DESIGN.** The Command Center now ships FIRST but LOCKED to the `/interview` surface: the client sees only the interview (plus its `/onboarding` progress screen) until closeout, when the full dashboard is revealed. The lock is STATE-DRIVEN off the canonical build-state fields `interviewComplete` / `buildCompletedAt` that the installer already reads/writes — there is **no separate CC "unlock" env var** and provisioning must not invent one. The interview-only view in front of an empty board before closeout is the intended experience, not a rogue/stale board and not a bug to "fix."
- **Two gates kept straight.** `32-command-center-setup/{SKILL.md,INSTALL.md,PREREQS.json}` and the `scripts/run-full-install.sh` interview-gate comments now state explicitly that the `interviewComplete` gate protects the seeding/materialization of the client's REAL zero-human workforce (departments, roles, agents) — NOT the shipping of the locked CC shell. The real-workforce gate stays; the locked shell can ship ahead of it.
- **No behavior change here.** The provisioning sequence and the interview-complete precondition are unchanged; only docs and gating comments were edited. Enforcement of the redirect/unlock itself (P0-5) lands separately in the Command Center repo.

## [v17.0.14]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(provisioning): Big July 3rd Update Wave 4 — #16 prove-zhe persona count derived (not hardcoded) + #12 done-degraded retry regression test

Wave 4 of The Big July 3rd Update (onboarding side). The ZHE prover's persona-count expectation is de-hardcoded so it tracks the persona library as it grows, and the Wave 2 done-degraded/retry gate gains a hermetic regression lock. Both changes are disjoint from the rest of the tree — no production logic outside the prover moved, and no client-name leaks on any changed line.
- **#16 (prove-zhe persona count derived, not hardcoded):** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/prove-zhe.py`'s persona-canonical check (b) now **DERIVES** the expected persona count from the persona index (`persona-categories.json` roster, currently 65) with a hard sanity **floor of 40**, replacing the stale hardcoded **54** literal. The expectation now tracks the persona library automatically as it grows and catches index-vs-disk drift — the on-disk persona dirs must match/exceed the index roster — while the floor keeps a missing/stub index from making the check trivially satisfiable. The Wave 1 receipt-equality assertions and the Wave 2 `ZHE_ENFORCE` default-on wrapping are preserved untouched.
- **#12 (done-degraded/retry regression lock):** added `tests/unit/cc-done-degraded-retry-gate.test.sh` — a hermetic regression test proving the Wave 2 **#6** done-degraded / retry gate **fails-closed** on script-missing sub-states and converges to a terminal `done`. #12 needed **no production change** (it was superseded by #6); this test locks that behavior so a future regression fails CI. It runs in `qc-static`.

## [v17.0.13]  -  2026-07-03  -  chore(security): Big July 3rd Update — externalize client roster from anti-leak gates + scrub residual client names (client names 100% off GitHub)

Externalized the client roster — the real client **names**, client **chat IDs**, and the **2 GHL location IDs** — out of the load-bearing anti-leak gates and into a gitignored, operator-local file so no client identifier lives in the tracked tree anymore. The gates read the roster from `$OPENCLAW_CLIENT_ROSTER` (else `~/.openclaw/client-roster.txt`) at runtime instead of embedding the names inline.

### What changed
- **Roster externalized** out of the gates that used to hardcode it: `scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`, the 3× `qc-no-personal-data.sh` (skills 38/39/40), `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/{test-how-to-use-docs.sh,test-presentation-dept-welcome.sh}`, and `tests/unit/library-gate-content.test.py`.
- **Two run modes, never fail-open.** *Box mode* (roster present) → fail-closed on any real client name. *Structural / CI mode* (roster absent) → warn that full-name detection is skipped, but still fail-closed on placeholder leaks and operator-path leaks — CI never passes on a real leak.
- **Placeholder-only example shipped:** `scripts/client-roster.example.txt` (no real data) documents the format; operators copy it to `~/.openclaw/client-roster.txt` per box.
- **Residual scrubs:** removed leftover client names from script comments, one SOP's example copy, test hostnames, CHANGELOG prose, and one provenance JSON (`working/changeorder_ledger/ROLE-07.json`).
- **Hermetic self-test:** `tests/fixtures/no-client-names/` — a planted-name fixture + `selftest-qc-assert.sh` prove the gate still bites without depending on any operator-local file.
- Cross-repo consistent with **command-center v4.59.5**.

### Operator follow-up
Create `~/.openclaw/client-roster.txt` on each box from `scripts/client-roster.example.txt` to enable full-name detection (box mode). Absent that file, the gates run structural/CI mode and stay fail-closed on placeholder/operator-path leaks.

## [v17.0.12]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(provisioning): Big July 3rd Update Wave 3 (part) — #9 build-progress producer→page contract + #10 stale canonical-floor INSTRUCTIONS + guard

Wave 3 of The Big July 3rd Update (onboarding side, part). Two parallel Opus subagents on disjoint files (both under `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/`), independently verified by a fresh Opus agent: PASS — #9 and #10 each confirmed with file:line + live-execution evidence, no client-name leaks on any changed line. Both hermetic suites re-run green on the assembled release tree (contract test all checks PASS; `test-repo-consistency.sh` 8/8). Wave 3's remaining issues **#11** and **#13** ship in the Command Center repo (paired **command-center v4.59.4**) and **#12** is still **OPEN**.
- **#9 (build-progress producer ↔ page contract):** `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/build-workforce.py` now emits `COMPANY_DIR/build-progress.json` in the exact shape the Command Center `/onboarding/building` page consumes (via `GET /api/onboarding/build-status`) — a `stage` / `message` / `documents_total` / `documents_complete` / `departments[]` / `eta_minutes` payload with `started_at`/`completed_at`/`updated_at` timestamps. The producer fires at every phase boundary of the real build (manifest → departments → per-department roles in-progress/complete → assembly → qc → terminal), writes atomically (temp file + `os.replace`, no partial reads, no stray `.tmp`), and is best-effort telemetry that never raises into the build. **No false complete:** the terminal `complete` stage is emitted only when the structural build actually finished (`_build_progress >= 100` AND every department `status == "complete"`); otherwise it leaves an honest non-terminal `qc` state so the page keeps polling instead of falsely showing "ready." Before this, nothing in the repo produced that file, so the page sat idle forever. The Command Center consumer route that reads it lands **separately in command-center v4.59.4**. New contract test `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-build-progress-contract.py` drives the producer across the full lifecycle, re-implements the route's directory scan to consume the file, and asserts every field the page reads is present + typed (all checks PASS).
- **#10 (stale canonical-floor size in INSTRUCTIONS.md):** the interviewer executes `INSTRUCTIONS.md` verbatim, so its hardcoded floor prose — "= 29", "22 mandatory + 7 universal-primary", and Listings Management pitched as a universal-primary vertical — was walking every live client through the WRONG department set. Corrected to **defer to the live count** from `scripts/list-canonical-departments.py` (and its ready-to-quote `floor_label`) = **22 mandatory + 6 universal-primary = 28** (Listings Management was demoted to an industry-gated real-estate-pack vertical in naming-map v2.6.1); the doc no longer hardcodes any floor number. An **additive forbidden-literal guard** in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` (rc=7) now fails if the retired "7 universal-primary" / "= 29" framing reappears, plus a contextual rule that fires only when "Listings Management" and "universal-primary" land on the SAME line (so the legitimate Real-Estate-Pack listing stays clean). `test-repo-consistency.sh` adds T6/T7/T8 to prove the guard bites on each drift (8/8 pass). Wave 1 **#7** (LATER = build-now, no 90-day deferral) content in `INSTRUCTIONS.md` and its guard are preserved untouched.

## [v17.0.11]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(provisioning): Big July 3rd Update Wave 2 — #4 presentations 32/32 roles + #14 provisioning-tests CI + #6 fail-closed done-stamp & ZHE_ENFORCE default-on

Wave 2 of The Big July 3rd Update. Two parallel Opus subagents on disjoint files, independently verified by a fresh Opus agent: PASS — #4, #14, and #6 each confirmed with file:line + live-execution evidence, no client-name leaks on any changed line.
- **#4 (presentations roster ↔ role-library drift):** the presentations roster (`23-ai-workforce-blueprint/suggested-roles/presentations-suggested-roles.md`) had drifted out of lockstep with the role library — `templates/role-library/_index.json` listed 32 presentations roles (all 32 `.md` templates on disk) but the roster, the build's instantiation source, carried only 26 `**Slug:**` entries, so 6 roles (attention-content-strategist, prompt-author-presentations, qc-specialist-image/prompt/speech/typography-presentations) were never instantiated. Fixed at the root: the 6 missing roles were appended to the roster (26→32, header Canonical Role Count updated) faithful to each template, and `scripts/test-department-instantiation.sh` now reads the canonical count LIVE from `_index.json` (`CANON_COUNT`) instead of a hardcoded literal across T1/T3/T7/T8 — the test still cross-checks roster == `_index.json` == instantiated folders and asserts real per-role content, so drift still fails it. `test-department-instantiation.sh`: 13 passed / 0 failed (was 8/10 on main).
- **#14 (provisioning tests were unwired):** new `.github/workflows/skill23-provisioning-tests.yml` runs all 5 hermetic provisioning suites (test-department-floor, test-decline-provenance-guard, test-reconciliation-engine, test-ws4-departments, test-department-instantiation) on push to `main` and on PRs touching `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/**`, failing the job if any suite exits non-zero. Deferred from Wave 1 until #4 landed (the suite was red on main before #4's fix), now unblocked. Only static test paths (no untrusted event input → no injection surface); deps limited to python3 + shasum.
- **#6 (false 'done' / ZHE_ENFORCE never blocking):** (a) `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh` FINAL block now inspects the board-provisioning sub-phase keys written by phases 6b/6c/6d/6e and, if any is present-and-not-`true` (or the ZHE gate recorded a failure), stamps `commandCenterStatus="done-degraded"` plus a `commandCenterDegradedPhases` list instead of `"done"` — so the resume path (which only skips when status == `"done"`) re-invokes the idempotent installer to auto-repair rather than falsely reporting done. Phase 6f (KPI rollup, WARN-by-design) is deliberately excluded and absent keys are not treated as a regression, so a legitimately-green box is never re-reddened. The install still exits 0 (degraded is a surfaced state, not a hard failure). (b) `ZHE_ENFORCE` now defaults to blocking — `${ZHE_ENFORCE:-1}` at both enforcement sites (run-full-install Phase 7z and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/verify-library-gate.sh`), with the explicit `ZHE_ENFORCE=0` WARN escape hatch retained; safe for fresh builds because prove-zhe exempts any box that has not completed the interview. `build-state-schema.json` adds the `done-degraded` enum + `commandCenterDegradedPhases` property. Wave 1's receipt-equality check (prove-zhe.py) is preserved untouched.

## [v17.0.10]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(provisioning): Big July 3rd Update Wave 1 — CLOSE the silent over-provision bug (#2/#3/#7/#8) + bulletproofing

Wave 1 of The Big July 3rd Update. Built by one coherent Opus subagent (the four issues interlock in `build-workforce.py`), independently verified by a fresh Opus agent: PASS — silent over-provision bug CLOSED, all three provisioning suites green, all six invariants confirmed with file:line + live evidence. All fixes route through ONE new shared reader so the drift that caused the bug cannot recur.
- **#2 (PROVEN decline-normalization mismatch vector):** NEW `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/canonical_decline.py` — the single provenance-gated decline reader + one `norm()` (lowercase, strip non-alphanumerics). `build-workforce._canonical_decline_set` and `department-floor` now BOTH delegate to it, returning the identical normalized set; `reconcile_canonical_floor` tests `_decline_norm(cid) in declined` (was raw), so a decline keyed `Video`/`billing_finance` is **honored, not force-built**. `record-dept-decision.sh` now validates `--dept` against the live canonical list + recorded customs (rejects unknown/misspelled ids, fail-safe). New lockstep cases T5a–T5d prove builder+floor agree and the dept is not built.
- **#3 (no decision-completeness gate):** `build_from_config` now calls `_enforce_decision_coverage_or_refuse` BEFORE reconcile — in the genuine-interview path, any missing yes/no/later on an auto-unioned canonical/universal dept **fail-closes** (`RECONCILIATION_PENDING` handoff, exit 88; ledgers `decisionCoverage` + `declineRejections`; never stamps `interviewComplete`). Configured customs count as implicit-yes (no false refusal). Added a `decisionCoverage` verdict to `qc-interview-completion.py`.
- **#7 (LATER = BUILD-NOW):** reworded INSTRUCTIONS.md to drop the "ask me again in 90 days" / `90-day-reassessment.md` promise — `later` is built now and counted exactly like `yes`; no defer path/cron/reassessment file. Added `evaluate_forbidden_literals()` to `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` (rc=7) that fails if any deferral phrasing reappears.
- **#8 (custom-dept declines unenforced):** `build_from_config` filters provenance-declined CUSTOM departments out of `selected_departments` (normalized shared set) before reconcile.
- **Bulletproofing:** (a) the one shared reader (imported by build-workforce, department-floor AND qc-interview-completion); (b) decision-coverage ledger in build-state; (c) `provisioning-receipt.json` at build end with an EXPECTED-SET EQUALITY invariant (mandatory+universal+vertical+accepted-customs+laters − declines == built), and `prove-zhe.py` wired to assert it (fails on OVER-provision, not just "at least the floor"; absent receipt = non-blocking so legacy boxes don't regress); (d) NEW `prove-handover.sh` — a receipt-backed, fail-closed handover gate (floor rc=0 + receipt equality + live no-declined-dept-on-disk re-verify).

## [v17.0.9]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(provisioning): Big July 3rd Update Wave 0 — no demo seeding on client installs (#1 installer)

Wave 0 of The Big July 3rd Update (functioning-audit fixes), onboarding side. Built by subagents, independently verified (fresh Opus, PASS). Pairs with command-center **v4.59.3** (the seed.ts/migrations side of #1 + #5).
- **#1 (installer side):** `32-command-center-setup/scripts/run-full-install.sh` — corrected the stale "idempotent drizzle migrations" comment (migrations run in `getDb()`), and documented that demo seeding is disabled by default: the installer leaves `DEMO_SEED` **unset**, so `seed.ts`'s new no-demo default applies on client boxes (no demo agents/tasks injected on install or `--update-only`). `SKIP_DEMO_SEED` is no longer used.
- **#14 (deferred to Wave 2):** wiring the 5 Skill-23 provisioning tests into CI is deferred until issue #4 (presentations role-instantiation) lands — `test-department-instantiation.sh` currently **fails on main** (the exact bug #4 fixes), so the gate would be red before its fixes exist. Adding it now would block all merges. Skill 32 `skill-version` → v12.9.24 (G3).

## [v17.0.8]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(verification): Fable re-audit sub-8.5 fixes — presentations Kanban delivered→done contract (cross-repo), client-facing intake wording, Skill-6 CHANGELOG, cc-compat pin → v4.59.1

### Risk: low-medium — the one behavior change is the presentations board-close contract (fail-soft, already fail-soft before), plus doc/wording + a version-pin bump. Pairs with Command Center **v4.59.1** (PR trevorotts1/blackceo-command-center#124, MERGED) which removed the orphan `'delivered'` terminal status so the two repos agree on `status='done'`+`process_certificate_sha`. No new skill/dept/role, no mission-control.db schema change. Off-limits/pinned: CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN regenerated (30eda4e3→5034cd9b over build_deck.py + run_signature_deck.py); Skill-52 zero-egress untouched. Independently re-verified: G1/frontmatter/no-client-names/G3, qc-assert-repo-consistency, content-hash `--check`, artifact-versioning T8 **14/0**, presentation `sync_check` IN SYNC, renderer pin MATCH, intake guard **85/0**, Kanban contract test, cc_board mock, welcome wording **39/0**, Skill-51 `verify.sh`, and **zero banned phrases** in any client-facing surface.

### From the July-3 VERIFICATION-REPORT (Fable graded its own plan's execution; 13/16 ≥8.5, these 3 were <8.5):
- **P1-1 Kanban close never landed (was 6.0) — the real fix.** `build_deck.py` emitted `status='delivered'` at run-end, which the Command Center's `UpdateTaskSchema` enum rejects (400), and mid-run `status='done'` moves 422'd the cert gate — so presentations cards advanced to `in_progress` and never closed (hidden by fail-soft; the mock accepted `'delivered'`). Now: the **runner** (`run_signature_deck.py`) fires the terminal close `status='done'`+`process_certificate_sha` **after `prove-deck.py` mints the PROCESS-CERTIFICATE**; `build_deck.py`'s terminal close is guarded to fire only when the cert is already on disk (no premature 422); mid-run P4/P8 progress goes to the `/api/tasks/{id}/activities` endpoint (not a task-status change); a movement receipt (`working/checkpoints/cc-board.json`) records every advance + a `≥1 successful advance` visibility check; new `test_cc_contract.py` pins the CC status enum + proves the runner emits `done`+cert while `build_deck` stays cert-guarded; the mock now rejects any status outside the 10-value enum.
- **Client-facing intake wording (was 6.5).** The one-question-at-a-time doctrine had landed in the guard/record layers but not the words a client reads first. Fixed the banned phrases in `send-presentation-dept-welcome.sh` (first message) + the how-to template + 33 generated `how-to-use-this-department.md` docs + `CLIENT-WEBINAR-DECK-SOP.md` §0.5 + `director-of-presentations.md`/`00-START-HERE.md`; extended `presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh` to grep-ban both legacy phrases across the welcome script, template, and dept docs (can't drift back). `IDENTITY.md`/`SOUL.md`/`first-time-onboarding` verified already clean, left untouched.
- **Skill-6 CHANGELOG (was 8.0).** `06-ghl-install-pages/CHANGELOG.md` now records v16.12.0 / v17.0.2 / v17.0.4 / v17.0.7 + a note that `SKILL.md metadata.version` tracks the repo version by design.
- **cc-compat pin drift → fixed (operator ruling).** `commandCenter.minVersion` + `pinnedTag` bumped v4.56.1 → **v4.59.1** so a fleet refresh pulls the current, fixed Command Center (historical v4.56.1 references in the notes preserved). Bumping the pin means the next client refresh pulls current CC — done at Trevor's explicit direction.

## [v17.0.7]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(audit): July-3 P2/P3 polish sweep — Skill-6 id re-validation, campaign board-carding, marker SSOT, persona drift-guard, dept-wide intake doctrine, cc-compat + gitlink hygiene

### Risk: low — additive fail-soft / doc / test / hygiene changes across independent surfaces; no new skill, department, role, Command Center endpoint, or `mission-control.db` schema change; repo-only (no fleet rollout). Client runtime unchanged (all engines on the CLIENT's own providers, never Anthropic/operator keys). No off-limits/pinned enforcement file weakened: Skill-6 off-limits core tools byte-identical; every wired campaign skill's hash-pin re-stamped and its `verify.sh` re-proven exit 0; the CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN still MATCHES; Skill-52's zero-egress invariant deliberately left intact (NOT carded). No client names, credential values, or plists. Independently re-verified on the assembled tree: G1 `--check`, frontmatter gate, no-client-names, G3, `qc-assert-repo-consistency`, content-hash `--check` (render cross-check), presentation `sync_check` IN SYNC, renderer pin MATCH, persona mirror-drift + `test-persona-selector.sh` (no live-DB write), Skill-6 capture pytest, Skill-51 `verify.sh`, intake-conversation guard, and all six wired skills' `verify.sh` — all green.

### Skill-6 form-id hardening (audit P1-5 remainder + P3)
`06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_form_builder.py` `_capture_form_id` now re-validates the captured id server-side against a conservative shape (`re.fullmatch(r'[A-Za-z0-9]{15,30}')`) and returns `""` on any mismatch — the raw cross-origin `_eval` result is never trusted as an id. `_screenshot` now logs its previously-swallowed exception (control flow unchanged, still best-effort). New negative cases added to `tests/test_ghl_form_builder_capture.py` (pytest 23/23). Off-limits core tools untouched; MIX architecture intact.

### Campaign board-carding — receipt-backed, FAIL-SOFT (audit P2 / PRD §10 option a)
One byte-identical, stdlib-only `mc_board.py` (open + `in_progress` at run-begin, `done` on delivery, receipt at `working/checkpoints/mc-board.json`) wired into the `run_*.py` entrypoint of **49-signature-funnel, 50-email-engine, 53-book-writer, 55-product-bio, 56-sales-page-assets, 57-social-media-in-a-box**, mirroring Skill-48 `cc_board` + Skill-51 `_board_patch_phase`. Layered fail-soft (module never raises, wrappers + caller each try/except); a disabled/unreachable board is a clean no-op. Proven end-to-end: a real Skill-49 run against an unreachable board logged the failure and still certified (exit 0). **52-avatar-alchemist deliberately NOT carded** — its `aa_egress_gate.py` fails CLOSED on any `urllib`/POST (enforced zero network egress) and `aa_director.py` is hash-pinned; a board POST would violate that invariant. Every wired skill's orchestrator hash-pin was re-stamped and its `verify.sh` re-proven.

### Version-marker single source of truth (audit P2 / PRD §9)
New `scripts/version-markers.json` (authoritative, **11 markers**) is now read by both `scripts/bump-version.sh` (stale 9-marker header corrected; startup SSOT guard aborts if the checked count ≠ the manifest) and `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` (VERSION-MARKERS dimension now derives its set from the manifest — previously it checked only 10 and would have missed a drifted `23/SKILL.md` frontmatter). `.github/workflows/version-consistency.yml` no longer re-implements its own inline 9-marker check — it now delegates to the authoritative `bash scripts/bump-version.sh --check` (G1/G2/G3 guards and the cc-compat integrity step are byte-identical and untouched). All three now agree at 11. The `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py` VERSION-MARKERS dimension degrades gracefully (inline fallback marker-set) when the SSOT manifest is absent — e.g. in the `test-artifact-versioning.sh` minimal sandbox that doesn't copy `scripts/` — so it never hard-fails a sandbox while still using the SSOT (and catching real drift) on the full repo.

### Persona mirror drift-guard + dead-key cleanup (audit P2/P3 / PRD §3 remainder)
New `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/test-dept-domain-mirror.py` (no import, no execution, zero DB/network) fails if `DEPT_DOMAIN_TAGS` stops covering every live `_index.json` department, if a dead pre-canonical key returns, if a tag leaves the known vocab, if build-workforce's two `dept_to_domains` copies diverge, or if the selector pool silently narrows/grows vs the matrix. `persona-selector-v2.py` dropped the 8 provably-dead pre-canonical keys (41→33 == the 33 live canonical slugs); no `ALIAS_MAP` target stranded, no live-dept tag value changed. `test-persona-selector.sh` still A1–A8 PASS; the mirror test is 11/0; verified no live persona DB write.

### Department-wide intake doctrine — one question at a time (Trevor's ruling, dept-wide half)
`SOP-SIGPRES-01`, `deck-intake-driver.py`, and the three sibling role docs (`signature-presentation-architect.md`, `brainstorming-buddy-presentations.md`, `SOP-SIGPRES-00`) are aligned to the two-layer model already applied to Skill-51 in v17.0.6: the CONVERSATION is choice-first (QUICK vs IN-DEPTH) + one question at a time (batching trips `AF-INTAKE-BATCH`, a QC/Healer conversation autofail that NEVER gates the build), while the 8 answers + frame are still RECORDED as ONE atomic block proven by `prove_sp_intake.py` (`AF-SP-8Q-SPLIT`). Corrected the previously-inverted architect.md line that conflated the two layers. `manifest_version` unchanged (23 — no phase/autofail/role/build_deck symbol changed); the four affected `_index.json` `content_sha`s re-stamped (surgical, gate-verified via `hash-content-manifest.py --check` render cross-check).

### Hygiene
- `cc-compat.json`: added the missing Skill-51 (v16.3.0) and Skill-57 (v16.9.0) registration notes, mirroring the 49/56 convention (versions confirmed from git tags).
- Removed the orphan `projects/mission-control` gitlink (a broken submodule pointer, no `.gitmodules`, left from before the Command Center moved to its own repo) (audit P2 / PRD §7).
- Skill-version bumps for every touched skill (G3): 06, 23, 49, 50, 53, 55, 56, 57.

## [v17.0.6]  -  2026-07-03  -  fix(audit): presentations Kanban cards now move + Skill-51 one-question-at-a-time intake (AF-INTAKE-BATCH) (July-3 audit P1-1 + PR #440 salvage)

### Risk: low-medium — a fail-soft board-progress hook + an intake-conversation doctrine change on the Signature Presentation path. No new skill/department/role, no Command Center endpoint, no `mission-control.db` schema change, no fleet rollout (repo-only). The deterministic record gate `prove_sp_intake.py` and the sacred slide method are UNCHANGED; the new intake behavior is a QC/Healer-scanned conversation autofail that NEVER gates `build_deck.py`. No off-limits/pinned enforcement file broken (the CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN is regenerated to match). No client names, credential values, or plists. Independently re-verified: presentations-lockstep pin formula MATCHES, `sync_check.py` IN SYNC, Skill-51 `verify.sh` exit 0, new intake guard 13/0, `test_cc_board.py`/`test_preflight.py` green (per agent), frontmatter gate + G1 + G3 + no-client-names PASS.

### P1-1 — presentation Kanban cards never moved
`build_deck.py` created the board card at run-start but nothing advanced it (`patch_phase` had zero callers), so every Skill-51 deck looked frozen forever. Added a fail-soft `_board_patch_phase()` (mirrors Skill-48's `ad_director._board_move`: lazy `import cc_board`, wrapped in try/except, never raises, no-op when the board is disabled or there's no `task_id`) and wired it at **4 phase boundaries**: P4-RENDER `in_progress`, P4-RENDER `done`, P8-ASSEMBLE `done`, P9-DELIVER `delivered` (auto-attaches the PROCESS-CERTIFICATE sha when present). `CANONICAL-RENDERER-PIN.sha256` regenerated to match the edited renderer.

### Intake doctrine — one-question-at-a-time wins (Trevor's ruling) + PR #440 salvage
Salvaged only PR #440's `AF-INTAKE-BATCH` guard cleanly onto v17.0.6 (dropped #440's stale v16.1.10 version markers / manifest / dept-doc reverts — the revert-trap). The Signature Presentation owner CONVERSATION is now choice-first (QUICK vs IN-DEPTH) and asks **one question at a time**; dumping the batch (or opening with no quick-vs-in-depth choice) trips `AF-INTAKE-BATCH`, an intake-conversation autofail enforced by the QC/Healer scan that never gates the build. The 8 answers are still assembled into ONE atomic machine record for the intake prover (which already requires `asked_all_at_once=False`, i.e. split). New CI guard `.github/workflows/presentation-intake-conversation-guard.yml` + `tests/unit/presentation-intake-conversation.test.sh`. Confirmed no conflict with the v17.0.1 P-SP-CLAIM / AF-SP-TYPE-UNDECLARED gate (orthogonal). NOTE: the department-wide SOP (`SOP-SIGPRES-01…ONE-BLOCK…`) + `deck-intake-driver.py` still carry one-block framing — that SOP-locked reconstruction is a tracked follow-up (July-3-Fixes).
