## [v14.3.15]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(updater): WIRE_WORKSPACE_DIR 2026.x dual-write + A3 content-hash working/ exclusion

Two targeted updater fixes that broke the v14.3.12 roll on a client VPS (core-sentinel-missing) and a client Mac box (A3 hash gate blocks version stamp).

FIX 1 — WIRE_WORKSPACE_DIR mis-place (`update-skills.sh`). The wiring phase resolved the core-update write target with a naive `$HOME/clawd`-first heuristic. On VPS boxes that still carry a legacy `/data/clawd/` directory the heuristic resolved to that dead path while the running agent reads AGENTS.md from `/data/.openclaw/agents/main/AGENTS.md` (the 2026.x agent dir). The gate then reported `core-sentinel-missing` even though wiring completed. Fix: (a) prefer `obs_resolve_workspace` (the same resolver already used by `write_update_pending_flag`) over the legacy heuristic; (b) also detect the active 2026.x agent dir (`$HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/` on Mac, `/data/.openclaw/agents/main/` on VPS) and dual-write the sentinel there so it lands in whichever path the running agent reads from. The sentinel-check gate is also extended to cover the agent dir, preventing spurious re-wiring on boxes already wired via that path.

FIX 2 — A3 content-hash `*/working/*` exclusion (`scripts/skill-content-hash.sh`). Skill 48's installer self-tests write `scripts/working/af-coverage.json` (and other ephemeral artifacts) into the hashed skill directory at install time. Those files are absent from the clean source tree, so the DEST digest always differed from the SRC digest, causing A3 to fail and the version stamp to never write — leaving the box stuck at the pre-update version. Fix: add `*/working/*` to the existing exclusion list (same rationale and scope as the `*/node_modules/*` and `*/OpenMontage/*` exclusions introduced in v14.0.1). Genuine shipped skill content (`.md`/`.py`/`.sh`) is never placed in a `working/` directory, so this exclusion does not weaken the content-integrity gate.

VERIFICATION: `bash -n update-skills.sh` and `bash -n scripts/skill-content-hash.sh` pass on bash 5.x and macOS bash 3.2. `scripts/bump-version.sh --check` reports all 10 markers at v14.3.15. On-box proof: run on a VPS with legacy `/data/clawd/` — sentinels now land in `/data/.openclaw/agents/main/AGENTS.md` (core-sentinel-missing resolved) and the A3 gate PASSES with matching SRC=DEST digest, version stamp writes v14.3.15.

## [v14.3.14]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(skill6): native page builds render REAL content again — nested section→row→col→custom-code blob (kills the v14.3.11 blank-page regression)

Root cause: since v14.3.11, `new_page_blob()` (`06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_rest_canvas.py`) built EMPTY pages. The v14.3.11 "golden template" rewrite loaded the captured funnel golden and re-minted every element id, but never rewrote the parent `child` arrays (`section.metaData.child → row.child → col.child`). The custom-code element was thereby ORPHANED from its row, so GoHighLevel's renderer hydrated the page chrome but dropped the content — a blank section. (A later interim attempt to emit a single FLAT `type=code` element directly in `section.elements` did not 500 but ALSO rendered blank: a flat element's `rawCustomCode` is NOT rendered in the preview — proven live; that is precisely why the website golden stashes its HTML in `trackingCode.footerCode`. The only structure GoHighLevel renders custom HTML from is the nested `meta=custom-code` element reached through the child chain.)

Fix (`tools/ghl_rest_canvas.py`): `new_page_blob()` now emits the render-verified NESTED structure — `section → row → col → custom-code` — minting fresh `section`/`row`/`col`/`custom-code` ids and wiring every `child` array (and `metaData.child`) to those SAME ids in a single pass, so the parent→child links can never be orphaned. The page HTML lands in the `meta=custom-code` element's `extra.customCode.value.rawCustomCode`. It also inlines the authoritative theme captured live from the render-verified golden: `general.general.colors` (the 18-entry palette), top-level `pageStyles`, `settings.settings.typography.colors`, and the generic section `metaData`/`general` — so the renderer's hydration reads of `colors` and `metaData.title` resolve (their absence 500s with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'colors')" / "(reading 'title')", both reproduced live). `trackingCode` header/footer/body are emptied so no template HTML leaks into the page. `assert_renderable_shape()` invariant 5 now locates the custom-code element by its `rawCustomCode` PAYLOAD PATH rather than a type/meta label, so it validates the nested funnel shape, the flat website shape, and any future carrier.

Live proof: built a net-new funnel page into the operator scratch location `Mct54Bwi1KlNouGXQcDX` (Convert and Flow) via the real create→autosave path and viewed it at its `/preview/<pageId>` URL in a headless browser — a real multi-section landing page rendered (visible hero with background image + headline + sub-copy + CTA, a trust band, three feature cards, a split section with a visible `<img>`, and a testimonial). HTTP 200, 0 console errors, image loaded (naturalWidth 1600), no blank section, no leaked template content. Full-page screenshot captured. Draft only — NOT published, NOT rolled to the fleet.

63 passed in `tests/test_ghl_rest_canvas.py` (incl. the B6 theme/colors + custom-code-element contract suite).

## [v14.3.13]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(skill6): GHL credential resolution searches every alias + every env store (kills the six-month image-step false-fail) + folds B7 SOP docs (closes PR #356)

Root cause: the Skill-6 image/media step false-failed `"GHL LOCATION PIT not found"` on a LOCATION Private Integration Token the operator had used for SIX MONTHS. The token was sitting in `~/.openclaw/secrets/.env` under `GOHIGHLEVEL_API_KEY` the whole time, but `ghl_media.resolve_location_pit()` checked only two env-var names in the LIVE process environment and never opened the canonical store. In a clean agent shell (where the gateway/launchd wrapper had not exported `secrets/.env`) both vars read empty and the tool fail-loud — treating "env var empty" as "credential missing" rather than "env not loaded".

Fix 1 (the false-fail — `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_media.py`): `resolve_location_pit()` / `resolve_location_id()` now resolve from EVERY known LOCATION-class alias AND, when the live env is empty, parse the canonical env STORES directly (`~/.openclaw/secrets/.env` → `~/clawd/secrets/.env` → `~/.openclaw/workspace/.env`) — the same multi-alias/multi-store pattern already used for the Google 3-alias key and for `KIE_API_KEY` in `ghl_image_stage`. LOCATION-PIT aliases: `GOHIGHLEVEL_API_KEY` → `GHL_API_KEY` → `GOHIGHLEVEL_LOCATION_PIT` → `GHL_LOCATION_PIT`. Location-id aliases: `GOHIGHLEVEL_LOCATION_ID` → `GHL_LOCATION_ID` → `GOHIGHLEVEL_ALLOWED_LOCATION_IDS` → `CAF_ALLOWED_LOCATION_IDS` (first id). The resolver NEVER falls back to an agency-class name (`*_AGENCY_PIT` / `*_AGENCY_API_KEY` — agency tokens 401 for media); if only an agency token is present it says so. The honest-fail message now names exactly which vars and stores were checked and tells the agent to `source ~/.openclaw/secrets/.env` and retry. No secret VALUES are committed (env var NAMES only). New `tests/test_ghl_media_cred_resolution.py` (18 MOCK-only cases) locks the behaviour with generic `pit-FAKE…` / `LOCFAKE…` fixtures and a redirected fake store — the real store is never read.

Fix 2 (docs — folds B7 / PR #356 into the SOP + adds the credential rule): `v2-autonomous-build-sop.md` gains §2.0.1 credential preflight (env-var→store table, AGENCY≠LOCATION warning, step-0 `source secrets/.env`, HARD RULE: real research across all env stores before any `honest_fail`, and the failure must name what it checked); §3 Images rewritten to the `ghl_image_stage.run_image_pipeline` entrypoint cross-referencing §2.0.1; §7.1 forbidden-shortcuts gains the row banning a "credential not found" verdict on an empty env var without a store search. Also folded from PR #356: §2.05 method-decision, §2.06 theme/colors object, §4.1 embed-widget flow, §7 sealed-mode verifier, §7.1 forbidden shortcuts. `SKILL.md` gains a GoHighLevel media/PIT credential block (where the LOCATION PIT + location id + KIE key live, the resolution order, the agency-401 warning, the no-false-fail rule). PR #356 is consolidated here and closed; its SKILL.md half had already landed in v14.3.11.

508 passed / 15 skipped / 0 failed (skill 06). guard-ghl-method-decision PASS. guard-ghl-verify-unfakeable PASS. qc-ghl-install-pages PASS (exit 0, 1 expected WARN on white-label URL).

## [v14.3.12]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(skill6): render_check agent-browser 0.27.0 compatibility + cc_board secret fallback + KIE timeout/retry hardening

Fix 1 (BLOCKER): `render_check()` in `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_builder.py` used two agent-browser commands that do not exist in agent-browser 0.27.0 — `html --output <path>` and `console-log --json`. The correct commands are `get html html` (stdout is rendered HTML, written to dom_path) and `console` (plain-text output, wrapped as records). The broken commands caused `render_check()` to fail on every client box, making the un-fakeable QC gate non-functional. Also corrected: screenshot now captures the tmp path from stdout and copies to png_path; console parsing now handles plain-text lines instead of expecting JSON. All 449 pytest + 3 guard scripts pass.

Fix 2 (durable): `board_config()` secret read in both `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/cc_board.py` and `48-facebook-ad-generator/scripts/cc_board.py` now falls back to `CC_WEBHOOK_SECRET` when `WEBHOOK_SECRET` is absent: `env.get("WEBHOOK_SECRET") or env.get("CC_WEBHOOK_SECRET") or ""`. This lets the board producer find the secret regardless of which env store names it.

Fix 3 (robustness): `ghl_media.generate_images()` subprocess call (`06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_media.py`) now has a hard 1800-second timeout (overridable via `KIE_SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT` env var) to prevent hung KIE processes from stalling builds indefinitely. `run_image_pipeline()` in `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_image_stage.py` now retries up to 3 attempts with 30-second backoff before reporting an honest FAIL — never returns a stub image URL. KIE live verification: KIE_API_KEY is present in env stores; live test not run inline due to 5-min initial poll wait in kie_generate.py — timeout/retry improvement ships; FLAG: live CDN URL verification deferred to first real client build.

449 passed / 15 skipped / 0 failed. guard-ghl-verify-unfakeable PASS. guard-ghl-method-decision PASS. qc-ghl-install-pages PASS (exit 0, 1 expected WARN on white-label URL).

Root cause: the pre-flight fabricated a PASS while every page 500ed ("Cannot read properties of undefined reading 'colors'") and funnel pages were blank. B2: `ghl_verify.render_check()` requires HTTP 200 + marker in rendered JavaScript-hydrated DOM (not raw bytes); `ghl_gate.require_pass()` is the only path to a PASS — `.md`/`ledger.json` prose is structurally ignored; `SealedGateViolation` prevents live=True with a stub fetcher; `v2_dispatcher` calls the gate on disk before marking VERIFIED. B3: `ghl_method.py` classifier (DIRECT vs VERCEL_EMBED); `ghl_vercel.py` with `assert_embeddable()` hard gate; `ghl_ecosystem.py` form/calendar operators. B4: `ghl_image_stage.run_image_pipeline()` — Kie.ai text-to-image, CDN verify at HTTP 200, honest failure. B5: live-captured golden page-data JSONs (funnel + website) in `references/golden/`. B6: 449 tests passing. B7: SOP + SKILL.md sealed-gate + method-decision docs. B8: CI guards + `gates.json` enforcement entries. 449 passed / 15 skipped / 0 failed.

AB_MAX_SESSIONS stays 1 (one browser). Lock/TTL/circuit-breaker/EXIT-trap teardown from browser_manager.sh cover the entire batch unchanged. ## [v14.3.9]  -  2026-06-26  -  feat(rescue-rangers): X-Rescue-Secret sender plumbing + install-time secret seeding (backward-compatible; enforcement OFF)

## [v14.3.8]  -  2026-06-26  -  feat(skill6): cc_board.py producer + INTAKE SOP section — Goal A (per-skill tag; global version stays at v14.3.7)

Per-skill release for `06-ghl-install-pages` (skill-version.txt → v14.3.8). Global version markers stay at v14.3.7 — this tag tracks the skill-6 cc_board.py producer ship only. NEW `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/cc_board.py`: fail-soft board card producer that posts to `POST /api/tasks/ingest` (Command Center >= v4.52.0); stdlib only; `job_type` → department slug routing; `--selftest` exits 0 (no network); `--demo` for live board proof. NEW `06-ghl-install-pages/v2-autonomous-build-sop.md` INTAKE section (run FIRST, before P0/P1/P2 gates). Per `06-ghl-install-pages/CHANGELOG.md` for the full per-skill entry. PR #349.

Closes the long-open Rescue Rangers webhook-auth gap: every escalation sender now reads `RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_SECRET` from its env and adds an `X-Rescue-Secret` header ONLY when the var is set — so boxes with the secret authenticate to the n8n relay while boxes without it continue to escalate unauthenticated (backward-compatible). The n8n relay enforcement switch stays OFF; this commit is the sender (box) half only.

CANONICAL FORM — AGENTS.md uses the bash-array `_RR_SECRET_ARGS` pattern (two distinct argv tokens `-H` and `X-Rescue-Secret: <val>` — correct curl argument passing, no word-splitting risk). All script senders (all verified `#!/usr/bin/env bash`) use the `${RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_SECRET:+-H X-Rescue-Secret:${RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_SECRET}}` form (tokenizes cleanly because the secret is a hex/alphanumeric string with no spaces).

SEEDING — `install.sh` now seeds `RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_SECRET` into the box's gateway-inherited env (`openclaw.json` env.vars) + `secrets/.env` from the operator's own env var of the same name — mirroring the existing `RESCUE_RANGERS_HELP_CHAT_ID` pattern. Empty operator env => box escalates unauthenticated; picks up the secret on next install/update.

SENDERS UPDATED: `AGENTS.md` (canonical client-agent escalation curl + doc note), `install.sh` (seeding), and the full script sender set: `scripts/disk-usage-alert.sh`, `scripts/agent-browser-reaper.sh`, `scripts/index-model-drift-check.sh`, `scripts/pre-july14-embedding-migration-check.sh`, `scripts/check-company-root.sh`, `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/browser_manager.sh`, `scripts/resume-onboarding.sh`, `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/resume-workforce-build.sh` (×3 sites), `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/closeout-readiness-watchdog.sh`.

ENFORCEMENT OFF: no relay-side reject of unauthenticated calls is included here. Escalation works with or without the var set. The n8n webhook soft→enforce flip is an out-of-repo operator decision, handled after the secret is distributed fleet-wide.

NO SECRET VALUE COMMITTED. `RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is referenced as an env-var name only; no literal value appears in git. All 10 version markers at v14.3.8 via `scripts/bump-version.sh v14.3.8`. `bash -n` clean on all modified scripts. Diff scanned: no secret value, no client name.

Supersedes and closes PR #346 (`feat/rescue-rangers-gaps`, base v14.3.3) — the new safer bash-array AGENTS.md form replaces the older `${VAR:+-H ...}` form from that PR; the script senders carry the same additive backward-compatible change.

## [v14.3.7]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(workforce-engine): correct the v14.3.4 CHANGELOG commit references + confirm the installer index auto-provisioning is already live

Closeout of the two real follow-ups from the Workforce-Engine review. Documentation/changelog truth only — no runtime/skill logic changed in this release; the global version rolls v14.3.5 -> v14.3.7 across all 10 markers via `scripts/bump-version.sh` (the patch skips v14.3.6 because `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` was already decoupled to v14.3.6 by the v14.3.5 presentation ship — bumping to v14.3.6 would have left the 06 browser-manager markers changed without an 06 skill-version bump and tripped CI guard G3, so the global line steps to v14.3.7 to roll 06's skill-version in lockstep).

INSTALLER INDEX AUTO-PROVISIONING — VERIFIED ALREADY LIVE, NO CHANGE NEEDED. The review claimed `install.sh` "only got a version-string bump" and still lacked the promised Step-6b index auto-provisioning, so a fresh/updated box would fall back to a ~63-minute re-embed. That claim is FALSE against current main: `install.sh` already carries a complete Step-6b prebuilt-index provisioning block (`_PIDX_*`). It reads `shared-utils/prebuilt-index/INDEX-MANIFEST.json` (48 personas, 7615 chunks, sha256 `5554ec1e…`, asset `prebuilt-index-v2.0.0/gemini-index.sqlite.gz`), no-ops when the index is already present (existing-chunks >= manifest-chunks guard), downloads the v2.0.0 release asset, sha256-verifies BEFORE decompress, installs atomically (tmp + `mv -f`), and routes EVERY failure path (download fail / sha mismatch / decompress fail / missing manifest) through `warn`+continue — it never `exit`s, so a provisioning hiccup can never break an install (additive, idempotent, fail-safe). Because the provisioning is already on main, none was added — a duplicate would have been wrong. No risky installer change was shipped or needed.

CHANGELOG TRUTH — v14.3.4 SECTION CORRECTED (the only substantive edit, already landed in this branch). (a) The phantom commit `c7a210ce` cited as the home of the `build-workforce.py` fabrication-consent gate DOES NOT EXIST in the repo (`git cat-file -t c7a210ce` -> "Not a valid object name"); the gate actually landed in `9e9ba795` (v14.3.2) and the reference now points there. (b) The populate-sops "already-authored substance skip" furnace kill was mis-credited to v14.3.4; the real skip-logic landed in `9e9ba795` (v14.3.2) and v14.3.4 (`ff03728f`) only corrected its log lines (`git show ff03728f -- '*populate-sops*'` = 5 insertions / 3 deletions, all `f"…"` print strings) — the credit is re-scoped accordingly. Every commit SHA written into the CHANGELOG (`9e9ba795`, `ff03728f`) was confirmed to exist via `git cat-file -t` before commit.

No client names or secrets. Lockstep re-verified green: `register-library-additions.py --check`=0 (419 roles / 110 sops / 13 personas), `hash-content-manifest.py --check`=0 (all content_sha match, no token leaks), `bash -n install.sh`=0, all 10 version markers agree at v14.3.7, and CI guard G3 satisfied (06 + 23 skill-version.txt both roll in lockstep with their changed content). The `_index.json` change is the version field only (unicode preserved — no escaping churn).

## [v14.3.5]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(presentations): presentation department hardened end-to-end (delivery, font gate, woven research, renderer identity, GoHighLevel folder by software, prompt floor, routing + permissions)

Closes the full presentation-department review (R1-R10 + the three independent verifies). The department now runs a deck to a finished, complete bundle, and every fix is enforced by a gate that bites on a negative fixture — not just documented. Rebased onto current main (ff03728f, v14.3.4); the `_index.json` content manifest was re-hashed against live disk AFTER the rebase (last step), and CI guards re-verified green.

DELIVERY NO LONGER BREAKS (Fix #2 + #10). The presenter speech filename is unified to the plural `PRESENTERS-SPEECH` across `build_deck.py` (`DELIVERABLES_REQUIRED` + the manifest producers at the 640/647/654 region), `PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json`, `MASTER-QC-AUTOFAIL-RULESET.md`/`SOP-SLIDE-00`, and the test fixture. The old singular spelling made a real run die at the final completeness check (`AF-BUNDLE-COMPLETE`, exit 5); the legacy singular is still accepted on READ so an older bundle is never flagged dirty. A new mechanical last-mile gate (`delivery_gate.py --selftest`, 7 cases) enforces the five-file client package whitelist, requires a GoHighLevel upload record, and ground-truths the destination; every destination receives the ENTIRE five-file package (not just the PowerPoint), and the Director -> Delivery Concierge handoff is wired.

REAL TINY-FONT GATE (Fix #6). `build_deck.py` gains a deterministic numeric rejector (`check_font_floor`): an 18pt body floor, a 4-5 step modular type-scale, and a 4.5 contrast minimum, with a negative test that proves it rejects 12pt/non-modular/low-contrast and passes a valid design. The Typography Architect now EMITS the size tokens the gate reads (`min_body_pt`/`type_scale_steps`) so the gate has real data instead of a vision opinion.

RESEARCH WOVEN ACROSS THE WHOLE DECK (Fix #7). Research facts/quotes/stats are mapped to specific slides (`research_map.json`, phase P-3.5-RESEARCH-MAP before P4-COPY), the copy template carries a "research used here" tag, and a breadth gate (`_chk_research_map`) REJECTS a deck that draws on one fact on one slide or fewer than the distinct-item floor — the gate previously only checked that a research pack existed.

RENDERER IDENTITY CORRECTED (Fix #1). Every guide/rulebook names the live builder `build_deck.py`/`process_manifest.json`; the retired `render_deck.py`/`render_manifest.json` is documented in `docs/LEGACY-RETIREMENT.md`.

GOHIGHLEVEL FOLDER CREATED BY SOFTWARE, NEVER A BROWSER (Fix #8). The media folder is created via the verified-working API pattern (presentations `ghl_media.py` re-exports the canonical Skill-48 `create_media_folder()` — `POST /medias/folder`, `Version: 2021-07-28`, location private integration token, 201 -> folder id; non-2xx falls back to a name-prefix, never a fabricated id). Images upload over the API (`POST /medias/upload-file`, multipart, `parentId`). Driving the GoHighLevel user interface in a browser is explicitly FORBIDDEN in the scripts and the role docs; the only browser-automation token in the department is that forbidding declaration. Documentation uses placeholders only — no client location ids or tokens.

PROMPT FLOOR 1,500 -> 5,000 (Fix #4). Every doc prompt floor now reads 5,000 to match the enforced `PROMPT_CHAR_FLOOR=5000`, including the master pipeline file and `slide-image-creator.md`.

GARBLED TEXT IS RE-GENERATED, NEVER OVERLAID (Fix #3). The "paste garbled text as a native text box" instruction is removed from the sub-guides and replaced with re-generate-the-image -> escalate-to-a-human; text is always baked into the image, and the native-text-overlay path raises `AF-OVERLAY-DELIVERED` (the only image composite exception is the real-logo PIL composite).

DARK-FLAG RECONCILED (Fix #5), ENFORCEMENT REGISTERED (Fix #11). The dark-theme flag the code reads and the value roles record are reconciled so a real dark-theme request is not wrongly blocked; the previously-unregistered intelligence-engine checks are registered in the manifest + ruleset and the pitch-engine checks have a test.

ROUTING + PERMISSIONS (Fix #9). A Presentations row is added to `SOP-00-Owner-Task-Routing.md`, and `build-workforce.py` grants `presentations` the SAME protective permission set graphics/video/audio get (`GENERATION_DEPT_IDS`), so the "no permission" headless stall stops.

LOCKSTEP / VERSIONING. Global version rolled to v14.3.5 across all 10 markers (`scripts/bump-version.sh`) incl. `cc-compat.json`; `06-ghl-install-pages` skill-version decoupled to v14.3.6 to satisfy G3 after bump-version.sh rolled its browser-manager version markers (markers only — no skill-06 logic change). All gates re-verified green (OPENCLAW_PLATFORM=mac): `sync_check`=0, `test_preflight`=0, `gate_integrity` (Guard A)=0, `doctrine_residual` (Guard B)=0, `run_signature_deck --plan`=0 (P-3.5-RESEARCH-MAP before P4-COPY; plural `PRESENTERS-SPEECH.md`), `delivery_gate --selftest`=0, `register-library-additions --check`=0 (419 roles), `hash-content-manifest --check`=0, and the presentations-lockstep layout-PDF present. No client names or secrets; the Command Center repo and `qc-scorer.ts` are untouched (the presentation card hookup shares the existing Command Center endpoint).

## [v14.3.4]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(workforce-engine): AI-Workforce-Interview anti-fabrication enforcement + subsystem furnace kills + persona/index wiring hardening

End-to-end ("in-the-end") hardening of the AI-workforce ENGINE clients use daily: the AI Workforce Interview, Book-to-Persona, persona matching + semantic persona-selection, the role/department/SOP index, and the Zero Human Experience handoff. Additive, separate from the concurrent rescue-rangers (v14.3.2), Skill-48, Skill-6, and presentation-dept work. Companion change set lands in `blackceo-command-center` (kanban/Command-Center wiring). Note: the P0 anti-fabrication GATE in `build-workforce.py` + `gemini-section-indexer.py` content-hash skip + `verify-wiring.sh` runtime-dir assertion + `install.sh` Step-6b index provisioning + the Skill-22/23 `INSTALL.md` repoints already merged to main inside v14.3.2 (commit `9e9ba795`); this release completes the coherent pair and adds the remaining buckets.

P0 FABRICATION (G1) — `qc-interview-completion.py`: the full-interview path can no longer record client answers the client never gave. A synthetic non-interactive transcript with no valid `ownerConsent{decision,source,decidedAt,decidedBy,sessionId}` opt-in record is now a HARD violation (`theme_id: non-interactive-synthetic-transcript`, exit 3) instead of a silent pass; the tailored-short-interview exemption now additionally requires a valid consent record. Pairs with the `build-workforce.py` consent gate already in main (v14.3.2) — fabrication is permitted ONLY when the client explicitly opts into self-setup/fast mode.

FURNACE KILLS (G2/G3/G4/G5/G13) — `resume-workforce-build.sh`: monotonic high-water-mark stuck detector (status oscillation can no longer reset the stuck counter), absolute total-ping ceiling, and a dispatch-overlap in-flight gate; the v14.1.5 durable PARK/stuck-cap is KEPT and only made stricter. `run-closeout.sh`: per-client concurrency lock (covers the cron + nohup-bypass + manual paths through the full paid window) + `.qualityHeld` re-entry short-circuit — kills 2-3x KIE.AI/Veo re-spend on held artifacts. `populate-sops-from-manifest.py`: the already-authored substance skip — so a resume re-fire does not re-author done custom depts — landed in v14.3.2 (`9e9ba795`); this release only corrects its log lines to report an accurate dispatched-vs-already-authored-skipped count (`ff03728f`, print lines only). `persona-inbox-watcher.sh`: bounded failure cap (3) + quarantine + escalate-once for un-convertible books (was ~144 full-pipeline runs/day forever). `semantic_task_fit.py` + `persona-selector-v2.py`: shared single task-embed across Stage-C retrieval and Layer-5 scoring (kills the double-embed), top-k chunk-averaged persona centroids, and a Stage-D LLM finalist cap / heuristic gate so a fresh unbounded box cannot fan out to ~160 LLM calls.

WIRING (G6) — `orchestrator.py` (Book-to-Persona): Phase-2 chunk-loop dedent (oversized books no longer drop all-but-last chunk), Mac/VPS persona path-divergence crash fix, and deterministic offline auto-classification of `domain[]`/`perspective[]` so auto-added personas are routable instead of invisible.

DOCS (G5/G7) — `22-.../INSTALL.md`: manifest-driven 48-persona prebuilt-index download + a HARD verify gate (was silently passing the stale 40-persona index).

LOCKSTEP: global version rolled to v14.3.4 across all 10 markers (`scripts/bump-version.sh`) incl. `cc-compat.json`; skill versions bumped for the touched skills — `22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system` v6.7.10, `37-zhc-closeout` v12.14.8 (23-ai-workforce tracks the global); `06-ghl-install-pages` skill-version decoupled to v14.3.5 to satisfy G3 after `bump-version.sh` rolled the four browser-manager version markers in that dir (markers only — no skill-06 logic change). Rebased onto current main (a4212f4e, v14.3.3); `_index.json` content manifest re-hashed against live disk on rebase. CI guards re-verified green against the changed files: bounded-workforce-build-resume (24/0), cron-owner-chat (83/0), closeout-watchdog static gates, qc-static (no-Anthropic-strings + no-flat-scoring + SOP-substance-models), full-funnel persona-selector A7 PASS.

## [v14.3.3]  -  2026-06-26  -  feat(paid-advertisement): Skill 48 producer-side Command Center board caller (ad jobs land + move on the Kanban, fail-soft)

Skill 48 (`48-facebook-ad-generator`) now actually posts each ad job to the Command Center board, so ad jobs appear as Kanban cards and move across statuses as the run progresses. This is the producer side of the live `POST/PATCH /api/ad-campaigns` endpoints shipped in Command Center v4.50.0 (merge `89b6d5e`); previously only the docs, a skipped smoke test, and a QC check referenced the board — no caller existed.

NEW — `48-facebook-ad-generator/scripts/cc_board.py`: a stdlib-only (`urllib`) caller that creates a campaign card and updates its stage/status. It is FAIL-SOFT by construction — a board outage, an unreachable host, or a missing token NEVER blocks or fails an ad job: every network path is double-guarded (inner `try/except` returns `None`/`False`, outer `except` in the director swallows), failures are emitted as greppable `[cc_board] ... failed; run continues ungrouped` stderr lines, and the `campaign_id` is still stamped into `s7-deliver-receipt.json` so S7-DELIVER and the `AF-FBAD-BOARD` QC check stay green offline.

AUTH PARITY — the caller signs the EXACT bytes it sends with `hmac.new(WEBHOOK_SECRET, raw_body, sha256).hexdigest()` into `x-webhook-signature` and sends `Authorization: Bearer MC_API_TOKEN`, byte-for-byte matching what the Command Center verifies (`createHmac('sha256', WEBHOOK_SECRET).update(rawBody)`); both are no-ops when the secret/token are unset. The caller's legal status transitions are a verified subset of the server's `LEGAL_TRANSITIONS`. Credentials are read from env/config only — never hardcoded, fail-soft if absent.

WIRING — `ad_director.py` calls `cc_board.create_campaign` / `set_stage_status` around the S7-DELIVER path (`_board_ensure_campaign` / `_board_move`); `cc-compat.json` `minVersion` + `pinnedTag` bump to `v4.50.0` so fleet-refresh and the skill-32 installer guarantee the endpoint is present (graceful degradation to ungrouped cards on an older board). Tests: `scripts/test_cc_board.py` (14 cases). Skill 48 QC and the manifest↔code↔ruleset lockstep remain green.

## [v14.3.2]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(rescue-rangers): migrate maintenance-roles doc escalation path from deprecated Telegram message-send to n8n webhook

Completes the Rescue Rangers pipeline end-to-end review (`feat/rescue-rangers-pipeline-fixes`). All three fix buckets confirmed and integrated; routing test PASSED with evidence (receipt + n8n relay execution + real-time push + rescue agent ACK + clean close — no client touched, no live remediation).

FIX — `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/suggested-roles/openclaw-maintenance-suggested-roles.md`: the "Rescue Rangers Escalation (applies to all 4 specialists)" governing rule and Role 13 description still instructed maintenance specialists to escalate via `openclaw message send -t "${RESCUE_RANGERS_HELP_CHAT_ID}"` — the deprecated bot-to-bot Telegram path that `AGENTS.md:519` explicitly forbids (bots cannot read other bots). Both locations are now updated: the governing rule shows the canonical webhook curl (`curl -s -X POST "$RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_URL" ...`) with an explicit "Do NOT use `openclaw message send`" guard; Role 13 description and its Tools line reference `$RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_URL` and clarify that owner on-change notifications still use `openclaw message send` (only escalations switch to the webhook). This matches the 6 safety-script fixes (`ef517e57`) and the SOP/role-file fixes already present in `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/openclaw-maintenance/`.

ROUTING TEST RESULT (operator-box only, no client touched): synthetic payload `{"action":"escalate","source":"ROUTING-TEST","agent":"synthetic","message":"[ROUTING TEST — DO NOT REMEDIATE]..."}` — HTTP 200 + ticket queued + n8n relay execution 82439 success (0.86s) + real-time push to Mac rescue-receiver at 10:22:42 UTC + `rescue-rangers` agent replied "ACK" (chars=3) + answer posted back to relay (relayStatus=200) + `✅ RESOLVED` close returned `closed_resolved`. All 4 signals confirmed. Evidence saved locally by operator.

PIPELINE MAP (from end-to-end review RR1–RR5): two entry paths (client-told + AI-auto-detect) converge on the same curl POST to `$RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_URL` (n8n relay `GdymshUbNb9eaOAC`, `https://main.blackceoautomations.com/webhook/rescue-rangers`). Relay Brain classifies, enforces 25/day cap, enqueues, posts `🆘` to operator group `-1003865262028` thread 3, and CF-pushes real-time to Mac `rescue-receiver.mjs` (127.0.0.1:8799, launchd `ai.openclaw.rescue-receiver`), which runs the `rescue-rangers` agent (`openclaw/deepseek-v4-pro:cloud`) and posts the answer back. 10-min fallback poller (`rescue-rangers-poller.sh`) provides redundancy; 2,164 lifetime executions, 0 errors. WF-2 (Trevor-Inbound triage) is the operator side-door.

VERIFICATION: routing test PASS (4 signals, live evidence) · bucket-1 scripts OK (ef517e57, 6 files) · bucket-2 doc OK (this commit, 1 file) · bucket-3 AGENTS.md OK (ef517e57) · RR5 Rescue Rangers operator group id `-1003865262028` confirmed not a client and not Trevor personal 5252140759.

## [v14.3.1]  -  2026-06-26  -  fix(skill6): enforce headless-only backup path, wire operator-only safety alarm, correct reaper cadence

Three scoped fast-follow fixes to Skill 6 (`06-ghl-install-pages`), all in the SHIP-WITH-FIX / nice-to-have tier (NOT rollout blockers — the blockers shipped v14.1.4 and the loop fix v14.1.5). No gate weakened; pure hardening + observability + doc hygiene.

FIX #3 — headless-only backup path is now ENFORCED, not doc-only. The pre-commit/CI guard `scripts/guard-agent-browser-managed.sh` previously only scanned `agent-browser <verb>` calls; it now also FAILS the build (new section 5) on any `headless=False` or an unguarded Playwright `launch(` under `06-ghl-install-pages/` — in `.py` code AND in language-tagged code fences of the docs. The bounded "Option B" dispatcher (`tools/v2_dispatcher.py`: coded 1800s wall-clock cap + max-inflight=1 + 30s backoff) is now the REQUIRED DEFAULT executor in `v2-autonomous-build-sop.md`; Option A's "3 retries" prose is now explicit operator opt-in. `v2_dispatcher.py` gains a runnable `--print-config`/`--selftest` entrypoint so the bounds are self-proving.

FIX #2 — the safety alarms are now WIRED, and OPERATOR-ONLY. `RESCUE_RANGERS_HELP_CHAT_ID` is read by two safety alarms (`tools/browser_manager.sh` breaker-trip + `scripts/agent-browser-reaper.sh` leak tripwire) but was never assigned, so both no-op'd silently — a stuck/looping/leaking box was invisible. `install.sh` now seeds it from the OPERATOR's OWN env var of the same name (gateway-inherited via `openclaw.json` env.vars — the same delivery path as `RESCUE_RANGERS_WEBHOOK_URL`); the chat id is NEVER a committed default. Both send sites now refuse any id that is not the operator GROUP (`^-100[0-9]+$` — a Telegram supergroup), so the alarm can physically never DM a client or an individual; empty/non-group id ⇒ stays silent (no client fallback). The reaper tripwire gains a durable rising-edge dedup stamp under `OC_ROOT`, so the hourly sweep alerts ONCE per breach episode and re-arms only after the condition clears.

FIX #1 — doc cadence corrected. Stale `*/10` / "every 10 minutes" wording for the host reaper was corrected to the real hourly cadence (`13 * * * *`, wired by `ensure-pipeline-crons.sh`) across `06-ghl-install-pages/` (SKILL.md, CORE_UPDATES.md ×2, ghl-browser-builder-full.md ×2, CHANGELOG.md). Gmail-2FA "~10-minute expiry" prose is unrelated and intentionally untouched.

VERIFICATION (all green, re-run on a fresh rebase onto v14.3.0): `guard-agent-browser-managed.sh`=0 (section 5 proven to FAIL a headed `.py` and a ```python``` fence, PASS on `headless=True` + bare prose) · `pytest tests/test_browser_manager_singleton.py tests/test_v2_dispatcher.py`=50 passed (incl. new `TestHeadlessOnlyGuard` + `TestRescueRangersOperatorOnly`, all chat-id-free) · `v2_dispatcher.py --selftest`=0 · lockstep: cron-owner-chat 83/83, no-operator-comingle 5/5, core-updates-wiring 18/18, bounded-resume 19/19, version-consistency v14.3.1 across all markers. Operator-group gate proven against all 12 known client/Trevor DM ids → only `-100…` operator group sends; reaper dedup proven: two consecutive over-cap runs ⇒ exactly 1 alert; breach→resolve→breach ⇒ 2.

## [v14.3.0]  -  2026-06-26  -  feat(paid-advertisement): Skill 48 ad foreman now SELF-CORRECTS fixable failures and PARKS+RESUMES dangerous ones instead of dying

Skill 48's Facebook/Instagram ad foreman (`ad_director.py`) used to HARD-ABORT the whole job on any failed check — throwing away every artifact already produced (and, on a paid run, already paid for). It now does the right thing per failure class, and never discards work:

WHAT CHANGED (plain language): fixable problems now self-heal and the job keeps going; genuinely dangerous problems save their spot and resume later instead of dying. No gate was weakened and no fake passes were introduced — every fix re-runs the real check against the really-fixed artifact.

TWO-TIER RECOVERY (`ad_recovery.py`, wired into the foreman):
- **RECOVERABLE failures** (a quality/rule problem fixable by redoing one artifact, plus transient hiccups): the foreman re-does ONLY the failing artifact using the gate's own feedback, up to a bounded budget (default 3, override `FBAD_MAX_FIX_ATTEMPTS`), re-runs the REAL checker, and CONTINUES the job on success. Only when the fix-attempt budget is exhausted does it escalate. A second anti-loop guard parks on `no_progress` when redos stop changing the produced artifact, so it can never spin forever.
- **NON-RECOVERABLE conditions** (over the money ceiling / out of balance, a fabrication-or-tampering check, or a not-yet-granted HUMAN approval): the foreman does NOT self-correct past these. It writes a DURABLE save-point (`PARKED.json` + a box-level pointer under the permanent `FBAD_OC_ROOT`, NOT a temp dir a reboot would wipe) capturing every attested phase, every artifact, the per-run receipt id, and which gate parked it — then PAUSES (park, not destroy).

RESUME (`scripts/unpark-ad-run.sh` + `ad_director.py --resume`): when the blocker clears (money added / human approves / the bad piece corrected), a resume re-enters at the exact last-incomplete phase and SKIPS everything already completed/paid — idempotent on the receipt id, so it never re-charges and never re-uploads. Re-validates existing receipts; the paid S5-IMAGE-GEN / S7-DELIVER phases are not re-run once attested.

INVARIANTS HELD: self-correct never bypasses a gate or fabricates a pass; the dangerous gates (overspend, fabrication/tampering, missing human approval) still STOP — now as a resumable park rather than a silent proceed OR a fatal abort; no work is ever discarded (every stop is a save-point); the anti-lie / no-false-"done" principle is fully intact. Reuses the same durable-checkpoint + explicit-resume philosophy shipped in the v14.1.5 loop fix.

LOCKSTEP HELD (manifest↔foreman↔ruleset↔checkers↔CI): the 37-autofail manifest (`universal-sops/fb-ad-craft/AD-PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json`) + ruleset now carry a per-code `recovery` classification — 25 `auto` (maxfix≥1), 12 `park` (maxfix=0). Every genuinely dangerous code is `park`/maxfix=0 (`AF-FBAD-IMAGE-TASKID`, `AF-FBAD-TARGETING-REAL`, `AF-FBAD-COST-CEILING`, `AF-FBAD-KIE-BALANCE`, `AF-FBAD-APPROVE`, `AF-FBAD-DEP-SKIPPED`, `AF-FBAD-TALLY-CROSS`, `AF-FBAD-QC-INDEPENDENCE`). `ad_sync_check.py` gains R4 (a dangerous gate can never be downgraded to self-correct — proven to bite). Guard A (`ad_gate_integrity_check.py`) now also enforces declared==enforced==tested for every recovery class. New negative suite `test_ad_recovery.py` drives the real CLI: auto-redo→DONE, budget-exhaust→park, fabrication→park (exit 5), human-gate→await→resume-after-approval→DONE with ledger event count + spend UNCHANGED. The legacy `--phase` hard-abort path is unchanged (GOOD attests through PUBLISH exit 0; BAD bypass HARD-ABORTS `AF-FBAD-DEP-SKIPPED` exit 2).

VERIFICATION (all green, independently re-run through the real `ad_director.py` CLI with a real simulated reboot): `ad_sync_check`=0 (manifest_version 2, 10 phases / 37 autofails / 8 roles, R1–R4 in lockstep) · `ad_gate_integrity_check` Guard A = 0 (37 codes; recovery-coverage auto-redo=25 / auto-budget-park=25 / park-immediate=12) · `test_ad_preflight`=0 · `test_ad_recovery`=0 · `qc-facebook-ad-generator.sh`=0 · `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`=0 · all skill `.py` py_compile + `.sh` bash -n clean. Skill's own `skill-version.txt` = 1.1.0. All 10 repo version markers → v14.3.0.

## [v14.2.0]  -  2026-06-25  -  feat(paid-advertisement): Skill 48 facebook-ad-generator — FB/IG batch-ad pipeline with a bulletproof gate-and-attest enforcement spine

New Skill 48 (`48-facebook-ad-generator`) in the existing `paid-advertisement` department. Turns a show/product bio + audience profile into a complete, PLAI-ready batch of 10 Facebook & Instagram ads, mirroring the Skill 47 (Movie Producer) bulletproof pattern: single source-of-truth manifest + Python gate-and-attest foreman + autofail QC ruleset + lockstep CI + GOOD/BAD fixtures.

WHAT THE SKILL PRODUCES (every run): ~70 overlay lines → human picks top 10 (human gate #1) → 10 ad bodies + 10 headlines + 10 image prompts (S2/S3/S4 run in parallel) → 10 square 1500×1500 images via Kie.ai gpt-image with the text BAKED INTO the image → a verified PLAI three-tier audience-targeting brief → images hosted in the client's GoHighLevel media library (public links, per-run folder) → a copy-paste-ready ad-text document (Notion → Google Doc → plain text; headline + body as separate blocks) → a PLAI-ready handoff package, then human gate #2 (approve-to-publish). PLAI is the only ad path — no direct Meta/Facebook API call.

ENFORCEMENT SPINE (a rule not auto-failed at a gate does not exist):
- `universal-sops/fb-ad-craft/AD-PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json` — single source of truth: 10 phases, real dependency map (not a straight line), 37 autofails, 8 roles.
- `ad_director.py` — the foreman: dependency-map gating (S2/S3/S4 parallel after pick-10; S6 after S2+S3; S7 after S5+S6), money math, run-id idempotency, non-skippable human + publish gates; documented exit codes (2 phase-skip, 3 bad receipt, 4 over money ceiling).
- `MASTER-AD-QC-AUTOFAIL-RULESET.md` Section-5 machine-readable table (37 AF rows) + `ad_build_check.py` offline receipt checks with anti-fakery blacklists.
- Lockstep guards `ad_sync_check.py` (manifest↔driver↔build-check↔ruleset↔roles↔SOPs) and `ad_gate_integrity_check.py` (Guard A: declared==enforced==tested); negative suite `test_ad_preflight.py`; GOOD/BAD fixtures `make-ad-fixtures.sh`; CI `.github/workflows/ad-pipeline-lockstep.yml`.

LOCKED DECISIONS WIRED: gpt-image model id auto-adopts future versions (`GPT_IMAGE_MODEL_PREFIX="gpt-image-"` + `startswith()`, never hardcoded to gpt-image-2 — gpt-image-2/3/N pass, dalle/flux/empty fail); money control = up-front estimate ≤ per-job ceiling + cheap LOCAL running tally that stops before crossing (not a per-image balance call) + one Kie balance preflight at start; unique run-id idempotency ledger so a retry never re-spends or re-uploads; two human pause points only (pick-10, approve). Independent QC: a step passes only with zero autofails AND an 8.5+ score from a worker other than the builder; below 8.5 auto-redoes only the failing piece. NO ad-policy gate and NO separate OCR/text-reading step (legibility is the human eyeball at approve + the render-proof), both by explicit decision.

GoHighLevel: new `create_media_folder()` (POST /medias/folder, header Version 2021-07-28, client's own location PIT) + name-prefix fallback, added to `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/ghl_media.py` (and the skill's vendored copy).

VERIFICATION (all green, independently re-run): `ad_sync_check`=0 (10 phases / 37 autofails / 8 roles in sync) · `ad_gate_integrity_check` Guard A = 0 (37 declared == 37 enforced == 37 tested) · `test_ad_preflight`=0 (every autofail negative-triggered) · `qc-facebook-ad-generator.sh` = 43 passed / 0 failed (GOOD run ATTESTS every phase through PUBLISH; BAD bypass HARD-ABORTS `AF-FBAD-DEP-SKIPPED` exit 2) · driven adversarially through the real `ad_director.py` CLI, all 37 declared autofails hard-abort, zero no-op gates · `qc-assert-repo-consistency.py`=0 · `qc-assert-no-client-names.sh`=0 · all skill `.py` py_compile + `.sh` bash -n clean.

ALSO: 2 new role seats (`facebook-instagram-ad-run-producer.md`, `direct-response-ad-copywriter.md`) + 8 SOPs (`SOP-FBAD-01..08`); `persona-selector-v2.py` gains a `paid-advertisement` `DEPT_DOMAIN_TAGS` entry (additive backstop); Skill 48 registered in root `install.sh` (append-only block) + README inventory. Skill's own `skill-version.txt` = 1.0.0. All 10 repo version markers → v14.2.0 (the `06` and `23` skill-version.txt bumps satisfy G3 for the shared additive edits). Net-new Command Center `POST /api/ad-campaigns` work is tracked separately in the `blackceo-command-center` repo and is NOT part of this release.

## [v14.1.5]  -  2026-06-25  -  fix(crons+breaker): durable PARK marker + breaker-aware self-disabling resume cron — permanently kills the stuck-build resume loop

Permanently fixes the 3-week "stuck build keeps re-firing" loop (the self-resuming workforce-build cron that drained an idle client's credits). Two linked root causes, both closed:

ROOT CAUSE 1 — the PARK marker was VOLATILE and effectively UNREAD. When the agent-browser circuit-breaker parked a flaky build it wrote its BLOCKED marker under `${TMPDIR}/agent-browser/breaker/` (`06-ghl-install-pages/tools/browser_manager.sh` old line 97 `LOCKDIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/agent-browser"` + the breaker file/marker under `$LOCKDIR/breaker`). TMPDIR is wiped on every reboot (`/var/folders/*` on macOS, tmpfs `/tmp` on Linux), so a parked build silently un-parked on restart. And the code comment claimed "the */15 resume cron honors this marker" — it did NOT: `23-ai-workforce-blueprint/scripts/resume-workforce-build.sh` never read it.

ROOT CAUSE 2 — the */15 `workforce-build-resume` cron was a NEVER-STOP token furnace. `resume-workforce-build.sh` (old lines 156-205, "Rule 8 / NEVER-STOP run accounting") only SLOWED a stuck build to ~every 2h and re-fired it FOREVER (≈96 agent turns/day into the gateway's refusal). Even a self-removed cron was resurrected weekly because `scripts/ensure-pipeline-crons.sh` `_ensure_workforce_build_resume` re-registered it on every `update-skills.sh` / `install.sh` run.

THE FIX (durable, breaker-aware, hard-capped, operator-only un-park):
- **DURABLE PARK marker** — `browser_manager.sh` now keeps the breaker counter + BLOCKED marker AND a canonical box-level PARK marker (`<openclaw-root>/workspace/.park/workforce-build.parked`) in the box's DURABLE state dir, NOT TMPDIR (lock + leases stay ephemeral, which is correct). Detection mirrors every pipeline script (VPS `/data` first, then Mac `$HOME`); falls back to the old ephemeral path only when no onboarded root exists (CI/dev), so the 31 `test_browser_manager_singleton.py` tests stay hermetic.
- **The resume path + the breaker both READ it.** `bm_breaker_check` refuses if either marker is present. `resume-workforce-build.sh` checks the same box-level marker FIRST every fire and, if parked, STOPS (self-removes its cron, exits) — auto-resume never happens silently. A breaker trip now ALSO writes the box-level marker, so a Skill-6 breaker trip stops the Skill-23 resume cron too.
- **Hard consecutive-stuck cap that auto-DISABLES the cron.** `resume-workforce-build.sh` tracks a progress fingerprint (department statuses + library/closeout state, excluding volatile timestamps). After `MAX_STUCK_FIRES` (default 24, override `WORKFORCE_RESUME_MAX_STUCK_FIRES`, floor 2) CONSECUTIVE fires with ZERO progress it PARKS the build, escalates ONCE to Rescue Rangers + operator, and self-removes the cron. A PROGRESSING build never trips it (the counter resets the instant state advances); pre-interview / pre-seed phases are never parked.
- **Registrar + installer are PARK-AWARE.** `ensure-pipeline-crons.sh` and `install.sh` skip re-registering a parked box's resume cron, so the self-disable STICKS across the weekly backfill (the old resurrection vector).
- **Intentional un-park path.** New `scripts/unpark-build.sh` (operator-only) clears the marker + breaker state + counters, resets the one-shot escalation flags, and re-registers the cron. `--status` / `--dry-run` / `--no-cron` modes included.
- **Enforcement.** New `tests/unit/bounded-workforce-build-resume.test.sh` (24 checks) + `.github/workflows/stuck-build-park-guard.yml` lock the fix in (durable marker, hard cap, self-disable, park-aware registrar/installer, breaker cross-stop, un-park path), mirroring the existing `bounded-resume-cron.test.sh` that already bounded the onboarding-resume cron.

OS-safe (Mac + Hostinger + Contabo): `bash -n` passes under bash 5.x AND macOS system bash 3.2 on every edited shell file; the agent-browser singleton guard PASSES, all 31 `test_browser_manager_singleton.py` tests pass, the cron-owner-chat guard (83 checks) and onboarding bounded-resume guard (19 checks) pass. Verified end-to-end under bash 3.2: a parked job stays parked across a simulated reboot, the resume cron does not re-fire a parked job, and a stuck job that fails K times is disabled instead of looping.

## [v14.1.4]  -  2026-06-25  -  security(agent-browser): BOX-LEVEL headless LOCK — no visible browser window can ever open on a client box (Mac or VPS)

Permanently closes the "visible browser window on a client box" risk. Skill 06 forced headless ONLY inside its own wrappers (`browser_manager.sh --headed false`), so three paths could still open a VISIBLE Chromium on a client screen: (1) a raw agent-typed `agent-browser open`; (2) the Skill-03 install smoke test; (3) an inherited/leftover `{"headed":true}` config — and a box on a Skill-6 bundle OLDER than the guard had ZERO protection. The lock now lives at the BOX level, not just the wrapper. OS-aware: Mac + Hostinger-VPS + Contabo paths unchanged. `bash -n` passes on every edited shell file; the agent-browser singleton guard PASSES and all 31 `test_browser_manager_singleton.py` tests pass.

- **(1) BOX-LEVEL HEADLESS PIN (the core lock) — `install.sh`.** `inject_shared_operator_secrets` now pins `AGENT_BROWSER_HEADED=false` into the GATEWAY-INHERITED env (`openclaw.json` `env.vars`, the same mechanism that delivers `PODBEAN_*` / `RESCUE_RANGERS_*` / Skill-44 GHL creds to every gateway child — confirmed gateway-inherited on BOTH Mac and VPS) PLUS `secrets/.env` (chmod 600) as a belt. EVERY browser the gateway spawns — including a raw `agent-browser open` and an old-guard Skill-6 call — now defaults to headless. Schema-safe (`env.vars` is an established key, unlike the removed `browser.agentBrowser` that crash-looped the strict 2026.6.8 schema); runs unconditionally on both platforms at Step 1.5.
- **(2) SMOKE-TEST FIX — `03-agent-browser/INSTALL.md`.** The Step-4 install smoke test `agent-browser open https://example.com` (ran headed during rollout) is now `agent-browser --headed false open https://example.com`.
- **(3) CONFIG SCRUB + GUARD — `install.sh`.** The existing `browser.agentBrowser` self-heal is extended to ALSO recursively strip any TRUTHY `headed` key anywhere in `openclaw.json` (top-level, `browser.headed`, `browser.<sub>.headed`, nested) so a leftover/inherited headed config can never override the pin. A benign `headed:false` is left untouched; backed-up + atomic; OS-portable (python3).
- **(4) VERSION-GATE B1 — `06-ghl-install-pages/tools/browser_manager.sh`.** `bm_ensure` (the one entrypoint every build path calls before the first open) now calls `bm_require_current_guard` FIRST: it refuses (75) if `AGENT_BROWSER_HEADED` resolves truthy, and refuses (76) if `browser_manager.sh` is OLDER than the fixed `BM_HEADLESS_LOCK_FLOOR=v14.1.4` (portable dotted-numeric compare via awk; bash-3.2 safe). An old-guard box can no longer open a visible window through the build path, and any auto-fired (silent-hourly-agentTurn furnace) build is gated through the same check. The standalone verbs propagate the refusal (`bm_ensure || exit $?`) so no false "ENSURED" prints.
- **(5) FLEET-DOC HEADED BYPASS — `Start Here.md`.** The Playwright fallback (Priority 2) and the macOS→Linux substitution table flipped from `headless: false`/`headless=False` to headless-everywhere (`headless: true`) — the one path the env pin can't reach (Playwright doesn't read `AGENT_BROWSER_HEADED`). Macs have a display, so headed there took over a client screen.
- **Why no headed exception exists:** the GHL headless-canvas wall was already cracked, so every browser task runs headless. No legitimate headed use remains on any client box.
- **Versions.** All 10 repo markers → v14.1.4 (incl. `BROWSER_MANAGER_VERSION` + the 3 other browser markers rolled by `bump-version.sh`); `06-ghl-install-pages/skill-version.txt` → v14.1.4 (G3); `03-agent-browser/skill-version.txt` → v6.5.8 (G3, INSTALL.md changed). No client names; no secret values printed.

## [v14.1.3]  -  2026-06-25  -  install.sh Linux/Contabo portability — OS-aware archive extraction (ditto on Mac; unzip→python3 zipfile fallback on Linux), removed the browser.agentBrowser config write that crash-looped fresh 2026.6.8 containers + self-heal strip of any legacy key, additive positional-form fallback for `openclaw cron create` flag drift on 2026.6.8, new Linux Step 2.5 installing jq/unzip/python3-pip, pip3→`python3 -m pip`, Mac-gated the persona-inbox-watcher. Mac + Hostinger-VPS paths verified unchanged.

Hardens `install.sh` so it runs cleanly on a FRESH Contabo / Debian Bookworm OpenClaw container (the box that crash-looped) while leaving the verified Mac and Hostinger-VPS install paths byte-for-byte unchanged. Adversarially verified GO across all three platforms; `bash -n install.sh` passes.

- **OS-aware archive extraction.** Mac stays on `ditto`; Linux uses `unzip` with a `python3 -m zipfile` fallback so a container missing `unzip` still extracts the package instead of dying on "unzip: command not found".
- **Removed the `browser.agentBrowser` config write + added self-heal.** OpenClaw 2026.6.8 tightened the config schema to `additionalProperties:false`; the unknown `browser.agentBrowser` sub-key made a fresh container refuse to start (crash-loop, took a Contabo box down). The installer no longer writes the key and SELF-HEALS any pre-existing one via a backed-up, atomic JSON round-trip. The self-heal log message now reports the truth — it only says "legacy key stripped" when a key was actually removed; the no-op case says "no legacy key found".
- **`openclaw cron create` flag-drift fallback.** Additive positional-form fallback for the 2026.6.8 cron CLI flag drift, so cron creation does not hard-fail on the new build.
- **New Linux Step 2.5 — base runtime tools.** Installs `jq`, `unzip`, and `python3-pip` (OS-aware, real Debian apt resolved past the Linuxbrew shim) BEFORE the download/extract step; non-fatal if apt is unavailable (downstream guards cover the gap). The two `apt-get` calls now pass `-o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=60` so a held dpkg lock fails-fast (waits up to 60s) instead of hanging the whole installer.
- **`pip3` → `python3 -m pip`.** Portable across containers where `python3-pip` is installed as a module with no `pip3` on PATH, and guarantees packages land in the same interpreter the import check uses.
- **Mac-gated the persona-inbox-watcher** so it only wires on Mac (the Linux path no longer attempts a Mac-only watcher).
- **Verified.** Mac + Hostinger-VPS paths verified unchanged; Contabo path verified GO. `bash -n install.sh` passes. Version bumped to v14.1.3 across all 10 repo markers (bump-version.sh).

## [v14.1.2]  -  2026-06-24  -  kie adapters: decode KIE resultJson JSON-string in poll paths (gemini-omni recordInfo, veo3 record-info, gpt-image-2 recordInfo) so the result URL is extracted on client boxes — fixes the live render-proof failure

Fixes a REAL bug the live 30-second render proof exposed in the shipped Kie adapters (confirmed live against api.kie.ai). KIE's poll endpoints `/api/v1/jobs/recordInfo` and `/api/v1/veo/record-info` return the `resultJson` field as a **JSON-ENCODED STRING** (a string whose contents are themselves JSON), NOT an already-parsed object. The shipped adapters read it as if it were a dict and called `.get()` on the raw string, so the result URL was never extracted on a client box. This mirrors the fleet reference script `37-zhc-closeout/scripts/generate-celebration-video.sh`, which pipes `jq -r '.data.resultJson'` (raw string) into a **second** `jq` to parse it (lines 499 and 592) — the two-stage decode that made the live proof succeed.

- **Fix — defensive decode in both adapters.** New module-level `_decode_result_json(raw)` helper in `47-movie-producer/kie-adapters/tools/video/kie_video.py` AND `tools/graphics/kie_image.py`: `str -> json.loads()` (returns `{}` on empty/malformed/non-dict), `dict -> used as-is` (tolerates an already-parsed object), `other -> {}`. `import json` added to both modules. `resultJson` is routed through it before reading the output URL(s) in **all three** poll paths: video gemini-omni-video `createTask -> /jobs/recordInfo` (`_poll_gemini_omni`), video veo3_fast `generate -> /veo/record-info` (`_poll_veo`), image gpt-image-2 `createTask -> /jobs/recordInfo` (`_poll_task`). The veo fallback now decodes a JSON-string `resultJson` (previously it only handled an already-parsed dict and silently dropped the JSON-string case).
- **Proof — `47-movie-producer/scripts/test_kie_adapter_resultjson_decode.py` (NEW, HARD CI gate).** Exercises each adapter's REAL GET-poll method against a captured `recordInfo` response shape with `resultJson` as a JSON string (no network, no `KIE_API_KEY`; `tools.base_tool` stubbed so the adapters import standalone) and asserts the result URL is extracted; plus the defensive contract (str/dict/empty/malformed/None/non-dict) and a regression guard proving the pre-fix `(str).get(...)` raised `AttributeError`. 16/16 assertions pass. Wired into `qc-movie-producer.sh` as a HARD assertion (now 64 passed / 0 failed).
- **Versions.** `47-movie-producer/skill-version.txt` → v14.1.2; all 10 repo markers → v14.1.2. No client names; no operator KIE_API_KEY value; no OpenMontage AGPLv3 source vendored.

## [v14.1.1]  -  2026-06-24  -  cron reconcile pass + agent-browser-reaper command-kind/hourly + bash-3.2 compat + CI delivery guard (fleet-wide announce-spam + token-furnace permanent fix)

## [v14.1.0]  -  2026-06-24  -  feat(video): Movie Producer anti-bypass enforcement layer — manifest + deterministic gate-and-attest driver + receipt validators + binary ruleset + lockstep/Guard-A/GOOD-BAD CI

Promotes the Movie Producer (Video Production) department from PROSE-only doctrine (Rule-Zero, ffprobe, budget, "do not skip stages") to a MECHANICALLY-ENFORCED, un-bypassable pipeline — the same interlocking enforcement spine the Presentations department uses. A rule not auto-failed at a gate does not exist; every doctrine rule is now a manifest autofail WITH an enforcing py_symbol AND a triggering negative test. `scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` exit 0; existing locking gates re-run green (register/hash --check, presentations sync_check); the OpenMontage AGPLv3 source is NEVER vendored — `executive_producer.py` is OUR code that gates AROUND OpenMontage.

- **Single-source manifest — `universal-sops/video-pipeline-craft/VIDEO-PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json` (`manifest_version:1`).** Declares the 5 ordered DMAIC phases (V-DEFINE/V-MEASURE/V-ANALYZE/V-IMPROVE/V-CONTROL), each with `order`/`owning_role`/`sop_refs`/`produces_artifact`/`gate_codes`/`preflight`; 13 `autofails[]` (`code`/`stage`/`level`/`in_ruleset`/`enforced_by`/`py_symbol`/`secondary_py_symbols`); `roles[]` (the Movie Producer entry carries `trigger_phrases[]` + `routing_note` — "produce a video"/"documentary montage"/"finished MP4 from a script" → Movie Producer; "edit THIS footage" → Skill 27); `deliverables_required[]` (job-manifest, render-receipt, final MP4 with `min_bytes`); and a `handoff_targets[]` table (captions→26, TTS→30, edit→27, storyboard→24, delivery→Head of Video Production).
- **Deterministic gate-and-attest driver — `47-movie-producer/scripts/executive_producer.py`.** A state machine over the manifest writing `working/checkpoints/video_process_manifest.json`. Before phase N it asserts EVERY lower-order phase is attested (by its declared `owning_role`) with its `produces_artifact` on disk; missing/wrong-role = HARD ABORT `AF-VID-PHASE-SKIPPED` exit 2. The ONLY escape is a logged owner-authorized skip (`phase_skip_approvals.json` `owner_approved:true`) — exactly how the free documentary-montage path skips V-ANALYZE. A present-but-invalid receipt is exit 3 (e.g. `AF-VID-NO-FFPROBE`, `AF-VID-FABRICATED-RECEIPT`). Phase-0 Kie balance pre-flight HARD-ABORTS a paid job exit 4 (`AF-VID-KIE-BALANCE`) before any paid dispatch. `--adhoc` is refused without a logged `adhoc_authorization.json`. Exit codes: 0 clean / 2 precondition / 3 receipt-validation / 4 balance.
- **Receipt validators — `47-movie-producer/scripts/video_build_check.py`.** Every `enforced_by:executive_producer` py_symbol (`_chk_brief_complete`, `_chk_measure_receipt`, `_chk_provider_audit`, `_chk_budget_cap`, `_chk_rule_zero_approval`, `_chk_render_receipt`, `_chk_kie_receipt_real`, `_chk_native_provider`, `run_postflight_gate`, `_chk_budget_overrun`, `kie_balance_preflight`). Validation, not presence: V-IMPROVE requires render-receipt `ffprobe_pass:true` + duration>0 + a video stream AND (when `kie_in_scope`) a REAL non-placeholder `kie_task_id` + `kie_result_url` AND no native provider key in the recorded `generation_env`.
- **Binary ruleset — `universal-sops/video-pipeline-craft/MASTER-VIDEO-QC-AUTOFAIL-RULESET.md`** with a Section-5 machine-checkable table (one row per `in_ruleset:true` autofail), checked BEFORE any 1-10 scoring.
- **SOPs append attestations (replaces prose "do not skip stages").** The master DMAIC SOP (`SOP--automated-video-production-specialist-openmontage-sops.md`, slug UNCHANGED) now ends each phase (9.1→V-DEFINE … 9.5→V-CONTROL) with a mandatory `ATTEST` step driving `executive_producer.py --phase`; the free-path V-ANALYZE owner-skip is documented; the DMAIC `## Define/Measure/Analyze/Improve/Control` headers are preserved so the Skill-47 DMAIC-header gate stays green.
- **Meta-gates + CI.** `test_video_preflight.py` trips every gate with a deliberately-failing fixture and emits `af-coverage.json`; `video_sync_check.py` reconciles manifest↔driver↔build-check↔ruleset↔SOP/role in both directions (exit 4 on drift); `video_gate_integrity_check.py` (Guard A) proves declared==enforced==tested (exit 1 on a no-op/untested gate). `make-video-fixtures.sh` materializes a GOOD run (attests V-CONTROL exit 0) and a BAD bypass run (HARD-ABORTS exit 2). `.github/workflows/video-pipeline-lockstep.yml` runs all of the above plus the GOOD/BAD self-test, the no-client-names guard, and `qc-movie-producer.sh` — any non-zero exit blocks the merge. `qc-movie-producer.sh` extended to assert the whole spine present + green (60 passed / 0 failed).
- **Versions.** `47-movie-producer/skill-version.txt` → v14.1.0; all 10 repo markers → v14.1.0; `VIDEO-PIPELINE-MANIFEST.json` `manifest_version:1`; `_index.json` content-hash re-stamped for the edited master SOP. Operator owns the commit/PR/tag/release step.

The `video` department role keeps its registered slug `automated-video-production-specialist-openmontage` (file NOT renamed) and gets a new DISPLAY TITLE: "Movie Producer (Automated Video Production)". `scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh` exit 0; all locking gates re-run green (register/hash/persona/skill-QC); INVARIANT_OK printed by sync-extensions converge.

Residual skill-dir-path refs: 0 (the only remaining `47-openmontage-production` string is in this CHANGELOG's prior v13.8.19 historical entry, which is intentionally not rewritten).
- **Multi-SOP reframe.** The single SOP file (`SOP--automated-video-production-specialist-openmontage-sops.md`, slug UNCHANGED) is now the MASTER DMAIC index + binding Rule-Zero/budget control, and links to 5 new sibling SOP files in `video/sops/`, each with full DMAIC coverage: `SOP--movie-producer-rule-zero-budget.md` (Rule-Zero/budget, [VID-MP-RZ-1..5]), `SOP--movie-producer-documentary-montage-pipeline.md` ([VID-MP-DM-1..5]), `SOP--movie-producer-short-form-pipeline.md` ([VID-MP-SF-1..5]), `SOP--movie-producer-vsl-pipeline.md` ([VID-MP-VSL-1..5]), `SOP--movie-producer-cross-role-handoff.md` ([VID-MP-CRH-1..5]). `register-library-additions.py` auto-discovers the 5 new files; `sops[]` grows 105 → 110. Master file retains all 5 `## Define/Measure/Analyze/Improve/Control` headers so the Skill-47 QC DMAIC-header gate stays green.
- **Pudovkin "Film Technique" persona QC.** `22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/personas/vsevolod-pudovkin-film-technique/persona-blueprint.md` cleared `QC_PENDING → QC_PASS` after a real QC pass: 14/14 spec sections present, dual-mode separation intact, full Section-8 agent-governance framework, zero `{{TOKEN}}`/TODO/placeholder leakage, six self-ratings (avg ~9.4/10), no client names. QC-review note stamped in the header.
